page_title: I'm having trouble logging in, what should I do?
breadcrumb: I'm having trouble logging in, what should I do? > My password isn't working
text:
I'm having trouble logging in, what should I do? > My password isn't working

If you're a UQ student or staff member, you can go to the password reset page to reset your UQ account password.

Your username is based on your student number but is slightly different.

**If your student number begins with a 5 or a 4:**

Your username starts with an ‘s’ followed by the first seven digits of your student number (e.g. s5123456 or s4123456).

**If your student number begins with a 3:**

These are older student numbers and have two possible combinations. Your username will either start with an:

- ‘s’ followed by the middle six digits of your student number. Drop the first and last digits from your student number and use the remaining digits. For example, if your student number is 31234567, your username is s123456.
- 's' followed by the first seven digits of your student number. For example, if your student number is 31234567, your username is s3123456.

Most staff usernames begin with 'uq', followed by the first initial of your given name and the first five letters of your surname. For example: uqjblogg.

Because many staff members can have the same surnames and first initials, usernames can also contain numbers. For example: uqjblog3.

If you still can't log in successfully, you can call or visit us on campus.

Phone: (07) 3346 4312

Contact: AskUs in the UQ Library

Phone: (07) 3365 6000

Phone: (07) 3365 6000

Phone: (07) 3346 4312

Contact: AskUs in the UQ Library

page_title: How do I complete the oral exam date and time polling task?
breadcrumb: How do I complete the oral exam date and time polling task?
text:
How do I complete the oral exam date and time polling task?

How do I complete the oral exam date and time polling task?

The oral exam date and time polling task enables the Chair of Examiners to poll examiners on their availability for an HDR candidates oral examination. A step-by-step guide on how to complete this can be found at Set oral exam date and time

page_title: What is SECaT/ SETutor?
breadcrumb: What is SECaT/ SETutor?
text:
What is SECaT/ SETutor?

SECaT refers to the Student Evaluation of Course and Teaching surveys, while SETutor refers to the Student Evaluation of Tutor surveys.

Through the SECaT and SETutor surveys each semester, students are able to provide feedback on their educational experiences relating to course and teaching practices in their enrolled courses.

The SECaTs are administered for Course Coordinators and Lecturers, while the SETutors are primarily administered for Tutors.  

For more information, please visit ITaLI's SECaT and SETutor webpage. Alternatively, you may refer to the Student Evaluation of Course and Teaching Procedure in UQ's Policy and Procedure Library (PPL).

page_title: How do I send the SECaT/ SETutor survey links to my students?
breadcrumb: How do I send the SECaT/ SETutor survey links to my students?
text:
How do I send the SECaT/ SETutor survey links to my students?

For SECaT, their personalised Online Survey Portal link is automatically sent to student's UQ emails directly. The surveys will be accessible after students log in.

Staff members will receive their unique SECaT/SETutor survey link directing them to the subject management dashboard in which they can find and share their personalised QR code with students. 

You can copy the QR code from the subject management dashboard and add it to the promotion slide to be shared in class with your students.

page_title: When and how are the SECaT and SETutor surveys administered?
breadcrumb: When and how are the SECaT and SETutor surveys administered?
text:
When and how are the SECaT and SETutor surveys administered?

Typically, the surveys open on Week 12 of each semester (Week 7 of Summer Semester), and close on the last Friday before the Semester Examination period.

Students will be notified via their UQ student emails when the surveys open, and will be provided with a link to the Online Survey Portal. 

The surveys can be accessed through the following modalities:

- directly accessible through the Online Survey Portal.
- directly via the link provided in their student emails
- directly via Learn.UQ (blackboard) pop-up message

For more information, you may refer to the Academic Calendar, or visit ITaLI's Course Teaching Feedback webpage.

page_title: How can I check my SECaT or SETutor survey response rate?
breadcrumb: How can I check my SECaT or SETutor survey response rate?
text:
How can I check my SECaT or SETutor survey response rate?

How can I check my SECaT or SETutor survey response rate?

You can monitor response rates for your course/s through the Response Rate Dashboard and use Subject Management to check individual courses, access survey QR codes and links. A User Guide is available for further details.

page_title: How can I request the removal of abusive, malicious, or discriminatory comments in my SECaT/ SETutor evaluation report?
breadcrumb: How can I request the removal of abusive, malicious, or discriminatory comments in my SECaT/ SETutor evaluation report?
text:
How can I request the removal of abusive, malicious, or discriminatory comments in my SECaT/ SETutor evaluation report?

You may do so by following the steps outlined in Section 3, Paragraph 37-38 of the Student Evaluation of Course and Teaching Procedure. 

Such comments have already been identified and removed through our language scan process. Nevertheless, you may identify more of such comments.

You may also request for the removal of comments which breach UQ policies, including but not limited to: 

- Student Code of Conduct Policy
- Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy
- Freedom of Speech and Academic Freedom Policy

For more information, please visit ITaLI's Course Teaching Feedback webpage.

page_title: My students responded to my SECaT/ SETutor survey. Why did I not receive an evaluation report?
breadcrumb: My students responded to my SECaT/ SETutor survey. Why did I not receive an evaluation report?
text:
My students responded to my SECaT/ SETutor survey. Why did I not receive an evaluation report?

This is most likely because from Semester 1, 2024 onwards, evaluation reports will only be generated for surveys with 5 or more responses, in accordance with the Student Evaluation of Course and Teaching Procedure.

This means that if your survey had fewer than 5 responses, you will not get any quantitative and qualitative data. 

Should you require help with increasing response rates for your survey, do consider checking out the SECaT interview series or the SECaTs at UQ series, where academics with high SECaT response rates shared their experiences and tips!

page_title: Setting up Okta MFA
breadcrumb: Setting up Okta MFA
text:
Setting up Okta MFA

UQ is progressively moving to Okta as the University's new Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) service from early to mid-2026. Okta will not be available on your account until you receive your personalised activation email.

- How do I complete my initial MFA setup with Okta?
- How do I set up synced passkeys for Okta MFA?
- How do I set up Google Authenticator or other TOTP authenticator apps?
- How do I set up Okta MFA with a self-use YubiKey?
- How do I activate MFA for multiple accounts?
- How do I register a new phone or device for Okta MFA?
- How do I remove a phone or registered device from Okta Verify?
- How do I MFA in a location without mobile coverage?
- How do I generate a temporary access code for MFA?
- What if I can't scan the Okta QR code?
- What is Windows Hello and how do I activate it?
- Why am I receiving prompts for Duo?
- Can I use my Apple Watch for MFA with Okta Verify?
- Can I set up MFA if my device is not compatible with Okta, or if I want to use another TOTP app?
- My 'Welcome to Okta' email activation link has expired. What should I do?

When you’re invited to set up with UQ’s Okta MFA app, you have a number of ways to get started depending on your account type – student, staff or part of our broader UQ community (alumni and past students).

Students, alumni and past students - choose whichever option suits, including:

- Okta Verify - on your mobile phone or computer (see set up steps below)
- Synced (or 'roaming') passkeys - such as Apple iCloud Keychain, Google Password Manager, Edge Password Manager or Samsung Pass
- Google Authenticator or similar app - generates a code on your preferred TOTP app
- Using your own YubiKey - once Okta Verify is set up.

Staff must start with setting up Okta Verify – either on your mobile phone or computer. Once that is set up, you can use:

- Okta Verify – on your mobile phone or computer (see set up steps below)
- Synced (or 'roaming') passkeys - optional once Okta Verify is set up, using e.g. Google Password Manager, Apple iCloud Keychain
- Using your own YubiKey - optional once Okta Verify is set up
- Temporary access code (TAC) - for one-off situations where your usual method is unavailable.

Note: You will not be asked to purchase the Okta Verify app. If you are prompted to purchase the app, please ensure you have selected the right one.

While UQ transitions to Okta, please keep Duo installed on your mobile phone until you are told it is no longer needed. A small number of UQ systems will still use Duo until the transition is complete.

page_title: Setting up Okta MFA
breadcrumb: Setting up Okta MFA > How do I complete my initial MFA setup with Okta? > Option 1: Set up using your mobile phone
h2: How do I complete my initial MFA setup with Okta?
h3: Option 1: Set up using your mobile phone
text:
Setting up Okta MFA > How do I complete my initial MFA setup with Okta? > Option 1: Set up using your mobile phone

You’ll receive a ‘Welcome to Okta – activate your account’ email from UQ. This includes a unique activation link to begin setup.

You'll need:

- Your UQ username and password (Students: find out what your UQ username is)
- Internet access
- A compatible mobile phone (iOS or Android). If you do not want to use your personal mobile phone for MFA, use the ‘Option 2: Set up directly on your computer’ instructions.

If you do not want to use your personal mobile phone for MFA, use Option 2 - 'Set up directly on your computer' instructions.

To activate:

- Open the ‘Welcome to Okta – activate your account’ email.
- Select the ‘Activate your Okta account’ button.
- Download and install Okta Verify on your mobile phone from the App Store (iOS) or Google Play (Android).
- Open the Okta Verify app and follow the instructions.
- ‘Scan the QR code’ shown on your browser to link your account. See ‘What if I can’t scan the Okta QR code?’ if needed and follow the prompts.
- Once complete, your mobile device is registered with MFA.

You can also set up Okta Verify directly on your laptop or desktop. This enables Okta Verify's FastPass feature, which uses your device's built in security (Windows Hello or Apple Touch ID) to reduce MFA prompts.

Before you start:

- Set up Windows Hello (Windows - staff login required), view instructions for students, or
- Set up Apple Touch ID (Mac) on your computer.

To activate:

- Log into the computer you want to activate Okta Verify on.
- 
	Open your ‘Welcome to Okta - activate your account' email and click on the activation link.
- 
	Open the Okta Verify app on your laptop or desktop:- 
		UQ-managed computers - if you can’t find Okta Verify using your computer ‘search’, installation instructions for Windows or Apple can be found here.
- 
		Personal computers - download Okta Verify and install. View a Quick Reference Guide for Okta download options.
 
- 
		
- 
	Open Okta Verify and select 'Get started'.
- 
	Enter https://signin.uq.edu.au/ (UQ-managed computers will automatically have this shown).
- 
	Sign in with your UQ username and password. Approve the prompt.
- 
	Click Enable when prompted if you want to enable Windows Hello or Apple TouchID. If you don’t complete this step, you will not be able to use Okta Verify’s FastPass feature.

Once set up, Okta Verify's FastPass is used automatically for MFA on that device. For more information on the difference between Okta, Okta Verify and FastPass refer to this FAQ.

Passkeys and hardware tokens (such as YubiKeys) may be available as alternative MFA options, depending on your account - see Can I use passkeys for Okta MFA? or Can I use a hardware token or ‘dongle’ for Okta MFA? for setup requirements.

Note: Even if you use FastPass on a UQ-managed device, you should keep Okta Verify installed on your mobile device or smartphone. You may need it again if you switch devices, access UQ systems from another non-FastPass enrolled computer or are asked to re-verify your identity for security reasons.

page_title: Setting up Okta MFA
breadcrumb: Setting up Okta MFA > How do I set up synced passkeys for Okta MFA?
h2: How do I set up synced passkeys for Okta MFA?
text:
Setting up Okta MFA > How do I set up synced passkeys for Okta MFA?

If you want to use a synced (or ‘roaming’) passkey, you will need to set one up in your password manager of choice (e.g. iCloud Keychain or Google Password Manager), before following the steps below. This is something you do outside of Okta, in your own time, using your personal device or account. Once set up, Okta will detect it automatically - it won't create one for you.

Staff - you must enable Okta Verify on your phone or computer before adding a passkey to your account.

Set up a synced (or 'roaming') passkey using the following steps:

- Sign into your Okta ‘My Settings’ dashboard (https://signin.uq.edu.au/account-settings/home)
- Click on ‘Security methods’, ‘Manage Security methods’ button.
- Click on the 'Passkeys', ‘+ Set up’ button.
- Follow the prompts.
- Your device will automatically discover your browser-stored passkeys (e.g. Google Password Manager, iCloud Keychain).
- You will be prompted to verify your identity, and your passkey will be enabled.

UQ students, alumni and past students may use Google Authenticator or other preferred TOTP apps, in place of Okta Verify.

Before you start:

- You can select as part of your initial activation – by opening your 'Welcome to Okta - activate your account' email and select Activate your Okta account
- Or afterwards - by going to your Okta dashboard’s security methods, and select + Set up under Google Authenticator (https://signin.uq.edu.au/account-settings/security)

Then use the following steps when prompted to set up an authenticator:

- Select Google Authenticator. Despite the name, you can use any compatible authenticator app in the next step.
- Open your authenticator app, tap the + icon
- Select Scan a QR code
- Scan the QR code shown on your screen. Your account will be added automatically.
- If you can't scan the QR code (i.e you are already following these steps on your phone), select Can't scan? on screen and enter the setup key shown into your app manually instead.
- Enter the six-digit code generated by your app to verify setup is complete.

These are instructions to set up self-use YubiKeys only. Staff can purchase YubiKeys via UQeMarket. This is not the same process as using a UQ-lab assigned YubiKey.

The setup and ongoing use steps follow the general flow. Some prompts will look different depending on whether you are using Windows, macOS, or Linux. Follow the on-screen prompts.

- Okta Verify is already set up on either your mobile phone or computer.
- If your YubiKey already has a PIN, have it ready. If not, you will create one during setup. This PIN is unique to your YubiKey and separate from any other passwords or PINs you use. Keep a note of it, as you will need it each time you use your YubiKey.
- If you need to change or reset your YubiKey PIN, follow the Yubico manufacturer's instructions, before registering it for Okta MFA.

page_title: Setting up Okta MFA
breadcrumb: Setting up Okta MFA > How do I set up Okta MFA with a self-use YubiKey? > Initial setup
h2: How do I set up Okta MFA with a self-use YubiKey?
text:
Setting up Okta MFA > How do I set up Okta MFA with a self-use YubiKey? > Initial setup

- Connect the YubiKey to the device (computer / tablet).
- On your computer, go to your Security Methods on your Okta ‘My Settings’ dashboard (https://signin.uq.edu.au/account-settings/security)
- Click on the Set up link under the ‘Security Key or Biometric Authenticator’ section. You will be prompted to verify your identity via MFA again.
- On the ‘Set up security key or biometric authenticator’ pop-up, click on the Set up button.
- On prompt to 'Save your passkey', it will store on your computer by default. To store it on your YubiKey, plug in your YubiKey and select Change.
 Windows only: Windows may prompt you to use Windows Hello (your device PIN, face ID or fingerprint) first. If this happens, select Use a different device or More options to continue with your YubiKey.
- On prompt to 'Choose where to save your passkey', select Security key.
- On prompt to ‘Enter your security key PIN’, enter a PIN of your choice that is at least 4 digits and click OK.
	- First time using your YubiKey: create a PIN of at least 4 characters, confirm it, and click OK.
- YubiKey used before: enter your existing PIN and click OK.
 
- Touch your YubiKey (gold logo) - this is a presence check to confirm it is not being done remotely (this is not a biometric scan).
- You will now see your YubiKey registered under your Okta ‘Security Key or Biometric Authenticator’ items and receive a ‘MFA device activated’ email, by way of confirmation.

When you next sign in with Okta using your YubiKey, you can use your PIN.

If you have Okta Verify setup on your computer, it will attempt a FastPass MFA login using biometrics. Cancel that request first, before using your YubiKey.

- Connect the YubiKey to the device (computer/tablet).
- Go to https://signin.uq.edu.au and enter your username. Click Next.
- Click Verify with something else and then select ‘Security Key or Biometric Authenticator’.
- Select Security Key.
- Enter your PIN on the Sign in with a Passkey prompt and select OK.
- Touch your YubiKey (gold logo) - this is a presence check to confirm it is not being done remotely, not a biometric scan.
- You are now logged in.

Note: If you have a token with a generated code (e.g. Duo dongle), it is not compatible with Okta Verify. You will need to purchase a YubiKey or FIDO2 standard token.

Staff who use hardware tokens because they prefer not to use a personal mobile phone for MFA can alternatively use Okta Verify (FastPass) on their UQ-managed desktop. This lets you sign in using your desktop’s built in security (e.g. Windows Hello or Apple Touch ID). No personal device or hardware token is required. For instructions on how to register your UQ-managed laptop or desktop, refer to ‘How to install Okta Verify on your laptop or desktop’.

page_title: Setting up Okta MFA
breadcrumb: Setting up Okta MFA > How do I activate MFA for multiple accounts?
h2: How do I activate MFA for multiple accounts?
text:
Setting up Okta MFA > How do I activate MFA for multiple accounts?

If you have both a student and staff account, you will need to set up Okta MFA for both accounts. You will receive a ‘Welcome to Okta’ email for each account  (e.g. if you are both a staff and student at UQ, you will receive an email for each to that account’s email address).

When setting up MFA on your second account, your browser will need to be logged in with the account that requires MFA activation. To ensure you are logged in with the correct account, use one of the following solutions:

- Open a private or incognito browsing window.
 Set up a separate profile in your web browser, by following these instructions for Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge.
- Open a different web browser than you would normally use (e.g. Firefox, Chrome, Edge).
 Using the new browser window, follow the FAQ instructions ‘How do I complete my initial MFA setup with Okta?’.

Okta supports using the same phone number on multiple UQ accounts.

If you need to register a new or additional mobile device, follow these steps from your laptop or desktop. You will be prompted to sign in.

- Sign in to your Okta Dashboard (https://signin.uq.edu.au)
- Go to your 'My Settings' menu (click on this link, or find under your Okta profile top right dropdown).
- Select ‘Security methods’ from the left-hand menu.
- Under the ‘Okta Verify’ section, select ‘+ Set up another’. This will allow you to register a new mobile device.
- Enter your UQ account password when prompted.
- After successful confirmation of your password you will see a ‘Set up security methods’ setup assistant.
- Select ‘Okta Verify -> Set up now’.
- Download Okta Verify on your new mobile device from the App Store (iOS) or Google Play (Android).
- Open the Okta Verify app and follow the instructions. When prompted, scan the QR code shown on your computer. You have now successfully linked your account to your registered device.
- You will receive an email confirming you have successfully activated a new device.
- If there are any devices listed under ‘Security methods’ that you no longer use, select ‘Remove’ so it can no longer be used to access your account.

If you have lost, damaged or temporarily misplaced or forgotten the phone you have registered for Okta MFA, contact ITS Service Desk for assistance.

Go to your 'My Settings' menu (available under your Okta profile, top right dropdown).

- Select ‘Security methods’ from the left-hand menu.
- Under the ‘Okta Verify’ section, click on the ‘Remove’ button beside the device you want to deregister.
- You will be prompted to confirm you want to continue with de-registration of Okta Verify for that device. Click on ‘Yes’.
- Okta Verify will send you a push notification to confirm that you are initiating the action. Click ‘Yes, it’s me’ and complete the prompts.
- You have now successfully removed your device from Okta Verify.
- You will receive an email confirming you have deactivated your device.

If you have lost, damaged or temporarily misplaced or forgotten the phone you have registered for Okta MFA, contact ITS Service Desk for assistance.

page_title: Setting up Okta MFA
breadcrumb: Setting up Okta MFA > How do I MFA in a location without mobile coverage?
h2: How do I MFA in a location without mobile coverage?
text:
Setting up Okta MFA > How do I MFA in a location without mobile coverage?

If you are accessing UQ systems and cannot get mobile coverage for MFA, use the following steps to generate a one-time temporary code (i.e. OTP).

When logging into Okta:

- Enter your username.
- When prompted for MFA click the 'Verify with something else' link.
- Click the 'Okta Verify Enter a code' button.
- Open the Okta Verify app on your phone.
- Click the icon in the Okta Verify app, making note of the 6-digit code.
- Enter the 6-digit code in your web browser.

You can generate a temporary MFA passcode for a limited period, e.g. up to 48 hrs. Self-service temporary access codes (TACs) are available on your Okta user dashboard.

Note: Okta Temporary Access Codes replace Duo Bypass Codes.

To generate a TAC:

- Sign in to your Okta Dashboard (https://signin.uq.edu.au)
- Select the 'Request access' button under 'My Apps'
- Select the 'Request Temporary Access Code' tile
- Follow the prompts to select the length of time you require (maximum 48hrs)
- Scroll down to the Chat section of the screen to view your temporary access code. You will also receive an 'Okta Access Requests' email containing this code.
- Use the code when prompted to MFA.

Note: your TAC is valid starting immediately and will expire based on the length of time you have selected.

If you have lost, damaged or temporarily misplaced or forgotten the phone registered for Okta MFA or you have forgotten your Windows Hello PIN, contact ITS Service Desk for assistance.

If you’re unable to scan the QR code during set up:

- Click on the ‘Can’t Scan?’ button underneath the QR code image.
- You’ll be prompted to provide either your email or text for your registration link. We recommend using the text method for the mobile device you are registering, for a more seamless experience.
- If you select ‘text’, you will receive a text with a link. Click on the ‘Activate your Okta account’ link in the text. The link in the text expires after a short time, so be sure to complete the activation promptly.
- If you select ‘email’, enter your UQ email. You will receive an email titled ‘Okta Verify Push registration’. Click on the ‘Activate your Okta account’ link. The link in the email expires after a short time, so be sure to complete the activation promptly.
- For both methods, clicking on the link will open the Okta Verify app (if installed). If it’s not installed, you’ll be directed to download it first.
- When the app opens, follow the prompts to enable account activation. Tap ‘Done’ to complete the registration.
- Once activated, return to your browser to finalise any remaining steps.

page_title: Setting up Okta MFA
breadcrumb: Setting up Okta MFA > What is Windows Hello and how do I activate it?
h2: What is Windows Hello and how do I activate it?
text:
Setting up Okta MFA > What is Windows Hello and how do I activate it?

Windows Hello is required for Okta FastPass use on Windows devices. Windows Hello is a feature available in Windows 10 and Windows 11 that allows users to sign in to their devices without using a traditional password. Instead, it utilises biometric authentication methods, such as:

- Facial Recognition: Uses an infrared camera to recognise your face.
- Fingerprint Recognition: Requires a compatible fingerprint reader.
- PIN: A numeric code that serves as an alternative to passwords.

Note: The biometric data used to support Windows Hello is stored on the local device only. It doesn't roam and is never sent to external devices or servers. This separation helps to stop potential attackers by providing no single collection point that an attacker could potentially compromise to steal biometric data. Even if an attacker could obtain the biometric data from a device, it couldn't be converted back into a raw biometric sample recognisable by the biometric sensor.

During the transition to Okta, certain applications will continue to use Duo as an Identity Provider and Multifactor Provider (see list below). For most other UQ platforms, Okta will handle multifactor authentication.

Applications that will continue to use Duo through to 2026:

- Remote Access (RDP) for ITS and EAIT
- Remote Linux Command Line (SSH) for IMB, RCC-HPC and EAIT
- VPN connections using SBL

At the moment, no. Apple Watch cannot currently be used with Okta Verify to approve MFA requests. You’ll need to use your iPhone for authentication.

The current MFA policy requires a biometric (something you are) to confirm the verification. Okta treats both biometrics (Face ID / Touch ID) and a device PIN as valid options.

- On iPhone: this works as expected - you’ll see a Face ID/Touch ID prompt or be asked for your PIN.
- On Apple Watch, however, the PIN prompt isn’t passed through to Okta. As a result, the verification response fails and the authentication can’t complete.

page_title: Setting up Okta MFA
breadcrumb: Setting up Okta MFA > Can I set up MFA if my device is not compatible with Okta, or if I want to use another TOTP app?
h2: Can I set up MFA if my device is not compatible with Okta, or if I want to use another TOTP app?
text:
Setting up Okta MFA > Can I set up MFA if my device is not compatible with Okta, or if I want to use another TOTP app?

While we understand staff may have a preference for a different authentication app or prefer not to use a personal phone, there are several options available to you:

- Install Okta Verify on your computer: You don't need a mobile phone to use Okta Verify when installed on your computer. This is particularly recommended for UQ staff.
- Use a passkey: Passkeys are a secure, password-free way to verify your identity and are supported on most modern devices. See How do I set up synced passkeys for Okta MFA?
- Use a YubiKey: A YubiKey is a small physical security key that plugs into your device. See How do I set up Okta MFA with a self-use YubiKey?

If you are concerned your current device isn’t compatible, refer here for a list of Android and Apple operating systems and internet browsers currently supported by Okta.

If you are a student or part of our broader UQ community, you are able to set up Google Authenticator or similar app, which generates a code on your preferred TOTP app.

As a staff member, if you’re unable to use any of these options, or your device isn’t on the Okta-supported list, you may need to apply for a ‘TOTP exemption’. 

To apply for a TOTP exemption:

- Sign in to your Okta Dashboard (https://signin.uq.edu.au)
- Select the 'Request access' button under 'My apps'.
- Select the 'Request Exemption - Allow ToTP' tile
- Click the 'Request access' link
- Complete the 'Please outline the reason why this exemption is required' and 'How long is it required for?' fields
- Click 'Submit new request'.

You will receive an email within two business days from UQ’s Cyber Security Risk and Assurance team to advise you as to whether the request has been approved or rejected.

You can view your request history in your Okta ‘Requests’ dashboard.

If you did not activate your Okta account using the link in your email within 90 days, the link will have expired. This will not stop you from setting up Okta Verify.

You will be prompted to set it up the next time you log in to a UQ system. When prompted, follow the 'Set up security methods' steps — you do not need to use the activation link in your 'Welcome to Okta' email.

The activation link is provided as a convenient way to get started, but it is not the only way to set up your account.

page_title: Can I enrol after Census Date?
breadcrumb: Can I enrol after Census Date?
text:
Can I enrol after Census Date?

The University does not allow students to enrol or add courses after Census Date, unless there are extraordinary and exceptional circumstances.

If we approve late course enrolment after Census Date, you must pay all fees in full.

- I'm a Commonwealth Supported student (i.e. a student eligible for HECS-HELP).
 You must pay full fees for a course (or courses) before you are allowed to enrol. Under the Higher Education Support Act (2003) the University is not permitted to enrol a Commonwealth Supported student after Census Date. The alternative is to enrol you as a full-fee-paying student. Please note that because you are enrolling after Census Date you are also not eligible for FEE-HELP.
- I pay full tuition fees (i.e. international students, most postgraduate coursework students).
 You must pay all relevant tuition fees for the course (or courses) plus an administrative charge of $200 before you are allowed to enrol.

You must have the support of your faculty to enrol or add a course after Census Date. Late applications are assessed by the Academic Registrar.

page_title: I receive 'Authorised access required' or '403 access request' when attempting to open the COVID declaration form. What do I do?
breadcrumb: I receive 'Authorised access required' or '403 access request' when attempting to open the COVID declaration form. What do I do?
text:
I receive 'Authorised access required' or '403 access request' when attempting to open the COVID declaration form. What do I do?

At times, Single Sign-On can cause trouble for those who have both student and staff accounts. The best course of action to complete the COVID declaration would be to access the link provided in your email using a private or incognito browser. This will prevent auto-log in and ensure you are logging in with the correct account details.

To do so, please:

- navigate to your email notification and hover your mouse over the blue button with "Click here ...".
- right click and select "Copy Link"
- open an incognito or private browser window in either Chrome or Firefox
 (File -> new incognito window or File -> new private window)
- right click in the URL bar of the new incognito or private window and select "Paste and go..."

Log in with the correct credentials and follow the prompts on the form to submit your disclosure.

page_title: How do I remove or forget a wifi network?
breadcrumb: How do I remove or forget a wifi network?
text:
How do I remove or forget a wifi network?

It may help to remove or forget a wifi network if you:

- are having trouble connecting to wifi after changing your password
- can’t connect to wifi and your device isn’t asking for a username and password.

Your device may think it has the correct password saved when it doesn’t.

Here's what you need to do:

- Select the Start menu in your taskbar.
- Open 'Settings', then select 'Network & internet' on the left menu.
- Select 'WiFi', then select 'Manage known networks'.
- Click 'Forget' next to the wifi network you want to remove.

- Select Start menu in your taskbar at the bottom left of your screen.
- Go to 'Settings' and then 'Network & Internet'.
- Select 'WiFi' on the left menu, then select 'Manage known networks'.
- Select the network you want to remove, then click 'Forget'.
- Now, try reconnecting to wifi by re-entering your username and password.

- Click the Apple menu on the top left of the screen, then select 'System Preferences...'
- Select 'Network'.
- Select 'Wi-Fi' and then go to 'Advanced...'
- Select the network you want to remove and click the minus sign underneath the 'Preferred Networks' box.
- Now, try reconnecting to wifi by re-entering your username and password.

- Open 'Settings', then select 'Wi-Fi'.
- Tap the information (i) icon to the right of the network you want to remove.
- Tap 'Forget This Network'.
- Tap 'Forget' to confirm.
- Now, try reconnecting to wifi by re-entering your username and password.

- Open 'Settings', then select 'Wi-Fi'.
- Tap and hold the network you want to remove, then select 'Forget network'.
- Now, try reconnecting to wifi by re-entering your username and password.

page_title: Room Bookings at UQ
breadcrumb: Room Bookings at UQ
text:
Room Bookings at UQ

UQ is currently transitioning to a new resource booking system. During this period, two platforms are in use:

Rooms Plus - the new system, used for booking central learning spaces.

UQ Book It - used for booking other resources, including:

- Faculty/School timetabled learning spaces
- Non-timetabled learning spaces
- social areas
- equipment
- vehicles
- parking spaces

To book meeting rooms, see Booking meeting rooms with Outlook.

To book hot desks, see Reserve hot desks and spaces.

page_title: How do I activate Duo MFA on my mobile device?
breadcrumb: How do I activate Duo MFA on my mobile device?
text:
How do I activate Duo MFA on my mobile device?

To activate MFA on your mobile device, you’ll need:

- your mobile device (e.g. smartphone). If you don't have a smartphone, submit an IT request.
- a computer or other device.

Please ensure that your choice of mobile application for MFA (e.g., Duo) is downloaded before commencing the process of activating MFA.


Only download Duo and other reputable MFA applications (e.g., Google Authenticator, Microsoft Authenticator) from official sources like Google Play and the Apple App Store, or the Duo website. Do not use third-party applications, or a QR code-scanning application, during setup.


You will never be requested to pay for MFA – if you encounter a payment request in a third-party application, please exit immediately without proceeding.

Only download Duo and other reputable MFA applications (e.g., Google Authenticator, Microsoft Authenticator) from official sources like Google Play and the Apple App Store, or the Duo website. Do not use third-party applications, or a QR code-scanning application, during setup.

You will never be requested to pay for MFA – if you encounter a payment request in a third-party application, please exit immediately without proceeding.

- On your mobile device, download the Duo Mobile app from Google Play (Android) or the App Store (iOS).
- On your computer, go to the MFA Portal. If you have multiple UQ accounts, ensure you are logged in with the account that required MFA activation. If unsure, use an incognito/private browser window, or use a different web browser (e.g. Firefox, Chrome, Edge).
- Enter your date of birth and click ‘Submit’.
- Click ‘Add a new device'.
- Select the type of device you wish to add:
	- If you are using your mobile phone to activate MFA for your first or only UQ account, select 'Mobile phone' for the device type.
- If you are using your mobile phone to activate MFA for additional UQ accounts, select 'Mobile phone' for the device type. If this is not successful, select 'Tablet' as the device type.
 
- Enter your mobile number and click 'Continue'.
- Select your device's operating system and click 'Continue'.
- Click ‘I have Duo Mobile Installed’.
- On your mobile device, open the Duo Mobile app and tap the plus icon (+) to add a new account.
- Tap 'Allow' if the app asks for permission to access your camera, so it can open a QR code scanner. Focus it on the QR code (square barcode) on your computer screen.
- Tap 'Save'.
- On your computer, click 'Continue'.

You can now use your registered device when logging in to UQ websites and systems that require MFA. You will be prompted whenever this is necessary.

How do I get my student username and password?

If you're new to UQ, you'll need to activate your student account to receive your username and password.

If you're a previous student and have forgotten your password, you can reset your student account password.

If you're new to UQ, you'll need to activate your student account to receive your username and password.

If you're a previous student and have forgotten your password, you can reset your student account password.

page_title: How do I configure and access my computer's local storage on my virtual desktop?
breadcrumb: How do I configure and access my computer's local storage on my virtual desktop?
text:
How do I configure and access my computer's local storage on my virtual desktop?

Not available on the web browser version.

When you access your virtual desktop for the first time, a prompt will appear asking to access your computer's local storage. Click 'Allow' when this appears.

If you are denied access, or have cancelled the prompt, you can configure it using the instructions below.

- Minimise the screen of your virtual desktop.
- Right-click the Omnissa Horizon Client icon on the system tray in the bottom right, then select 'Settings'.
 
 
 
- Click 'Drive & Folder Sharing' within the left-hand menu.
- Click 'Add'.
 
 
 
- Select your default location folder. This is usually C:\Users\username.
- Click 'OK'.
- Click 'Apply', then 'OK'.

- Minimise the screen of your virtual desktop.
- Click 'VMware Horizon Client', then 'Preferences...'
 
 
 
- Select 'Sharing'.
- Click the '+' icon.
 
 
 
- Select the default location folder, then click 'Add'.

- Open the 'Start' menu, then select 'File Explorer'.
- Your computer's local storage will appear as a mapped network drive as shown in the image below.

page_title: What is a GPA (grade point average) and how can I calculate my GPA?
breadcrumb: What is a GPA (grade point average) and how can I calculate my GPA?
text:
What is a GPA (grade point average) and how can I calculate my GPA?

Your grade point average (GPA) is the average of the grades you have achieved in all courses in which you have enrolled, weighted by the unit value of each course.

See my.UQ for advice on how to calculate your GPA.

If you are studying at another institution, this GPA calculation method may not apply to you – contact our Domestic Admissions team at admissions@uq.edu.au or International Admissions team at study@uq.edu.au for advice.

page_title: How do I pin and unpin applications on my computer?
breadcrumb: How do I pin and unpin applications on my computer? > Windows
text:
How do I pin and unpin applications on my computer? > Windows

On Windows, you can group or arrange your most commonly used programs on both the **Start menu** and the **taskbar**.

#### Pinning an application

- Select the**Start menu**.
- Find the program you want to pin, and right-click it.
- Select **Pin to Start**.

#### Organising the application tiles

Select and drag each program’s tile to its new location.

#### Unpinning an application

- Select the **Start menu**.
- Right-click the program you wish to unpin.
- Select **Unpin from Start**.

#### Pinning an application

- Select the **Start menu**.
- Find the program you want to pin, and right-click > **More**>**Pin to taskbar**.

#### Unpinning an application

- Right-click the program you wish to unpin.
- Select **Unpin from taskbar**.

There are two ways to create shortcuts (or pin applications) to your Mac desktop or dock.

This method creates shortcuts in **both desktop and dock**.

- In Finder, locate the item you want to create a shortcut to.
- Select the programs or documents. You can select multiple items by holding **Control**and selecting each one.
- Hold the mouse button down, and drag the items to your desktop or dock.

This method creates shortcuts in the **dock only**.

- In Finder, locate the item you want to create a shortcut to.
- Select the item to add to the dock.
- Enter **Control-Shift-Command-T**.

To unpin applications from the desktop or dock, drag them to the **Trash**.

page_title: Where and how can I access my SECaT/ SETutor survey evaluation reports?
breadcrumb: Where and how can I access my SECaT/ SETutor survey evaluation reports?
text:
Where and how can I access my SECaT/ SETutor survey evaluation reports?

You can access evaluation reports on UQ Reportal, or via the Reporting of course and teaching feedback webpage on ITaLI's website.

Access will be dependent on the data access rules stated in Paragraph 47, Section 4 of the Student Evaluation of Course and Teaching Procedure. 

Please request for UQ Reportal access if you have not already done so. Do also note that reports are only released after the grades finalisation date for each semester. 

For more information, please visit ITaLI's Course Teaching Feedback webpage.

page_title: Is smoking permitted at the residential colleges?
breadcrumb: Is smoking permitted at the residential colleges?
text:
Is smoking permitted at the residential colleges?

Although UQ became smoke-free on 1 July 2018, UQ recognises the unique living arrangements of our residential college students. The new UQ Health, Safety and Wellness policy developed around smoking will not capture residential colleges as all colleges are independent of UQ. The limited number of smoking points around residential colleges are for college residents' use only, because these students live at these colleges. These college smoking areas will continue to be regulated by the respective colleges' smoking policies.

page_title: Which UQ Digital Workspace desktop has the software I need?
breadcrumb: Which UQ Digital Workspace desktop has the software I need?
text:
Which UQ Digital Workspace desktop has the software I need?

There are four types of UQ Digital Workspace desktops available to students, depending on course requirements:

- Student-Desktop (General use)
- Students-Desktop-SS (Application specific)
- Student-Desktop-GPU1 (Graphics processing unit enabled)
- Student-Desktop-GPU2 (Graphics processing unit enabled)

Below are lists of the software available to each desktop type. Please note:

- Not all software will be available unless permission has been granted to a course for its students to access the software. For example, if a course has only been granted access to the SPSS and Stata applications, only those applications will only be available.
- Software with license restrictions will not be available by self-service. These applications need to be requested by the course coordinator via our online form: https://support.staff.uq.edu.au/app/opa/uq_digital_workspace_request
- Please allow up to 2 weeks for new software to be added into UQDW. This will allow time for validation of use in a virtual environment and testing.

page_title: Are SECaT and SETutor responses confidential?
breadcrumb: Are SECaT and SETutor responses confidential?
text:
Are SECaT and SETutor responses confidential?

Student responses are confidential, and individually-identifiable data are not made available to academic staff. Additionally, to enhance confidentiality:

- No SECaT and SETutor surveys are generated when a course has fewer than 5 students enrolled.
- No evaluation reports are generated if a course has fewer than 5 responses.

UQ will keep a record of student information in relation to SECaT and SETutor to comply with its legal obligations.

For more information regarding confidentiality, please refer to Section 4 of the Student Evaluation of Course and Teaching Procedure.

page_title: Should I submit my immunisation record forms even if I have not completed all of the doses/screenings by the due date?
breadcrumb: Should I submit my immunisation record forms even if I have not completed all of the doses/screenings by the due date?
text:
Should I submit my immunisation record forms even if I have not completed all of the doses/screenings by the due date?

Yes. You must submit your immunisation records by the due date for verification and advice from the Immunisation Record Team (IRT).

Some vaccination schedules rely on minimum periods between doses and test dates and it can take several months to complete your record. By submitting your records by the due date regardless of whether it is complete, you may be granted a 'Conditional’ status which means you have met the minimal immunisation or screening requirements to begin placements. You will need to submit any outstanding evidence wihin a certain time frame to complete your record. You will be notified of any revised due dates when your record is reviewed. If you do no provide the outstanding evidence, you will become 'non-compliant' at the end of semester and unable to attend your placements until your record is fully complete.

page_title: What do academic advisors do and how can I make an appointment with one?
breadcrumb: What do academic advisors do and how can I make an appointment with one?
text:
What do academic advisors do and how can I make an appointment with one?

Academic advisors provide specialist help in course selection and can look at your individual study history to make personalised recommendations on your study plan.

If you have any questions about how to progress through your degree, or about what courses you need to complete to satisfy program requirements, you can make an appointment to meet with an advisor in your faculty:

- Faculty of Business, Economics and Law
- Faculty of Engineering, Architecture and Information Technology
- Faculty of Health, Medicine and Behavioural Sciences
- Faculty of Humanities, Arts and Social Sciences
- Faculty of Science

If you are studying a dual degree, you may belong to one or two faculties. Contact the relevant faculty that administers each program with any questions.

page_title: What happens if I refuse to stop smoking when I'm asked to?
breadcrumb: What happens if I refuse to stop smoking when I'm asked to?
text:
What happens if I refuse to stop smoking when I'm asked to?

The success of this approach will depend on the thoughtfulness, consideration and cooperation of smokers and non-smokers. All students and staff share in the responsibility for helping UQ campuses to go smoke-free. Anybody who continually refuses to comply with the smoke-free policy will be managed according to the relevant misconduct policies.

page_title: How do I update passwords on my phone or tablet?
breadcrumb: How do I update passwords on my phone or tablet?
text:
How do I update passwords on my phone or tablet?

Your device should automatically ask for your new password when you try to check email or access wifi.

If you aren't automatically prompted to enter your new password:

- 
	
		**For iPhone or iPad:**Follow the instructions for connecting to wifi or setting up your email and re-enter your UQ username and new password.
- **For other devices:**Go to your phone's settings. Submit an IT request for help.

page_title: Why can't I add a course in mySI-net?
breadcrumb: Why can't I add a course in mySI-net?
text:
Why can't I add a course in mySI-net?

If you're unable to add courses, it may be because:

- you haven't completed all of the tasks in mySI-net (student login required) – select the 'Tasks and Messages' tile to complete these.
- you haven't completed a mandatory requirement, such as providing a verifiable Unique Student Identifier (USI) or completing the Academic Integrity Modules.
- the cut-off date for adding courses has passed – see our advice on late enrolment.
- you've taken an interruption of studies – contact your faculty or school for assistance.
- you're a Study Abroad student – contact UQ International for assistance.
- you don't have permission to enrol in the course – contact your faculty or school for assistance
- you haven't been activated yet – contact your faculty or school for assistance.

For more information, see our advice on how to enrol.

page_title: How do I activate MFA for multiple accounts?
breadcrumb: How do I activate MFA for multiple accounts?
text:
How do I activate MFA for multiple accounts?

If you have both a student and staff account, you will need to set up MFA for both accounts.

When setting up MFA on your second account, your browser will need to be logged in with the account that requires MFA activation. To ensure you are logged in with the correct account, use one of the following solutions:

- Open a private or incognito browsing window.
- Open a different web browser than you would normally use (e.g. Firefox, Chrome, Edge).

Using the new browser window, follow the instructions at How do I activate MFA on my mobile device? to enable MFA for your second account.

DUO supports using the same phone number on multiple UQ accounts. Only one account will be shown in the DUO mobile app, when using the same phone number.

page_title: How do I clear my browser history (including cookies and cache)?
breadcrumb: How do I clear my browser history (including cookies and cache)? > Desktop browsers
text:
How do I clear my browser history (including cookies and cache)? > Desktop browsers

For most web browsers, you can use a short cut to clear your browser history:

- for Windows, use Ctrl + Shift + Delete, and
- for Mac, use Command-Shift-Delete.

If this doesn’t work, look for instructions for your browser in the list below.

If your browser isn’t listed, search in its Help menu for ‘clear browser history’ or ‘clear cache’.

If you can’t find your browser version, choose **About** from the Help menu or the browser menu. In Internet Explorer and Firefox, press **Alt** to show the menu bar.

Once complete, close all browser windows and reopen Chrome.

Open the links below to:

Open the links below to:

Follow the instructions at View and delete browsing data in Microsoft Edge, then close all browser windows and reopen Edge.

Follow the instructions at How to clear browsing history on mobile and desktop, then close all browser windows and reopen Opera.

How to clear your cache, cookies and history changes depends on your Android model and the browser you use. You should be able to clear your cache and data by following these steps:

- Go to **Settings**.
- Choose **Apps**or**Application Manager**.
- In the lists of installed apps, choose your web browser. Select **Clear Data**, then**Clear Cache**.

Follow the instructions at Delete your Chrome browsing history, then close all browser windows and reopen Chrome.

Follow the instructions at Delete your Chrome browsing history, then close all browser windows and reopen Chrome.

Follow the instructions at Clear the history and cookies from Safari on your iPhone, iPad, or iPod touch, then quit and reopen Safari.

page_title: How do I Duo MFA if I forget my device?
breadcrumb: How do I Duo MFA if I forget my device?
text:
How do I Duo MFA if I forget my device?

If you have forgotten your device, you can generate a temporary MFA passcode for a limited time period, e.g. 8 hours, by accessing the MFA Management portal:

- When asked to MFA, select 'Need help?'
 
 
 
- Click the MFA recovery link: https://identity.uq.edu.au/mfa/recovery/login
 
 
 
- Log in with your UQ username and password.
- Enter your date of birth, then click ‘Continue’.
- Answer the security questions that appear, then click ‘Continue’.
- Under 'Temporary MFA passcode', click 'Generate passcode'.
- Select the number of hours you will need access for and click 'Generate passcode'.
- Write or print the passcode on a piece of paper and keep it with you; the code will be valid for any MFA authentication within the specified time period.

page_title: What can I do if I am not immune to hepatitis B even though I've had a full course of hepatitis B vaccinations?
breadcrumb: What can I do if I am not immune to hepatitis B even though I've had a full course of hepatitis B vaccinations?
text:
What can I do if I am not immune to hepatitis B even though I've had a full course of hepatitis B vaccinations?

Those who do not have an adequate level of protective hepatitis B surface antibodies despite a full course of vaccinations should seek medical advice to discuss their options. Often a booster dose is recommended. This can be followed with a repeat blood test at least four weeks later to check for an immune response. If this does not achieve immunity, and you have been tested for hepatitis B virus infection, options can include further doses and possibly intradermal vaccinations.

For further information and guidance, please contact immunisation@uq.edu.au. Further information is also available in the Australian Immunisation Handbook.

page_title: How many people can attend a Zoom meeting or webinar?
breadcrumb: How many people can attend a Zoom meeting or webinar? > Zoom meetings
text:
How many people can attend a Zoom meeting or webinar? > Zoom meetings

A regular Zoom meeting can accommodate up to 300 participants at the same time. This can be increased to 1000 participants upon request.

Submit an IT request to request access to a Large Meeting licence and include a brief explanation of why the licence is required.

Zoom webinars can accommodate up to 1000 participants at the same time.

If you think your event will exceed 1000 participants, you can:

- hold multiple sessions, or
- provide a recording of the event to those unable to attend.

If these options are not suitable, you can purchase a Zoom Webinar licence which can accommodate up to 10000 participants. 

**Please note:**The minimum cost of a larger licence is currently about $3450.

Submit an IT request if you require further information.

page_title: What is my Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) number?
breadcrumb: What is my Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) number?
text:
What is my Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) number?

You can find your Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) number on the top right-hand side of the CoE issued to you by UQ. If you don't have a copy of your CoE, contact Student Visa Offcer to request a copy.

You can find your Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) number on the top right-hand side of the CoE issued to you by UQ. If you don't have a copy of your CoE, contact Student Visa Offcer to request a copy.

page_title: When are exam results released and where can I view my grades?
breadcrumb: When are exam results released and where can I view my grades?
text:
When are exam results released and where can I view my grades?

Final grades are available on mySI-net at the end of each semester. The release date is listed on the important exam dates page. Final grades are also released via SMS to Australian mobile numbers only. If you want to receive your grades via SMS, make sure your current Australian mobile number is entered into mySI-net.

To view an unofficial studies report which lists your final grades:

- Log in to mySI-net.
- Select the 'Enrolments' tile
- Select 'Studies Report'.

If you require an official academic transcript, see degree certificates, transcripts and documents.

**Please contact your course coordinator to request marks or feedback for individual exams and other assessment items.**

page_title: How do I change the resolution size of an image on a Windows PC?
breadcrumb: How do I change the resolution size of an image on a Windows PC?
text:
How do I change the resolution size of an image on a Windows PC?

Follow the instructions below to change the pixel resolution of an image stored on your Windows PC.

You may want to see the current resolution of an image to check if it needs to be resized.

- Using a Windows Explorer window, browse to the image you want to check, right-click on the file, then click Properties.
- Click the Details tab, then check the Dimensions field for the image's pixel resolution.

The easiest method for resizing an image on a Windows PC is to use the built-in Photos app.

- Using a Windows Explorer window, browse to the image you need to resize, then double-click on the file to open it. Unless you have changed the default application Windows uses to view images, the image should open with the Photos app. If the image does not open with the Photos app when double-clicked, you can instead right-click on the image's icon, mouse over 'Open with', then select the Photos app from the list.
- Click the 3-dot ... icon at the top of the image window, then click 'Resize image'.
- With the Pixels option selected, enter the required width of the image. The height field will update automatically to maintain the aspect ratio of the image.
 If you a resizing an image for use of UQ's digital signage displays, the required resolution is 1920x1080px.
- Move the Quality slider to 100%.
- Click Save, select a location to save the resized image, then click Save.

page_title: When should I start looking for accommodation in Brisbane?
breadcrumb: When should I start looking for accommodation in Brisbane?
text:
When should I start looking for accommodation in Brisbane?

We suggest you start looking for a place to live as soon as you have accepted your offer to study. Finding a permanent place to live takes time. You need to consider the properties you view carefully - after all this is where you will be living while you undertake your studies.

Generally, students who feel rushed or pressured are the ones who have problems with their accommodation later on because they take one of the first properties they inspect.

Prior to your arrival, you should try to shortlist and query potential properties on UQ Rentals. Please be aware that, the closer to semester students arrive, the more competition there is for long term accommodation. If you are travelling from an area outside of Brisbane, interstate or overseas and have not yet arranged accommodation, we recommend you book for around 1-2 weeks so you can physically inspect properties.

Please visit Short Term Accommodation for further information. 

Apart from our UQ approved on campus and off–campus accommodation, we recommend you do not sign any lease or pay any money until you have inspected the property. A lease is a legally binding document.

To learn more about your accommodation options, tenancy rules of renting in Queensland and more, contact the Accommodation Team – accommodation@uq.edu.au or 1300 275 870.

page_title: What is considered full-time study?
breadcrumb: What is considered full-time study?
text:
What is considered full-time study?

At UQ, a standard full-time study load is 8 units (generally 4 courses) per semester. 'Full-time' is defined as 75% or more of a standard full-time study load, i.e. 6 units (generally 3 courses) or more per semester .

A single course usually has a value of 2 units, but this can vary. To find the unit value of a course, search for the course on the Programs and Courses website. The unit value is displayed under 'units'. Your Electronic Course Profile (ECP) will also have the unit value in the 'course details' section.

International students with a student visa have to complete their program by the end date on their Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE). This means most students with a student visa enrol in 8 units per semester. If you are an international student and want to change your study load, contact your Faculty for advice.

If you are upgrading your selection rank through one year of full-time equivalent (1FTE) tertiary study, you must complete 16 units (8 units per semester). 15 units or less does not equal 1FTE for admission purposes. For more information or advice please contact UQ Admissions.

page_title: What is the difference between credit and exemption?
breadcrumb: What is the difference between credit and exemption?
text:
What is the difference between credit and exemption?

Where a student has undertaken formal, non-formal or informal learning prior to enrolling at UQ, that study, up to a specified limit, may be used or ‘credited’ towards a program of study at UQ. A credit transfer reduces the number of units required to complete your program.

An exemption may be granted where your prior learning is recognised as a substitute for a course or courses listed in your program requirements. An exemption does not reduce the number of units required for completion of your program. Instead you can take a different course in place of the one that has been exempted.

For information on applying for credit or exemption, please see the Future Students website or contact your Faculty.

page_title: What happens if I don't register a device for Duo MFA?

text:
What happens if I don't register a device for Duo MFA?

What happens if I don't register a device for Duo MFA? Home Information and services Until you register a device for MFA, you'll be unable to access some UQ websites and systems. If you require assistance, submit an IT request.

text:
How do I use my SECaT/ SETutor evaluation reports to improve my course/ teaching?

For more information, please visit ITaLI's webpages for the SECaT and SETutor, or access the Student Evaluation of Course and Teaching Procedure.

page_title: Why do I need to provide Hepatitis B dose dates as well as blood test results to confirm immunity (Form B requirements only)?
breadcrumb: Why do I need to provide Hepatitis B dose dates as well as blood test results to confirm immunity (Form B requirements only)?
text:
Why do I need to provide Hepatitis B dose dates as well as blood test results to confirm immunity (Form B requirements only)?

Programs such as Dentistry, Medicine, Midwifery and Nursing include higher risk activities that require more rigorous evidence of hepatitis B protection. 


A positive blood test indicates current immunity, however there is very little evidence to indicate this immunity will be long-lasting unless a full course of hepatitis B vaccinations has been completed. Please upload records of past hepatitis B vaccine doses along with any blood test results to confirm compliance. If no dose dates are provided, the Immunisation Record Team (IRT) may request additional dose(s).

page_title: What does ‘prerequisite’ mean in a course profile?
breadcrumb: What does ‘prerequisite’ mean in a course profile?
text:
What does ‘prerequisite’ mean in a course profile?

A prerequisite course provides the foundational knowledge needed to progress to the next course. You must have successfully passed any course/s listed as prerequisites of the course you want to enrol in. Prerequisites for enrolment in a course are listed on the course's electronic course profile (ECP) and the course page on the Programs and Courses website.

*Prerequisites for enrolling in a course are different to prerequisites for entry into a program.*

page_title: I can't access my SECaT/ SETutor survey, what do I do?
breadcrumb: I can't access my SECaT/ SETutor survey, what do I do?
text:
I can't access my SECaT/ SETutor survey, what do I do?

Please check that the surveys are currently open. Otherwise, here are some troubleshooting tips that may help address your issue: 

- Clear the browser cache using CTRL + F5.
- Use a different browser if one doesn't work.
- Give around 1 to 2 minutes for the survey to fully load, as there may be a significant number of other students trying to access the portal during the same time period. If it still doesn't work, a refresh might help.
- Use the UQ network or UQ VPN to access the survey.

page_title: As an international student, if I interrupt my studies can I stay in Australia?
breadcrumb: As an international student, if I interrupt my studies can I stay in Australia?
text:
As an international student, if I interrupt my studies can I stay in Australia?

If you are an international student and approved to interrupt your studies for compassionate or compelling reasons, you may have to return to your home country for the period of the interruption or you may be permitted to remain in Australia. This is at the discretion of the Department of Home Affairs.

Please note that UQ is required by law to notify the Department of Home Affairs when international students interrupt their studies. You can contact the Department of Home Affairs on 131 881 for advice.

See the my.UQ website for important information on interrupting your studies.

A testamur (also known as an award or degree certificate) is given to students when they graduate. See our my.UQ website for information on degree certificates, transcripts and documents.

page_title: Can the public use UQ Library's facilities at St Lucia?
breadcrumb: Can the public use UQ Library's facilities at St Lucia?
text:
Can the public use UQ Library's facilities at St Lucia?

UQ Library welcomes visitors from the public to use our spaces and on-site collections.

Our opening hours for Semester 1 and 2 vary across our libraries:

- Architecture and Music Library (Building 51): 7:30am–7:30pm Monday to Friday and 12pm–5pm on Saturday
- Biological Sciences Library (Building 94): 7am–10pm Monday to Friday and 7am-6pm, Saturday and Sunday
- Central Library (Building 12): 7am–10pm Monday to Friday and 7am–6pm, Saturday and Sunday
- Dorothy Hill Engineering and Sciences Library (Building 50): 7am–10pm Monday to Friday, and 7am-6pm, Saturday and Sunday
- Duhig Tower (Building 2): 7am–10pm Monday to Friday and 7am–6pm, Saturday and Sunday
- Fryer Library (Building 2): by appointment only, Monday to Thursday. Appointments between 9:30am and 12:30pm and 1:30pm and 4:30pm
- Walter Harrison Law Library (Building 1): 7am–10pm Monday to Friday, and 7am-6pm, Saturday and Sunday.

Please note, the libraries are not always staffed during these hours and opening hours at some locations are reduced outside of Semester 1 and 2 – we recommend you contact us before visiting.

**A UQ ID card is required for access to library spaces after hours.**

page_title: Who is authorised to certify copies of documents?
breadcrumb: Who is authorised to certify copies of documents?
text:
Who is authorised to certify copies of documents?

You must take the original documents to a representative who is authorised to certify them. The certifying person must be currently employed in one of the professions listed below:

- Justice of the Peace (who must also provide their registration number)
- Solicitor / lawyer / barrister
- Police officer ranked Sergeant or above, or in charge of a station, or
- Staff member of an Australian Embassy or Consulate.

In addition, for international students, the following are also acceptable:

- Notary Public,
- An Officer within the official records department or a person in a senior position (for example, Provost, Head of School, Dean) at the institution that originally issued the documents, or
- UQ's authorised representatives (i.e. agents).

How do I request an extension for my assessment?
