page_title: What courses does UQ offer over summer (December to February in Australia)?
breadcrumb: What courses does UQ offer over summer (December to February in Australia)?
text:
What courses does UQ offer over summer (December to February in Australia)?

Enrolling in Summer Semester courses means you can fast-track your degree.

UQ offers a selection of intensive undergraduate and postgraduate courses from award programs over the Australian summer (December to February). Most of these courses would normally take at least 1 standard semester to complete.

See the Summer Semester page for information on what courses you can study and how to enrol.

UQ also offers non-degree language courses during the summer at the Institute of Modern Languages.

page_title: How do I transfer files between my computer and the web browser virtual desktop?
breadcrumb: How do I transfer files between my computer and the web browser virtual desktop?
text:
How do I transfer files between my computer and the web browser virtual desktop?

If you are using the web browser version of the virtual desktop and need to access files on your computer, you can transfer them to and from the virtual desktop.

- In the virtual desktop, click the side bar menu button on the left of the screen.
- Click the 'Open File Transfer panel' button.
- Select the 'Upload' tab.
- Drag and drop the file(s) into the window, or select 'Choose Files'.

- Click the 'Start' menu, then open 'File Explorer'.
- Open 'Documents'.

If you need to access a file from within an application:

- When you are in the application's Open menu, type %userprofile%\Documents into the address bar, then press Enter.
- If you don't see the file, click the file type drop down and select 'All Files'.

- In the virtual desktop, click the side bar menu button on the left of the screen.
- Click the 'Open File Transfer panel' button.
- Select the 'Download' tab.
- With the 'Download' tab open, select the file(s) you want to transfer, then press 'Ctrl + C' on your keyboard to start the transfer.
 
 
 
- The file(s) will appear green. Click the download icon to save the file(s) to your computer.

page_title: I think antivirus software is causing my UQ computer to run slow. What can I do?
breadcrumb: I think antivirus software is causing my UQ computer to run slow. What can I do?
text:
I think antivirus software is causing my UQ computer to run slow. What can I do?

It's normal to experience a slight performance impact when your antivirus software scans your computer. Restart your computer to ensure you have the latest updates installed.

If your computer is running very slowly, submit an IT request for advice.

It's normal to experience a slight performance impact when your antivirus software scans your computer. Restart your computer to ensure you have the latest updates installed.

If your computer is running very slowly, submit an IT request for advice.

page_title: What is a practical or prac?
breadcrumb: What is a practical or prac?
text:
What is a practical or prac?

What is a practical or prac? Home Information and services A practical is a 'hand-on' class which allows you apply the theories you are learning in your course in practical situations. For example in a science course, practicals may include conducting experiments in a laboratory.

page_title: What is 'swotvac' and when is it?
breadcrumb: What is 'swotvac' and when is it?
text:
What is 'swotvac' and when is it?

UQ doesn't officially use the term 'swotvac' and instead uses 'revision period', which you will find on the academic calendar.

Swotvac is short for swot vacation. This is the week before end of semester examinations, when students have a week off from classes to study for exams.

page_title: What is the difference between withdrawing and interrupting when it comes to my Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) as an international student?
breadcrumb: What is the difference between withdrawing and interrupting when it comes to my Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) as an international student?
text:
What is the difference between withdrawing and interrupting when it comes to my Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) as an international student?

When you apply to withdraw from your program your Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) will be cancelled and the University will notify the Department of Home Affairs that you have ceased your studies. If you are already in Australia holding a student visa and withdraw from your program, you have 28 days to:

- leave Australia,
- enrol in an alternative program, or
- apply for a different visa.

If you choose to leave Australia your student visa will be subject to cancellation.

Interruptions to your study can only be approved in compassionate or compelling circumstances and are assessed on a case by case basis. If you have not provided sufficient supporting documentation, your request will be treated as a withdrawal and your CoE will be cancelled.

If your request to interrupt your studies is approved, your CoE will be suspended rather than cancelled. You may be permitted to remain in Australia during this period of interruption, however this is at the discretion of the Department of Home Affairs.

See the my.UQ website for more information about withdrawing from a course or program and interrupting your studies.

page_title: Does class attendance count towards the grade for my course?
breadcrumb: Does class attendance count towards the grade for my course?
text:
Does class attendance count towards the grade for my course?

It depends on which courses you are studying. For example, in some courses tutorial attendance forms part of the course assessment and will count towards your grade for that course. Check the 'Assessment' section in the course profile for each of your courses to find out if attendance is included as part of your assessment.

For further advice, contact your Course Coordinator. You can find your Course Coordinator's contact details under the 'Course Information' section of the course's profile.

page_title: What is a bridging program?
breadcrumb: What is a bridging program?
text:
What is a bridging program?

Bridging programs assist prospective students to meet program requirements. Depending on your previous study and the bridging program you select, completion of a UQ-approved bridging program (PDF, 60KB) may provide you with a selection rank and help you to meet subject prerequisites. Bridging programs may consist of both bridging courses and enabling courses.

**If you've completed at least one full-time year of bachelor-level (or higher) study, a bridging program cannot be used to increase your entry rank**. Contact UQ Admissions for advice on +61 7 3365 2203 or at admissions@uq.edu.au.

page_title: What are electives?
breadcrumb: What are electives?
text:
What are electives?

Most programs have compulsory and elective courses.

**Electives** are courses (subjects) you can choose, while **compulsory courses** are mandatory courses that you must study meet your program requirements. Electives, when added to your compulsory courses, make up the total number of units needed to complete your degree.

Electives allow you to study topics that interest you. Depending on your program, you may be able to select electives from other faculties. Your program rules specify how many electives you can study and where these can be selected from.

For a list of courses you can study and your program rules, search the Programs and Courses website for your program. Your course list and program rules are available on the program page.

page_title: How do I save my Microsoft Forms data?
breadcrumb: How do I save my Microsoft Forms data?
text:
How do I save my Microsoft Forms data?

To make a copy of the forms you have created in Microsoft Forms and save the collected data, please follow these steps:

- Open Microsoft Forms and go to the form you want to save.
- Click the ellipsis menu (three horizontal dots) under your profile picture for more form settings.
- Select 'Print Form'.
- A new dialogue box will open. In the 'Destination' drop down list, choose 'Save as PDF'.
- Click 'Save'. A new dialogue box will open where you can choose where to save the file. Choose a secure location, such as your local drive or an external storage device.

- With the selected form still open, navigate to the 'Responses' tab.
- Click the dropdown arrow next to the Excel button and select 'Download a copy'. This will download an Excel file with all the responses.
- Move the file from the Downloads folder to your chosen storage location.

If you have multiple forms, repeat the above steps for each one to ensure all data is saved.

page_title: I am an International student. Can I have the relevant immunisations and screening tests in my home country prior to commencing study at UQ?
breadcrumb: I am an International student. Can I have the relevant immunisations and screening tests in my home country prior to commencing study at UQ?
text:
I am an International student. Can I have the relevant immunisations and screening tests in my home country prior to commencing study at UQ?

Yes, you are strongly encouraged to have the relevant vaccinations in your home country prior to commencing your study at UQ as it can take some time to complete all requirements.

Before departure:

- Obtain a written record (in English) of all your vaccination and blood test results. Have all outstanding vaccinations prior to departure where possible.
- Complete the Tuberculosis (TB) self-assessment form. If coming directly from a country with a high TB burden, ideally the latent TB test should be 3 months after arrival into Australia (to exclude the incubation period).
- If your program requires a Student Immunisation Record Form (e.g. Form A or Form B for Healthcare Worker students), you should complete these with your medical practitioner provided the medical practitioner is able to complete the records in English.

On arrival:

- 
	If you still have outstanding vaccinations or screeninig tests, visit a medical practitioner or register for an appointment with UQ Health Care to complete the Student Immunisation Record form (if not completed in home country in English).
- 
	Bring your vaccination history documentation to your appointment.
- 
	Organise your TB screening if required. UQ Tuberculosis Screening Information Sheet

page_title: I have not been able to find all my vaccination records. Will I need to repeat them?
breadcrumb: I have not been able to find all my vaccination records. Will I need to repeat them?
text:
I have not been able to find all my vaccination records. Will I need to repeat them?

If you are unable to access your immunisation history, your medical practitioner will be able to guide you. If you are a healthcare student please take Immunisation Record Form A or Form B with you to your appointment. You **may** be able to have a blood test for some of the diseases to determine if you are already immune.

page_title: I want to take a break from my studies. What should I do?
breadcrumb: I want to take a break from my studies. What should I do?
text:
I want to take a break from my studies. What should I do?

See the my.UQ website for advice on interrupting studies. There are different requirements for domestic and international students.

We recommend discussing your situation with an academic advisor in your faculty.

See the my.UQ website for advice on interrupting studies. There are different requirements for domestic and international students.

We recommend discussing your situation with an academic advisor in your faculty.

page_title: How can I start studies in a program again after deferring or interrupting my studies?
breadcrumb: How can I start studies in a program again after deferring or interrupting my studies?
text:
How can I start studies in a program again after deferring or interrupting my studies?

You need to follow certain steps, depending on whether you deferred or interrupted your studies. See my.UQ for information on returning to study.

You need to follow certain steps, depending on whether you deferred or interrupted your studies. See my.UQ for information on returning to study.

page_title: What is the Medical College Admission Test (MCAT)?
breadcrumb: What is the Medical College Admission Test (MCAT)?
text:
What is the Medical College Admission Test (MCAT)?

Sitting the Medical College Admission Test (MCAT) is a requirement for entry into certain programs for international applicants.

The MCAT has four sections:

- Biological and Biochemical Foundations of Living Systems,
- Chemical and Physical Foundations of Biological Systems,
- Psychological, Social, and Biological Foundations of Behaviour, and
- Critical Analysis and Reasoning Skills.

The MCAT is conducted by the Association of American Medical Colleges (AAMC). See the AAMC website for more information on the MCAT exam and MCAT scores.

To find out if you need to sit the MCAT for entry to a program, search for the program on our Future Students website – entry requirements are listed under the 'Program details' tab.

page_title: How do I change my password?
breadcrumb: How do I change my password?
text:
How do I change my password?

Your UQ account provides access to various systems including email, mySI-net and Blackboard.

If you've forgotten your password, and you have a mobile phone number or personal email address registered in my.UQ dashboard, you can reset your UQ password online.

You can also change your UQ account password.

If you don’t have a mobile phone number or personal email address registered in my.UQ dashboard, contact AskUs.

For more information, see UQ accounts and passwords.

If you don’t have a mobile phone number or personal email address registered in my.UQ dashboard, contact the ITS Service Desk.

For more information, see UQ accounts and passwords.

If you have an "em" username you can change your password at the Change your UQ account password page. If you have forgotten your password contact AskUs to reset your password. We will email you with a temporary password and a link allowing you to reset it.

page_title: Where do I find the course profile for my course?
breadcrumb: Where do I find the course profile for my course?
text:
Where do I find the course profile for my course?

To view an course profile for a course you're enrolled in:

- Log in to mySI-net (student login required).
- Select the 'Enrolments' tile.
- Click on the blue information icon beside the course code.

You can also view a course's profile on the Programs and Courses website.

Some course profiles may not be available until the first week of classes.

page_title: How do I change a Team's name in Microsoft Teams?
breadcrumb: How do I change a Team's name in Microsoft Teams?
text:
How do I change a Team's name in Microsoft Teams?

Follow these steps to rename an existing Team within Microsoft Teams.

- Open Microsoft Teams on your desktop app or web browser.
- Click on the “Teams” icon in the left sidebar.
- Find the team you want to rename and click on the three dots (ellipsis) next to the team name.
- Select “Manage team” from the dropdown menu.
- Go to the “Settings” tab at the top of the team’s management page.
 Please note: If you do not have permission to manage the selected team, you will not see the "Settings" tab. Please contact the owner of the Team to request the required changes.
- In the “Team name and description” section, click the edit icon (pencil) next to the team name.
- Enter the new name in the text field provided.
- Click “Save” or press Enter to apply the changes.

page_title: What is meant by interruption to studies?
breadcrumb: What is meant by interruption to studies?
text:
What is meant by interruption to studies?

Interruption to studies refers to ceasing study for a period of time after starting a program. See my.UQ for more information on interrupting studies.

Interruption to studies refers to ceasing study for a period of time after starting a program. See my.UQ for more information on interrupting studies.

page_title: GraphPad PRISM licensing changes for 2026
breadcrumb: GraphPad PRISM licensing changes for 2026
text:
GraphPad PRISM licensing changes for 2026

In April 2025 Dotmatics, the vendor for GraphPad PRISM, was acquired by Siemens AG.

Dotmatics/Siemens has implemented a strategic price model change, introducing a complete shift in licensing metrics (named user) and a significant price increase. The new licence incurs a cost of $260 USD per user per annum.

The Digital Learning Sub-Committee has reviewed the proposed 2026 pricing for GraphPad Prism, which has unfortunately increased from A$220K/year to A$2.5M/year, and has determined this to be unsustainable. As a result, ITS will no longer centrally fund GraphPad licences for coursework students from Semester 1, 2026. Teaching staff are asked to consider the following options for courses that currently use GraphPad Prism:

- Use the free GraphPad Prism student licences available from the vendor for coursework purposes.
- Change to alternative software for statistical analysis and graphing, such as SPSS, STATA, JMP, R, JASP, or LabPlot.
- For faculties seeking a broader solution, a full procurement process can be initiated to source a replacement product at a more reasonable cost.

For more information about this decision, please book a consultation.

ITS has some funding available to support staff/RHD researchers in 2026 for GraphPad PRISM.

With the named-user GraphPad PRISM licence, you will be permitted to use your single licence on two devices, allowing it to be installed on both a work device and a home device.

A survey will be sent to existing research users of GraphPad PRISM in November 2025 to confirm if a licence is required for 2026.

Note: subsequent funding has not been finalised for research, and schools, faculties and institutes may be required to self-fund the $260 USD per licence from 2027. Other applications are available to use, which have appropriate site licensing, such as SPSS and JMP. Free and open-source options are also available, such as R, JASP, and LabPlot.

If you have any further questions regarding the licensing for research, please submit a request to ICT Software Licensing.

page_title: What is the census date?
breadcrumb: What is the census date?
text:
What is the census date?

The census date is the deadline to finalise your enrolment for each study period. By then, you need to have added or withdrawn all subjects that you wish to change from your original enrolment.

Your enrolment at the census date will determine your fee liability (the amount you owe for all fees and charges) for that study period.

The census date is also the deadline to:

- Arrange HECS-HELP, FEE-HELP or SA-HELP for that study period,
- Provide your Tax File Number (TFN) to the university, and
- (If you receive Commonwealth financial assistance and are in your first semester of study), provide a verifiable USI to UQ.

The census date varies according to your courses and their associated teaching periods. To check the census date for each of your courses, click on the 'Enrolments' tile in mySI-net (student login required). The census date is displayed in the first row of each course summary table. If you do not yet have access to mySI-net, search for the census dates on the important university dates webpage.

For more information about late enrolment, see enrolment and class allocation.

For more information about census dates and Commonwealth support, see the Australian Government's Study Assist website.

page_title: Which UQ Digital Workspace desktop has the software I need?
breadcrumb: Which UQ Digital Workspace desktop has the software I need?
text:
Which UQ Digital Workspace desktop has the software I need?

There are four types of UQ Digital Workspace desktops available to students, depending on course requirements:

- Student-Desktop (General use)
- Students-Desktop-SS (Application specific)
- Student-Desktop-GPU1 (Graphics processing unit enabled)
- Student-Desktop-GPU2 (Graphics processing unit enabled)

Below are lists of the software available to each desktop type. Please note:

- Not all software will be available unless permission has been granted to a course for its students to access the software. For example, if a course has only been granted access to the SPSS and Stata applications, only those applications will only be available.
- Software with license restrictions will not be available by self-service. These applications need to be requested by the course coordinator via our online form: https://support.staff.uq.edu.au/app/opa/uq_digital_workspace_request
- Please allow up to 2 weeks for new software to be added into UQDW. This will allow time for validation of use in a virtual environment and testing.

page_title: What do I do if I can't sit my exam?
breadcrumb: What do I do if I can't sit my exam?
text:
What do I do if I can't sit my exam?

Students are expected to be available to sit their exams at the scheduled date, time and campus of their course enrolment.

If you can't sit your exam (e.g. if you are unwell), you may apply for a deferred exam which will be scheduled within a set period. Refer to my.UQ for information on deferring an exam.

If you require alternative exam arrangements (e.g. disability, timetable clashes), see my.UQ for information on alternative exam arrangements.

page_title: How can I get a letter confirming that I'm a student at UQ?
breadcrumb: How can I get a letter confirming that I'm a student at UQ?
text:
How can I get a letter confirming that I'm a student at UQ?

A standard enrolment statement can be used to prove current enrolment and to demonstrate that The University of Queensland is accredited as a university.

This document lists details including:

- your name, date of birth and student number
- your program name
- your attendance (full-time or part-time)
- study dates of your current and/or future enrolment.

Other types of enrolment statements include confirmation of address, jury duty, Sponsored Students and UQ accreditation.

For further information about enrolment statements and other certified documents, see my.UQ.

Another option (not available for HDR students) is to print an enrolment status report from mySI-net. The PDF version of this document lists:

- your name, date of birth and student number
- full details of your program and course enrolment.

To view the report:

- Log in to mySI-net (student login required).
- Select the 'Enrolments' tile.
- Select 'Enrolment Status Report' from the side menu.

page_title: What can I do if I'm having problems connecting on the UQ Digital Workspace?
breadcrumb: What can I do if I'm having problems connecting on the UQ Digital Workspace? > Make sure your Omnissa Horizon Client is up-to-date.
h3: Make sure your Omnissa Horizon Client is up-to-date.
text:
What can I do if I'm having problems connecting on the UQ Digital Workspace? > Make sure your Omnissa Horizon Client is up-to-date.

If you're using a UQ computer, you can use the Company Portal (Windows) or Self Service (Mac) to self-install the latest version of the Omnissa Horizon Client.

If you're using a personal computer, or UQ computer with no self-install option, visit the Omnissa Website to download the latest version.

Virtual applications and desktops work best if you have an active, stable internet connection. If you're using a virtual application or desktop through 3G or 4G networks, your connection may be slower than through wifi or cable connections. You'll also need to be aware of your mobile data usage.

Disable any VPN clients running in the background.

- Disable any pop-up blockers
- Using a HTML5 capable browser such as Microsoft Edge, Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox.

The following ports are required to be open in your computer or network firewall to use the UQ Digital Workspace:

- TCP 443 (HTTPS)
- TCP/UDP 8443 (Horizon BLAST)

page_title: What is a graduate diploma?
breadcrumb: What is a graduate diploma?
text:
What is a graduate diploma?

A graduate diploma is an Australian Qualifications Framework (AQF) level 8 postgraduate coursework qualification that typically requires 1 year (or 2 semesters) full-time enrolment or equivalent to complete.

You can search for graduate diploma programs on the Future Students website.

Some disciplines offer articulation pathways – these begin at graduate certificate level and progress to graduate diploma level and then to master's level (or beyond). As a general rule:

- a graduate certificate is the equivalent of one semester of full-time study,
- a graduate diploma is the equivalent of one year of full-time study, and
- a master's by coursework may take between one year and two years of full-time study and includes a research component.

page_title: I can't connect to wifi on campus. What should I do?
breadcrumb: I can't connect to wifi on campus. What should I do?
text:
I can't connect to wifi on campus. What should I do?

If you've tried to connect to wifi on campus but it's not working, here are some things you can try.

If you’re trying to connect to eduroam, make sure you’re entering your username in the format **username@uq.edu.au**. For example:

- s1234567@uq.edu.au for students
- uqjblog3@uq.edu.au for staff.

If you're a visiting student or staff member from another university, use the same eduroam login details as you normally use at your own institution. For example, QUT students should use **username@qut.edu.au**.

To check you're using the correct password, try signing in to another UQ system, like my.UQ or mySI-net. If you get an error, try resetting your password.

If you can't connect to wifi and your device isn't asking for a username or password, try removing (or forgetting) the wifi network and then reconnecting.

This often happens if you've recently changed your password.

Sometimes background processes in your device’s memory can get locked or interfere with each other. Restarting your device will often resolve problems.

If you’re using iOS, try resetting your network settings:

- Launch 'Settings' then go to 'General'.
- Select 'Reset'.
- Select 'Reset Network Settings' and enter your passcode.

- Download 'geteduroam' from the Google Play Store.
- Open the app, search for **The University of Queensland**, then select it.
- Tap 'Next'.
- Enter your:
	- UQ account username in the format **username@uq.edu.au**
- UQ account password.
 
- UQ account username in the format 
- Select 'Connect to network'.

If you're using Android 8 (Oreo), Android 9 (Pie) or Android 10, check your settings match these:

- EAP method: PEAP
- Phase 2 authentication: MSCHAPV2
- CA certificate: Do not validate
- Domain: uq.edu.au

- IP settings: DHCP
- Proxy: None

For some Chinese phones (e.g. Huawei), you may need to select a different authentication setting:

- 
	Phase 2 authentication: MSCHAPSV2

Make sure:

- 
	your timezone is set to 'Australian Eastern Time Zone UTC+10:00' 
- 
	the time on your device is the same as the local time. 

This is particularly worth checking if you’ve just arrived from interstate or overseas. If your device is set to a different timezone it can cause problems with network security checks.

It's best to always run the latest version of an operating system where possible.

If you’re using:

- 
	a Mac, make sure your operating system is as up-to-date as possible. 
- 
	Windows, make sure any security updates and antivirus software are current. 

It may not be possible to connect to eduroam using older devices or systems (particularly Windows XP). Older technologies may not support the required encryption (WPA2/AES encryption).

page_title: What is QTAC?
breadcrumb: What is QTAC?
text:
What is QTAC?

QTAC is the Queensland Tertiary Admissions Centre. QTAC processes applications for the majority of the undergraduate and selected postgraduate programs offered by Queensland universities.

If you're a domestic prospective student, you apply through QTAC for undergraduate and selected postgraduate UQ programs.

If you are an international prospective student currently studying Australian Year 12 or the International Baccalaureate in Australia, you apply through QTAC for undergraduate UQ programs.

All other international prospective students should submit an application directly to UQ or through an approved UQ agent in your country.

For more information, see our advice on applying to study at UQ.

page_title: What do I do after accepting my offer?
breadcrumb: What do I do after accepting my offer?
text:
What do I do after accepting my offer?

We’re excited you’re going to join us at UQ!

The following resources will step you through everything you need to know about becoming a UQ student.

**If you are an undergraduate or postgraduate coursework student:**

Follow the steps on the Starting at UQ website.

**If you are a higher degree by research (HDR) student:**

Follow the steps on the Get Started page.

page_title: Who will be policing the smoke-free UQ initiative?
breadcrumb: Who will be policing the smoke-free UQ initiative?
text:
Who will be policing the smoke-free UQ initiative?

In creating a healthier environment, UQ encourages all staff, students and visitors to be part of a positive culture change. Transitioning to smoke-free sites requires respect for others – both non-smokers and smokers.

Staff, students, visitors and contractors smoking on campus can expect to be reminded of the smoke-free initiative by others and requested to either discontinue smoking or to move off campus if they wish to continue smoking.

An educative and supportive approach will be embraced by UQ, with a view to providing information about the smoke-free policy and campus boundaries to those who continue to smoke on university grounds.

page_title: I changed my password and now Outlook doesn't work, what should I do?
breadcrumb: I changed my password and now Outlook doesn't work, what should I do?
text:
I changed my password and now Outlook doesn't work, what should I do?

If you are on-campus and using a UQ Windows computer, log off and and log back in to synchronise your new password with UQ systems.

If you are off-campus and using a UQ Windows computer, you can log off and connect to the VPN before logging back in.

If you are on-campus or off-campus and using a UQ Mac computer, follow the instructions to update your UQ password on a Mac device.

When you reopen Outlook, log in with your UQ username and password on the UQ Authenticate pop-up screen to reconnect.

page_title: Where can I see my exam results?
breadcrumb: Where can I see my exam results?
text:
Where can I see my exam results?

Contact your course coordinator for individual assessment results, including exam results. See my.UQ for information on final grades.

Contact your course coordinator for individual assessment results, including exam results. See my.UQ for information on final grades.

page_title: How can I prepare for the CASPer test?
breadcrumb: How can I prepare for the CASPer test?
text:
How can I prepare for the CASPer test?

Examples, types of questions and practice tests are available on the CASPer website. You are strongly advised to not respond to any suggested training programs that may appear when you access the CASPer website. These are often costly and are not helpful.

For more information about how UQ is using the CASPer test, see Applying for the Bachelor of Veterinary Science (Honours).

page_title: How do I do a manual scan with the antivirus software on my UQ computer?
breadcrumb: How do I do a manual scan with the antivirus software on my UQ computer? > Windows
text:
How do I do a manual scan with the antivirus software on my UQ computer? > Windows

To scan all files on your computer:

- Open 'File Explorer'.
- Select 'This PC'.
- Right-click your C drive and select 'Scan with Cortex XDR'.

To scan a folder or file, right-click the folder or file and select 'Scan with Cortex XDR'.

There is no manual scan available on Apple macOS computers.

page_title: How do I access my previous immunisation records?
breadcrumb: How do I access my previous immunisation records?
text:
How do I access my previous immunisation records?

There are several options for obtaining your immunisation history.

- You can access your immunisation history statement if you have registered for myGov. Sign into your account and access the 'Medicare' or 'My Health record' sections if you have registered for these. The information on your immunisation history statement comes from the Australian immunisation register (AIR) which lists your childhood immunisations received in Australia since 1996 and adult immunisations received from October 2016 including COVID-19 vaccinations.
- For immunisations that don't appear on the history statement, you will need to contact your vaccine service provider, such as your medical practice, hospital, local council or travel clinic, to obtain a written record.
- A family-held vaccination record such as your ‘baby health book’, an official medical statement or document recording vaccines received at school is acceptable.
- All immunisation evidence must be provided in English with your identity clearly visible.

Further information is available on the Department of Health Website at https://www.health.gov.au/health-topics/immunisation/getting-vaccinated/check-immunisation-history.

It is advisable to keep your immunisation records indefinitely.

page_title: How can I get a degree completion statement?
breadcrumb: How can I get a degree completion statement?
text:
How can I get a degree completion statement?

A completion statement can be used to confirm that you've completed the requirements of your program before you graduate. This statement meets the current requirements of the Department of Home Affairs and most consulates.

A completion statement can only be issued once your grades have been finalised and all program requirements have been met.

This document lists details such as:

- your name, date of birth and student number
- your program name
- the date you started the program
- your attendance (full-time or part-time)
- the date you met your program requirements
- your graduation ceremony date
- the program's language of instruction
- confirmation that you've completed the program's academic requirements and the date you officially completed these on.

To request a completion statement, see my.UQ.

page_title: Can I enrol in a course that is not within my program?
breadcrumb: Can I enrol in a course that is not within my program?
text:
Can I enrol in a course that is not within my program?

Some programs at UQ allow you study electives in another discipline. The number of elective units that you can undertake and where these can be selected from is listed in your program rules. However, if you wish to enrol in an extra course that is not within your program rules, see my.UQ for information on studying courses outside your program rules.

page_title: When will I know the outcome of my request for an assignment extension?
breadcrumb: When will I know the outcome of my request for an assignment extension?
text:
When will I know the outcome of my request for an assignment extension?

When will I know the outcome of my request for an assignment extension?

We endeavour to assess the majority of applications within 48 hours. Ensure you allow sufficient time for processing and provide all the required documentation. Requests submitted without the correct supporting documentation will delay the outcome.

page_title: Should I install the 32-bit or 64-bit version of Office 365 on my personal computer?
breadcrumb: Should I install the 32-bit or 64-bit version of Office 365 on my personal computer? > Windows computers
text:
Should I install the 32-bit or 64-bit version of Office 365 on my personal computer? > Windows computers

In most cases, you should install the 64-bit version of Office 365. The 64-bit version is installed by default when you download it from Microsoft Online (UQ login required).

You should choose to install the 32-bit version if you still work with older applications and add-ins that may not be compatible.

For more information about choosing the 64-bit or 32-bit version, see the Microsoft Office knowledge base.

You can't have both the 32-bit and 64-bit versions installed at the same time. You'll first need to uninstall the current version of Office 365 on your computer.

Once you've uninstalled the current version, to change versions:

- Go to Microsoft Online (UQ login required).
- Click on your profile icon at the top right of the screen.
- Select ‘View account’.
- Select 'Office apps' on the left menu.
- Select ’32-bit’ or ’64-bit’ in the ‘Version’ drop-down menu.
- Click ‘Install Office’.
- Follow the on-screen instructions to complete the installation.

Only the 64-bit version of Office 365 is available for Mac computers.

page_title: What letters can I add after my name once I graduate?
breadcrumb: What letters can I add after my name once I graduate?
text:
What letters can I add after my name once I graduate?

Once you graduate, you can add post-nominal letters (award abbreviations) after your name to show your educational qualifications. For example, if you graduated from the Bachelor of Arts, your post-nominal letters would be BA.

To find your award abbreviation, search for your program on Programs and Courses and select the year you commenced under 'Program Year'. The award abbreviation is shown next to the program title.

page_title: What is the difference between the 32 unit, 24 unit and 16 unit master's programs?
breadcrumb: What is the difference between the 32 unit, 24 unit and 16 unit master's programs?
text:
What is the difference between the 32 unit, 24 unit and 16 unit master's programs?

Some disciplines offer master's programs of varying lengths – usually either 16 units, 24 units, or 32 units. For example, the following programs are available:

- Master of Biotechnology (16 unit)
- Master of Biotechnology (24 unit)
- Master of Biotechnology (32 unit)

The number of units reflects how many courses need to be completed to satisfy the program's requirements. At UQ, most courses are 2 units each.

The 32 unit program includes foundation courses, and depending on your previous qualifications and/or experience, you may be better suited to a 24 unit or 16 unit program.

If you are trying to choose between different programs in a suite, pay careful attention to entry requirements.

Some disciplines offer articulation pathways; these begin at graduate certificate level and progress to graduate diploma level, and then progress to master's level (or beyond). As a general rule:

- a graduate certificate is the equivalent of one semester of full-time study
- a graduate diploma is the equivalent of one year of full-time study
- a masters by coursework may take between one year and 2 years of full-time study and usually, although not always, includes a research component.
- a master's degree which includes a research component can be used for entry into UQ Higher Degree by Research programs.

Search our Future Students website to find the available programs in your discipline. For assistance with selecting a program, Ask UQ.

page_title: What do I do if I've lost my phone or Duo MFA-enabled device?
breadcrumb: What do I do if I've lost my phone or Duo MFA-enabled device?
text:
What do I do if I've lost my phone or Duo MFA-enabled device?

If you don't have access to your MFA device, you can:

- login to the MFA Management portal and generate a temporary MFA passcode (valid for up to 24 hrs), or
- register another device for MFA if you have one available.

If you think your device is permanently lost or stolen, you should remove the device from MFA so that it can no longer be used to access your account.

Submit an IT request for further assistance regarding lost or stolen devices.

- Login to the MFA Management portal by answering your personal security questions.
- Under 'Temporary MFA passcode', click 'Generate passcode' and choose the number of hours you'll need access for.
- Click 'Generate passcode'.

Write or print the passcode on a piece of paper and keep it with you.

When you are prompted for MFA, select 'Other options, then 'Duo Mobile passcode' and enter your temporary passcode.

- While in the MFA Management portal, under 'Device management', select 'Add a new device' and follow the steps.
- Whenever you are prompted for MFA, select ‘Other options’ and choose this device.

- While in the MFA Management portal, under 'Device management', locate the device and select 'Remove'. You can easily add the device again later if it's found.
- Submit an IT request for further assistance regarding lost or stolen devices.

page_title: How do I get feedback on my exam?
breadcrumb: How do I get feedback on my exam?
text:
How do I get feedback on my exam?

If you require feedback on your exam, contact your course coordinator by email to make arrangements.

See the my.UQ website for information on querying a result.

If you require feedback on your exam, contact your course coordinator by email to make arrangements.

See the my.UQ website for information on querying a result.

page_title: As an international student, if I withdraw from my studies how long will I be able to stay in Australia?
breadcrumb: As an international student, if I withdraw from my studies how long will I be able to stay in Australia?
text:
As an international student, if I withdraw from my studies how long will I be able to stay in Australia?

If you are in Australia on a student visa and you withdraw from your program, you have 28 days to:

- leave Australia,
- enrol in an alternative program, or
- apply for a different visa.

If you choose to leave Australia, your student visa may be cancelled.

UQ is required by law to notify the Department of Home Affairs when international students withdraw from their studies. You can contact the Department of Home Affairs on 131 881 for advice.

See the my.UQ website for important information on withdrawing from a course or program.

page_title: Where can I find a Justice of the Peace (JP) or a Commissioner for Declarations (Cdec) on campus?
breadcrumb: Where can I find a Justice of the Peace (JP) or a Commissioner for Declarations (Cdec) on campus?
text:
Where can I find a Justice of the Peace (JP) or a Commissioner for Declarations (Cdec) on campus?

Justice of the Peace and Commissioner of Declaration services are currently unavailable on campus.

You can find a list of available Justices of the Peace on the Queensland Government's website.


Justice of the Peace and Commissioner of Declaration services are currently unavailable on campus.

You can find a list of available Justices of the Peace on the Queensland Government's website.

page_title: About Okta MFA
breadcrumb: About Okta MFA
text:
About Okta MFA

- What's the difference between Okta, Okta Verify and Okta FastPass?
- Does multi-factor authentication (MFA) use my data on my smartphone?
- Why do I need to use multi-factor authentication (MFA)?
- What devices or methods can I use for multi-factor authentication (MFA)?
- Do I need to use Okta MFA every time I log in?
- Why don't all UQ sites use MFA?
- **How do I MFA in a location without mobile coverage?**
- **Current Country Blocklist**
- **Okta Data Residency**

- Okta - the sign-in and identity management platform
- Okta Verify - the MFA app within Okta, that confirms it's really you when you sign in
- Okta FastPass - an optional feature of Okta Verify that lets you sign in securely using your laptop or desktop's built-in security, such as Windows Hello or Apple Touch ID, without using your mobile phone to MFA.

Okta Verify neither stores nor accesses personal data on your MFA-registered device.

Okta Verify push authentication requests require a minimal amount of mobile data that falls well within a typical push notification.

Cyber attacks are becoming increasingly prevalent in today’s technology landscape. Passwords and usernames are often hacked and then used to log into other online accounts. Multi-factor authentication (MFA) will significantly increase your UQ account’s security and protect it from compromise.

If a hacker gets access to your UQ account, they could potentially obtain access to sensitive University data, or your personal information. Consequent data breaches can have severe personal, business and financial implications.

There are several options available for using Okta Verify for MFA.

- Mobile devices are a convenient and most common way to use MFA. You will need to install the Okta Verify app from Google Play (Android) or the App Store (iOS) on your phone.
- Installing Okta Verify on your desktop or laptop. This method also allows you to use your device’s built-in security (such as Windows Hello or Apple Touch ID) for a passwordless experience. Staff are encouraged to use this method on their UQ-managed laptop or desktop. View a Quick Reference Guide.
- Passkeys are an additional method for completing MFA. Refer to ‘Can I use passkeys for Okta MFA?’.
- Hardware tokens or 'dongles' (e.g. Yubikeys) can also be used by staff, in place of using a personal mobile phone. You must first set up Okta Verify on your UQ-managed desktop or laptop. Refer to 'Can I use a hardware token or 'dongle' for Okta MFA?'

There is no cost associated with using Okta Verify, however you will need to have network connection to complete the MFA activity.

If you don’t have a network connection, follow the FAQ instructions for how to ‘MFA in a location without mobile coverage’.

page_title: About Okta MFA
breadcrumb: About Okta MFA > Do I need to use Okta MFA every time I log in?
h2: Do I need to use Okta MFA every time I log in?
text:
About Okta MFA > Do I need to use Okta MFA every time I log in?

MFA is required when accessing UQ websites and services that use UQ Authenticate.

The frequency will differ depending on whether you are using a UQ-managed device or a personal (unmanaged) device for your desktop or laptop to access UQ systems.

If you are using an assigned, UQ-managed desktop or laptop:

- Once you have been registered for Okta, you will only require an initial login using your computer’s local security method (e.g. passcode, fingerprint or face ID), per session.
- Until the Okta rollout has completed (mid-2026), certain applications will continue to use Duo. For more information, please refer to the FAQ under Setting Up MFA section: Why am I receiving prompts for Duo?

If you are using a personal desktop or laptop:

- In most cases, you will need to MFA daily, when accessing UQ systems.
- Always carry your authenticator (i.e. smartphone with app) when accessing UQ websites and services.

Our goal is to connect all UQ websites and systems to multi-factor authentication (MFA), to enhance the security at UQ. As we continue to implement these new security measures, you will see more sites connect to MFA.

If you are accessing UQ systems and cannot get mobile coverage for MFA, please contact the ITS Service Desk, and they will provide you with access to the below workaround method.

When logging into Okta:

- Enter your username.
- When prompted for MFA click the 'Verify with something else' link.
- Click the 'Okta Verify Enter a code' button.
- Open the Okta Verify app on your phone.
- Click the icon in the Okta Verify app, making note of the 6-digit code.
- Enter the 6-digit code in your web browser.

As per the Okta IP Access Policy, the following countries are blocked from accessing and authenticating to Okta:

- Cuba
- Iran
- North Korea
- Syria
- Crimea
- Donetsk People's Republic (DNR)
- Luhansk People's Republic (LNR) of Ukraine

As UQ moves to Okta Verify for MFA, staff traveling to the countries listed above will not be able to log into UQ systems that require authentication.

Note: In the past it was possible to configure a bypass for Duo MFA; however, as the Okta platform manages the entire authentication process, a bypass of MFA no longer resolves the issue.

Okta, as an organisation, is headquartered in the United States; however, all data pertaining to UQ, including personal data, is stored in Australia, primarily in Sydney, with Melbourne serving as the disaster recovery site.

This has met IRAP PROTECTED certification.

Okta + Data Residency 

Okta Security Trust Center | Powered by SafeBase

The most sensitive data that is stored is on the User Profile, which is very similar to the information stored in LDAP.

          Okta Security Trust Center | Powered by SafeBase

The most sensitive data that is stored is on the User Profile, which is very similar to the information stored in LDAP.

page_title: I'm going on holidays and don’t want to pay for my permit parking. What can I do?
breadcrumb: I'm going on holidays and don’t want to pay for my permit parking. What can I do?
text:
I'm going on holidays and don’t want to pay for my permit parking. What can I do?

If you're going on extended leave, you may be able to make changes to your permit. For more information, see our advice on cancelling permits while on leave.

If you're going on extended leave, you may be able to make changes to your permit. For more information, see our advice on cancelling permits while on leave.

page_title: What happens to my UQ student email if I'm not enrolled or after I graduate?
breadcrumb: What happens to my UQ student email if I'm not enrolled or after I graduate? > UQ Graduate (before 2024)
text:
What happens to my UQ student email if I'm not enrolled or after I graduate? > UQ Graduate (before 2024)

If you graduated from UQ before 2024, you will retain access to your alumni mailbox.

If you enrol in a new course at UQ, your alumni mailbox will continue to be available for personal use, while a new student mailbox will be created for the duration of your enrolment.

Your student mailbox will close 6 months after your conferral date; however, you will be notified before this occurs. Any correspondence sent to this mailbox will then be forwarded to your personal email address registered with my.UQ.

If you enrol in a new course at UQ, your previous student mailbox will be reissued for the duration of your enrolment.

If you return to study at UQ, your alumni mailbox will continue to be available for personal use, while a new student mailbox will be created for the duration of your enrolment.

After an allotted period of inactivity, your student mailbox will be closed; however, you will be notified before this occurs. Any correspondence sent to this mailbox will then be forwarded to your personal email address registered with my.UQ.

If you return to study at UQ, your student mailbox will be reissued for the duration of your enrolment.

page_title: What is a semester?
breadcrumb: What is a semester?
text:
What is a semester?

The university teaching year is divided into three semesters:

- Semester 1 – generally February to June,
- Semester 2 – generally July to November, and
- Summer Semester – generally December to February.

In most programs, Summer Semester is optional. For more specific dates, see the academic calendar.

page_title: How does My eQuals work?
breadcrumb: How does My eQuals work?
text:
How does My eQuals work?

My eQuals is a platform for universities to issue digitally certified electronic documents to their students. Students and graduates maintain access to the documents for life and can share these academic records and other certified documents with third parties online – controlling who can see their documents and for how long.

page_title: How do I set up Duo MFA if my device is not compatible with Duo, or if I want to use another app?
breadcrumb: How do I set up Duo MFA if my device is not compatible with Duo, or if I want to use another app?
text:
How do I set up Duo MFA if my device is not compatible with Duo, or if I want to use another app?

If your device is not compatible with the Duo app, or if you'd prefer to use an alternative software-based app option, you can set up a Time-based one-time password (TOTP).

- On your phone, download and install an alternative software-based app option. This could be:
- On your computer, login to the MFA Management portal. If you don't have the device currently registered for MFA, you can login to the MFA Management portal by answering your personal security questions.
- Select 'Add a new device'.
- Choose 'Time-based one-time password (TOTP)', then click 'Continue'.
 
 
 
- Click 'Create TOTP'.
 
 
 
- Open your alternative software based authenticator app on your phone and scan the QR code on screen.
 
 
 
- A 6-digit number will display on the app which you can use to MFA. The number keeps changing after a short period of time.

When prompted to MFA, enter the generated 6-digit number generated from your app, then click 'Verify'.

page_title: What is the difference between undergraduate and postgraduate programs?
breadcrumb: What is the difference between undergraduate and postgraduate programs?
text:
What is the difference between undergraduate and postgraduate programs?

The first type of study a student completes at university is generally referred to as an undergraduate program. This may be an associate degree, bachelor degree or certificate.

An undergraduate program can be followed by a postgraduate program. This may be a bachelor honours degree, graduate certificate, graduate diploma, master’s degree or doctoral degree.

To find an undergraduate or postgraduate program, search for the field that you would like to study on the Future Students website, and on the results page filter for undergraduate or postgraduate.

page_title: How do I transfer credit for previous studies undertaken at another university?
breadcrumb: How do I transfer credit for previous studies undertaken at another university?
text:
How do I transfer credit for previous studies undertaken at another university?

Other tertiary courses you have completed can be considered for credit transfer only if their content and workload are comparable to UQ's courses. To see what credit or exemptions you may be eligible for, see our advice for undergraduate and postgraduate students.

Credit is only assessed for current UQ students. If you're a current UQ student (i.e. you have received an offer and accepted a place in a UQ program), read the applying for credit information, complete the credit or exemption request (student login required) online and upload details of the courses (course profiles) previously studied along with a copy of your official transcript (either scanned or via secure URL such as My eQuals).

Complete applications usually take at least 2 - 4 weeks to process.

page_title: What is the program and course catalogue?
breadcrumb: What is the program and course catalogue?
text:
What is the program and course catalogue?

The program and course catalogue (also known as the Programs and Courses website) is the official, complete list of programs and courses offered by UQ.

The program and course catalogue (also known as the Programs and Courses website) is the official, complete list of programs and courses offered by UQ.

page_title: What is a qualification?
breadcrumb: What is a qualification?
text:
What is a qualification?

A qualification is a formal record awarded at the completion of successful study.

Qualifications include, but are not limited to:

- Senior Secondary Certificate of Education
- Certificate I
- Certificate II
- Certificate III
- Certificate IV
- Diploma
- Advanced Diploma
- Bachelor Degree
- Graduate Certificate / Graduate Diploma
- Masters Degree
- Doctoral Degree

When applying you will be required to show evidence of all previous studies which you have completed or attempted.

page_title: What sports clubs are at UQ?
breadcrumb: What sports clubs are at UQ?
text:
What sports clubs are at UQ?

UQ Sport offers a broad range of sporting clubs.

At the St Lucia campus you can join the AFL, athletics, baseball, basketball, beach volleyball, boat (rowing), boxing, canoe, cheerleading, cricket, cycling, diving, football (soccer), handball, hockey, judo, karate, kung fu, mountain, powerlifting and weightlifting, rugby league, rugby union, sailing, swimming, table tennis, tae kwon do, tennis, touch football, triathlon and adventure racing, ultimate disc, volleyball or wakeboard and waterski clubs.

At the Gatton campus, you can join the hockey, netball, rugby union or volleyball clubs. UQ Sport also offers many other non-club sporting and fitness activities such as bubble soccer, dancing, pilates, and martial arts.

page_title: Can international students at UQ go on exchange?
breadcrumb: Can international students at UQ go on exchange?
text:
Can international students at UQ go on exchange?

Yes, you can participate in the semester-based exchange as long as you meet the required eligibility criteria.


However, you cannot study on exchange in your country of passport if you hold a temporary residency visa for Australia (international student visa or provisional Business Innovation and Investment visa (subclass 188)).

page_title: Can I get a discount on my fees?
breadcrumb: Can I get a discount on my fees?
text:
Can I get a discount on my fees?

No, there are no discounts offered on your University fees.

If you are offered a discount on your fees by a third party, do not use that service, as it may potentially be a scam.

Scammers may contact you on social media or be recommended by a friend and offer an arrangement where you pay them a discounted amount and they undertake to pay the full amount to the University on your behalf. They make payment to the University using stolen credit card(s) and these payments are eventually declined.

Victims lose the money they paid to the scammer and several weeks into semester they find out their fees are still outstanding. Stay up to date with the latest scam warnings on Scamwatch.

page_title: How do I check what version of Zoom is installed on my device?
breadcrumb: How do I check what version of Zoom is installed on my device? > Windows and Mac computers
text:
How do I check what version of Zoom is installed on my device? > Windows and Mac computers

- Within the Zoom app, select your profile icon on the top-right of the screen.
- Select 'Help', then 'About Zoom Workplace'.

- Within the Zoom app, select 'Settings' on the bottom-right of the screen.
- Select 'About'.

page_title: How do I access the Library's online resources?
breadcrumb: How do I access the Library's online resources?
text:
How do I access the Library's online resources?

Use Library Search to find and access online books, the full text of journal articles, databases and other resources. When searching:

- use the search categories drop-down menu to search for different types of materials, such as databases
- ensure you are signed in to the Library website for more results and/or easier access
- filter your results to show resources that are available online.

**If you are unable to view a resource**:

- access may be limited to UQ staff and students:
	- UQ alumni members – see Resources for UQ Alumni
- community, hospital or other Library members – see Databases for Library walk-ins
 
- licensing restrictions may apply that limit availability (e.g. limits on the number of users, restrictions on off-campus access or a restriction to a particular group of users)
- you can place a request for journal articles, book chapters and other materials.

Access to materials can sometimes be complicated. Contact us if you have problems accessing the resource you need.

page_title: Am I an international or domestic applicant?
breadcrumb: Am I an international or domestic applicant?
text:
Am I an international or domestic applicant?

You are a domestic applicant if you are currently:

- a citizen of Australia
- a permanent resident of Australia (including humanitarian visas), or
- a citizen of New Zealand.

You are an international student if you are currently:

- a temporary resident of Australia
- a permanent resident of New Zealand, or
- a resident or citizen of any other country.

If you are a dual citizen of Australia, you are considered a domestic applicant.

If you are applying, or intend to apply for permanent residency in Australia, you will still need to apply for admission at UQ as an international student. If permanent residency is granted during the application process you will need to provide further documentation.

If you are currently in Australia on a permanent or temporary resident visa, you will be required to provide some additional information so that UQ can verify your status and study rights.

page_title: How do I select my major or change my major (add or change my plan in SI-net)?
breadcrumb: How do I select my major or change my major (add or change my plan in SI-net)?
text:
How do I select my major or change my major (add or change my plan in SI-net)?

Plan is the term used to describe your major, specialisation, minor, extended major or field of study in your UQ program. If you want to change your undergraduate or postgraduate program, see the information on myUQ.

Before declaring or changing your plan in SI-net, check your program rules. Your program may not allow changes within a specific number of units before completion. You'll find your program rules on the Programs and Courses website.

- Log in to mySI-net (student login required)..
- Select the 'Enrolments' tile and then click the 'Plans' link.
- Select your plan and click 'Save'.

If you have already changed your plan in SI-net and you no longer have the option of changing your plan, contact your faculty or school for further advice. The faculty can assist you in nominating your chosen plan.

page_title: How do I map my student home drive on my computer?
breadcrumb: How do I map my student home drive on my computer?
text:
How do I map my student home drive on my computer?

If you're using a UQ computer on-campus, your student home drive will be automatically mapped when you log in.

If you're using a personal computer and are off-campus, connect to the VPN first, then follow the instructions below to map your student home drive.

- Open the 'Start' menu, then select 'File Explorer'.
- Select 'This PC' from the left-hand side menu.
- Select the 'Computer' tab, then click 'Map network drive'.
- Change the 'Drive' field to 'H:'.
- Enter the following path in the 'Folder' field
 \\student-home.storage.uq.edu.au\home
- Untick the 'Reconnect at sign-in' box.
- Tick 'Connect using different credentials'.
- Click 'Finish'.
- Enter your UQ username in the format uq\uqusername (e.g. uq\s4123456) then enter your password and click 'OK'.

- From your dock, open 'Finder'.
- Open the 'Go' menu at the very top of your screen and select 'Connect to Server...'
- Enter the following path as the 'Server Address':
 smb://uqusername:*@student-home.storage.uq.edu.au. For example: smb://s1234567:*@student-home.storage.uq.edu.au
- Click the + icon to add the drive to your 'Favourites'. Click 'Connect'.
- Select 'Registered User'.
- Enter your UQ account username in the format uq\uqusername (e.g. uq\s4123456).
- Enter your UQ password. Select 'Connect'.

page_title: Do I have access to the Library during the Summer break?
breadcrumb: Do I have access to the Library during the Summer break?
text:
Do I have access to the Library during the Summer break?

New students have access to the Library six weeks prior to the start of semester. You are considered to be a new student if you previously had a library record which has expired or if you have withdrawn or interrupted your studies.

For example if you:

- were enrolled in Semester 2 you should have uninterrupted access to the Library over the break
- graduated and completed your program last semester, and are enrolled in courses for the coming semester, you will have access to the Library six weeks prior to the start of semester.

page_title: When asking for an assignment extension or a deferred exam, what should I do if I didn't get a doctor's certificate within a reasonable time?
breadcrumb: When asking for an assignment extension or a deferred exam, what should I do if I didn't get a doctor's certificate within a reasonable time?
text:
When asking for an assignment extension or a deferred exam, what should I do if I didn't get a doctor's certificate within a reasonable time?

If you are applying for an extension of an assessment on medical grounds, you must obtain a medical certificate on or before the assessment item's due date and time. If you are applying to defer an exam on medical grounds, you must obtain a medical certificate no later than two business days after the date of the original examination.

If you obtain a certificate after the due date for the relevant application, your doctor must explain how the nature of your condition made it impossible, or unsafe, for you to have obtained a medical certificate sooner.

See my.UQ for more information about applying for an assessment extension or deferring an exam.

page_title: Who is available to help me with more personal matters?
breadcrumb: Who is available to help me with more personal matters?
text:

Staff and their immediate family members can access up to 6 sessions of free, confidential counselling or coaching support online health and wellbeing resources.
