page_title: How can I get help completing an Extend Studies and New Confirmation of Enrolment form?
breadcrumb: How can I get help completing an Extend Studies and New Confirmation of Enrolment form?
text:
How can I get help completing an Extend Studies and New Confirmation of Enrolment form?

We recommend applying for a new CoE at least three months before your visa is due to expire. Visit the my.UQ website for information on renewing your visa or requesting a new CoE.

You can only submit an Extend Studies and New Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) request if you are an international student and are currently, or have previously been, active in a UQ program.

If you are having trouble submitting the form, check the following:

- all required fields are completed
- there are no error messages displayed on the form
- you are submitting the form using a Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox browser.

If you still need help, contact Student Central for advice.

page_title: If I apply for a masters by coursework program and my GPA is not high enough, what are my entry options?
breadcrumb: If I apply for a masters by coursework program and my GPA is not high enough, what are my entry options?
text:
If I apply for a masters by coursework program and my GPA is not high enough, what are my entry options?

If you do not meet the GPA requirement for the program that you have applied for, some disciplines offer program suites which begin at graduate certificate level and progress to graduate diploma level and then master's level (or beyond). These programs may have differing entry requirements and provide a pathway to the master's qualification.

For further information about postgraduate program suites, see applying for postgraduate coursework study.

For further information about the specific program that you are interested in, including entry requirements, early exit points and further study options, search for your program on our Future Students website.

In some cases, when you have applied for a master's program you may be made an articulation offer to commence in alternative program to give you an opportunity to meet the required GPA. You would need to attain the required GPA for the alternate program to be permitted to articulate/program change into the subsequent master's program.

For further assistance Ask UQ.

page_title: What is a course profile?
breadcrumb: What is a course profile?
text:
What is a course profile?

A course profile gives you the key information about a course. It outlines the course aims and learning objectives, required resources, assessment criteria and due dates, and other important information.

Course profiles are available on the Programs and Courses website. If you're a current student, your course profiles are also available on mySI-net (student login required) and on your course's Blackboard site.

page_title: What is my UQ email address if I studied prior to 2024?
breadcrumb: What is my UQ email address if I studied prior to 2024?
text:
What is my UQ email address if I studied prior to 2024?

- before 24 March 2011, your primary address is firstname.lastnamexx@uqconnect.edu.au (xx is a number).
- after 24 March 2011, your primary address is firstname.lastnamexx@uq.net.au (xx is a number).

There are multiple email addresses attached to your account, which are used for administrative and software licensing purposes.

Addresses include:

- s1234567@student.uq.edu.au
- firstname.lastnamexx@uqconnect.edu.au
- firstname.lastnamexx@uq.net.au.

Emails sent to any of these addresses go to your same mailbox. They aren't separate email accounts.

If you return to UQ to complete further studies, your previous email address will be available for personal use and a new student mailbox will be created for the duration of your enrolment. For more information, please see What happens to my UQ student email if I’m not enrolled or after I graduate.

page_title: How do I update to the latest version of Zoom?
breadcrumb: How do I update to the latest version of Zoom?
text:
How do I update to the latest version of Zoom?

Find out how to check which version of Zoom you have installed.

- Within the Zoom app, select your profile icon on the top-right of the screen.
- Select 'Check for updates'.
- If there is a newer version of Zoom available, it will download automatically.
- To finish the installation, click 'Update'.

- Open the App Store.
- Tap your profile icon at the top right of the screen.
- Scroll down to see pending updates. Tap 'Update' next to the Zoom app to update it.

You may wish to keep your Zoom and other iOS apps automatically up-to-date. See Apple's website for more information.

- Open the Google Play app.
- Tap the menu icon, then 'My apps & games'.
- Tap 'Update' on the 'ZOOM Cloud Meetings' app.

You may wish to keep your Zoom and other Android apps automatically up-to-date. See Update your Android apps for more information.

page_title: What is SAP?
breadcrumb: What is SAP?
text:
What is SAP?

What is SAP? Home Information and services SAP is an acronym for Student Access Plan. For more information refer to Diversity, Disability and Inclusion.

page_title: How can I get help completing an Extension of Assessment Due Date form?
breadcrumb: How can I get help completing an Extension of Assessment Due Date form?
text:
How can I get help completing an Extension of Assessment Due Date form?

You can only submit an Extension of Assessment Due Date (EADD) form (through 'My Requests' on my.UQ) for courses that do not have a finalised grade. You will need to submit an individual request for each assessment item that you require an extension on. If you require an extension for multiple assessment items, you will need to submit multiple EADD requests.

From semester 2 2020, the EADD form may also be used to request exemption from a penalty due to late submission of an online examination.

If you are having trouble submitting the form, check the following:

- all required fields are completed
- there are no error messages displayed on the form
- you are submitting the form using a Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox browser.

If you still need help, contact Student Central for advice.

page_title: How do I contact UQ Security for general enquiries?
breadcrumb: How do I contact UQ Security for general enquiries?
text:
How do I contact UQ Security for general enquiries?

How do I contact UQ Security for general enquiries? Home Information and services For all non-urgent enquiries, call (07) 3365 1234. For all emergencies, call (07) 3365 3333.

page_title: Can I sit the CASPer test more than once?
breadcrumb: Can I sit the CASPer test more than once?
text:
Can I sit the CASPer test more than once?

No, you cannot sit the test more than once in a year. The test results only apply to the year of the submitted application.

For more information about how UQ is using the CASPer test, see Applying for the Bachelor of Veterinary Science (Honours).

No, you cannot sit the test more than once in a year. The test results only apply to the year of the submitted application.

For more information about how UQ is using the CASPer test, see Applying for the Bachelor of Veterinary Science (Honours).

page_title: How do I connect to the VPN before I log into my UQ Windows computer?
breadcrumb: How do I connect to the VPN before I log into my UQ Windows computer?
text:
How do I connect to the VPN before I log into my UQ Windows computer?

UQ provides an alternative VPN connection vpn.uq.edu.au/SBL that allows you to connect to the VPN before you log into your UQ Windows computer. This is only required when you:

- change your UQ account password while off-campus or working from home
- log into a new computer for the first time while off-campus
- connect to your home folder 'H:' while off-campus.

You should use vpn.uq.edu.au as the connection address for general use.

If your computer is connecting to vpn.uq.edu.au/SBL by default, you can change it by selecting it from the drop down menu in the Cisco Secure Client window.

To connect to the VPN before you log into your UQ Windows computer:

- On your computer's login screen, click the computer icon on the bottom right highlighted in the screenshot below.
 If you don't see this icon, contact IT support.
 
 
 
- In the server address selection window, type in vpn.uq.edu.au/SBL, then click Connect.
- Your credentials for the SBL VPN use the same username but your password is a combination of your UQ password and a 6-digit authentication code. The authentication code is generated by the Duo Mobile app or a Duo token. 
	- Enter your UQ username.
- Generate a passcode using the Duo Mobile app or a Duo token. Please ensure the Duo app on your device is up-to-date.
- Enter your password, followed by a comma and the 6-digit authentication code, E.g. password,123456.
 
- Sign in to your computer with your UQ username and password.

page_title: What is a course coordinator?
breadcrumb: What is a course coordinator?
text:
What is a course coordinator?

What is a course coordinator? Home Information and services Course coordinators are responsible for coordinating the development, preparation, delivery and assessment of designing, planning and teaching a course. Your course coordinator's name and contact details can be found on your course's profile.

page_title: Who can I contact for visa advice?
breadcrumb: Who can I contact for visa advice?
text:
Who can I contact for visa advice?

If you are a UQ student, you can contact the registered migration agent available at UQ Union's Student Advocacy and Support (SAS) for free migration and visa advice. Book in advance as appointments are in high demand. Please note that the SHOC migration agent cannot provide assistance with applications for permanent residency.

The Migration Agents Registry Authority maintains a list of registered migration agents in Australia. Be aware these agents generally charge a fee for service.

If you are unable to pay for a migration agent, the Refugee and Immigration Legal Service (RAILS) may be able to assist. RAILS run evening advice sessions where immigration law advice is provided by volunteer migration agents.

You can also contact the Department of Home Affairs on 131 881 for advice.

page_title: What is considered part-time study?
breadcrumb: What is considered part-time study?
text:
What is considered part-time study?

At UQ, a standard full-time study load is 8 units (generally 4 courses) per semester. A part-time study load is defined as enrolment in less than 75% of the standard full-time study load, i.e. less than 6 units (generally 3 courses) per semester.

A single course usually has a value of 2 units, but this can vary between courses. To find the number of units allocated to a particular course, search for the course on the Programs and Courses website. The unit value is displayed under 'units'. Your course's electronic course profile (ECP) will also have the unit value in the 'course details' section.

International students with a student visa have to complete their program by the end date on their Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE). This means most students with a student visa enrol in 8 units per semester. If you are an international student and want to change your study load, contact your Faculty for advice.

page_title: How do I renew my student ID card?
breadcrumb: How do I renew my student ID card?
text:
How do I renew my student ID card?

If your ID card has expired because your graduation semester has changed, you can get a free replacement card. See my.UQ for steps on how to replace a student ID card.

If your ID card has expired because your graduation semester has changed, you can get a free replacement card. See my.UQ for steps on how to replace a student ID card.

page_title: Can I attend a later graduation ceremony?
breadcrumb: Can I attend a later graduation ceremony?
text:
Can I attend a later graduation ceremony?

If you don't attend your graduation ceremony the first time around, you're able to attend a later ceremony within a year of your original graduation period.

If you are planning to defer your graduation ceremony please indicate your preference via the registration portal. Please ensure that you complete your Graduation Information Check and ensure your mailing address is up to date. For more information please see the Graduations website.

Please note that you are only deferring your attendance at a ceremony and not your graduation itself and therefore you will graduate in absentia at the original ceremony. Your digital documents will be issued within 48 hours and graduation documents will be posted to you following the graduation period.

See my.UQ for more information on graduation ceremonies.

page_title: I missed my exam. Can I apply for a deferred exam?
breadcrumb: I missed my exam. Can I apply for a deferred exam?
text:
I missed my exam. Can I apply for a deferred exam?

I missed my exam. Can I apply for a deferred exam? Home Information and services You may be eligible for a discretionary deferred exam. See my.UQ for information on one-off discretionary deferred exams.

page_title: What do I do if my assignment is late?
breadcrumb: What do I do if my assignment is late?
text:
What do I do if my assignment is late?

What do I do if my assignment is late? Home Information and services Refer to your course's electronic course profile (ECP) on Blackboard for individual course procedures. There are different procedures for different courses and faculties.

page_title: How do I use Company Portal?
breadcrumb: How do I use Company Portal? > What is Company Portal?
text:
How do I use Company Portal? > What is Company Portal?

"Company Portal" is a direct replacement for "Software Centre" on UQ managed Windows PCs. It provides access to self-install additional software and displays the name of your device along with any other devices you've been associated with.

- Click the Start menu and type 'company portal' into the search. Open the Company Portal application.
- On the Microsoft sign in screen, enter your UQ username followed by '@uq.edu.au'. For example, uqjblog3@uq.edu.au. Click 'Next'.
 
 
 
- On the UQ Authenticate screen, log in with your UQ username and password.
 
 
 
- Ensure 'Allow my organisation to manage my device' is ticked, then click 'Yes, all apps'.
 
 
 
- Click 'Done'.

- Go to the "Apps" section to view available software.
- Click the icon of the software package you want to install.
- Click the 'Install' button to start the installation.
- Visit the "Downloads & updates" section to check the progress of new software installations, and to view a list of previously installed applications.

- Visit the "Downloads & updates" section.
- Select the application you wish to uninstall.
- Click the "Uninstall" or "Reinstall" button option.

- 
	Open Company Portal.
- 
	Click on the "Settings" cog at the bottom-left side.
- 
	Click "Sync".
 
 
 
- 
	After a few minutes, the application should be available.

Sometimes, Company Portal can get "stuck" installing apps, or not show any apps at all. If you've tried a "manual sync" but that hasn't resolved the problem, you can try "resetting" the Company Portal app.

- Open the Windows "Settings" app from the Start Menu.
- Select "Apps".
- Select "Installed Apps"
- Click the "..." next to "Company Portal", then select "Advanced Options.
 
 
 
- Click on "Reset".
 
 
 
- Launch Company Portal again and it should behave normally.

If you cannot find the Company Portal on your workstation, or you have any other questions relating to software on UQ managed PCs, please log a request with the ITS Service Desk.

page_title: What is Honours? How can I do Honours?
breadcrumb: What is Honours? How can I do Honours?
text:
What is Honours? How can I do Honours?

Honours is an Australian Qualifications Framework (AQF) Level 8 qualification.

Honours program usually involves the completion of a self-directed research project in a specialised area of interest under the supervision of an academic staff member.

Honours programs can take the form of either:

- A 'built in' honours year – these are usually 4 or 5 year programs (e.g. Bachelor of Laws (Honours), Bachelor of Physiotherapy (Honours), Bachelor of Engineering (Honours)).
- An additional honours year – high-achieving students can apply for separate 1-year honours programs following the completion of an AQF 7 bachelor program (e.g. Bachelor Arts (Honours), Bachelor of Science (Honours), Bachelor of Nursing (Honours)).

Honours is awarded in the following classes:

- Class I
- Class IIA
- Class IIB
- Class IIIA
- Class IIIB

Class of honours usually depends on your GPA. Check your program rules for more information.

page_title: What is a Commonwealth supported place (CSP) and am I eligible?
breadcrumb: What is a Commonwealth supported place (CSP) and am I eligible?
text:
What is a Commonwealth supported place (CSP) and am I eligible?

A Commonwealth supported place (CSP) is a place in a higher education program which is partially funded by the Australian Government.

The Australian Government subsidises a CSP by paying part of the fees for the place directly to the university. The subsidy amount is not a loan and students do not have to pay the subsidy amount back. However, students are required to contribute towards their study and pay the remainder of the fee known as the 'student contribution amount' for each unit they are enrolled in.

A student enrolled in a Commonwealth supported place is referred to as a Commonwealth supported student.

For more information and to find out if you are eligible, see the Study Assist website.

page_title: Can I commence study at UQ mid-year? What programs are available?
breadcrumb: Can I commence study at UQ mid-year? What programs are available?
text:
Can I commence study at UQ mid-year? What programs are available?

Yes, some programs at UQ are available for mid-year entry. You can find which programs are available mid-year by selecting Semester 2 under the filter option on the find a program page.

If you are a Higher Degree by Research candidate, you can find further information about research quarters.

page_title: What is a unit?
breadcrumb: What is a unit?
text:
What is a unit?

A unit represents the value of individual courses that contribute to the total unit requirements of a program. Most UQ courses have a value of 2 units (sometimes displayed as #2) and a standard study load is 8 units per semester.

You can find the total unit requirement of a program by searching for the program on the Study website – go to the 'Overview' tab and the total unit requirement is listed in the 'See program rules' link under the 'What you'll study' heading.

page_title: What is my OSHC policy number?
breadcrumb: What is my OSHC policy number?
text:
What is my OSHC policy number?

For all students that have OSHC organised by UQ, their policy number begins with P00 followed by 7 digits. If you are unsure of your policy number please check the Allianz Care Australia website for more information.

For all students that have OSHC organised by UQ, their policy number begins with P00 followed by 7 digits. If you are unsure of your policy number please check the Allianz Care Australia website for more information.

page_title: How do I recover a deleted Zoom recording from Trash?
breadcrumb: How do I recover a deleted Zoom recording from Trash? > Recover deleted Zoom cloud recording
text:
How do I recover a deleted Zoom recording from Trash? > Recover deleted Zoom cloud recording

- Sign into https://uqz.zoom.us
- Select 'Recordings' on the left menu.
- Select 'Trash' on the top right of the screen.
- Select 'Recover' for the recording to be recovered.
 
 
 
- Confirm by selecting 'Recover'.
- The recording will now be recovered and available in your Zoom cloud recordings.
 
 
 
- The recovered recording will automatically be moved back to trash after 1 day and should be downloaded.

- Open the Recycle Bin from your desktop.
- Right-click on the recording you want to recover, then select 'Restore'.

Your recording will be available again on the Recordings page.

- Click the Trash icon in your dock.
- Right-click on the recording you want to recover, then select 'Put Back'.

Your recording will be available again on the Recordings page.

page_title: Where can I find an assignment coversheet?
breadcrumb: Where can I find an assignment coversheet?
text:
Where can I find an assignment coversheet?

Where can I find an assignment coversheet? Home Information and services Assignment coversheets are usually available online through your course's blackboard page, or your faculty or school website. If you can't locate an assignment coversheet, contact your course coordinator.

page_title: What is a graduate certificate?
breadcrumb: What is a graduate certificate?
text:
What is a graduate certificate?

A graduate certificate is an Australian Qualifications Framework (AQF) level 8 postgraduate coursework qualification that typically requires 0.5 years (or 1 semester) full-time enrolment or equivalent to complete.

You can search for graduate certificate programs on the Future Students website.

Some disciplines offer articulation pathways – these begin at graduate certificate level and progress to graduate diploma level and then to master's level (or beyond). As a general rule:

- a graduate certificate is the equivalent of one semester of full-time study,
- a graduate diploma is the equivalent of one year of full-time study, and
- a master's by coursework may take between one year and two years of full-time study and usually (although not always) includes a research component.

page_title: What do I do if I'm late to my exam?
breadcrumb: What do I do if I'm late to my exam?
text:
What do I do if I'm late to my exam?

Make sure you give yourself enough time to travel to your exam venue. Plan to arrive at least 30 minutes before your exam's scheduled start time and have your UQ student ID card ready to present at the door.

If you're late to your exam you'll be allowed to enter the exam venue only if you're there within the first 60 minutes of the exam starting. You won't get additional exam time to make up for being late.

For advice on what to do if you arrive late to your exam, see the 'Arriving at an exam venue' section on the Sitting an exam page.

page_title: How do I sync Zoom meetings from my calendars on Zoom for iOS?
breadcrumb: How do I sync Zoom meetings from my calendars on Zoom for iOS?
text:
How do I sync Zoom meetings from my calendars on Zoom for iOS?

The Zoom mobile app for iOS can check your calendars for any Zoom meetings and add them to your upcoming meetings in Zoom. If you have reminders enabled, Zoom will send you reminders for Zoom meetings added to these calendars.

To sync your calendars:

- Open the Zoom app on your device.
- Tap 'Settings'.
- Tap 'Meetings'.
- Tap 'Synced Calendars'.
- Ensure 'Sync Zoom Meetings from Calendars' is toggled on.
- Tap any calendars that you would like synced with Zoom. A blue tick will appear next to the calendars you've selected.

If you're receiving reminders for meetings that you're not in, you can either disable Zoom reminders or stop syncing the calendar the Zoom meeting is in.

page_title: How do I contact my tutor, lecturer or course coordinator?
breadcrumb: How do I contact my tutor, lecturer or course coordinator?
text:
How do I contact my tutor, lecturer or course coordinator?

To contact staff on your course, sign in to mySI-net to view your list of enrolled courses, then select the 'Course Information' link (the blue button between the course code and course name) for the relevant course to access all tutor, lecturer or course coordinator (course-contributor) details.

page_title: What do I do if I'm not satisfied that my grades reflect my performance?
breadcrumb: What do I do if I'm not satisfied that my grades reflect my performance?
text:
What do I do if I'm not satisfied that my grades reflect my performance?

If you're unhappy with an assessment mark or final grade, there are steps you can take to address your concerns. See my.UQ for information on querying a result.

If you're unhappy with an assessment mark or final grade, there are steps you can take to address your concerns. See my.UQ for information on querying a result.

page_title: What is academic penalty?
breadcrumb: What is academic penalty?
text:
What is academic penalty?

If you withdraw from your course after the last date to withdraw without academic penalty, an academic penalty is applied to your program. It is referred to as 'withdrawal with academic penalty' and effectively means that a failing grade of zero (0) is recorded. The result that appears will be a grade of 'K' and this will lower your grade point average (GPA).

Generally, if you withdraw after the census date, but before the last date to withdraw without academic penalty, you will receive a grade of Withdrawn (W). This grade is not included in the calculation of your GPA. However, different circumstances apply for courses in teaching periods and flexibility scheduled courses, where the last date to withdraw without academic penalty is the same as the census date.

See the my.UQ website for information on withdrawing from a course or program.

page_title: How do I take the CASPer test?
breadcrumb: How do I take the CASPer test?
text:
How do I take the CASPer test?

To take the test, visit the CASPer website. Exact dates will be available on the CASPer website once they are finalised.

To take the test there are technical requirements. As CASPer is not a UQ function and information could change without notice, see the technical requirements section of the CASPer website.

The test can be taken at home, at school, at your local library or in another location of your choice. You may choose to use headphones to block out any surrounding noise and distractions.

For more information about how UQ is using the CASPer test, see Applying for the Bachelor of Veterinary Science (Honours).

page_title: What is an academic transcript?
breadcrumb: What is an academic transcript?
text:
What is an academic transcript?

Your academic transcript is an official legal record of all the courses you have completed at UQ. Both current UQ students and graduates can order an academic transcript.

Your academic transcript is an official legal record of all the courses you have completed at UQ. Both current UQ students and graduates can order an academic transcript.

page_title: I have withdrawn from my course or program, how long will my refund take?
breadcrumb: I have withdrawn from my course or program, how long will my refund take?
text:
I have withdrawn from my course or program, how long will my refund take?

I have withdrawn from my course or program, how long will my refund take? Home Information and services We will process a refund of any funds owing to you within 28 days of submission of your completed refund request.

page_title: How can I delete an Adobe digital signature?
breadcrumb: How can I delete an Adobe digital signature?
text:
How can I delete an Adobe digital signature?

To delete an Adobe digital signature (digital ID) in Adobe Acrobat, follow the instructions at ‘Delete your digital ID’ on the Adobe Digital IDs page.

For more information about digital signatures, see Digitally sign PDFs (staff login required) on the Systems Training Hub.

page_title: How can I invite my family and friends to attend my graduation?
breadcrumb: How can I invite my family and friends to attend my graduation?
text:
How can I invite my family and friends to attend my graduation?

International students can request a graduation invitation statement to support visa applications for family and friends to attend your graduation ceremony. It can be provided to organisations and government agencies (e.g. Department of Home Affairs).

This statement includes:

- the names of the family and friends you wish to invite to your graduation
- a statement that The University of Queensland has no objection to the invitation request provided all immigration regulations are met.

For further information about invitation statements and other certified documents, see our advice on certified documents from UQ.

page_title: Who is available to help me with accommodation matters?
breadcrumb: Who is available to help me with accommodation matters?
text:
Who is available to help me with accommodation matters?

Finding a great place to live can enhance your experience while studying at UQ. As such, it’s important to make informed decisions regarding your accommodation.

Students who encounter problems while renting often do so because they do not understand their rights and responsibilities as tenants and the rules and processes of renting accommodation. If you have never rented in Queensland before, it is wise to seek assistance before you commit to a property.


Accommodation Services

The Accommodation Team at UQ can assist with:

- Discussing your accommodation options
- General tenancy advice and where to get support
- Share housing advice
- Assistance with the UQ Rentals platform


If you do require assistance with your rental matter, students can:


- Refer to UQ’s Tenancy Advice
- Contact the Accommodation Team at accommodation@uq.edu.au, phone 1300 275 870

**External Resources:**

**The Residential Tenancies Authority (RTA)**is a state government statutory authority that assists both tenants and providers to understand their rights and responsibilities. They can also assist in the event that there is a disagreement. Services include:

- Tenancy information and support
- Bond management
- Dispute resolution
- Investigations and prosecutions
- Policy and education services


**Queensland Statewide Tenant Advice and Referral Service (QSTARS)**

This is a free advice and referral service for Queensland tenants. QSTARS is managed by Tenants Queensland (TQ). QSTARS aims to provide all Queensland renters with high quality, free, independent tenant advisory services that assist tenants to manage and sustain their tenancy.


Services QSTARS provides to tenants include:

- Advice and assistance to understand your tenancy rights and responsibilities
- Support to resolve your tenancy issue
- Assistance with talking to your lessor or agent
- Help to write a letter or fill in tenancy forms
- Help to attend or prepare for a QCAT tenancy tribunal hearing
- Referral to other services if needed

For more information visit their website: http://www.qstars.org.

If you require further assistance, please contact the Accommodation Team at accommodation@uq.edu.au or on 1300 275 870.

page_title: What are prerequisites for entry into a program?
breadcrumb: What are prerequisites for entry into a program?
text:
What are prerequisites for entry into a program?

Prerequisites are minimum entry requirements for admission to a program.

They are intended to ensure that everyone entering a program has studied and achieved an appropriate level of knowledge in subject areas necessary to undertake the program successfully. Applicants who satisfy the prerequisite requirements are then eligible to compete for a place on the basis of the selection rank allocated to their qualifications.

For more information, see:

Applying for undergraduate study

Applying for postgraduate coursework study

See the Future Students website for full entry requirements for each of UQ's programs.

International students should also view International equivalent studies and Overseas subject equivalents.

If you have any questions regarding Year 12 international entry equivalents, or if you can't find your country on the lists, contact International Admissions for advice.

page_title: What is a faculty?
breadcrumb: What is a faculty?
text:
What is a faculty?

A faculty is an organisational unit responsible for delivering academic programs. Some faculties have a number of sub-divisions called schools.

UQ's faculties are:

- Faculty of Business, Economics and Law (BEL)
- Faculty of Engineering, Architecture and Information Technology (EAIT)
- Faculty of Health, Medicine and Behavioural Sciences (HMBS)
- Faculty of Humanities, Arts and Social Sciences (HASS)
- Faculty of Science

See our my.UQ website for advice on contacting your faculty or school.

page_title: What should I do if I see someone smoking on campus?
breadcrumb: What should I do if I see someone smoking on campus?
text:
What should I do if I see someone smoking on campus?

We hope that respect for others will guide all of us during the transition towards smoke-free sites. Staff, students and visitors can play a role in UQ's smoke-free initiative by increasing awareness to those smoking on campus through signage, quit support, available resources, social media and information online.

This can be a difficult conversation to have. If you're unsure how to have the conversation with someone smoking, you can refer to the signs on campus, or use the following tips to guide you:

- Always remember that smoking is an addiction; sometimes smoking is associated with stress or an addiction to nicotine so it's important to be mindful of this when explaining the policy.
- Smile, introduce yourself and politely tell the person that UQ is smoke-free.
- Respectfully request the smoker to either put out the cigarette safely or move to an area off campus if they wish to keep smoking.
- If the person complies, remember to thank them. If the person becomes angry or confrontational, excuse yourself and report the incident to UQ Security.
- If you find you have a regular issue at a particular location, contact UQ Security.

page_title: What does program type mean?
breadcrumb: What does program type mean?
text:
What does program type mean?

Program type refers to the level of a program within the Australian Qualifications Framework (AQF) (e.g. Bachelor, Bachelor (Honours), Graduate Certificate etc.).

Program type refers to the level of a program within the Australian Qualifications Framework (AQF) (e.g. Bachelor, Bachelor (Honours), Graduate Certificate etc.).

page_title: How will I know if I have completed my program requirements and can graduate?
breadcrumb: How will I know if I have completed my program requirements and can graduate?
text:
How will I know if I have completed my program requirements and can graduate?

Once you're identified as a potential graduand, you'll be invited to complete a Conditional Graduation Application form in mySI-net (student login required) about 6 weeks before the official release of grades in Semester 1 and 2. This communication will be sent via email on a rolling basis as graduation checks are completed by your faculty.

See my.UQ for more information on graduation ceremonies.

page_title: Can I study full-time online?
breadcrumb: Can I study full-time online?
text:
Can I study full-time online?

It depends on the program that you are studying. Most programs are not available for full-time external delivery. You can see if a program is offered externally by searching for the program on the Study website and viewing the delivery location.

You should enrol in courses offered in in-person mode where they are available. You should only enrol in courses offered in external delivery mode if you are unable to come to campus or if your course is not offered in-person mode.

page_title: How do I get a progress check from the Faculty of Science to make sure I am on track to graduate?
breadcrumb: How do I get a progress check from the Faculty of Science to make sure I am on track to graduate?
text:
How do I get a progress check from the Faculty of Science to make sure I am on track to graduate?

First refer to your Program Rules and the Faculty of Science Study Planners, to check whether you are on track to complete your program. To find your Program Rules, search the Programs and Courses site for your program and select the year you commenced. Program Rules are found under the 'Program Requirements' section.

If you are still unsure about your remaining program requirements, you can contact the Faculty office on (07) 3365 1888 or enquire@science.uq.edu.au to request a progress check. You will need to provide your full name, student number, program and major/field of study, so that we can determine whether you are on track.

You should have completed at least half of your program prior to requesting a progress check, however some exceptions may be made if you have provided a comprehensive study plan.

You can also arrange academic advising appointments to discuss course selection and plan out your future study. If you wish to make an appointment with an Academic Advisor, drop into the Faculty of Science office or call the Faculty on (07) 3365 1888.

If you are in a dual program, it is important that you also check your progress with the faculty who owns the other component of your dual program.

page_title: Can I print the My eQuals digital copies?
breadcrumb: Can I print the My eQuals digital copies?
text:
Can I print the My eQuals digital copies?

You are able to print the digital My eQuals documents, however when printed, documents have no authentic status and will not contain the security features of the online or PDF digital versions. UQ will continue to issue hard copy academic records (testamurs, transcripts and AHEGS only) in addition to the versions issued through My eQuals.


Please note, UQ will not issue hard copy certified documents such as Completion statement, Enrolment statement, Invitation Statement, Thesis statement or Other certified statement.


For further information on academic records and certified documents see my.UQ.

page_title: How do I upload data from my local device to my student network drive (H Drive)?
breadcrumb: How do I upload data from my local device to my student network drive (H Drive)?
text:
How do I upload data from my local device to my student network drive (H Drive)?

- On your device, go to the my.UQ Dashboard.
- Select 'My files' from the left menu.
 
 
 
- Log in with your UQ username and password if you see the UQ Authenticate screen.
 
 
 
- On the My Files screen, enter your UQ password again , then click 'Login'.
 
 
 
- Click 'Upload'.
 
 
 
- Click 'Select files'.
- Choose the file(s) you want to upload from your device.

page_title: I’m having trouble connecting to the VPN. What can I do?
breadcrumb: I’m having trouble connecting to the VPN. What can I do?
text:
I’m having trouble connecting to the VPN. What can I do?

If you’re having problems connecting to the VPN, first check your UQ username and password:

- Try logging in to the VPN service.
- If this fails, take a screenshot of the error and submit an IT request with the screenshot attached.

If you can log on but can’t connect:

- Your current software might be out of date. Try downloading and reinstalling the VPN client.
- Some schools, faculties, centres (and others) may have their own firewalls on their servers, which can block VPN access. Contact your local IT officer or admin staff to find out if the VPN will work for you.

If you're a UQ staff member, you'll need to authenticate using multi-factor authentication each time you connect to the VPN. 

If you’re still having problems, submit an IT request with a screenshot of the Message History. You can find this by following the instructions below for your operating system.

- On the Cisco Secure Client window, click the cog wheel icon, then select AnyConnect VPN.
- select the Message History tab.
 


 


macOS

- On the Cisco Secure Client window, click the Statistics icon.
- Select the AnyConnect VPN tab.
- Click the Message History tab.

page_title: How do I export bookmarks and settings from Chrome and Firefox?
breadcrumb: How do I export bookmarks and settings from Chrome and Firefox? > Mozilla Firefox
text:
How do I export bookmarks and settings from Chrome and Firefox? > Mozilla Firefox

Exported passwords are not encrypted. Delete the exported files after importing them to your new device.

The recommended process is to sync your passwords and bookmarks with Microsoft Edge, which is linked to your UQ Microsoft 365 account. You can then import that data into Chrome or Firefox if needed.

To import into Firefox from Edge, follow the instructions on the Firefox website.

To import into Chrome:

- Open Chrome.
- In the address bar, type chrome://settings/importData. Alternatively:
	- Click the 3 dots on the top right of the screen.
- Select 'Bookmarks and lists', then 'Import bookmarks and settings...'
 

Save your exported files in your UQ OneDrive. This ensures they are backed up and easy to access during setup on your new device.

page_title: How do I get a student discount (concession) on public transport fares?
breadcrumb: How do I get a student discount (concession) on public transport fares?
text:
How do I get a student discount (concession) on public transport fares?

How do I get a student discount (concession) on public transport fares?

As of 30 November 2024, the transport minister announced 50 cent flat fares will now be permanent for all customers travelling on the Translink network.

Tertiary students no longer need to apply for a tertiary concession fare on their adult card.

page_title: Where can I park on weekends?
breadcrumb: Where can I park on weekends?
text:
Where can I park on weekends?

Paid parking does not apply on weekends and public holidays, however all other parking regulations still apply. For more information, see our parking rules and regulations.

Paid parking does not apply on weekends and public holidays, however all other parking regulations still apply. For more information, see our parking rules and regulations.

page_title: Can I go on exchange in my final semester?
breadcrumb: Can I go on exchange in my final semester?
text:
Can I go on exchange in my final semester?

You must have a minimum of 6 units left in your degree to be able to study overseas in your final semester. However, please take note of the risks involved:

- Your UQ graduation will very likely be delayed*. It can take 4 - 8 weeks for the host university to issue the exchange transcript, and a further 4 weeks for the credit to be transferred to your UQ program.*.
- If you fail any courses, you will almost certainly have to return to UQ to finish your degree.
- Flexibility with courses is necessary when going on exchange. If you only have core courses remaining in your degree, we don't recommended that you go on exchange in your final semester, as we can't guarantee the courses you need will be offered, or that you will be able to enrol in them.
- You won't be eligible for an OS-Help loan.


* All credit transfer from your exchange must be completed before you graduate. Therefore, your faculty must have the official transcript from your host university well before the graduation ceremony. Please be aware that it can take at least two months to receive host university transcripts, so plan your graduation accordingly. Delayed graduation will most likely delay admission/enrolment in further studies.

page_title: How do I manage multiple UQ mailboxes?
breadcrumb: How do I manage multiple UQ mailboxes?
text:
How do I manage multiple UQ mailboxes?

If you are a current student and also have an alumni mailbox from previous studies at UQ, you will have two mailboxes to manage:

- Student mailbox (@student.uq.edu.au)
- Alumni mailbox (@uqconnect.edu.au or @uq.net.au)

Your student mailbox is the primary account where you will receive all communication related to your current enrollment at UQ. It is important that this mailbox is checked regularly. This is also the account you will use to access Microsoft 365.

Your alumni mailbox is a personal account that will receive emails from anyone you provide with the email address.

To log in to both mailboxes at the same time, you will need to use two different web browsers (e.g. Chrome, Firefox, Edge) or incognito mode. When you log in to a UQ account, you are directed to UQ Authenticate. If you are already logged in and try to access a different account in the same browser, it will take you back to your current session. This happens because the browser can only handle one login at a time.

We recommend designating a different web browser for each account. For example, use Chrome for your student mailbox and Firefox for your alumni mailbox.

This is the recommended process to access your student mailbox.

- Log in to your my.UQ dashboard. You will be directed to UQ Authenticate where you will enter your student username (e.g. s9871234) and password.
- Select 'Email – Student' from the menu on the left. Alternatively, if you have an alumni mailbox and would like to access it, select 'Email – Alumni'.
- The mailbox you selected will open in a new tab.

- Open the designated browser for your mailbox.
- Open Outlook.com.
- Sign in using your UQconnect email address (e.g. firstname.lastname@uqconnect.edu.au) and password.

Please note: Your UQconnect email address is the login credentials for your alumni mailbox. If you were following the same process to access your student mailbox, you would need to use the long form of your student username in the format username@uq.edu.au (this is not a valid student email address and can only be used as a login ID).

Using an email client like Outlook helps you manage multiple mailboxes because it lets you access, organise and respond to emails from different accounts in one place.

- Launch the desktop application of Outlook (this must be Outlook classic).
- Add another account:
	- Go to 'File' > 'Add Account'.
- Enter the email address and password for the mailbox you want to add.
- Follow the prompts to complete the setup.
 
- Switch between accounts:
	- Once both accounts are added, you can switch between them by clicking on your profile picture or initials in the top right corner.
- Select the account you want to switch to from the dropdown menu.

page_title: How do I manage multiple UQ mailboxes?
breadcrumb: How do I manage multiple UQ mailboxes? > To more effectively manage your mailboxes, we recommend the following: > Email Forwarding
h2: To more effectively manage your mailboxes, we recommend the following:
text:
How do I manage multiple UQ mailboxes? > To more effectively manage your mailboxes, we recommend the following: > Email Forwarding

Set up automatic forwarding from one mailbox to the other. For example, forward all emails from the alumni mailbox to the student mailbox. This way, you only need to check one mailbox regularly.

Please note: When forwarding emails to another mailbox, be aware that links may not work as the receiving account has a different identity and therefore does not have the same permissions as the original mailbox. 

If you are employed by UQ, you are not permitted to forward your staff email; however, you can forward other email to this mailbox. For example, if you are a HDR student, you can forward your student email to your staff mailbox.

Schedule regular times to check and clean up both mailboxes. This can prevent one mailbox from becoming overwhelming and ensure you do not miss important emails.

Reminder: Regularly check your junk, spam, and archive folders to make sure you don’t miss any important messages that might have been automatically filtered there.

page_title: What courses can I study to meet a subject prerequisite for entry to an undergraduate program?
breadcrumb: What courses can I study to meet a subject prerequisite for entry to an undergraduate program?
text:
What courses can I study to meet a subject prerequisite for entry to an undergraduate program?

Subject prerequisites are usually Queensland Year 12 authority subjects, but they may also be met through interstate or international subject equivalents, tertiary study or bridging courses.

If you're a prospective or current **domestic** student – and you haven't previously met a subject prerequisite – you may choose to undertake a bridging course or tertiary-level subject to meet the required prerequisite. Before undertaking a course, please confirm how long it will take to complete and if it's the best option for your study plans, as this may affect when you will be eligible to apply for the bachelor program. Contact UQ Admissions for advice at admissions@uq.edu.au.

If you're a prospective **international** student, contact UQ International for advice.

See our Future Students website for information on applying for undergraduate study.

page_title: What is the difference between award and non-award study?
breadcrumb: What is the difference between award and non-award study?
text:
What is the difference between award and non-award study?

Award students are enrolled in a program offered by UQ that leads to a degree, certificate or diploma issued by UQ. Students are given an award certificate (also called testamur or degree certificate) at the completion of their program. Search our Future Students website for award programs offered at UQ.

Non-award students are enrolled in a course or courses at UQ, but they are not enrolled in an academic program that leads to the conferral of a university award. Examples of non-award enrolment include students enrolled in the Enhanced Studies Program, cross-institutional students, and Study Abroad students. See the Future Students website for information on applying for non-award study.

page_title: What is an enrolment statement?
breadcrumb: What is an enrolment statement?
text:
What is an enrolment statement?

A standard enrolment statement can be used to prove your enrolment and to demonstrate that The University of Queensland is an accredited university.

This statement includes:

- your name, date of birth and student number
- your program name
- your attendance (full-time or part-time)
- study dates of your current and/or future enrolment

Other enrolment statements are available for:

- confirmation of address
- jury duty
- sponsored students
- UQ accreditation

page_title: How do I book meeting rooms at the Translational Research Institute (TRI)?
breadcrumb: How do I book meeting rooms at the Translational Research Institute (TRI)?
text:
How do I book meeting rooms at the Translational Research Institute (TRI)?

How do I book meeting rooms at the Translational Research Institute (TRI)? Home Information and services Go to the TRI Intranet website (TRI login required) for further information about booking meeting rooms at the Translational Research Institute (TRI).

page_title: How many hours should I allocate to each course?
breadcrumb: How many hours should I allocate to each course?
text:
How many hours should I allocate to each course?

It's recommended that you spend approximately 10 hours studying per week for each 2 unit course, including contact time, during semester. Some courses will require more hours.

For help with managing your time and planning your study, see our advice on time management.

page_title: How do I set a metered network connection on my Windows computer?
breadcrumb: How do I set a metered network connection on my Windows computer?
text:
How do I set a metered network connection on my Windows computer?

If you are travelling, working from a mobile phone, or have a limited amount of data on your internet plan, you can set your network connection as a 'metered' connection. Some apps might work differently on a metered connection to help reduce your data usage and some updates for Windows won't be installed automatically. Ensure to turn off the metered connection setting when it's no longer needed to receive important Windows updates.

To turn on the metered connection setting:

- Click the network icon in the system tray on the bottom right of the screen.
- Select 'Properties' next to your network.
 
 
 
- Scroll down to the 'Metered connection' section.
- Switch on the button 'Set as metered connection'.

page_title: I have tested positive for latent tuberculosis. What does this mean and can I still commence healthcare placements?
breadcrumb: I have tested positive for latent tuberculosis. What does this mean and can I still commence healthcare placements?
text:
I have tested positive for latent tuberculosis. What does this mean and can I still commence healthcare placements?

Latent TB infection implies that a person is infected but does not have the disease currently. Whilst the person does not feel ill, and is not infectious, they may develop active TB disease in the future. The Immunisation Record Team (IRT) will review your results and advise if there are any other requirements before a healthcare placement. In most cases, you will be allowed to commence your placement but you may need to see a doctor and have a chest x-ray prior to commencing the placement. It is important that students with a positive latent TB test have follow up with a TB Service, to prevent personal and public health problems in the future. As it can take some time to get an appointment with the TB Service, please arrange the appointment as soon as possible. Students with latent TB will be required to provide a clearance letter from the TB service to UQ after their visit and within four months to avoid non-compliance. Please communicate with the Immunisation Record Team (IRT) by email (immunisation@uq.edu.au) if you have any questions or delays. Further information is available on this TB screening information sheet.

page_title: What is external attendance mode?
breadcrumb: What is external attendance mode?
text:
What is external attendance mode?

What is external attendance mode? Home Information and services External courses are delivered entirely online, and students must participate online for learning and assessment. Note: students may be required to sit exams at a UQ campus or an approved off-campus exam centre.

page_title: Where can I find a microwave or hot water at UQ St Lucia?
breadcrumb: Where can I find a microwave or hot water at UQ St Lucia?
text:
Where can I find a microwave or hot water at UQ St Lucia?

Where can I find a microwave or hot water at UQ St Lucia? Home Information and services If you need to heat up your lunch, see the UQ Union's microwave rooms.

page_title: Why can’t I submit an online Credit or Exemption form?
breadcrumb: Why can’t I submit an online Credit or Exemption form?
text:
Why can’t I submit an online Credit or Exemption form?

You can only submit a Credit or Exemption request if you are active in a UQ program. If you are not program active, you will receive the following error message when trying to access the Credit or Exemption form:

"Unable to load details as you are not active in any programs."

If you are a new student, you will need to accept your offer and correspondingly be activated in your program.

If you are a returning student, please contact your faculty to discuss re-activation options if you have not already started this process.

For more information, visit Credit and exemptions for previous study.

page_title: As an international student, can I study part-time?
breadcrumb: As an international student, can I study part-time?
text:
As an international student, can I study part-time?

If you are an international student living in Australia on a student visa, you are required to enrol in a full-time study load.

Part-time study for student visa holders is only possible if you have been approved by your faculty for a reduced study load.

page_title: Okta MFA - Lost or damaged devices

page_title: Will spelling and grammar mistakes affect my CASPer score?

text:
Will spelling and grammar mistakes affect my CASPer score?

CASPer is not testing you for spelling and grammar, however it is important to take care with your responses and ensure they are clear.

For more information about how UQ is using the CASPer test, see Applying for the Bachelor of Veterinary Science (Honours).

page_title: What is a professional doctorate?
breadcrumb: What is a professional doctorate?
text:
What is a professional doctorate?

A professional doctorate is a structured research program designed for experienced professionals to make a significant contribution to original knowledge and/or research integrated practice in their profession. Professional doctorates promote the generation of knowledge and research skills to enhance professional practice.

UQ offers two models of professional doctorates, both leading to an Australian Qualifications Framework (AQF) Level 10 qualification:

**Model 1:** Programs with a structure that allows domestic students to undertake the program as part of the Research Training Program (RTP funded).

RTP funded professional doctorates are managed by the Graduate Research School, with students enrolled as research students from the start of their program. The Doctor of Veterinary Clinical Science is currently available as an RTP funded professional doctorate.

**Model 2:** Programs with a structure that does not allow domestic students to undertake the program as part of the Research Training Program or where a determination has been made by the relevant faculty that RTP places will not be made available (non-RTP funded and therefore full fee). Professional doctorates that are non-RTP funded are managed by the relevant faculty.

To find the available programs, see the Programs and Courses and select ‘browse by faculty’. Professional doctorates may be listed in either the ‘postgraduate program’ or ‘research program’ results under 'Professional Doctorate Programs'.

page_title: The comment left by the Immunisation Records Team (IRT) states my Hepatitis B dose dates do not align with Australian Guidelines. Why must I provide evidence of a fourth dose or confirmation from my GP?
breadcrumb: The comment left by the Immunisation Records Team (IRT) states my Hepatitis B dose dates do not align with Australian Guidelines. Why must I provide evidence of a fourth dose or confirmation from my GP?
text:
The comment left by the Immunisation Records Team (IRT) states my Hepatitis B dose dates do not align with Australian Guidelines. Why must I provide evidence of a fourth dose or confirmation from my GP?

The recommended schedule states a minimum of 1 month between the first and second dose, and 4-6 months between dose 1 and 3, which is based on evidence of vaccination effectiveness (though there are exceptions to this schedule). If your dose dates do not comply with the Australian Guidelines, the Immunisation Records Team (IRT) may ask for evidence of an additional dose to ensure long lasting protection, or confirmation from your GP that you are protected from hepatitis B. Refer to the Australian Immunisation Handbook for further information regarding dose date intervals.

page_title: How do I check my university fees payment?
breadcrumb: How do I check my university fees payment?
text:
How do I check my university fees payment?

To check your fees payment, log in to mySI-net, and click on the 'Financials' tile to view the charges and payments for specific periods. Select 'Payments Received' for additional information.

Payments usually take up to 5 business days to display in mySI-net (depending on the payment method used).

After the census date, you can view or email yourself a copy of your Statement of Course Changes and Payments Tax Invoice.

For UQ-Ochsner students, payments of USD tuition fees are not available in mySI-net. See my.UQ for information on how to pay your USD fees.

text:
What is a certified document?

Certified documents are digitally-signed, original statements or academic records that are (electronically) issued by the University via the My eQuals platform.

Certified documents are digitally-signed, original statements or academic records that are (electronically) issued by the University via the My eQuals platform.

page_title: What is a coursework program?
breadcrumb: What is a coursework program?
text:
What is a coursework program?

Coursework is defined under the Australian Qualifications Framework (AQF) as a method of teaching and learning that leads to the acquisition of skills and knowledge that does not include a major research component. Bachelor's programs and postgraduate coursework programs are all coursework programs.

Search the Future Students website for programs available at UQ.

page_title: With the changes to UQ's smoking policy, what about the rights of smokers?
breadcrumb: With the changes to UQ's smoking policy, what about the rights of smokers?
text:
With the changes to UQ's smoking policy, what about the rights of smokers?

The policy is about where people can smoke so that their smoke doesn’t cause discomfort and harm to others. Non-smokers have a right to breathe clean air that is not contaminated by carcinogens and other harmful substances in tobacco smoke.

Hand-in-hand with this, UQ encourages those who smoke to seriously consider the adverse health effects to themselves. There are a range of supports available if you choose to quit smoking.

During your time at UQ, you'll need to remember a number of important dates every semester. See get to know UQ for an overview of important dates in the academic calendar.

If you're enrolled in teaching periods, you should also check the calendar for teaching periods.

Some course dates diverge from semester schedules. Check the tables under each faculty on the course date variations page to see whether any of your courses have unique dates.

page_title: How do I change the default program to open files?
breadcrumb: How do I change the default program to open files?
text:
How do I change the default program to open files?

Windows and Mac systems can only automatically open one program for each kind of file, but many file types – such as DOC, PDF or HTML files – can be opened by more than one program.

To change the default program for a particular file type, follow the instructions below for your operating system.

- Find the file you want to open in File Explorer.
- Right-click on the file and select 'Properties'.
- In the ‘General’ tab, you can see the file type and the program it opens with now. To change this program, select 'Change...'
- You’ll see a list of programs on your device that can open this file. Select the program you want, and click 'OK'.
- Back in ‘General’, you’ll see the new program next to ‘Opens with’.
- Click 'Apply', then 'OK' to exit ‘Properties’.

- Right-click on the file you want to open in Finder.
- Select 'Get Info' in the pop-up menu.
- Expand 'Open with'.
- You’ll see a drop-down list of programs on your device that can open this file. Select the program you want, and select 'Change All...'
- Select 'Continue' when the Finder asks for confirmation.

What do I do if I need to apply for Elite Athlete Status for multiple semesters or teaching periods?

breadcrumb: How many guest tickets to my graduation ceremony can I order?
text:
How many guest tickets to my graduation ceremony can I order?

Graduating students receive 2 guest tickets as part of their attendance fee. Graduands will be able to purchase additional tickets from Monday 8 December 2025 via the registration portal. Guest tickets will be $20 and will be sold until capacity is reached.

See my.UQ for more information on guest tickets for December ceremonies.

page_title: What are the student contribution amounts for Commonwealth supported students?
breadcrumb: What are the student contribution amounts for Commonwealth supported students?
text:
What are the student contribution amounts for Commonwealth supported students?

For students in a Commonwealth supported place (CSP), the Australian Government pays some of your fees and you pay the rest, this is called your 'student contribution amount'.

The maximum student contribution amount payable in each year is set by the Australian Government (see the Study Assist website for details). The student contribution amount payable for each course depends on a number of factors:

- the field/area of study for the course,
- the unit value of the course,
- whether you're considered a commencing or continuing student, and
- whether you're enrolled in a program considered as one of the following:
	- Professional Pathway Psychology
- Professional Pathway Social Work
- Postgraduate Clinical Psychology.
 

See fee schedules for details.

You can view your actual student contribution amount liability in mySI-net after you enrol and once invoices are available.

page_title: How many days extension can I request for an assessment?
breadcrumb: How many days extension can I request for an assessment?
text:
How many days extension can I request for an assessment?

How many days extension can I request for an assessment?

Each extension request is assessed on a case by case basis and depends on the supporting documentation you provide. The number of days granted is usually the number of days you have been impaired prior to the submission deadline.

You'll need to catch up, talk to your peers, check your course profile and Blackboard for what you've missed. If it’s a compulsory session, you can lose marks for not attending and you may require documentation for your absence.

Contact your course coordinator for further advice.

page_title: What is a graduand?
breadcrumb: What is a graduand?
text:
What is a graduand?

At UQ, a graduand is a student who has successfully completed all of the requirements of their program, but has not yet been formally conferred their award (e.g. bachelor degree). Once an award is conferred upon a graduand, they become a graduate of UQ.

page_title: How do I manage the waiting room in a Zoom meeting?
breadcrumb: How do I manage the waiting room in a Zoom meeting?
text:
How do I manage the waiting room in a Zoom meeting?

By default, non-UQ participants are initially placed in the waiting room. The meeting host is required to admit people into the meeting before they can participate.

When participants are in the waiting room, a notification appears above the 'Participiants' icon.

If there is only one person in the waiting room, selecting the 'Admit' button on the notification will admit that person into the Zoom meeting.

If there are multiple people in the waiting room, selecting 'See Waiting Room' on the notification will open the Participants view.



From the Participants view, you can admit everyone in the waiting room to the Zoom meeting by selecting 'Admit all'. Alternatively, you can admit or remove individuals by moving the mouse over their name and selecting 'Admit' or 'Remove'.



Participants are moved into the meeting within 30 seconds of being admitted.

The Participants view can be closed by selecting 'Participants'.

NOTE: If you remove a participant from the waiting room or the meeting, they will not be able to rejoin.

If an external participant, or a UQ participant who hasn't signed into Zoom with their UQ account, joins a Zoom meeting, they will be placed in the waiting room. Find out how to sign into Zoom with your UQ account.

When in the waiting room, the meeting title is displayed so you can confirm you have joined the correct meeting.

Summer Semester is the third semester in the academic year. For most students, studying in Summer Semester is optional. Summer Semester runs between late November and mid-February.

Summer Semester is the third semester in the academic year. For most students, studying in Summer Semester is optional. Summer Semester runs between late November and mid-February.

page_title: I created an Adobe digital signature, but now I can’t find it. What do I do?
breadcrumb: I created an Adobe digital signature, but now I can’t find it. What do I do?
text:
I created an Adobe digital signature, but now I can’t find it. What do I do?

If you created your digital signature (digital ID) in Adobe Acrobat, the file will not appear in Adobe Reader – and vice versa.

To view the digital signature in Adobe Acrobat, you need to make Adobe Acrobat DC your default program for opening PDFs.

For more information about digital signatures, see Digitally sign PDFs (staff login required) on the Systems Training Hub.

page_title: How do I save my OneDrive files?
breadcrumb: How do I save my OneDrive files? > Considerations
text:
How do I save my OneDrive files? > Considerations

- OneDrive data can be saved from:
	- OneDrive online (All platforms)
- OneDrive Desktop app (Windows or Mac)
 
- It is recommended that data is saved via the OneDrive desktop app to reduce interruptions and failures.
- Ensure you have enough space on your device to store the downloaded data. Check how much data you have.
- Allocate a significant amount of time for this process if you have large amounts of data in your OneDrive account.
- Files can be downloaded to Android and iOS devices, but this is not recommended.

- Install and log in to the OneDrive desktop app.
- Sync all your online files to the desktop app (may take some time).
	- For Windows: Click on the cloud icon > Settings cog > Setting > Sync and backup > Files On-Demand > Download all files.
- For Mac: Click on the cloud icon > Preferences > Preferences tab > Files On-Demand > Download all OneDrive files now.
 
- Ensure ALL your files have been downloaded to your computer. This is indicated by a checkmark beside each file.
- Select the folders or files you want to keep, right click on the selection and then select copy.
- Open the drive storage, or external device, where you wish to move the data, and right click to paste.

- Log into your OneDrive online account > Select My files.
- Select the check boxes of the file or files that you want to download. You can select all files by clicking the checkbox in the row heading.
- Click Download. Depending on the amount of data it may take some time for all files to be downloaded.
- All files selected will be downloaded to the downloads location specified on your computer (usually the Downloads folder).

Note: If you select multiple files, they will download together as a single zipped folder.

page_title: Where else can I park if the car park is full?
breadcrumb: Where else can I park if the car park is full?

Where else can I park if the car park is full?

If you're at the St Lucia campus, see the current parking availability or contact PF Assist on (07) 3365 2222.

page_title: How do I register for the CASPer test?
breadcrumb: How do I register for the CASPer test?
text:
How do I register for the CASPer test?

To register to complete the CASPer test, visit the CASPer website and select 'sign-up for the CASPer test'.

You will need to register at least 3 days before your preferred test date to allow for account verification and payment processing timelines. When registering, you will be asked to provide government-issued photo identification (such as a passport, driver licence or learner permit card), as well as your QTAC application ID number. If you don't have valid government-issued photo identification, you can use a student ID along with a copy of your birth certificate. If you have any questions about this process, contact support@takecasper.com.

For more information about how UQ is using the CASPer test, see Applying for the Bachelor of Veterinary Science (Honours).

- Login
- Click 'Add a device'

- Click 'Continue'.
