	- insert your YubiKey
- touch the YubiKey to register
 
- Click 'Continue' to finish set up.

- When prompted to MFA, insert the YubiKey into your computer.
 
 
 
- Touch the security key when prompted.

page_title: How do I change courses?
breadcrumb: How do I change courses?
text:
How do I change courses?

There are cut-off dates for adding and dropping courses for each study period and there may be other implications (e.g. Centrelink benefits, student visa conditions, financial liability, travel concessions) which you should take into consideration if you are thinking of adding or dropping course(s). Please check the academic calendar to see the list of relevant cut-off dates.

To add or drop a course:

- Log into your mySI-net (student login required) student record.
- Click on the 'Enrolments' tile.
- On the 'Enrolment Summary' tab, click on either the 'add course' or 'drop course' link for the relevant semester and complete all steps until you get a confirmation message.

If you want to change your program, see my.UQ for information on changing programs.

page_title: What is the difference between an AHEGS and an academic transcript?
breadcrumb: What is the difference between an AHEGS and an academic transcript?
text:
What is the difference between an AHEGS and an academic transcript?

An academic transcript is a legal record of your entire enrolment at UQ and includes your GPA for each semester or trimester. You can order an academic transcript at any point during your studies. The Australian Higher Education Graduation Statement (AHEGS) is a snapshot document containing information about a specific degree and is only available when you graduate.

page_title: How do I access a building after hours?
breadcrumb: How do I access a building after hours?
text:
How do I access a building after hours?

Students receive after-hours access to UQ Libraries and the Multi Faith Chaplaincy automatically when your ID Card is printed. If you require access to other buildings, please contact your faculty or school, or PF Assist at pfassist@pf.uq.edu.au or (07) 3365 2222.

page_title: Do the grades from my global experience count towards my GPA at UQ?
breadcrumb: Do the grades from my global experience count towards my GPA at UQ?
text:
Do the grades from my global experience count towards my GPA at UQ?

No, the grades you receive at your host university do not count towards your UQ GPA. If you have successfully passed all of your (pre-approved) courses at the host university (according to the host university passing requirements), you should receive credit towards your UQ degree without your cumulative GPA being affected.

Keep in mind that the host university transcript, which must be received before credit transfer towards your UQ program can be completed, will list the grades you received while overseas. Those grades may count if you want to:

- apply to change programs at UQ
- apply for honours
- apply for postgraduate study
- be considered for the University Medal
- apply for graduate positions/other job opportunities
- apply for foreign visas.

In these cases, contact your faculty for advice.

page_title: What is a program plan or study plan?
breadcrumb: What is a program plan or study plan?
text:
What is a program plan or study plan?

A program plan, or study plan, is an outline of courses to be studied in consequential semesters as part of a program.

See the my.UQ website for a list of available study planners and guides.

If you are a new student, see the Starting at UQ website for advice on planning your program.

page_title: What is a completion statement?
breadcrumb: What is a completion statement?
text:
What is a completion statement?

A completion statement can be used to confirm that you’ve completed the requirements of your program before you graduate. This statement meets the current requirements of the Department of Home Affairs and most consulates.

A Completion Statement can only be issued once your grades have been finalised and all program requirements have been met.

The statement includes:

- your name, date of birth and student number
- your program name
- the date you started the program
- your attendance (full-time or part-time)
- the date you met your program requirements
- your graduation ceremony date
- the program's language of instruction
- confirmation that you've completed the program's academic requirements and the date you officially completed these on

page_title: What is a compulsory course?
breadcrumb: What is a compulsory course?
text:
What is a compulsory course?

A compulsory course, or core course, is a course that you must successfully complete to meet the requirements of your program.

See our my.UQ website for advice on choosing your courses.

A compulsory course, or core course, is a course that you must successfully complete to meet the requirements of your program.

See our my.UQ website for advice on choosing your courses.

page_title: Why do I need to provide my immunisation records to UQ?
breadcrumb: Why do I need to provide my immunisation records to UQ?
text:
Why do I need to provide my immunisation records to UQ?

If you are at increased risk of vaccine preventable diseases (VPDs) due to your study program, work tasks or work environment, UQ has a duty of care and responsibility to control and minimise this risk through vaccinations and screening tests. Transmission of VPDs has the potential to cause serious illness and avoidable death for the person concerned, their workplace contacts, including importantly patients or vulnerable people, as well as others in the broader community. Failure to comply may result in cancellation of your placement or you may be unable to complete the practical requirements of your degree or higher degree program. Further program advice can be obtained by contacting your school or faculty.

page_title: How do I import favourites and passwords into Microsoft Edge?
breadcrumb: How do I import favourites and passwords into Microsoft Edge?
text:
How do I import favourites and passwords into Microsoft Edge?

To import your bookmarks (favourites), saved passwords, and other browsing data into Microsoft Edge:

- Open Microsoft Edge.
- In the address bar, type edge://settings/importData and press Enter.
 
 
 
 Alternatively:- 
		Click the 3 dots > Settings > Profiles > Import Browser data > Import from other browsers > Choose what to import.
 
- 
		
- In the Import browser data window:
	- Select the browser you want to import from (e.g. Chrome, Firefox).
- Choose the data types to import, e.g. Favourites, Saved passwords, History.
 
- Click 'Import'.
 
 
 
- Repeat the steps if you wish to import data from other browsers.

page_title: How can I become a Student Ambassador?
breadcrumb: How can I become a Student Ambassador?
text:
How can I become a Student Ambassador?

The UQ School Liaison team employs approximately 50 Student Ambassadors each year from a range of different study areas and backgrounds. Student Ambassadors are called on to represent UQ at school visits, career markets and events such as Open Day and Tertiary Studies Expo. Ambassadors are recruited in February each year.

To apply, contact the UQ School Liaison team at student.recruitment@uq.edu.au. Interested students are required to attend a group selection day, complete appropriate paperwork and be eligible for a Queensland Government Blue Card. Student Ambassadors are essentially the face of UQ and are therefore required to have a working knowledge of their study area, the campuses and the facilities available to students.

page_title: How is the Graduate Medical School Admissions Test (GAMSAT) scored?
breadcrumb: How is the Graduate Medical School Admissions Test (GAMSAT) scored?
text:
How is the Graduate Medical School Admissions Test (GAMSAT) scored?

The GAMSAT is conducted by the Australian Council for Educational Research (ACER). They produce and score the tests each year.

GAMSAT consists of three sections:

**Section 1** – Reasoning in Humanities and Social Sciences

**Section 2** – Written Communication

**Section 3** – Reasoning in Biological and Physical Sciences.

Candidates will receive a score for each of the 3 sections, as well as an overall score and a percentile ranking. The unweighted average GAMSAT score is used to select applicants. The percentile rank is not used. The GAMSAT cut-off scores change each year depending on demand. For previous cut-offs, see applying for UQ's Doctor of Medicine.

See the ACER website for more information on the structure and content of the GAMSAT and how results are calculated.

page_title: What is an incompatible course?
breadcrumb: What is an incompatible course?
text:
What is an incompatible course?

Courses are incompatible if their content is very similar, or if it substantially overlaps. You can’t enrol in a course if you've studied any courses listed as incompatible to that course. See my.UQ for advice on choosing your courses.

Courses are incompatible if their content is very similar, or if it substantially overlaps. You can’t enrol in a course if you've studied any courses listed as incompatible to that course. See my.UQ for advice on choosing your courses.

page_title: Where can I buy textbooks and course materials?
breadcrumb: Where can I buy textbooks and course materials?
text:
Where can I buy textbooks and course materials?

You can buy textbooks online from a range of academic publishers or online platforms.

The UQ Library is another option for accessing course reading materials.

You can buy textbooks online from a range of academic publishers or online platforms.

The UQ Library is another option for accessing course reading materials.

page_title: Can I apply for more than one discretionary deferred exam?
breadcrumb: Can I apply for more than one discretionary deferred exam?
text:
Can I apply for more than one discretionary deferred exam?

You only have one opportunity to request a discretionary deferred exam for the duration of your time at the University. See my.UQ for information on one-off discretionary deferred exams.

You only have one opportunity to request a discretionary deferred exam for the duration of your time at the University. See my.UQ for information on one-off discretionary deferred exams.

page_title: I am locked out of my UQ student account. How do I regain access?
breadcrumb: I am locked out of my UQ student account. How do I regain access?
text:
I am locked out of my UQ student account. How do I regain access?

You may be locked out of your UQ account or multifactor authentication (MFA) as a security precaution.

 

The best way to regain access to your account is to contact us.

The best way to regain access to your account is to contact us.

- UQ students: contact AskUs in the Library
- UQ staff: request IT support.

page_title: What is SAS?
breadcrumb: What is SAS?
text:
What is SAS?

Student Advocacy and Support (SAS), formerly know as SHOC (Student Help on Campus), is a free service for UQ students from UQ Union. SAS can provide you with assistance on matters relating to the following services:

- education and equity
- employment
- gender and sexuality
- legal
- migration
- welfare.

page_title: Can I graduate early without completing my master's degree?
breadcrumb: Can I graduate early without completing my master's degree?
text:
Can I graduate early without completing my master's degree?

In some cases it is possible to stop studying for a masters and graduate with a lower qualification of graduate certificate or graduate diploma. This is possible when there is a suite of programs available. Please note though that you will not automatically be graduated from the lesser award. You must request an early exit via your faculty in advance of the finalisation of grades for your final semester of study. You should discuss the potential implications of making a change with your faculty early in the semester.

To find out if your program has early exit points, search for your program in Future Students and then read through the program details.

page_title: What is a 'dual program' or 'dual degree'?
breadcrumb: What is a 'dual program' or 'dual degree'?
text:
What is a 'dual program' or 'dual degree'?

A dual program (also called a dual degree) allows you to combine two UQ degrees and study them at the same time. Studying a dual program may expand your career options, and may allow you to complete two degrees in a much shorter time than if you completed the degrees one after the other.

For example, if you studied a Bachelor of Business Management, this would take 3 years fulltime. If you then went on to study a Bachelor of Information Technology this would take a further 3 years fulltime, totalling 6 years. Whereas, if you combined both programs as a dual program – Bachelor of Business Management/Information Technology – this would take a total of 4 years fulltime.

In a dual program, your workload will not increase. You’ll take the same number of courses each semester as you would if you were studying a single program. Dual programs allow you to save time and money, with most being completed in 4 years fulltime.

Once you fulfil the requirements of both degrees, you will graduate with two qualifications. If there's a combination of programs you'd like to study that's not listed as dual program on the Future Students website, contact your faculty or school about your study plans.

page_title: Will I be eligible to graduate if I'm required to sit a deferred or supplementary examination in my final semester?
breadcrumb: Will I be eligible to graduate if I'm required to sit a deferred or supplementary examination in my final semester?
text:
Will I be eligible to graduate if I'm required to sit a deferred or supplementary examination in my final semester?

No, if you're granted a deferred examination or supplementary assessment and subsequently complete program requirements when this grade is finalised, you'll be conferred the award in the next available graduation period. For example, if you were conditional to graduate at the end of Semester 1, but you have a deferred exam, your graduation would be end of Semester 2 in the December ceremonies.

page_title: What is a tutorial?
breadcrumb: What is a tutorial?
text:
What is a tutorial?

What is a tutorial? Home Information and services A tutorial is a small group learning session run by a tutor. Tutorials are designed to help you explore and understand course material presented in lectures.

page_title: How can I get help completing a certified document request?
breadcrumb: How can I get help completing a certified document request?
text:
How can I get help completing a certified document request?

Before submitting a certified document request, please ensure that you've read and understood our information on my.UQ.

If you’re having trouble submitting the request, check that:

- all required fields are completed
- there are no error messages displayed on the request
- you’re submitting the request using a Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox browser

If you still need help, contact Student Central for advice.

page_title: I want to have a BBQ or function on campus with alcohol, how can I organise this?
breadcrumb: I want to have a BBQ or function on campus with alcohol, how can I organise this?
text:
I want to have a BBQ or function on campus with alcohol, how can I organise this?

If you plan on having alcohol at your event or function, you'll need permission. To apply for permission, see the information on alcohol on campus.

If you plan on having alcohol at your event or function, you'll need permission. To apply for permission, see the information on alcohol on campus.

page_title: How do I set a default digital signature in Adobe Acrobat DC?
breadcrumb: How do I set a default digital signature in Adobe Acrobat DC?
text:
How do I set a default digital signature in Adobe Acrobat DC?

To set a default digital signature (digital ID) in Adobe Acrobat, follow the instructions at ‘Specify the default digital ID’ on the Adobe Digital IDs page.

For more information about digital signatures, see Digitally sign PDFs (staff login required) on the Systems Training Hub.

page_title: How do I resolve errors when enrolling my phone number for Duo MFA?
breadcrumb: How do I resolve errors when enrolling my phone number for Duo MFA?
text:
How do I resolve errors when enrolling my phone number for Duo MFA?

You may encounter the following error during enrolment:

'This number is not usable for authentication. Please contact your administrator.'

This error usually occurs if you are activating MFA for multiple user accounts (e.g. a staff account and a student account) and have already enrolled your mobile phone and mobile number for MFA with an existing account.

To use your mobile phone for MFA on additional accounts, it is necessary to add it as a 'Tablet' device. See 'How do I activate MFA for multiple accounts?' for more information.

page_title: When are my fees due?
breadcrumb: When are my fees due?
text:
When are my fees due?

The due dates for your fees are listed on the academic calendar for standard semesters or on the important dates tables for non-standard teaching periods. Fees due dates for your courses will also be displayed on your mySI-net (student login required) 'Financials' homepage.

If you're a commencing student, see our Starting at UQ website for more information about student finances.

page_title: How do I get my degree authenticated for an Apostille Certificate?
breadcrumb: How do I get my degree authenticated for an Apostille Certificate?
text:
How do I get my degree authenticated for an Apostille Certificate?

Australian educational documents sometimes need to be legalised by the Australian Government before they can be used overseas.

If you require your graduation documents to be authenticated by UQ, so that you can obtain an Apostille Certificate from the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT), Student Central can place a stamp on your original degree certificate (testamur) and/or academic transcript verifying that it is a ‘true and accurate record’. See the DFAT website for information about getting an Apostille Certificate.

page_title: I am having problems signing in to My eQuals. Where can I find access information?
breadcrumb: I am having problems signing in to My eQuals. Where can I find access information?
text:
I am having problems signing in to My eQuals. Where can I find access information?

All current students, newly conferred graduates and past students with a student login will be provided access to their My eQuals documents via their student login.

Past students without a student login will be required to nominate an email address at the point of purchase. Details of how to gain access will be provided to this email address.

page_title: I have a GPA between 4.0 and 4.49. Can I still do an MBA?
breadcrumb: I have a GPA between 4.0 and 4.49. Can I still do an MBA?
text:
I have a GPA between 4.0 and 4.49. Can I still do an MBA?

In these cases, you may be offered an articulation offer for the Graduate Diploma in Business Administration (GDipBA).

Upon successful completion of the GDipBA (8 courses or 16 units), with a minimum grade point average of 4.5, you can articulate into the Master of Business Administration (MBA) program to complete the remaining 4 courses (8 units). Once you've completed the GDipBA program and met the program GPA requirements, you'll need to apply for a program change into the MBA (via mySI-net).

page_title: Can I request that the full text of my Higher Degree by Research (HDR) thesis be made available open access via UQ eSpace?
breadcrumb: Can I request that the full text of my Higher Degree by Research (HDR) thesis be made available open access via UQ eSpace?
text:
Can I request that the full text of my Higher Degree by Research (HDR) thesis be made available open access via UQ eSpace?

**If you have completed a Higher Degree by Research (HDR) thesis (PhD or MPhil):**

Complete the Request for Open Access to Thesis form (PDF, 170KB) to provide permission to make the full text of your UQ HDR thesis available open access. Email the completed form to graduateresearchschool@uq.edu.au.

If you wish to request open access for your Undergraduate, Honours, Masters (by coursework) or Professional Doctorate thesis, go to the open access request information for non-HDR theses.

For further detail, email UQ eSpace.

page_title: How can I get help completing a Credit or Exemption form?
breadcrumb: How can I get help completing a Credit or Exemption form?
text:
How can I get help completing a Credit or Exemption form?

If you need help with completing the online Credit or Exemption form, watch the help video for a step-by-step guide. Please ensure you complete this form using a Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox browser.

You can only submit a Credit or Exemption request if you are active in a UQ program. For further information about eligibility and requirements for applying for credit, see credit and exemptions for previous study.

If you have confirmed that you have an active program, check the following list to ensure you have completed the form correctly:

- all required fields are completed
- there are no error messages displayed on the form
- you are submitting the form using a Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox browser.

If you are still experiencing issues, contact Student Central for advice.

page_title: What is an invitation statement?
breadcrumb: What is an invitation statement?
text:
What is an invitation statement?

A standard invitation statement can be used by currently enrolled international students to support visa applications for family and friends to visit you in Australia. It can be provided to government agencies (e.g. Department of Home Affairs).

This statement includes:

- your name, date of birth and student number
- your program name
- your attendance (full-time or part-time)
- study dates of your current and/or future enrolment
- the names of the family and friends you wish to invite
- a statement that The University of Queensland has no objection to the invitation request provided all immigration regulations are met

A graduation invitation statement can be used by international students to support visa applications for family and friends to attend your graduation ceremony. It can be provided to organisations and government agencies (e.g. Department of Home Affairs).

This statement includes:

- the names of the family and friends you wish to invite to your graduation

- a statement that The University of Queensland has no objection to the invitation request provided all immigration regulations are met

page_title: I am a healthcare worker student. Can I provide evidence in other formats, such as an immunisation history statement instead of a completed UQ Immunisation Record Form?
breadcrumb: I am a healthcare worker student. Can I provide evidence in other formats, such as an immunisation history statement instead of a completed UQ Immunisation Record Form?
text:
I am a healthcare worker student. Can I provide evidence in other formats, such as an immunisation history statement instead of a completed UQ Immunisation Record Form?

It is preferred that all healthcare worker (HCW) students submit a UQ Immunisation Record Form (Form A or Form B) completed by their medical practitioner. These forms align with the requirements of UQ and Queensland Health. However documentation clearly generated by a health care provider including immunisation certificates, Immunisation History Stetements and laboratory results are also acceptable provided they contain your details (name and date of birth) and are recorded in English. Alternatively, your medical practitioner can add the new evidence to the Immunisation Record Form as long as they sign and date beside the new entry.

Please note HCW students also need to submit the Tuberculosis Risk Assessment form for students.

Letters from parents, approximate dose dates and unlabelled medical evidence will not be accepted.

page_title: Where can I download referencing software like EndNote?
breadcrumb: Where can I download referencing software like EndNote?
text:
Where can I download referencing software like EndNote?

UQ staff and students can install EndNote referencing software on their computers. It is also available on Library computers.

If you are a staff member from the Royal Brisbane and Women's Hospital, contact the UQ Herston Health Sciences Library to get a copy of EndNote.

page_title: How do I book an appointment with the Global Experiences Team?
breadcrumb: How do I book an appointment with the Global Experiences Team?
text:
How do I book an appointment with the Global Experiences Team?

You can book an appointment through StudentHub (student login required). See the Global Experiences website for a list of advisers and the regions they look after.

Appointments with the Global Experiences Team are available between 9.00am – 3pm most days during semester.

If you want to make an appointment outside these hours or during the holiday periods, email iget@uq.edu.au.

page_title: Do I still graduate if I'm going to do honours next year?
breadcrumb: Do I still graduate if I'm going to do honours next year?
text:
Do I still graduate if I'm going to do honours next year?

Yes, but only if you're finishing a bachelor degree this semester and plan on completing an additional one year honours program next year. You'll graduate from your pass degree (e.g. Bachelor of Science) at the end of the current semester and then graduate a second time when you've completed the requirements for the separate honours degree (e.g. Bachelor of Science (Honours)). You can choose to attend both or just one ceremony.

If you're completing a degree with a 'built in' honours year (e.g. Bachelor of Laws (Honours)), you'll graduate once – after you've completed the entire program.

page_title: What will graduation cost?
breadcrumb: What will graduation cost?
text:
What will graduation cost?

From 27 October, you can register your attendance through GFP Events.

Attending a graduation ceremony often involves a number of different costs. UQ has consolidated some of these costs into a single package.

The package will ensure that graduands comply with UQ’s academic dress policy and will facilitate a smooth registration process on the day.

The cost of registering to attend your graduation is $100, **after** **the registration due date graduates** will be charged a $50 late fee** **on top of the attendance fee. 

This registration cost includes:

- academic dress hire, including a trencher or bonnet (graduation cap) for you to keep
- tickets for 2 guests to attend the ceremony
- a beverage of your choice
- a digital download of your stage photo (via the GFP events website)
- a cylinder memento

There is no discount for providing your own academic dress.

Learn how to register for your December ceremony.

page_title: How can I access a full text copy of a UQ thesis?
breadcrumb: How can I access a full text copy of a UQ thesis?
text:
How can I access a full text copy of a UQ thesis?

You can search for UQ theses (including PhD dissertations) in:

- Library Search
- UQ eSpace online repository.

These may be available online or in physical format. Online theses will be either open access and freely available to all, or available only to UQ staff and students.

Check our Theses guide for techniques for finding UQ, Australian and International theses.

- Find the item in Library Search.
 Theses are held in different locations. You may be able to borrow the thesis or you may need to make an appointment to view it in the FW Robinson Reading Room in Fryer Library.
- In the Get it section of the item record:
	- Log in to select Place a request
- Select your preferred Pickup location.
 Pickup locations where you are expected to retrieve the thesis from the shelf yourself will not be listed and the item record will have a location and call number to help you find the item on the shelf.
- Click Submit.
 
- If Fryer Library is the only pickup location option, you also need to book an appointment to view the thesis.
	- In the booking form, note that you have placed a request for the thesis.
- If you prefer, you can wait until you receive an email confirming the item has arrived before making the booking. Bookings can be made one week in advance.

Check Library Search for an open access, online copy of the thesis.

If the thesis is not open access:

- Request a copy through your local library (charges may apply).
- Check how libraries can order copies of UQ theses.
- Contact Fryer Library to arrange to view the physical format thesis.

page_title: What happens if I smoke on campus now that UQ is smoke-free?
breadcrumb: What happens if I smoke on campus now that UQ is smoke-free?
text:
What happens if I smoke on campus now that UQ is smoke-free?

What happens if I smoke on campus now that UQ is smoke-free? Home Information and services Any staff member, student or visitor who smokes on a UQ campus or UQ-controlled site may be approached by staff, students or campus security and reminded about our smoke-free policy.

page_title: What is a PASS class?
breadcrumb: What is a PASS class?
text:
What is a PASS class?

Some courses at UQ offer PASS classes. PASS stands for peer assisted study session. PASS classes are designed to complement lectures, tutorials and other course materials, and can help you understand how to apply theories.

PASS Leaders are high-achieving students who have recently completed the course that you're studying and are there to help you explore and gain a better understanding of course concepts.

page_title: Can I pay my fees through a third party?
breadcrumb: Can I pay my fees through a third party?
text:
Can I pay my fees through a third party?

No, never pay your University fees through a third party, as a third-party payment method is potentially a scam.

Scammers may contact you on social media or be recommended by a friend and offer an arrangement where you pay them a discounted amount and they undertake to pay the full amount to the University on your behalf. They make payment to the University using stolen credit card(s) and these payments are eventually declined.

Victims lose the money they paid to the scammer and several weeks into semester they find out their fees are still outstanding, and risk cancellation of enrolment for non-payment. Stay up-to-date with the latest scam warnings on Scamwatch.

page_title: Can I defer my commencement of the Doctor of Medicine (MD) program?
breadcrumb: Can I defer my commencement of the Doctor of Medicine (MD) program?
text:
Can I defer my commencement of the Doctor of Medicine (MD) program?

The University of Queensland does not generally permit deferment to applicants admitted to the Graduate Entry MD program; except to those students who are in the final year of a bachelor degree at time of application and who need to undertake additional training relating to that degree. Deferment will be permitted for these applicants to enable them to:

- Undertake a recognised one year research or postgraduate program(ie Honours or Master's degree)
- Complete non-degree requirements for the purpose of obtaining professional registration related to the students current bachelor's degree, or
- Commence a PhD program at the University of Queensland (only). PhD candidates (UQ only) are permitted a maximum of two years deferment to complete laboratory requirements of their PhD program. After that period, the PhD enrolment must change to part-time study and the MD studies must commence in a full-time capacity.

page_title: Can I change the authentication method I use for Duo MFA?
breadcrumb: Can I change the authentication method I use for Duo MFA?
text:
Can I change the authentication method I use for Duo MFA?

Duo will automatically remember the last successfully used authentication method. However, you can choose from the available MFA authentication methods and devices each time you are prompted to MFA.

- When prompted to MFA, select 'Other options'.
 
 
 
- Choose one of the other options to authenticate:
	- Duo Push - Send an authentication request to your mobile device.
- Duo Mobile passcode - Enter a passcode generated from the Duo Mobile app.
- Hardware token - Enter a 6-digit time-based one-time password (TOTP), or enter a code from your Duo hardware token.
- Bypass code - Enter a bypass code provided by the ITS Service Desk.

page_title: For how many years of study can I receive HECS-HELP?
breadcrumb: For how many years of study can I receive HECS-HELP?
text:
For how many years of study can I receive HECS-HELP?

HECS-HELP is a loan that enables eligible students in Commonwealth supported places (CSP) to defer payment of their student contribution amounts via the tax system.

You are entitled to undertake 7 Years (7 EFTSL) of full-time study or part time equivalent of Commonwealth supported study at any Higher Education Instution. This is referred to as your Student Learning Entitlement (SLE). Any study that you undertake as a CSP from 1 January 2022 will count towards your SLE. For more information, see the Study Assist website.

page_title: Does tutorial participation contribute to the final grade for a course?
breadcrumb: Does tutorial participation contribute to the final grade for a course?
text:
Does tutorial participation contribute to the final grade for a course?

For some courses, tutorial participation forms part of the assessment. You can find an assessment summary for your course in the electronic course profile (ECP). If you are unsure of any assessment items, contact your course coordinator.

For some courses, tutorial participation forms part of the assessment. You can find an assessment summary for your course in the electronic course profile (ECP). If you are unsure of any assessment items, contact your course coordinator.

page_title: Can I study for actuary qualifications at UQ?
breadcrumb: Can I study for actuary qualifications at UQ?
text:
Can I study for actuary qualifications at UQ?

UQ doesn't offer a dedicated qualification in actuary studies. If you're interested in finance or economics, you might like to consider the Bachelor of Advanced Finance and Economics (Honours).

UQ doesn't offer a dedicated qualification in actuary studies. If you're interested in finance or economics, you might like to consider the Bachelor of Advanced Finance and Economics (Honours).

page_title: How many courses do I have to take on exchange?
breadcrumb: How many courses do I have to take on exchange?
text:
How many courses do I have to take on exchange?

The number of courses that you need to take while on exchange at your host university could vary between 2-12 individual courses. Each host university is quite different, so it's important to understand the full-time study load required by your host.


You can find the full-time study load for each of the host universities on the individual host university page. The full-time study load is usually listed in 'credits', 'ECTS', 'hours', 'units' or 'courses'.


To transfer a full-time study load (8 UQ units) of credit back to your UQ degree, you must successfully complete the full-time study load listed for your host university.


For example:

- 
	A full-time study load at Purdue University, USA is 15 credits per semester. You must complete 15 credits of approved study at Purdue to gain 8 units of credit back at UQ. This is generally achieved by completing 5 Purdue courses worth 3 credit points each. 
- 
	A full-time study load at University College Dublin, Ireland is 30 ECTS per semester. You must complete 30 ECTS of approved study at University College Dublin to gain 8 units of credit back at UQ. This is generally achieved by completing 6 UCD courses worth 5 ECTS each.

page_title: What is graduate entry?
breadcrumb: What is graduate entry?
text:
What is graduate entry?

What is graduate entry? Home Information and services Some programs require you to have completed an undergraduate degree to be eligible for entry. These programs are called 'graduate entry' programs.

page_title: Can I charge my laptop at Central Library?
breadcrumb: Can I charge my laptop at Central Library?
text:
Can I charge my laptop at Central Library?

Yes, staff and students can borrow a free laptop charging locker for up to 24 hours.

You'll find these on Level 1, Central Library. Make sure you bring your UQ ID card to borrow a locker. Instructions for use are displayed on the locker screen.

You can also use monitors and docks where you can connect your USB-C compatible laptop or device (this includes some tablets) at a study spot with a 24-inch screen, keyboard, and mouse. They are located on:

- Level 3 Architecture and Music Library
- Level 1 Central Library
- Postgraduate study centre, level 4 Central Library
- Level 2 Dorothy Hill Engineering and Sciences Library
- Postgraduate study area, level 4 Duhig Tower
- Level 2 Duhig Tower
- Level 6 Dutton Park Health Sciences Library
- Level 2 JK Murray Library
- Level 2 Walter Harrison Law Library.

page_title: Am I eligible for Elite Athlete Status?
breadcrumb: Am I eligible for Elite Athlete Status?
text:
Am I eligible for Elite Athlete Status?

To be eligible for Elite Athlete Status, you must be representing Australia or an Australian State or Territory in an ASC supported and recognised sport. If you fall outside of this definition you can still apply, however the decision will be at the Academic Registrar’s discretion.

For more information about eligibility and considerations for elite athletes, see the Programs and Assessments for Elite Athletes Procedures [3.50.07]. For more information about the UQ Elite Athlete Program, see the UQ Sport website.

page_title: Can I go on the same short-term global experience twice?
breadcrumb: Can I go on the same short-term global experience twice?
text:
Can I go on the same short-term global experience twice?

Can I go on the same short-term global experience twice? Home Information and services No, you cannot go on the same short-term global experience twice. You can complete a second short-term global experience, but it must be with a different institution or organisation.

page_title: What do I need to do if I'm not attending my graduation ceremony?
breadcrumb: What do I need to do if I'm not attending my graduation ceremony?
text:
What do I need to do if I'm not attending my graduation ceremony?

You should still complete your Graduation Information Check through the invitation email you received, even if you're not planning to attend a ceremony. You'll be graduated in absentia and your graduation documents will be issued to you after the ceremony period.

If you've changed address, it's important that you update your mailing address in mySI-net by the address change deadline.

See my.UQ for more information on preparing for graduation.

page_title: What is a timetable clash?
breadcrumb: What is a timetable clash?
text:
What is a timetable clash?

For end-of-semester exams, a timetable clash occurs when:

- two of your exams are timetabled in the same session, or
- three exams are scheduled on a single calendar day during the end-of-semester examination period.

For mid-semester exams, a timetable clash occurs when:

- two of your exams are timetabled for the same session, or
- three exams are scheduled on a single calendar day outside of scheduled classes (i.e. on a Saturday).

See my.UQ for information on alternative exam arrangements.

page_title: Can courses studied in a graduate certificate or graduate diploma program count towards a coursework master's program?
breadcrumb: Can courses studied in a graduate certificate or graduate diploma program count towards a coursework master's program?
text:
Can courses studied in a graduate certificate or graduate diploma program count towards a coursework master's program?

Some disciplines offer a master's program which includes a graduate certificate and graduate diploma. In many cases, the successful completion of courses in the graduate certificate can build towards the graduate diploma which then builds towards the completion of the master's program.

For further information about postgraduate program suites, see applying for postgraduate coursework study.

For information about UQ programs, including entry requirements, early exit points and further study options, search for a program on our Future Students website.

page_title: How do I Duo MFA before an online exam?
breadcrumb: How do I Duo MFA before an online exam?
text:
How do I Duo MFA before an online exam?

There are four options to MFA before an online exam:

- Advise the exam supervisor that you need to log in with MFA using your mobile phone device, then turn off your device and place it on the floor. When authenticating with MFA, ensure you click 'Trust this browser' so you are not required to reauthenticate.
- Advise the exam supervisor that you need to log in with MFA using a YubiKey (USB device), then confirm that the device is removed and put away.
- Advise the exam supervisor that you need to log in with MFA using a Duo token, then confirm that the device is put away.
- Generate a temporary passcode from the MFA Management portal. You should choose this option if your exam requires you to clear your browser's cookies and cache.

If you have **technical problems**, AskUs exam support is available throughout the exam period.

page_title: Do I need to get my qualifications and transcripts translated into English?
breadcrumb: Do I need to get my qualifications and transcripts translated into English?
text:
Do I need to get my qualifications and transcripts translated into English?

Yes, professional English translations are required for all non-English documents sent as part of your application.

The Institute of Modern Languages (IML-UQ) provides pay-by-use NAATI certified translation and interpreting services for the UQ community and the general public.

Internationally, one of UQ's International Educational Representatives may be able to help you organise document translation and certification.

page_title: What is a certificate?
breadcrumb: What is a certificate?
text:
What is a certificate?

At UQ, a certificate usually refers to a graduation certificate, which is also called a testamur. See our my.UQ website for information on degree certificates, transcripts and documents.

A certificate can also refer to a level of qualification. See the Australian Qualifications Framework (AQF) for information on different qualification levels.

page_title: How do I find accessible housing on UQ Rentals?
breadcrumb: How do I find accessible housing on UQ Rentals?
text:
How do I find accessible housing on UQ Rentals?

The search function in UQ Rentals allows the comparison of properties on accessibility features, such as ground-floor amenities, access ramps, internal lifts, vibrating alarms, and adapted bedrooms and bathrooms.

To search on these metrics, complete a general search, then press the “Mobility Facilities” button on the Results Page for these options.

If you continue to have difficulty finding a property that is right for you, please contact the Accommodation Team at accommodation@uq.edu.au or on 1300 275 870.

page_title: How do I renew or change my Overseas Student Health Cover (OSHC)?
breadcrumb: How do I renew or change my Overseas Student Health Cover (OSHC)?
text:
How do I renew or change my Overseas Student Health Cover (OSHC)?

If you have your OSHC cover with Allianz Care Australia, you can apply to renew or change your OSHC online through the Allianz Care Australia website. Otherwise, contact your government-approved OSHC provider for options available to renew or change your cover.

page_title: What documents do I need to supply for a credit or exemption request?
breadcrumb: What documents do I need to supply for a credit or exemption request?
text:
What documents do I need to supply for a credit or exemption request?

If your prior study was at UQ, you do not need to provide any documentation with your request.

If your prior study was undertaken at another institution, you will need to supply:

- Official course profiles from each course. This should include extended syllabus, aims and objectives, contact hours, texts and references and assessment. An extract from the institution's handbook is usually not sufficient.
- Official academic transcript/s. This can either be scanned and uploaded, or by providing a URL for a digital academic transcript issued via a platform such as My eQuals.

For information about eligibility and requirements for applying for credit, visit credit and exemptions for previous study.

page_title: How often are lectures held?
breadcrumb: How often are lectures held?
text:
How often are lectures held?

It depends on which courses you are planning on studying. To find out which classes you must attend for a course, check the course's electronic course profile (ECP). To find a course's ECP and see the required classes:

- Go to the Programs and Courses website.
- Search for and select a course and starting semester.
- Select 'Course Profile'.
- Read the 'Contact Hours Per Week' section under the 'Course Details' section.
- Read the 'Timetable section'.

For help with understanding class contact hour codes, see the 'Contact hours' section on the Choosing your courses page.

You can also use the Public Timetable to see class times and venues. However, the Public Timetable will show **all** available class times for a course, and you may not need to attend all classes. Make sure you read all information under 'What day, what time and where' to help you understand how many classes of each type you need to attend for each course.

page_title: How do I download my student H drive files?
breadcrumb: How do I download my student H drive files?
text:
How do I download my student H drive files?

- On your computer, go to the my.UQ Dashboard.
- Log in with your UQ username and password on the UQ Authenticate screen.
- Select 'My files' from the left menu.
 
 
 
- Enter your UQ password, then click 'Login'.
- Select the file(s) you wish to download.
 To select multiple files, hold Ctrl (Windows) or Command (Mac) on your keyboard whilst selecting the files.
- Click 'Download'.
 
 
 
 A zipped folder will download to your computer containing the files.

page_title: How many exchange places are available?
breadcrumb: How many exchange places are available?
text:
How many exchange places are available?

The number of exchange places available at each host university varies greatly. The Semester-based Exchange Program is reciprocal, which means that the number of students that are sent to study at UQ determines the number of places that UQ students have available to them overseas.


If there are limited places at a host university, the allocation of places will consider, but is not limited to:

- places available at host university
- minimum criteria relevant to specific exchange partners
- academic suitability: discipline/course availability
- eligibility for Australian Government funding to partners in Asia
- whether you have previously participated in a UQ student exchange program
- academic merit (including cumulative GPA and number of units completed)


If you've been on exchange through UQ before, you'll be considered after students who have never been on exchange through UQ.


Due to the restrictions and popularity of some partners, it's best to be flexible with your preferences – this way you'll also be more likely to have an enjoyable exchange experience.

page_title: I work full-time. Can I complete a program at night or on weekends?
breadcrumb: I work full-time. Can I complete a program at night or on weekends?
text:
I work full-time. Can I complete a program at night or on weekends?

Majority of UQ courses are taught on campus during regular hours. Some UQ courses may be offered outside of regular hours or externally, however we cannot guarantee that you will be able to complete your entire program this way.

You can see if a program is offered externally by searching for the program on our Future Students website and viewing the delivery location – programs you can complete online will list 'external' as the delivery location.

Students can enrol in a mixture of internal and external courses for convenience. To search for external courses on our Future Students website:

- Search for a program.
- Select the program and then select 'See all courses in this program'.
- Select individual courses – the 'Mode' column will show if a course is offered externally.

For more information on course offerings, contact us.

page_title: Is deferring and interrupting my studies the same thing?
breadcrumb: Is deferring and interrupting my studies the same thing?
text:
Is deferring and interrupting my studies the same thing?

No. Deferring your studies means to delay starting your program to a future semester. If you have started studying and you want to take a temporary break, this is called interrupting your studies.

No. Deferring your studies means to delay starting your program to a future semester. If you have started studying and you want to take a temporary break, this is called interrupting your studies.

page_title: How many units are my courses worth?
breadcrumb: How many units are my courses worth?
text:
How many units are my courses worth?

Most UQ courses are 2 units each. To find out the unit value of a course, search for the course on the Programs and Courses website and select the relevant teaching period – the course's unit value is listed under 'Units' in the information box.

Most UQ courses are 2 units each. To find out the unit value of a course, search for the course on the Programs and Courses website and select the relevant teaching period – the course's unit value is listed under 'Units' in the information box.

page_title: How can I clear passwords from my browser?
breadcrumb: How can I clear passwords from my browser?
text:
How can I clear passwords from my browser?

The process for removing saved passwords depends on the web browser you are using:

- Firefox on Windows: Click the three lines menu > 'Settings' > 'Privacy & Security' > 'Logins and Passwords' > 'Saved Logins...'
- Firefox on Apple: Go to the 'Firefox' menu > 'Preferences' > 'Privacy & Security' > 'Logins and Passwords' > 'Saved Logins...'
- Google Chrome on Windows: Hold down Control + Shift + Delete > tick 'Passwords & other sign-in data'
- Google Chrome on Apple: Hold down Command + Shift + Delete > tick 'Passwords & other sign-in data'
- Microsoft Edge: Click the ellipsis > 'Settings' > 'Passwords'
- Safari: Go to the 'Safari' menu > 'Preferences' > 'Passwords'. For Macs you can also use Keychain to clear passwords.

For more help, do a Google search for your browser or contact AskUs in the UQ Library.

page_title: I signed up to My eQuals and logged in with my email address, why do I see no documents?
breadcrumb: I signed up to My eQuals and logged in with my email address, why do I see no documents?
text:
I signed up to My eQuals and logged in with my email address, why do I see no documents?

I signed up to My eQuals and logged in with my email address, why do I see no documents?

This option is only available to past students who no longer have a student login. If you fall into this category, access details will be sent to the email address you nominated during the ordering of your documents.

page_title: Can I study a course without being enrolled in a program?
breadcrumb: Can I study a course without being enrolled in a program?
text:
Can I study a course without being enrolled in a program?

Yes, this is called non-award study. Non-award study is where you enrol in a course, but you're not officially enrolled in a program (i.e. a degree). See our Future Students website for information on applying for non-award study.

Yes, this is called non-award study. Non-award study is where you enrol in a course, but you're not officially enrolled in a program (i.e. a degree). See our Future Students website for information on applying for non-award study.

page_title: Who do I contact if My eQuals documents are incorrect or not up to date?
breadcrumb: Who do I contact if My eQuals documents are incorrect or not up to date?
text:
Who do I contact if My eQuals documents are incorrect or not up to date?

Use the contact details below if you believe your My eQuals certified documents are not correct.

For academic records (including degree certificates, transcripts and AHEGS), contact Student Progression:

- Phone: (07) 3365 9194
- Email: transcripts@uq.edu.au

For other certified statements (e.g. completion statement): contact Student Central.

page_title: What is the MD?
breadcrumb: What is the MD?
text:
What is the MD?

The Doctor of Medicine (MD) is a 4 year graduate entry medical program at UQ.

There are 2 entry pathways to the MD program – Provisional Entry for school leavers and Graduate Entry for all other applicants.

The Doctor of Medicine (MD) is a 4 year graduate entry medical program at UQ.

There are 2 entry pathways to the MD program – Provisional Entry for school leavers and Graduate Entry for all other applicants.

page_title: If I am found guilty of misconduct will this appear on my university records?
breadcrumb: If I am found guilty of misconduct will this appear on my university records?
text:
If I am found guilty of misconduct will this appear on my university records?

Your guilty finding will be recorded on the University's Student Conduct database. The only penalties which will appear on your studies report or official academic transcript are removal of credit for a course (appearing as a grade of G (credit cancelled or refused – generally on disciplinary grounds)) and expulsion.

page_title: I've lost my degree certificate. What do I do?
breadcrumb: I've lost my degree certificate. What do I do?
text:
I've lost my degree certificate. What do I do?

I've lost my degree certificate. What do I do? Home Information and services If you've lost your testamur (degree certificate), see my.UQ for information on how to request a replacement paper testamur.

page_title: What is the International Student Admissions Test (ISAT)?
breadcrumb: What is the International Student Admissions Test (ISAT)?
text:
What is the International Student Admissions Test (ISAT)?

ISAT is the International Student Admissions Test and is required for international student applications to certain programs.

To find out if the program that you are interested in requires the completion of the ISAT test, search for your program on our Future Students website – entry requirements will be listed under the 'Program details' tab.

ISAT is conducted by the Australian Council for Educational Research (ACER). They produce and score the tests each year.

ISAT scores for critical reasoning and quantitative reasoning. ISAT also gives an overall score and a percentile rank.

More information can be found on the ACER website.

page_title: How do I enable Zoom end-to-end encryption (E2EE) for meetings?
breadcrumb: How do I enable Zoom end-to-end encryption (E2EE) for meetings?
text:
How do I enable Zoom end-to-end encryption (E2EE) for meetings?

End-to-end encryption (E2EE) is now available for Zoom meetings. Using E2EE in Zoom meetings will disable the following features:

- Join before host
- Cloud recording
- Live streaming
- Live transcription
- Breakout Rooms
- Polling
- Zoom Apps
- Zoom Whiteboard

- Sign into the Zoom web portal
- Select 'Settings' then select 'Meeting'.
- Toggle on 'Allow use of end-to-end encryption', then select 'enable' when prompted to enable 'Display Okta Authentication for end-to-end encryption' (this is required).

It is recommended that the 'Default encryption type' is set to 'Enhanced encryption' and that end-to-end encryption is only used for meetings requiring this.

- Schedule a new meeting using the Zoom client or the Zoom web portal (you can also enable E2EE by editing existing meetings).
- By default, all meetings will use 'Enhanced encryption' if configured as above.
- To change to E2EE, select the 'End-to-end encryption' option.
 
 
 
- Save the meeting schedule.

page_title: I already have a degree. What should I study if I want to be a teacher?
breadcrumb: I already have a degree. What should I study if I want to be a teacher?
text:
I already have a degree. What should I study if I want to be a teacher?

If you already hold a bachelor's degree, UQ offers two postgraduate teacher-preparation programs. These allow students to register as either a primary or secondary teacher: the Master of Teaching (Primary) and the Master of Teaching (Secondary). Entry requirements are listed under the 'Program details' tab of each program page.

See the School of Education website for information on the pathways to studying education at UQ.

page_title: How can I invite my family or friends to visit me in Australia while I study at UQ?
breadcrumb: How can I invite my family or friends to visit me in Australia while I study at UQ?
text:
How can I invite my family or friends to visit me in Australia while I study at UQ?

Currently-enrolled international students can request a standard invitation statement to support visa applications for family and friends to visit you in Australia. It can be provided to government agencies (e.g. Department of Home Affairs).

This statement includes:

- your name, date of birth and student number
- your program name
- your attendance (full-time or part-time)
- study dates of your current and/or future enrolment
- the names of the family and friends you wish to invite
- a statement that The University of Queensland has no objection to the invitation request provided all immigration regulations are met.

For further information about invitation statements and other certified documents, see our advice on certified documents from UQ.

We understand that due to the impacts of COVID-19 and current travel restrictions, it may be difficult for your family to visit you in Australia at the moment. It's a difficult time to be without family support. If you're feeling socially isolated, please contact Student Life for information on alternative support options.

page_title: I'm receiving the Zoom message 'This meeting is for authorized attendees only'. What should I do?
breadcrumb: I'm receiving the Zoom message 'This meeting is for authorized attendees only'. What should I do?
text:
I'm receiving the Zoom message 'This meeting is for authorized attendees only'. What should I do?

UQ Zoom meetings may be restricted to UQ Zoom accounts only. You will receive the following message if you try to connect to a restricted meeting and aren't signed into Zoom with your UQ account: 'This meeting is for authorized attendees only'.

If you receive this message:

- Click 'Sign in to Join'.
- Click 'Sign in with SSO'.
- Enter 'uqz' as the Company Domain, then click 'Continue'.
- Sign in with your UQ username and password.

page_title: Can I sit my exam off-campus?
breadcrumb: Can I sit my exam off-campus?
text:
Can I sit my exam off-campus?

There are limited circumstances when students may apply to sit their exam off-campus. Different eligibility criteria apply depending on whether you are an internal, external or multi-modal student.

See the my.UQ website for information on applying to sit an exam off-campus.

page_title: Why are my enrolments not showing up in My Timetable?
breadcrumb: Why are my enrolments not showing up in My Timetable?
text:
Why are my enrolments not showing up in My Timetable?

Why are my enrolments not showing up in My Timetable?

There is a small time delay when you enrol in mySI-net and when it appears in My Timetable. Check that you are looking at the correct year. Select the alternate year from the top right of the header on My Timetable.

page_title: I signed up to My eQuals with Facebook / Google+ / LinkedIn, why do I see no documents?
breadcrumb: I signed up to My eQuals with Facebook / Google+ / LinkedIn, why do I see no documents?
text:
I signed up to My eQuals with Facebook / Google+ / LinkedIn, why do I see no documents?

To access and view your documents, you will need to log in to My eQuals with your UQ student login first. For help, see My eQuals.


Otherwise, you may need to simply verify your email address and activate your account.


To verify your email address:


Log in to My eQuals using your Facebook / Google+ / LinkedIn details.

In the top right-hand menu, click your name to display a drop-down menu.

Select 'Profile settings'.

On this screen, check your email address status.

If listed as 'Not verified', send a verification email by selecting 'Verify' under the 'Actions' column.

Open the email and select 'Activate my account'

page_title: How long is the CASPer test and what sort of questions will I have to answer?
breadcrumb: How long is the CASPer test and what sort of questions will I have to answer?
text:
How long is the CASPer test and what sort of questions will I have to answer?

As CASPer is not a UQ function and information could change without notice, see:

- the FAQ section of the CASPer website for the length of the test, and
- the Understanding and preparing section of the CASPer website to find out the types of questions that the test contains.

For more information about how UQ is using the CASPer test, see Applying for the Bachelor of Veterinary Science (Honours).

page_title: What is a school?
breadcrumb: What is a school?
text:
What is a school?

At UQ, a school is a sub-division of a faculty – in other words, a faculty is a collection of schools. The school that teaches a course is listed on each course's electronic course profile (ECP) as the 'Coordinating Unit'.

See our my.UQ website for advice on contacting your faculty or school.

page_title: How do I copy and paste between my computer and the UQ Digital Workspace?
breadcrumb: How do I copy and paste between my computer and the UQ Digital Workspace?
text:
How do I copy and paste between my computer and the UQ Digital Workspace?

Cutting, copying and pasting between your computer and the UQ Digital Workspace works in the same way as if you were normally working on your computer.

- On your computer, or on the virtual desktop/app, highlight the text/image you want to copy.
- Press Ctrl + C on your keyboard to copy, or right-click and select 'Copy'.
- Go to the location you want to paste, whether that be your computer or virtual desktop/app.
- Press Ctrl + V on your keyboard to paste, or right-click and select 'Paste'.

- On your computer, or on the virtual desktop/app, highlight the text/image you want to copy.
- Press command + C on your keyboard to copy, or right-click and select 'Copy'.
- Go to the location you want to paste, whether that be your computer or virtual desktop/app.
- Press command + V on your keyboard to paste, or right-click and select 'Paste'.

Dragging and dropping files only works if the virtual desktop/app is opened through the Omnissa Horizon Client. It does not work on the browser client.

To drag and drop a file:

- From your computer, or on the virtual desktop/app, locate the file you want to move.
- Click and hold the file, then at the same time, drag it across to your computer, or virtual desktop/app.

page_title: How do I create or edit my email signature in Outlook?
breadcrumb: How do I create or edit my email signature in Outlook?
text:
How do I create or edit my email signature in Outlook?

Signatures in Outlook are managed slightly differently depending on which version of Outlook you are using. Follow the section below that matches your setup.

Signatures must be set up separately for each Outlook version and device you use.

- Click 'File', then select 'Settings'.
- Click 'Accounts', then 'Signatures'.

- Click 'Add signature'.
- Enter a name for the signature.
- Add and format your signature in the editor. Refer to the Digital Assets page for UQ email signature formatting instructions and options to download various email signature banners.
- Tick the optional boxes to:
	- Include a link to my bookings page in my signature
- Set default for new messages
- Set default for replies and forwards.
 
- Click 'Save'.

- Click the pencil icon next to the signature you want to edit.
- Make any changes as needed.
- Click 'Save'.

- Click 'File', then select 'Options'.
- Click 'Mail', then 'Signatures...'

- Click 'New'.
- Enter a name for the signature, then click 'OK'.
- Add and format your signature in the editor. Refer to the Digital Assets page for UQ email signature formatting instructions and options to download various email signature banners.
- Select if you want the signature to be assigned to 'New messages' and/or 'Replies/forwards'.
- Click 'OK', then 'OK' again.

- Select the signature to edit.
- Make any changes as needed.
- Click 'OK' to save.

- Click 'Outlook' from the menu bar, then select 'Settings...'
- Select 'Signatures'.

- Click '+'.
- Enter a name for the signature.
- Add and format your signature in the editor. Refer to the Digital Assets page for UQ email signature formatting instructions and options to download various email signature banners.
- Click 'Save'.
- Select if you want the signature to be assigned to 'New messages' and/or 'Replies/forwards'.
- Close the Signatures window.

- Select the signature, then click 'Edit'.
- Make any changes as needed, then click 'Save'.
- Close the Signatures window.

- Log in to the my.UQ dashboard and click 'Email'.
- Click the cog icon to open your settings.
- Click 'Accounts', then 'Signatures'.

- Click 'Add signature'.
- Enter a name for the signature.
- Add and format your signature in the editor. Refer to the Digital Assets page for UQ email signature formatting instructions and options to download various email signature banners.
- Tick the optional boxes to:
	- Include a link to my bookings page in my signature
- Set default for new messages
- Set default for replies and forwards.
 
- Click 'Save'.

- Click the pencil icon next to the signature you want to edit.
- Make any changes as needed.
- Click 'Save'.

- In the Outlook app, tap your profile icon.
- Tap the Settings cog wheel.
- Tap 'Signature'.
- Enter or update your signature text. Changes are saved automatically.

Mobile signatures are text only and do not support advanced formatting or images.

page_title: Can I borrow a laptop from the Library?
breadcrumb: Can I borrow a laptop from the Library?
text:
Can I borrow a laptop from the Library?

Yes, we offer:

- 24-hour loans to UQ students, UQ College students, staff and hospital staff members
- 28-day loans to undergraduate, coursework postgraduate and UQ college students.

Laptops can be borrowed from our free self service laptop loan lockers. You'll find these on Level 1, Central Library near the AskUs service point, and in Dorothy Hill Engineering and Sciences Library, Dutton Park Health Sciences Library, Herston Health Sciences Library and JK Murray Library (UQ Gatton).

Make sure you bring your UQ ID card to borrow and return the laptop. Instructions for use are displayed on the locker screen.

Monthly (28 day) laptop loans are also available for undergraduate and coursework postgraduate students who are enrolled in the current semester at the AskUs Desk:

- on Level 2 of the Dorothy Hill Engineering and Sciences Library during opening hours.
- on Level 2 of the JK Murray Library (UQ Gatton) during opening hours

Make sure you bring your card to borrow a laptop from these AskUs desks.

**Higher Degree by Research students**: contact your supervisor or administrative staff within your school or institute if you need assistance with a laptop for your studies.

page_title: How do I pay my deposit by Flywire?
breadcrumb: How do I pay my deposit by Flywire?
text:
How do I pay my deposit by Flywire?

After accepting your offer, you will receive an email when your invoice is ready. Allow 2 business days for the invoice to be issued.

When you receive this email:

- Log in to My Applications (student login required).
- Click the payment request notification. You will then be directed to the payment page.
- Select the 'Flywire' payment method to initiate a secure payment. Follow the step-by-step instructions to pay. If you have any problems with the payment process, contact Flywire’s support team.

The University will process your payment and if everything is satisfactory we will issue a Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE). A CoE is an official document that confirms you have accepted a place in a UQ program and you have paid your deposit. You need a CoE to apply for a student visa. Log in to My Applications (student login required) to view your CoE once it has been issued.

For more information, see the Flywire Payer Guide (student login required).

page_title: How do I get a new degree certificate?
breadcrumb: How do I get a new degree certificate?
text:
How do I get a new degree certificate?

To protect against the fraudulent use of testamurs (degree certificates), we don't reissue paper certificates except when the original has been permanently lost, damaged or destroyed. If you have changed your name, you can also apply to update your testamur, but you'll have to return the original.

See my.UQ for more information on degree certificates, transcripts and documents..

page_title: Can I go on exchange for longer than a year?
breadcrumb: Can I go on exchange for longer than a year?
text:
Can I go on exchange for longer than a year?

One year is the maximum period of exchange at a single partner university.


Depending on UQ faculty approval, it may be possible for you to complete more than one exchange at multiple partner universities.


For more information, see our advice on semester-based exchange.

page_title: What is Flywire?
breadcrumb: What is Flywire?
text:
What is Flywire?

UQ has partnered with Flywire to offer a secure and streamlined way for international students to make a payment from their home country. Flywire offers competitive foreign exchange rates and no hidden fees. You can track the progress of your payment online and receive email and text alerts each step of the way. Multilingual customer support via phone, chat and email is available 24/7 if you need assistance with your payment.

See my.UQ for more information.

page_title: How can I get help completing a Re-mark of Assessment form?
breadcrumb: How can I get help completing a Re-mark of Assessment form?
text:
How can I get help completing a Re-mark of Assessment form?

A Re-mark of Assessment (RoA) is accessed via 'My Requests' on my.UQ. When submitting an online request for Re-mark of Assessment, only eligible courses will be available for selection. That is, the following criteria must exist:

- Course must be currently enrolled or has a finalised grade; and
- Course belongs to a term that commenced in the last twelve months.

You will only be able to submit an RoA request for one assessment item. You will need to submit separate requests if you want to request RoA for multiple assessment items.

Also check the following list to ensure you have completed the form correctly:

- All required fields are completed.
- There are no error messages displayed on the form.
- You are submitting the form using a Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox browser.

If you are still experiencing issues submitting the online form, please contact the Student Central for further assistance.

page_title: I got a parking ticket, but I paid for parking. What do I do?
breadcrumb: I got a parking ticket, but I paid for parking. What do I do?
text:
I got a parking ticket, but I paid for parking. What do I do?

If you think there may be cause for your infringement to be waived, there's an appeals process. See our advice on lodging an infringement appeal.

If you think there may be cause for your infringement to be waived, there's an appeals process. See our advice on lodging an infringement appeal.

page_title: Can I pre-book accommodation before I arrive in Brisbane?
breadcrumb: Can I pre-book accommodation before I arrive in Brisbane?
text:
Can I pre-book accommodation before I arrive in Brisbane?

If you are travelling from an area outside of Brisbane, interstate or overseas and have not yet arranged accommodation, we recommend you book short term/ temporary accommodation for around 1-2 weeks so you can physically inspect properties. For further information, please visit Short Term Accommodation.

It is possible to complete a lease agreement with a Provider prior to landing in Brisbane, though for private rentals, we strongly recommend that you don’t sign a lease agreement or begin a tenancy without personally inspecting the property first. UQ does not inspect private rental properties. 

For our approved providers (both on and off-campus), they: 

- have formalised agreements with the University of Queensland to provide a high standard of care
- their buildings have been inspected by UQ staff


If you require further assistance, please contact the Accommodation team – accommodation@uq.edu.au or 1300 275 870.

page_title: Can I take language courses as part of my degree?
breadcrumb: Can I take language courses as part of my degree?
text:
Can I take language courses as part of my degree?

Many programs at UQ allow you to take a language as a major or minor, and many others allow students to take language courses as electives. Contact your faculty to ask about the structure of your degree and whether languages can be taken as part of it.

You may also like to consider studying a concurrent Diploma in Languages.

page_title: What is a bachelor's program?
breadcrumb: What is a bachelor's program?
text:
What is a bachelor's program?

A bachelor's program is an Australian Qualifications Framework (AQF) level 7 qualification that usually requires 3 to 4 years of full-time study to complete.

Bachelor's programs are designed to provide you with the knowledge and skills to work in a specialist area, and within a bachelor's program there is often an opportunity to choose further areas of specialisation through majors. Some bachelor's programs can be taken in conjunction with another bachelor's degree as a dual program. Bachelor's programs are often the first step towards postgraduate study.

Search the Future Students website for bachelor programs available at UQ.

page_title: Where is UQ Security located?
breadcrumb: Where is UQ Security located?
text:
Where is UQ Security located?

UQ Security is located at:

- St. Lucia – Prentice Building 42
- Gatton – NW Briton Building 8101

page_title: Where do I access My Timetable?
breadcrumb: Where do I access My Timetable?
text:
Where do I access My Timetable?

Where do I access My Timetable? Home Information and services To access My Timetable, log into your my.UQ Dashboard and select 'My Timetable' from the side menu.

page_title: How do I change my course delivery mode?
breadcrumb: How do I change my course delivery mode?
text:
How do I change my course delivery mode?

Some courses may be available in more than one delivery mode, depending on course requirements.

To find out the available delivery modes for your courses, search for the course on the Programs and Courses website – the available delivery modes will be listed under ‘Mode’.

If, due to travel restrictions, you commence study online by enrolling in external mode courses and travel to Queensland is possible later in the semester, you may be able to change to an internal course mode.

Whether you can change course mode during the semester depends on timing and course requirements. You will be able to change course mode on mySI-net up until the end of the Week 2 of the semester by using the drop/add course process.

Any changes required after Week 2 will need to be requested through a Late Addition of Course (LAOC) request.

page_title: I expect to graduate soon, but UQ has not contacted me. What do I do?
breadcrumb: I expect to graduate soon, but UQ has not contacted me. What do I do?
text:
I expect to graduate soon, but UQ has not contacted me. What do I do?

If you haven't heard from UQ regarding your graduation by the end of June (for July ceremonies) or the middle of November (for December ceremonies), and you expect to graduate, contact your faculty requesting an assessment of your eligibility to graduate.

See my.UQ for more information on graduation ceremonies.

page_title: What are gateway, capstone and cornerstone courses in my Bachelor of Arts program?
breadcrumb: What are gateway, capstone and cornerstone courses in my Bachelor of Arts program?
text:
What are gateway, capstone and cornerstone courses in my Bachelor of Arts program?

Gateway courses appear at the beginning of your major (or extended major) and introduce the subject area (where no prior knowledge is assumed). Gateway courses usually make up prerequisites for more advanced courses within the major (or extended major) – allowing students to progress through their studies.

For the bulk of the major (or extended major), students are generally able to select courses of most interest to them. As such, not all students studying a particular subject area will complete the same courses throughout their degree.

Cornerstone and capstone courses are designed to ensure students learn the most important aspects of a particular subject area. These courses maintain the integrity of the major and ensure a base standard of knowledge that all students will have upon their graduation.

page_title: What are program and requirements?
breadcrumb: What are program and requirements?
text:
What are program and requirements?

Program and course requirements list all the courses offered in your program and any requirements that are specific to your program. You should consult these when planning your program.

If you are a new student, see the Starting at UQ website for advice on planning your program.

If you are a current student, see the my.UQ website for advice on choosing your courses.

page_title: Is it possible to fail the CASPer test?
breadcrumb: Is it possible to fail the CASPer test?
text:
Is it possible to fail the CASPer test?

There are no right or wrong answers. The test is identifying qualities for suitability for veterinary science. You are advised to respond to the questions as clearly and effectively as you can within the timeframe.

For more information about how UQ is using the CASPer test, see Applying for the Bachelor of Veterinary Science (Honours).

page_title: Where is the best place to park at UQ?

text:
Where is the best place to park at UQ?

text:
What do I do if I can't see the option to enter a passcode for Duo MFA?

It's likely you have selected the option to automatically send a 'Duo Push' notification to your device when you log in.

To authenticate using a passcode when Duo has already sent a push notification:

- Click 'Other options' on the MFA prompt.
- Select the 'Duo Mobile passcode' option.
- Enter the passcode generated from the Duo Mobile app, then click 'Verify'.

However, please still submit an IT request so that these needs can be captured. Software that's not able to run on the UQ Digital Workspace will continue to be delivered to physical computers until the platform can be expanded to support them. 

Students and staff can request new software on the UQ Digital Workspace by submitting a:
