page_title: Why would I want to link my My eQuals account to social media (Facebook, Google, LinkedIn) or private email address?
breadcrumb: Why would I want to link my My eQuals account to social media (Facebook, Google, LinkedIn) or private email address?
text:
Why would I want to link my My eQuals account to social media (Facebook, Google, LinkedIn) or private email address?

Linking your account with social media or a private email account will allow you to log in using your linked account details when you return to My eQuals. You may find the social media login quicker to use, or your personal email easier to remember because you use it every day. In addition, we are aware that some past students cease to use their student login. If you link your social media or private email account before this happens, you will still be able to access your My eQuals account.

If you choose to link your My eQuals account to social media you will not be sharing your documents with any of these platforms. Linking simply allows you to use a social media or email account to log in to your My eQuals account. However, you are still responsible for the security of your social media login. If your social media account is hacked, those responsible may also be able to access your My eQuals account. Similarly, if you share your login with friends and family, which you shouldn't, they could access your My eQuals account.

page_title: Can I start a program in Summer Semester?
breadcrumb: Can I start a program in Summer Semester?
text:
Can I start a program in Summer Semester?

Most programs are not offered for commencement in Summer Semester.

To find out when you can start a program, search for the program on the Future Students website – program start dates are listed under the 'Program details' tab.

Most programs are not offered for commencement in Summer Semester.

page_title: Outlook Classic for uqconnect mailboxes
breadcrumb: Outlook Classic for uqconnect mailboxes
text:
Outlook Classic for uqconnect mailboxes

Some alumni and past-students may receive the following pop up message for those needing to import/export mail, calendar and contact items to or from their legacy account with the New Outlook:

“This account cannot be added. The licence provided by your work or school does not enable access to Outlook for Windows.”

Alumni and past-students can still install Outlook Classic to manage their uqconnect emails.

Please follow the below guide on how to install Outlook Classic as well as how to export/import your emails and contacts.

- Open this link: https://support.microsoft.com/en-au/office/install-or-reinstall-classic-outlook-on-a-windows-pc-5c94902b-31a5-4274-abb0-b07f4661edf5
- Click Download.
- Once downloaded, follow the prompts to install it on your computer.

The Outlook classic installation window may look like it has stopped or is not doing anything, sometimes up to 30 minutes. Please just wait for the process to finish and the process completed prompt to show up. Do not shut down or restart your computer during this time.

Once Outlook Classic has completed installation:

- Open Outlook (classic). Please do not change it to New Outlook as it will no longer work with your account either via a prompt or the ‘New Outlook’ toggle.
- You may see the following pop up, please select Change on the bottom right corner to change the auto-detected and connected account. This is likely to happen if you were already using a personal Microsoft account on that computer.
 If you don't see this pop-up, continue to step 3.
 
 
 
- Enter your uqconnect.edu.au email address, then click Next.
- Enter your password. You will be prompted to sign into UQ Authenticate with your UQ student username and password, then complete a multi-factor authentication challenge.
- Untick Allow my organization to manage my device, then click No, this app only to complete the setup.
 
 
 
- Once your email has completed its import into Outlook Classic, follow the Microsoft guides below on how to export/import your email as you require.

page_title: What software is available on library computers?
breadcrumb: What software is available on library computers?
text:
What software is available on library computers?

**Software on Library computers**

The Library provides a standard set of software on our workstations for students and staff. We cannot provide software for personal use.

page_title: What is the definition of ‘smoking’? Are electronic cigarettes and vapes considered smoking at UQ?
breadcrumb: What is the definition of ‘smoking’? Are electronic cigarettes and vapes considered smoking at UQ?
text:
What is the definition of ‘smoking’? Are electronic cigarettes and vapes considered smoking at UQ?

Smoking is the inhalation and exhalation of the smoke of burning tobacco, herbs or drugs.

Smoking also refers to the use of electronic cigarettes or other devices. Electronic cigarettes (e-cigarettes, e-cigars, vapour pens, or a personal vapour) or any product where there is a hand-to-mouth action and results in the expulsion of a vapour.

page_title: Where can I smoke on campus?
breadcrumb: Where can I smoke on campus?
text:
Where can I smoke on campus?

Smoking is not permitted on UQ sites. This includes all UQ fleet vehicles and all other motor vehicles while on campus.

We strongly recommend that when smokers move off site, they choose an area that is not remote, is safe and is well-lit at night.

Below is a list of boundary maps for most campus locations:

page_title: Is a ‘low positive’ or 'doubtful immunity' blood test result to Measles or Mumps or Rubella acceptable?
breadcrumb: Is a ‘low positive’ or 'doubtful immunity' blood test result to Measles or Mumps or Rubella acceptable?
text:
Is a ‘low positive’ or 'doubtful immunity' blood test result to Measles or Mumps or Rubella acceptable?

No. The Immunisation Records Team (IRT) cannot verify your MMR status as complete if your blood test result indicates a ‘low positive’ result to either Measles, Mumps or Rubella. In this case, please provide 2 appropriate dose dates of MMR. If you are only providing a blood test result, the result must state that the result shows "immunity" to Measles, Mumps and Rubella for it to be acceptable.

page_title: How do I unenrol my personal Microsoft device from being managed by UQ?
breadcrumb: How do I unenrol my personal Microsoft device from being managed by UQ?
text:
How do I unenrol my personal Microsoft device from being managed by UQ?

Microsoft Intune is the management solution used at UQ to manage UQ-owned devices. If you have accidentally enrolled your personal device whilst installing Microsoft 365 apps, follow the instructions below to unenrol it:

- Click the Windows 'Start' menu, then open 'Settings'.
- Select 'Accounts' on the left menu, then select 'Access work or school'.
- Click on your UQ account, then click 'Disconnect'.
- Click 'Yes' to confirm account removal.
- Once complete, restart your computer to apply the changes.

After removing your account and restarting your computer, open a Microsoft 365 application, e.g. Word. You should be prompted to sign in again. Sign in with your university account here.

Make sure to untick 'Allow my organization to manage my device', then click 'OK'.

University policies are intended for UQ-owned devices only; therefore, it is important to untick 'Allow my organization to manage my device'.

page_title: I want to return a book to the Library by post. What address do I send it to?

page_title: How do I manage my wired internet connection?
breadcrumb: How do I manage my wired internet connection? > About UQ Internet Management System (IMS)
text:
How do I manage my wired internet connection? > About UQ Internet Management System (IMS)

For security, UQ wired networks at UQ campuses and sites require authentication before devices can access external websites and resources.

Sometimes, you may be able to access UQ pages but not external websites. This can occur if you do not have an active Internet session. You can activate an Internet session via the UQ Agent (Windows), UQ IMS Portal (macOS) or your my.UQ Dashboard.

If you are transitioning from Wi-Fi to a wired connection by connecting to a dock, you may need to manually activate an Internet session.

Please note: Wireless connections activate an Internet session automatically.

Certain services like Outlook and Teams may still be accessible without an active Internet session. These services are treated as exceptions due to their critical nature for UQ operations and can function without full external internet access.

The UQ Agent application can be used to manage your wired Internet connection if you're using an ITS-managed Windows computer. The agent automatically activates and manages your Internet session for you if you are connected to the network at login.

You can access the UQ Agent in the system tray at the bottom right of the screen.

To activate an Internet session manually, right-click on the UQ Agent icon in the system tray and select ‘Start'.

If the UQ Agent is not available, it can be installed via Company Portal.

You can use the UQ IMS Portal application to manage your wired Internet connection if you're using an ITS-managed macOS computer. The agent automatically activates and manages your Internet session for you if you are connected to the network at login.

You can access the UQ IMS Portal in the menu bar at the top right of the screen.

To activate an Internet session manually, click on the UQ IMS Portal icon in the menu bar and select ‘Force Login’.

If the UQ IMS Portal is not available, it can be installed via Self Service.

If you do not have the agent/portal installed or you are using a non-managed device, you can activate and manage your wired Internet sessions through your my.UQDashboard > Internet Session (UQ login required). You can also activate an Internet session by visiting https://login.uq.edu.au

page_title: I'm interested in studying a language, but not as part of my degree. Does UQ offer non-degree courses or short courses?
breadcrumb: I'm interested in studying a language, but not as part of my degree. Does UQ offer non-degree courses or short courses?
text:
I'm interested in studying a language, but not as part of my degree. Does UQ offer non-degree courses or short courses?

The School of Languages and Cultures teaches languages as part of a degree or diploma.

The Institute of Modern Languages (IML-UQ) offers non-degree courses in more than 30 languages. IML courses are at different levels of proficiency and they range from 6 weeks to one year. All classes are held at the UQ St Lucia campus.

If your student visa expires before your ceremony, you may be able to extend your stay, but this will be based on individual circumstances. To discuss your options, contact the Australian Government Department of Home Affairs.

If your student visa expires before your ceremony, you may be able to extend your stay, but this will be based on individual circumstances. To discuss your options, contact the Australian Government Department of Home Affairs.

If you choose to withdraw, at any time, you must notify the Global Experiences Team and your host university/organisation of your decision in writing.

page_title: Can I study more than the equivalent of #8 UQ units while on exchange?
breadcrumb: Can I study more than the equivalent of #8 UQ units while on exchange?
text:
Can I study more than the equivalent of #8 UQ units while on exchange?

Enrolling in more than the equivalent full-time study load while on exchange is **strongly discouraged**.

You can only receive a maximum of #8 UQ units of credit per semester towards your UQ program from exchange studies. Any additional study over the full-time study load at the host university will not be credited towards your UQ program.

You can find the full-time study load (equivalent to #8 UQ units) required for your host university, by searching for the host university and viewing the the 'Academic Matters' tab.

text:
How do I select a minor?

Check your program rules to see whether you're able to complete a minor. Program rules for your program can be found on the Programs and Courses website.

If you're eligible and want to select a minor, contact your faculty.

Check your program rules to see whether you're able to complete a minor. Program rules for your program can be found on the Programs and Courses website.

page_title: What is a course?
breadcrumb: What is a course?
text:
What is a course?

A course is a distinct unit of study within a program for which a result is given – similar to a subject at school. Full-time students usually study 3 to 4 courses (6-8 units) per semester. Each course is identified by its alphanumeric code, a title and a fixed unit value.

See my.UQ for advice on choosing courses.

page_title: I manage a lab. What Duo MFA arrangements are required?
breadcrumb: I manage a lab. What Duo MFA arrangements are required?
text:
I manage a lab. What Duo MFA arrangements are required?

Multi-factor authentication (MFA) in most lab environments behaves exactly the same as MFA does normally.

Special considerations are only required for labs or areas where it is not possible, practical, or desirable for people to use normal MFA methods. Below are two other options in these cases:

MFA option, How it works, Recommended usage
Temporary passcode
Shared Duo token

UQ staff can generate temporary MFA passcodes at any time at the MFA management portal.

For regular or casual use, it is recommended you generate a passcode before entering the lab so that it is available when needed.

Download a poster for MFA passcodes (PDF, 137KB). Consider placing inside the lab.

Lab managers can follow the steps below to set up shared Duo tokens:

- Order the token(s) through UQeMarket (catalogue no. 080010). Two tokens per area are usually enough; larger areas may require more tokens.
- Request a MFA lab token setup (staff login required). The ITS Service Desk will create an entry for the lab at the MFA management portal under 'Lab management'.
- Install the tokens in accessible locations in the lab so they can be used easily when needed. Double-sided tape is recommended so they are fixed in place and placing beside posters.

Lab staff should be made aware of the MFA requirements and will need to self-register at the MFA management portal under 'Lab management' in order to use the lab's shared MFA tokens.

Download a poster for MFA tokens (PDF, 125KB). Consider placing inside the lab.

- Many labs contain computers which are not connected to the UQ network or only require access to limited resources (e.g. lab instruments). Devices such as these are unlikely to prompt you to MFA.
- Make sure that people who need to use a specific lab or area are aware of any alternative MFA arrangements that have been put in place.
- Consider whether information about MFA needs to be included in any documentation or procedures (e.g. lab induction).

page_title: How early should I start planning for my exchange?
breadcrumb: How early should I start planning for my exchange?
text:
How early should I start planning for my exchange?

It's best if you start planning for exchange as soon as you arrive at UQ, so that you can plan your UQ program to allow for the most flexible courses (electives) to be completed on exchange.


The application deadline for exchange is approximately 10 months before the start of the semester overseas. To make sure that your application is ready to submit by the deadline, we recommend starting your research 3 months before the application deadline.


See our advice about semester-based exchange for more information on planning your exchange.

page_title: What happens if I fail the Literacy and Numeracy Test for Initial Teacher Education Students (LANTITE)?
breadcrumb: What happens if I fail the Literacy and Numeracy Test for Initial Teacher Education Students (LANTITE)?
text:
What happens if I fail the Literacy and Numeracy Test for Initial Teacher Education Students (LANTITE)?

If you fail the test, you can register again and sit the next test.

You'll have 3 attempts to sit the LANTITE test. If you've sat the test and failed 3 times, contact the School of Education for advice.

The School of Education provides individualised preparatory support for the test – including access to Learning Advisors from Student Services. For more information about the available support, contact the School of Education.

page_title: How much do guest tickets to the graduation ceremony cost?
breadcrumb: How much do guest tickets to the graduation ceremony cost?
text:
How much do guest tickets to the graduation ceremony cost?

Guest tickets to graduation ceremonies are $20 each and they can be purchased via the registration portal from Monday 6 July 2026.

See my.UQ for more information on graduation ceremonies.

Guest tickets to graduation ceremonies are $20 each and they can be purchased via the registration portal from Monday 6 July 2026.

See my.UQ for more information on graduation ceremonies.

page_title: I am an international student and have completed my undergraduate program. As I now want to study a postgraduate program, what do I need to do about my student visa?
breadcrumb: I am an international student and have completed my undergraduate program. As I now want to study a postgraduate program, what do I need to do about my student visa?
text:
I am an international student and have completed my undergraduate program. As I now want to study a postgraduate program, what do I need to do about my student visa?

After you accept a new offer, you will receive a new Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) for your postgraduate program. Once you receive your new CoE, you can apply for a new student visa.

For queries relating to visas, see the Australian Government Department of Home Affairs website.

page_title: How do I contact my Faculty or School?
breadcrumb: How do I contact my Faculty or School?
text:
How do I contact my Faculty or School?

How do I contact my Faculty or School? Home Information and services See my.UQ for advice on contacting your faculty or school. If you're a HDR student, contact the Graduate School.

page_title: What is a recommended prerequisite?
breadcrumb: What is a recommended prerequisite?
text:
What is a recommended prerequisite?

A recommended prerequisite is a course that students are advised to take before progressing to another course.

See the my.UQ website for advice on choosing your courses.

A recommended prerequisite is a course that students are advised to take before progressing to another course.

page_title: How do I renew my blue card?
breadcrumb: How do I renew my blue card?
text:
How do I renew my blue card?

You'll be sent a reminder from Blue Card Services before your blue card expires.

You'll need to get a blue card renewal application from the Blue Card website. Complete this application and then contact your school for the next steps – don't send the application directly to Blue Card Services.

page_title: Can I apply for multiple short-term global experiences at once?
breadcrumb: Can I apply for multiple short-term global experiences at once?
text:
Can I apply for multiple short-term global experiences at once?

Unfortunately, you cannot apply for multiple short-term study or internships experiences at once.


If you'd like to change to an alternative short-term study or internship experience, email iget@uq.edu.au.

Unfortunately, you cannot apply for multiple short-term study or internships experiences at once.


If you'd like to change to an alternative short-term study or internship experience, email iget@uq.edu.au.
