page_title: What are my student contribution payment options as a Commonwealth Supported student?
breadcrumb: What are my student contribution payment options as a Commonwealth Supported student?
text:
What are my student contribution payment options as a Commonwealth Supported student?

If you are an Australian citizen, holder of an Australian permanent humanitarian visa, holder of a Pacific Engagement Visa, or a holder (or former) of an approved New Zealand special category visa you may elect to pay 'up front' or defer your payment via a HECS-HELP loan. If you are a New Zealand citizen or Australian permanent resident without a humanitarian visa you must pay your student contribution up front.

For more information, see my.UQ – HECS-HELP or Study Assist – Student contribution amounts.

page_title: What needs to be included in an academic reference when applying for a global experience?
breadcrumb: What needs to be included in an academic reference when applying for a global experience?
text:
What needs to be included in an academic reference when applying for a global experience?

Your academic reference should be a letter from a UQ academic staff member (professor or tutor etc.) commenting on your:

- participation and grades in their class,
- qualities, skills and attributes as a university student
- suitability for participation in the global experience.

Ask that the reference is printed on official UQ letterhead. If they don't have access to letterhead, ask them to send the content of the reference directly to the Global Experiences Team at __iget@uq.edu.au__.

page_title: What is an articulation offer?
breadcrumb: What is an articulation offer?
text:
What is an articulation offer?

An articulation offer applies to some postgraduate coursework programs. It is an offer to begin studying at a lower level than you applied for, with the ability to articulate/program change into the subsequent master's program.

If you do not meet the GPA requirement for the program that you have applied for, you may be made an articulation offer to commence in an alternative program to give you an opportunity to meet the required GPA. You would need to attain the required GPA for the alternate program to be permitted to articulate/program change into the subsequent master's program.

To see if this is a possibility for your area of interest, Ask UQ for advice.

page_title: What does course attendance mode mean?
breadcrumb: What does course attendance mode mean?
text:
What does course attendance mode mean?

Course attendance mode describes the way a course may be undertaken. Attendance modes include in-person and external.

To find out the available attendance modes for your courses, search for the course on the Programs and Courses website – the available attendance modes will be listed under ‘Mode’.

A course's Course Profile (CP) (available through mySI-net for the courses that you are enrolled in) will also have this information in the 'Course details' section. A course may be offered in more than one attendance mode.

page_title: Can I have my wedding photography at UQ?
breadcrumb: Can I have my wedding photography at UQ?
text:
Can I have my wedding photography at UQ?

You'll need to apply for approval, as there's high demand for wedding photography on campus. To apply, email the Security Events team at seo@pf.uq.edu.au or call (07) 3365 6003.

You'll need to apply for approval, as there's high demand for wedding photography on campus. To apply, email the Security Events team at seo@pf.uq.edu.au or call (07) 3365 6003.

page_title: What teaching activity recording options are there?
breadcrumb: What teaching activity recording options are there?
text:
What teaching activity recording options are there?

With lectures (and some other class activities) not being allocated to an on-campus location, lectures will not be automatically recorded with Echo360. A range of options for recording lectures and teaching activities are available.

Zoom is the best way to record lectures and other teaching activities where dual teaching is done using Zoom.

It is recommended Zoom meetings have the 'Automatically record meeting' 'In the cloud' option set in the advanced options when scheduling the teaching activity. See Organising a Zoom meeting for more details.

Zoom recordings can be made available to students through Blackboard by adding the Zoom recording to Learn.UQ.

If your teaching activity is scheduled with an Echo360 location, recordings can be automatically scheduled.

To have your activities scheduled for automatic recording, contact your timetable coordinator who can mark them to be recorded in the Syllabus Plus timetable system.

Your recording will be automatically published to your course Blackboard site on Learn.UQ.

If you are teaching using Zoom, it is recommended Zoom be used for recording. See the above section 'Dual mode lectures using Zoom'.

page_title: As a mature-aged student, how do I apply to UQ?
breadcrumb: As a mature-aged student, how do I apply to UQ?
text:
As a mature-aged student, how do I apply to UQ?

If you are a mature-aged student or finished high school a long time ago, you are welcome to apply for a program at UQ – there are no special entry requirements which apply.

First, search for your program on Future Students. Once you have selected a program, the entry requirements are shown under the 'Program details' tab. You will be required to meet all entry requirements to be considered for a place in a program. Application instructions are listed under the 'How to apply' tab.

If you are unsure if you meet the entry requirements for your program, or need help with your application, Ask UQ for advice.

page_title: Why does the Duo Mobile app need access to my camera?
breadcrumb: Why does the Duo Mobile app need access to my camera?
text:
Why does the Duo Mobile app need access to my camera?

When using multi-factor authentication (MFA) for the first time and registering your device, the Duo Mobile app will use your camera to scan a QR code displayed on the screen. For more information, see the DUO Knowledge Base.

When using multi-factor authentication (MFA) for the first time and registering your device, the Duo Mobile app will use your camera to scan a QR code displayed on the screen. For more information, see the DUO Knowledge Base.

page_title: How do I know if I am enrolled in a Commonwealth Supported Place (CSP)?
breadcrumb: How do I know if I am enrolled in a Commonwealth Supported Place (CSP)?
text:
How do I know if I am enrolled in a Commonwealth Supported Place (CSP)?

Select the 'Financials' tile in mySI-net and then select 'HECS-HELP' eCAF. If you're enrolled in a Commonwealth Supported Place (CSP), a HECS-HELP eCAF will be available.

See my.UQ for more information on HECS-HELP.

Select the 'Financials' tile in mySI-net and then select 'HECS-HELP' eCAF. If you're enrolled in a Commonwealth Supported Place (CSP), a HECS-HELP eCAF will be available.

See my.UQ for more information on HECS-HELP.

page_title: What happens if I apply for entry to the Bachelor of Veterinary Science (Honours) program, but do not sit the CASPer test?
breadcrumb: What happens if I apply for entry to the Bachelor of Veterinary Science (Honours) program, but do not sit the CASPer test?
text:
What happens if I apply for entry to the Bachelor of Veterinary Science (Honours) program, but do not sit the CASPer test?

From 2019 for domestic students and 2020 for international students, you will not be considered for admission to veterinary science at UQ unless you complete the Computer Based Assessment for Sampling Personal Characteristics (CASPer) test.

For further information, see Applying for the Bachelor of Veterinary Science (Honours).

page_title: What is cross-institutional study?
breadcrumb: What is cross-institutional study?
text:
What is cross-institutional study?

Cross-institutional study allows you to study at one university while being formally enrolled in another. Through cross-institutional study you can attend lectures and tutorials in courses not offered at your home university.

If you're a student from another university and you want to undertake cross-institutional study at UQ, see our Future Students website for information.

If you are a current UQ student and want to undertake cross-institutional study at a university other than UQ, see our my.UQ website for information.

page_title: If I study a dual degree, do I need to attend two graduation ceremonies?
breadcrumb: If I study a dual degree, do I need to attend two graduation ceremonies?
text:
If I study a dual degree, do I need to attend two graduation ceremonies?

No, you'll be allocated to one of the ceremonies associated with the faculty that administers the dual degree. To check which faculty administers your program, select the 'Program' tile in mySI-net (student login required).

Your allocated ceremony will also be confirmed in the email inviting you to attend your graduation. You'll received this email about 6 weeks before the graduation period.

page_title: What do researchers and Higher Degree Research (HDR) students need to do to meet the UQ eSpace requirements?
breadcrumb: What do researchers and Higher Degree Research (HDR) students need to do to meet the UQ eSpace requirements?
text:
What do researchers and Higher Degree Research (HDR) students need to do to meet the UQ eSpace requirements?

You need to:

- maintain a complete and accurate listing of your research outputs and datasets by regular review of your publication profile in UQ eSpace
- respond to emails from UQ eSpace to claim possible publications and review automatically claimed publications
- upload an open access copy of your research. In most cases, this is the accepted manuscript (post-print) version
- ensure you link your ORCID to UQ eSpace. This mandatory for HDR students
- develop and use your ORCID account
- use your ORCID when publishing and applying for grants.

For further information, contact UQ eSpace.

page_title: The Phish Alert Button isn’t responding when I click on it. What do I do?
breadcrumb: The Phish Alert Button isn’t responding when I click on it. What do I do?
text:
The Phish Alert Button isn’t responding when I click on it. What do I do?

The Phish Alert Button may not function if the KnowBe4 service becomes unavailable. Generally, this will be resolved quickly, so try again after a few minutes.

If it still doesn’t work, forward the email as an attachment to the CSOC team manually. If the button stops working completely, contact IT support.

page_title: Can I study part of my undergraduate degree overseas?
breadcrumb: Can I study part of my undergraduate degree overseas?
text:
Can I study part of my undergraduate degree overseas?

UQ provides students the opportunity to participate in a semester-based exchange or short-term global experience.


Most undergraduate programs at UQ allow students to spend at least one semester of exchange studying in another country. However, not all programs have the flexibility to allow students to participate in a semester-based exchange. See program exclusions to check if your program is on the excluded list.



Alternatively, students also have the option to participate in a short-term study or internship experience during the summer or winter breaks.

page_title: What assessment is required in my course?
breadcrumb: What assessment is required in my course?
text:
What assessment is required in my course?

The assessment required for a course is listed in your course's electronic course profile (ECP). Your course's ECP can be accessed through Blackboard or by searching for the course on the Programs and Courses website.

The assessment required for a course is listed in your course's electronic course profile (ECP). Your course's ECP can be accessed through Blackboard or by searching for the course on the Programs and Courses website.

page_title: I didn't receive a verification email from Matlab, what should I do?
breadcrumb: I didn't receive a verification email from Matlab, what should I do?
text:
I didn't receive a verification email from Matlab, what should I do?

Make sure you used the modified email address in the instructions - username@software.student.uq.edu.au. For example, s9871234@software.student.uq.edu.au.

Make sure your UQ email address is working. It can take a couple of days to set up if you have recently enrolled. Keep checking it by sending an email to your address to see if it goes through.

You can also try resetting your MathWorks password. If the password reset doesn't work, try re-registering using the Installing Matlab for students instructions (UQ login required).

page_title: As a postgraduate coursework student, what global experiences can I apply for?
breadcrumb: As a postgraduate coursework student, what global experiences can I apply for?
text:
As a postgraduate coursework student, what global experiences can I apply for?

As long as you meet the required eligibility criteria of the experience you're interested in completing, then you can participate in any global experience, including:

- semester exchange
- short-term study
- internships

There are some global experiences partner universities or organisations that do not allow postgraduates to attend, so carefully check any restrictions listed on their websites. Only some partner universities or organisations offer courses at the postgraduate level.

You'll need to discuss academic flexibility and possible credit options with your UQ faculty before you apply for a global experience, as you may need to re-arrange your standard program progression to fit in a global experience.

page_title: Can I participate in a short-term global experience in the holiday period before going on exchange for a semester?
breadcrumb: Can I participate in a short-term global experience in the holiday period before going on exchange for a semester?
text:
Can I participate in a short-term global experience in the holiday period before going on exchange for a semester?

Whilst it is possible, it is not recommended.


Travel, including a short-term study or internship experience, prior to a semester-based exchange can negatively impact your ability to obtain a student visa for both a short-term and semester-long experience. In some cases, students may need to choose to only participate in one global experience.


If you intend to participate in both a short-term experience and a semester-based exchange, it is your responsibility to confirm that you are logistically able to participate in both experiences.


For more information, see our advice on short-term study .

page_title: I have been accepted by UQ for my short-term global experience. Can I change my preference?
breadcrumb: I have been accepted by UQ for my short-term global experience. Can I change my preference?
text:
I have been accepted by UQ for my short-term global experience. Can I change my preference?

In most cases, yes. You'll need to discuss your specific circumstances with your Global Experiences Adviser and your UQ faculty.

Generally, you should be able to change if:

- you've applied to UQ before the deadline,
- you've met the published eligibility criteria for your preferred short-term global experience,
- it's more than 6 weeks before your preferred experience, and
- your preferred experience is still accepting applications.

For more information, email __iget@uq.edu.au__.

page_title: What should I do if I think I am going to fail?
breadcrumb: What should I do if I think I am going to fail?
text:
What should I do if I think I am going to fail?

First, have a chat with your course coordinator to express your concerns about your assessment. Depending on your circumstances and the time of semester, there may a number of options available to you (including withdrawing from your course). Contact your Faculty for advice on withdrawing and your study plan for future semesters.

Our Student Life team offer learning workshops and Learning Advisers, which may also assist you.

page_title: How many Bachelor of Arts programs can I undertake?
breadcrumb: How many Bachelor of Arts programs can I undertake?
text:
How many Bachelor of Arts programs can I undertake?

If you've successfully completed a Bachelor of Arts, you can apply to gain entry to complete another Bachelor of Arts. You may also be eligible for some credit from the first degree. Refer to the Bachelor of Arts page for information on available majors and how to apply.

page_title: Who do I contact if I cannot access a My eQuals document that has been shared with me?
breadcrumb: Who do I contact if I cannot access a My eQuals document that has been shared with me?
text:
Who do I contact if I cannot access a My eQuals document that has been shared with me?

Who do I contact if I cannot access a My eQuals document that has been shared with me? Home Information and services Contact the person who shared the document with you. They control who can access their documents and for how long a document can be accessed.

page_title: What is the Graduate Medical School Admissions Test (GAMSAT) and where is it held?
breadcrumb: What is the Graduate Medical School Admissions Test (GAMSAT) and where is it held?
text:
What is the Graduate Medical School Admissions Test (GAMSAT) and where is it held?

GAMSAT is the Graduate Medical School Admission Test developed by the Australian Council for Educational Research (ACER). The test helps to select students for graduate entry medicine, dentistry and health science degree programs.

To find out if the course that you're interested in requires the completion of the GAMSAT test, search for your program on Future Students and view the 'Admissions criteria' under the 'Program details' tab.

You can sit the GAMSAT at venues in Brisbane and Townsville, or at venues in other Australian and New Zealand locations listed on the GAMSAT website.

page_title: For my exchange, the Credit Precedents Database indicates that one UQ course equals one host university course. Is this correct?
breadcrumb: For my exchange, the Credit Precedents Database indicates that one UQ course equals one host university course. Is this correct?
text:
For my exchange, the Credit Precedents Database indicates that one UQ course equals one host university course. Is this correct?

Yes and no - it differs between host universities and courses.


If the precedent is current and shows a course-for-course match, then the content of the course is strong enough to fully equate the listed UQ course.


However, please be mindful that the Credit Precedents Database doesn't always accurately reflect how many overseas university courses are equivalent to a single UQ course. For example, in some cases you may need to take two or three courses at a host university to receive credit for one UQ course (2 units) due to the level of content within courses.


The credit precedent database should be used as a guide only, and students should always consider/crosscheck:

- the full-time study load required at the host university to gain 8 UQ units of credit (as listed on each host page)
- the CPD is a UQ wide database, and not all overseas courses listed are open to exchange students
- courses listed in the CPD may only be offered in a particular semester at the overseas university and students should crosscheck the semester offering within the university overseas university course catalogue
- precedents which have expired will need re-assessing
- the database does not include all courses available to exchange students

page_title: Can I go on a global experience to a university that isn't an exchange partner or one of the short-term study options?
breadcrumb: Can I go on a global experience to a university that isn't an exchange partner or one of the short-term study options?
text:
Can I go on a global experience to a university that isn't an exchange partner or one of the short-term study options?

If you're interested in going to a university that isn't one of UQ's exchange partners or one of the options for short-term study, then you would have to undertake the experience independently of UQ. By doing this, you will be considered a ‘free-mover’ student. This means that you will not be considered a UQ student for the purposes of any experience you choose to undertake.


As a free-mover student, you are not supported by The University of Queensland by any means (including funding, insurance, credit pre-approvals etc.) and are choosing to participate in a global experience without any assistance from UQ.


Additionally, as a free-mover, you are not eligible to defer the payment of tuition fees at an international host institution to Australian Government FEE-HELP / HECS, as you will be considered a full fee-paying international student paying fees directly to the host institution.


Upon return from the experience, you may be eligible to apply for credit and will need to consult with your School or Faculty regarding this process. Credit transfer for free-movers is high risk and is not guaranteed. International students returning to study in their home country should be aware that they may not be eligible to receive any credit for their studies, as credit is granted at the discretion of the Faculty. Faculty of Business, Economics and Law students should refer to the BEL Study Overseas Academic Considerations, as credit will not be granted unless pre-approved by IGET and the BEL Faculty.

page_title: What should I include in my letter of motivation/personal statement for my global experience?
breadcrumb: What should I include in my letter of motivation/personal statement for my global experience?
text:
What should I include in my letter of motivation/personal statement for my global experience?

A letter of motivation/personal statement should include:

- why you wish to participate in the exchange or short-term global experience
- why you've chosen the host university/organisation
- what you hope to get out of the experience
- what you think you could give back to the host university/organisation.

page_title: Why can't I access my Studies Report in mySI-net?
breadcrumb: Why can't I access my Studies Report in mySI-net?
text:
Why can't I access my Studies Report in mySI-net?

The 'Studies Report' function in mySI-net will normally be switched off from the Saturday after the last day of semester until the day after the official release of results for that semester.

The 'View Results' link will appear on the date of the official release of results for a semester and will remain active for approximately 4 weeks.

page_title: Where do I change my address in mySI-net?
breadcrumb: Where do I change my address in mySI-net?
text:
Where do I change my address in mySI-net?

To change your address in mySI-net (student login required), click on the 'Personal Details' tile and select 'Addresses' from the left-side menu. You'll be shown 3 different addresses:

- Your 'Permanent Address' is your usual address when not studying.
- Your 'Semester Address' is your address while studying during semester.
- Your 'Mailing Address' is the address you want your mail from UQ to be sent to.

To edit an address:

- Click on the address.
- Update the details.
- Select the 'Save' button.

page_title: What is a school recommendation?
breadcrumb: What is a school recommendation?
text:
What is a school recommendation?

What is a school recommendation? Home Information and services A school recommendation is a recommendation from a school or other secondary education provider on the abilities of a student.

page_title: What do I do if it's my final semester and my compulsory courses' lecture timetables clash?
breadcrumb: What do I do if it's my final semester and my compulsory courses' lecture timetables clash?
text:
What do I do if it's my final semester and my compulsory courses' lecture timetables clash?

Your electronic course profile (ECP) will show you the exact attendance requirements for each of your courses.

Video recordings and lecture slides are available on Blackboard for most courses.

If you are concerned about lecture attendance or a timetable clash, contact your faculty or school for advice.

page_title: What textbooks do I need for my course?
breadcrumb: What textbooks do I need for my course?
text:
What textbooks do I need for my course?

You can find all required and recommended readings on your course reading list. 

Your Course Coordinator may expect you to possess your own copy of a reading. These items will appear on your Course Profile and course reading list marked as Required - own copy needed.

page_title: Why didn't I get a QTAC offer for UQ?
breadcrumb: Why didn't I get a QTAC offer for UQ?
text:
Why didn't I get a QTAC offer for UQ?

There might be a range of reasons you didn't receive an offer, from not meeting entry requirements to incorrectly ordered preferences and missed due dates.

Contact UQ Admissions on (07) 3365 2203 or admissions@uq.edu.au if you're unsure why you didn't receive an offer.

page_title: What is the mySI-net Task Wizard?
breadcrumb: What is the mySI-net Task Wizard?
text:
What is the mySI-net Task Wizard?

It is a step-by-step guide used to complete important tasks before you commence your program and at the start of each semester. It will appear each time you log into mySI-net until all tasks are complete.

The task wizard can be accessed from the 'Task and Messages' tile in mySI-net.

You will not be able to enrol until all tasks are complete.

page_title: Where do I find a program's QTAC code?
breadcrumb: Where do I find a program's QTAC code?
text:
Where do I find a program's QTAC code?

Search for your program on our Future Students website – a program's QTAC code is shown on the 'Overview' tab.

You can also find a program's QTAC code by searching for the program on QTAC's course search.

Search for your program on our Future Students website – a program's QTAC code is shown on the 'Overview' tab.

You can also find a program's QTAC code by searching for the program on QTAC's course search.

page_title: What is a graduate?
breadcrumb: What is a graduate?
text:
What is a graduate?

A graduate is a student who has completed all of the requirements of a program and has been formally awarded the degree, diploma or certificate. The date of graduation is the date of the official ceremony for that program, whether a student attends the ceremony or not.

page_title: What is intensive attendance mode?
breadcrumb: What is intensive attendance mode?
text:
What is intensive attendance mode?

In intensive attendance mode, a course is taught in a continuous block of time over a short period.

To find out the available attendance modes for a course, search for the course on the Programs and Courses website – the available delivery modes will be listed under 'Mode'. To find the required contact hours for the course, select your starting semester and the required contact hours will be listed under 'Class hours' on the left-hand side. An explanation of the class contact codes is available on my.UQ under 'Contact hours'.

page_title: I'm a new student, and I get an error message when I try to log in to my student email account, what should I do?
breadcrumb: I'm a new student, and I get an error message when I try to log in to my student email account, what should I do?
text:
I'm a new student, and I get an error message when I try to log in to my student email account, what should I do?

If you get an error when you try to log in to your new student email, your student email may not have been created yet. This error should go away within 72 hours of activating your account.

If you're still getting an error after that time, submit an IT request.

page_title: How can I change my alternate email address in mySI-net?
breadcrumb: How can I change my alternate email address in mySI-net?
text:
How can I change my alternate email address in mySI-net?

How can I change my alternate email address in mySI-net? Home Information and services Students can request a change to their alternate email address in mySI-net via AskUQ.

page_title: Should I use a VPN on campus?
breadcrumb: Should I use a VPN on campus?
text:
Should I use a VPN on campus?

Generally, you don't need to use a VPN on either St Lucia or Gatton campus.

At Herston, connect to the VPN if you're using wifi and having problems accessing certain resources like shared drives or UQ finance pages.

Queensland Health staff who aren't on the UQ network will need to use the VPN for all UQ resources.

page_title: What do I do if I have Elite Athlete Status and have submitted a deferred exam request, but I'll be away for competition during the deferred exam period?
breadcrumb: What do I do if I have Elite Athlete Status and have submitted a deferred exam request, but I'll be away for competition during the deferred exam period?
text:
What do I do if I have Elite Athlete Status and have submitted a deferred exam request, but I'll be away for competition during the deferred exam period?

If you're away during the deferred exam period, you can't apply for another deferral. Instead, you may submit a request (student login required) to sit the exam in your location. Please note, requests for an Off-campus Exam will be accepted no later than 14 days before the first day of the examination period. If you think you may need to apply for an Off-campus Exam, please speak to your UQ Sport Elite Athlete Program staff as soon as possible.

For more information about eligibility and considerations for elite athletes, see the Programs and Assessments for Elite Athletes Procedures [3.50.07]. Fore more information about the UQ Elite Athlete Program, see the UQ Sport website.

page_title: What is a thesis statement?
breadcrumb: What is a thesis statement?
text:
What is a thesis statement?

A thesis statement can be used by higher degree by research (HDR) students who need to demonstrate that:

- your thesis has been submitted and you’re currently waiting for results, or
- you’re required to make changes to your thesis

This statement can be used to prove your current status to professional bodies, associations and government agencies (e.g. Department of Home Affairs).

There are 2 types of thesis statements available:

A thesis submission statement includes:

- your program name
- thesis submission date
- the date your results are expected

A thesis corrections statement includes:

- your program name
- the date your thesis examination results were received
- the date by which you’re required to complete any amendments to your thesis

For further information about thesis statements and other certified documents see my.UQ.

page_title: Why do I need to use multi-factor authentication (MFA)?
breadcrumb: Why do I need to use multi-factor authentication (MFA)?
text:
Why do I need to use multi-factor authentication (MFA)?

Cyber attacks are becoming increasingly prevalent in today’s technology landscape. Passwords and usernames are often hacked and then used to log into other online accounts. Multi-factor authentication (MFA) will significantly increase your UQ account’s security and protect it from compromise.

If a hacker gets access to your UQ account, they could potentially obtain access to sensitive University data, or your personal information. Consequent data breaches can have severe personal, business and financial implications.

page_title: Why don't all UQ sites use multi-factor authentication (MFA)?
breadcrumb: Why don't all UQ sites use multi-factor authentication (MFA)?
text:
Why don't all UQ sites use multi-factor authentication (MFA)?

Why don't all UQ sites use multi-factor authentication (MFA)?

Our goal is to connect all UQ websites and systems to multi-factor authentication (MFA), to enhance the security at UQ. As we continue to implement these new security measures, you will see more sites connect to MFA.

page_title: How do I log in with MFA if I can’t use my mobile phone in my workplace or a lab?
breadcrumb: How do I log in with MFA if I can’t use my mobile phone in my workplace or a lab?
text:
How do I log in with MFA if I can’t use my mobile phone in my workplace or a lab?

There are some areas at UQ where it may not be possible to use your phone for MFA, however alternative MFA options are available. If you are unsure about the MFA requirements in a particular area, please confirm with your local lab manager or submit an IT request.


Alternative MFA solutions available for labs are detailed below.

Temporary passcodes can be generated at any time through the MFA Management portal. They last for a limited time (8 hours) and can be used for MFA authentication during that time.

 

 

 

Generate a passcode before entering a lab, write or print it on a piece of paper and keep it on-hand for any MFA authentication prompts while you are working in the lab. Dispose of it when you are finished.

 

If you need to MFA inside the lab but don't have an MFA device or passcode available, it is possible to access the MFA Management portal via identity.uq.edu.au/mfa/recovery. Once your identity has been verified you will be able to generate an MFA passcode.

Some specific areas at UQ have MFA tokens installed permanently inside which can be used to generate MFA passcodes when needed. These tokens can be shared by all staff who use a particular lab. 

 

 

 

To use shared MFA tokens in a particular area, you must be registered to use them; this can be managed via the MFA Management portal. Find out how to use and manage the shared MFA tokens inside a lab.

 

If you require further assistance with MFA in your lab, please consult your local lab manager or submit an IT request.

If you manage a lab and need to organise shared MFA tokens for the lab, please request a MFA lab token setup (staff login required).

page_title: How do I MFA in a location without mobile coverage?
breadcrumb: How do I MFA in a location without mobile coverage?
text:
How do I MFA in a location without mobile coverage?

The Duo Mobile app can be used offline to generate one-time passcodes for MFA, even when there is no mobile network or wifi connectivity.

The Duo Mobile app can be used offline to generate one-time passcodes for MFA, even when there is no mobile network or wifi connectivity.

page_title: Does multi-factor authentication (MFA) use my data on my smartphone?
breadcrumb: Does multi-factor authentication (MFA) use my data on my smartphone?
text:
Does multi-factor authentication (MFA) use my data on my smartphone?

Duo Mobile push authentication requests require a minimal amount of mobile data – less than 2KB per authentication. This amount of data usage falls well within a typical push notification. For more information, see the DUO Knowledge Base.

Duo Mobile push authentication requests require a minimal amount of mobile data – less than 2KB per authentication. This amount of data usage falls well within a typical push notification. For more information, see the DUO Knowledge Base.

page_title: How does Duo store my data?
breadcrumb: How does Duo store my data?
text:
How does Duo store my data?

Duo is UQ's multi-factor authentication (MFA) provider. Duo is a cloud-based service, which is located outside Australia.

When you register to use the MFA service at UQ, some information is sent to Duo and stored on Duo servers. This information is used to provide you with MFA services, and is limited to:

- your UQ account username (e.g. ‘uqabcde’)
- details of the devices you use for authentication (e.g. your mobile phone number).

UQ will only provide Duo servers with the minimum required data to enable the service. By using this MFA service, you agree to the transfer of this information outside Australia. For more information, see the DUO Knowledge Base.

page_title: What do I do if my YubiKey or Duo token is lost, stolen or damaged?
breadcrumb: What do I do if my YubiKey or Duo token is lost, stolen or damaged?
text:
What do I do if my YubiKey or Duo token is lost, stolen or damaged?

If a token has been lost or stolen, please report this to the ITS Service Desk by submitting an IT request so that the token can be recorded as lost. This ensures it can no longer be used to access your UQ account or resources.

To continue accessing UQ websites and resources without a token you can generate a temporary MFA passcode that is valid for a limited time period, e.g. 8 hours, by accessing the MFA Management portal:

- When asked to MFA, select 'Need help?'
 
 
 
- Click the MFA recovery link: https://identity.uq.edu.au/mfa/recovery/login
 
 
 
- Log in with your UQ username and password.
- Enter your date of birth, then click ‘Continue’.
- Answer the security questions that appear, then click ‘Continue’.
- Under 'Device management', remove the lost, stolen or damaged Duo token.
- Under 'Temporary MFA passcode', click 'Generate passcode'.
- Select the number of hours you will need access for and click 'Generate passcode'.
- Write or print the passcode on a piece of paper and keep it with you; the code will be valid for any MFA authentication within the specified time period.

You can order a replacement Duo token through UQeMarket using catalogue number 080010. However, the Duo Mobile app (using your smartphone) is the most convenient way to use MFA for most people and may be preferable to using a token.

Tokens for staff are currently out of stock. If you require an external hardware device for MFA, you can order a YubiKey through TrustPanda. YubiKey 5 (NFC) is the recommended type. Please contact your financial or budget holder delegate regarding purchasing.

You can register your smartphone for MFA by installing the Duo Mobile app and activating via the MFA Management portal.

page_title: How do I change my default device for Duo?
breadcrumb: How do I change my default device for Duo?
text:
How do I change my default device for Duo?

Duo remembers your last used method of MFA.

If you ever want to choose a different device or Duo method than the one shown automatically, click ‘Other options’ near the bottom. This takes you to a list of all your available Duo authentication options. Click on the one you want to use and follow the instructions shown to complete logging in to the application.

page_title: Can I use multi-factor authentication (MFA) when travelling overseas?
breadcrumb: Can I use multi-factor authentication (MFA) when travelling overseas?
text:
Can I use multi-factor authentication (MFA) when travelling overseas?

Yes. For mobile devices (e.g. smartphone), you can use the 'Duo Push' authentication method wherever you have internet connectivity via mobile networks, or wifi.

If you don't have internet connectivity, you can use the Duo Mobile app on your device to generate a passcode offline.

Please be aware that you will not be able to MFA in the following countries or regions due to economic and trade sanctions:

- Cuba
- North Korea
- Iran
- Sudan
- Syria
- Crimea region
- Donetsk region
- Luhansk region
- Sevastopol region

page_title: I'm not receiving push notifications from Duo. How do I fix this?
breadcrumb: I'm not receiving push notifications from Duo. How do I fix this?
text:
I'm not receiving push notifications from Duo. How do I fix this?

Rebooting your device will usually address the issue. If you have iOS content restrictions (such as parental controls) enabled, this may interfere with Duo push notifications. See Duo's instructions for advice on how to avoid this.

If you are still experiencing issues, submit an IT request for advice.

page_title: What do I do if my Duo token code isn't working?
breadcrumb: What do I do if my Duo token code isn't working?
text:
What do I do if my Duo token code isn't working?

What do I do if my Duo token code isn't working? Home Information and services Staff Please contact IT support for assistance.

page_title: What do I do if I've clicked 'Trust this browser' when using multi-factor authentication (MFA), but I'm still asked to log in?
breadcrumb: What do I do if I've clicked 'Trust this browser' when using multi-factor authentication (MFA), but I'm still asked to log in?
text:
What do I do if I've clicked 'Trust this browser' when using multi-factor authentication (MFA), but I'm still asked to log in?

The ‘Trust this browser’ option works by storing a cookie in your web browser. Therefore, this option will only work for the browser you selected it on.

If you are using a different device, browser or incognito mode to where you selected ‘Trust this browser’, you will be prompted again.

You may be required to use MFA more frequently for some applications which provide access to sensitive or protected information (e.g. VPN), as they require a higher level of protection.

page_title: Do I need to use multi-factor authentication (MFA) every time I log in?
breadcrumb: Do I need to use multi-factor authentication (MFA) every time I log in?
text:
Do I need to use multi-factor authentication (MFA) every time I log in?

MFA is required when accessing UQ websites and services that use UQ Authenticate.

In most cases, you will need to MFA at least once every 30 days when accessing an application that uses UQ Authenticate. You will be required to MFA again if:

- You use a new browser.
- You clear your browser cookies/cache,
- You use a different computer.
- You work from a new location (i.e., your IP address changes).
- You use a new Wi-Fi connection.
- You use a high-risk application.
- The system detects that a risk-based event may have occurred.

Please always carry your authenticator (smartphone with app or YubiKey) when accessing UQ websites and services.

page_title: Where can I plan my timetable?
breadcrumb: Where can I plan my timetable?
text:
Where can I plan my timetable?

Where can I plan my timetable? Home Information and services You can plan your timetable while preferencing or by using the Public Timetable.

page_title: What is 'delayed viewing' in My Timetable?
breadcrumb: What is 'delayed viewing' in My Timetable?
text:
What is 'delayed viewing' in My Timetable?

As My Timetable produces a clash-free timetable, 'delayed viewing' gives you the option of choosing to view a lecture recording at a later time – instead of attending the lecture in person. This allows you to schedule another class at the same time as the lecture.

Students have been able to view recorded lectures previously, but this option now allows you to schedule the recorded lecture into My Timetable. Not all lectures are able to offer this option, and set-up has been decided by each Faculty.

If you have attendance questions related to your course assessment requirements, check the course's electronic course profile (ECP) or contact your Faculty.

page_title: How do I check how much storage my UQ email is using?
breadcrumb: How do I check how much storage my UQ email is using?
text:
How do I check how much storage my UQ email is using?

- Log into your UQ email through the Office 365 website.
- Click the cog icon on the top right of the page, then select 'View all Outlook settings'.
 
 
 
- Select 'General', then 'Storage'.
 
 

You will see how much storage your UQ email is currently using and how much space your folders are using.

You can also empty data from each of the folders if you want to free up space.

page_title: Is any special consideration given when assessing admission applications through QTAC?
breadcrumb: Is any special consideration given when assessing admission applications through QTAC?
text:
Is any special consideration given when assessing admission applications through QTAC?

QTAC aims to give the most equitable assessment possible by taking into account an applicant's educational disadvantage when assessing an application.

As part of your QTAC application, you may apply for special consideration if your circumstances have adversely affected your studies. For more information, see Educational Access Scheme.

page_title: What is provided in a Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) space?
breadcrumb: What is provided in a Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) space?
text:
What is provided in a Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) space?

What is provided in a Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) space? Home Information and services Bring your own device (BYOD) spaces will be equipped with: power outlets at every seat to charge your own device wireless network connectivity with capacity to support the number of seats in the space.

page_title: What do I do if I don't have access to a device to use in BYOD spaces?
breadcrumb: What do I do if I don't have access to a device to use in BYOD spaces?
text:
What do I do if I don't have access to a device to use in BYOD spaces?

UQ wants to make sure you can access lectures, communicate with your academics and fellow students and study at a time and place convenient to you. To be able to do this, most students own a laptop or other device. If your laptop suddenly dies, you don’t have the money to fix it or to purchase a new one, you can:

- borrow a laptop from the University
- visit the bring your own device page for laptop requirements, purchase discounts and for financial hardship assistance information.

page_title: How do I import or export rules on Outlook?
breadcrumb: How do I import or export rules on Outlook?
text:
How do I import or export rules on Outlook?

How do I import or export rules on Outlook? Home Information and services Follow the instructions on the Microsoft website to import or export Outlook rules.

page_title: Can I enrol in courses after the final date for addition of courses or alteration of enrolment?
breadcrumb: Can I enrol in courses after the final date for addition of courses or alteration of enrolment?
text:
Can I enrol in courses after the final date for addition of courses or alteration of enrolment?

You will need to apply for approval if you want to add or substitute courses after the final date for addition of courses or alteration of enrolment.

To add a course:

- Contact your faculty for advice.
- Submit a Late Addition of Course request.
- We will send an email to your student account with the outcome of your request.
- If approval is granted, the course will be added for you. Your financial liability may change.

See the my.UQ website for information on applying for late addition of course.

page_title: How do I download Zoom recordings?
breadcrumb: How do I download Zoom recordings?
text:
How do I download Zoom recordings?

To download Zoom recordings:

- Sign in to the UQ Zoom web interface.
- Select 'Recordings'.
- Enter the search criteria (e.g. date, meeting ID) if required to locate your recording.
- Click the ellipsis (3 dots) next to 'Share' and select 'Download'.

This will save the video recording with audio, the audio recording and transcription.

page_title: How do I allow external Zoom accounts join a restricted Zoom meeting?
breadcrumb: How do I allow external Zoom accounts join a restricted Zoom meeting?
text:
How do I allow external Zoom accounts join a restricted Zoom meeting?

Your Zoom meetings can be configured to restrict access to UQ accounts only. Zoom has the ability to specify external accounts allowing them to join restricted meetings, while still denying access to other external accounts or people connecting as guests.

- Sign into the UQ Zoom web interface.
- Schedule a new meeting, or edit an existing meeting.
- Tick 'Require authentication to join' and select who to restrict the meeting to.
- Select 'Add' next to 'Authentication Exception'.
 
 
 
- Enter the person's name and email address they are registered with Zoom. Multiple people can be added by selecting 'Add Participant'.
- Save once all participants have been added.
 
 
 
- Save the meeting for 'Authentication Exception' to be applied.

page_title: How to I view email messages by conversation?
breadcrumb: How to I view email messages by conversation? > Outlook Web App
text:
How to I view email messages by conversation? > Outlook Web App

- At the top right of the page, click the 'Settings' cog wheel icon.
- Under 'Conversation view', choose one of the following:
	- 
		To sort messages by conversation, select 'Newest on top' or 'Newest on bottom'.
- 
		To show email as individual messages, select 'Off'.
 
-

Follow the instructions on the Microsoft website to view email messages by conversation.

page_title: What is Duo Instant Restore and how do I use it?
breadcrumb: What is Duo Instant Restore and how do I use it?
text:
What is Duo Instant Restore and how do I use it?

What is Duo Instant Restore and how do I use it? Home Information and services Duo Instant Restore lets you reactivate your Duo MFA seamlessly when you get a new device, or reset your existing device. For instructions about using Duo Instant Restore, visit the Duo website at the links below for the device you're using. Apple iOS Android

page_title: What changes will I see with the new Duo MFA prompt?
breadcrumb: What changes will I see with the new Duo MFA prompt?
text:
What changes will I see with the new Duo MFA prompt?

A new Duo interface was recently rolled out and you will be automatically directed to the new Duo MFA prompt.

When you are asked to MFA, you will see a prompt like the one below. Your most secure MFA method will be chosen automatically. 

The below image shows an automatic push being sent to a mobile device.

If you click 'Other options', you can choose to MFA using different methods:

- Duo Push - Send an authentication request to your mobile device.
- Duo Mobile passcode - Enter a passcode generated from the Duo Mobile app.
- Hardware token - Enter a 6-digit time-based one-time password (TOTP), or enter a code from your Duo hardware token.
- Bypass code - enter a bypass code provided by the ITS Service Desk.
- Manage devices - Takes you to the device management portal. You'll be asked to authenticate first using one of the MFA methods.

After you have successfully authenticated, you may see a prompt asking you to trust this browser.

This is the same as the previous 'Remember me' option. Click either:

- 'Yes, trust browser' - you won't need to authenticate with MFA again for the specified time period (usually 7 days).
- 'No, do not trust browser' - you'll need to MFA again the next time you sign in on your browser.

Visit the Duo website for further information about the new Duo interface.

page_title: I receive an 'Access denied' or failed login message when trying to MFA from overseas. What should I do?
breadcrumb: I receive an 'Access denied' or failed login message when trying to MFA from overseas. What should I do?
text:
I receive an 'Access denied' or failed login message when trying to MFA from overseas. What should I do?

In order to comply with U.S. regulations, Duo blocks authentications from users whose IP address originates in a country or region subject to economic and trade sanctions enforced by the U.S. Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC).

If you attempt to MFA from a device with an IP address originating in an OFAC-regulated country or region, you will be blocked from completing your login and receive an error message.

Web-based applications will display the following error message: 'Access denied. Duo Security does not provide services in your current location.' Other applications may display a generic failed login message.

OFAC restrictions relevant to Duo currently apply to the following countries or regions:

- Cuba
- North Korea
- Iran
- Sudan
- Syria
- Crimea region
- Donetsk region
- Luhansk region
- Sevastopol region

If you are in one of these countries or regions, please contact IT support if you require any assistance.

Phone: 1300 738 082 (Australia wide)

page_title: Why can't I edit a booking that's occurring or happened in the past on UQ Book It?
breadcrumb: Why can't I edit a booking that's occurring or happened in the past on UQ Book It?
text:
Why can't I edit a booking that's occurring or happened in the past on UQ Book It?

Why can't I edit a booking that's occurring or happened in the past on UQ Book It?

UQ Book It only allows you to edit an upcoming booking. If you need to edit a booking that's currently happening, or has occurred in the past, please submit an IT request.

page_title: How do I set up MFA in China using a Huawei smartphone?
breadcrumb: How do I set up MFA in China using a Huawei smartphone?
text:
How do I set up MFA in China using a Huawei smartphone?

Duo (the main application used for MFA at UQ), along with other authenticator apps such as Google Authenticator, is unavailable to download from the Google Play store on Huawei and other Android OS-based smartphones in some overseas locations.

When you are unable to download one of these applications, there are other options available.

If you will still want to use the Duo Mobile app for MFA on a Huawei or other smartphone, download the Duo application file directly from the website.

On the internet, you may encounter third party copies of Duo or other mobile applications for MFA. To protect your smartphone and your data, only download applications from legitimate locations (e.g. Google Play store).  

If you're unable to use the Duo mobile app for MFA, you can use a:

- Time-based one-time password (TOTP)
- YubiKey (purchased by yourself)
- Temporary MFA passcode

page_title: What should I do if I have issues using MFA during an exam?
breadcrumb: What should I do if I have issues using MFA during an exam?
text:
What should I do if I have issues using MFA during an exam?

If you experience issues during an exam, please contact AskUs exam support.

Depending on the exam situation, this process differs:

If you experience issues using MFA during an exam in person, alert the supervisor by raising your hand. Then, the supervisor should contact AskUs to receive a one-time code, to provide to the student on a sticky note. This protocol also applies if you arrive at a venue without a device.

If you experience issues using MFA during an online exam, please contact AskUs exam support.

If you have **technical problems**, AskUs exam support is available throughout the exam period.

page_title: Why am I unable to find or download the Duo Mobile app on the app store?
breadcrumb: Why am I unable to find or download the Duo Mobile app on the app store?
text:
Why am I unable to find or download the Duo Mobile app on the app store?

Some older mobile operating systems do not support the Duo Mobile application, meaning that you will not be able to locate or use the application.

If your device does not support the Duo Mobile app, please familiarise yourself with MFA options that don’t require a smartphone.

page_title: What is Duo verified push?
breadcrumb: What is Duo verified push?
text:
What is Duo verified push?

Verified push uses the Duo Mobile app and offers an extra layer of protection by requesting a passcode in addition to the usual notification.

Some older mobile operating systems do not support verified push. If your device does not support verified push, please familiarise yourself with MFA options that don’t require a smartphone.

page_title: How do I turn on the 'new Outlook' on my UQ Windows computer?
breadcrumb: How do I turn on the 'new Outlook' on my UQ Windows computer?
text:
How do I turn on the 'new Outlook' on my UQ Windows computer?

The new Outlook for Windows brings the latest features with a fresh modern design and provides a more Office 365 integrated experience.

The option to try the new Outlook is available to select computers.

To activate the new Outlook, click the new Outlook toggle on the top right of your Outlook screen. This will restart Outlook to complete activation.

At this stage, the new Outlook is still a preview and so may have some issues with the following features:

- Archiving and opening .PST files
- COM add-ins
- Mailbox delegation
- Opening and saving .eml files
- Shared calendars
- Shared mailboxes

If you experience any of the above issues after switching on the new Outlook, you can revert back to the 'old Outlook' by toggling the switch off. This will restart Outlook and restore your previous application and settings.

If you decide to switch back to the 'old Outlook', you will be asked to provide feedback after toggling the switch off.

Providing feedback on your experience using the New Outlook is important. It helps Microsoft address any issues with performance or functionality that you may have encountered and will give them the opportunity to rectify these issues.

page_title: How can I find and access my course reading list?
breadcrumb: How can I find and access my course reading list? > UQ Students
text:
How can I find and access my course reading list? > UQ Students

You can access course reading lists, including required, recommended, and further readings, through the Library website, Library Search or Learn.UQ (Blackboard) course site.

Your reading lists should be available one week prior to the start of teaching. If you cannot find your reading list after following these instructions, contact your Course Coordinator for assistance.

You can find your course reading list by following these steps:

- Go to Talis Aspire (you will be prompted to log in)
- Search for the relevant course code.

Ensure you select the list associated with the correct teaching period.

If you still cannot access your list after following these steps, contact Learning Resources at learningresources@library.uq.edu.au.

The Publish course reading lists guide contains instructions on finding, accessing, editing and publishing your list.

page_title: How do I edit and publish a course reading list?
breadcrumb: How do I edit and publish a course reading list?
text:
How do I edit and publish a course reading list?

The Publish course reading lists guide contains instructions on finding, accessing, editing and publishing your list, including many short videos demonstrating key tasks.

When editing and publishing your reading list, you can improve your student's experience and enhance their learning by applying best practice.

Contact librarians@library.uq.edu.au for assistance from library staff.

page_title: What happens if I'm not accepted by the host university for my semester-based exchange?
breadcrumb: What happens if I'm not accepted by the host university for my semester-based exchange?
text:
What happens if I'm not accepted by the host university for my semester-based exchange?

We'll try to find you another host institution to attend, however, usually by this time in the process most positions are filled or host universities have closed their applications. You'll need to be willing to attend a host university that may not have been one of your top preferences.


If you don't want to be placed in a different university, but are still keen to go on a global experience, we would recommend that you complete a new application by the next relevant application deadline.

page_title: Can I go travelling before my global experience starts?
breadcrumb: Can I go travelling before my global experience starts?
text:
Can I go travelling before my global experience starts?

We don't recommend personal travel in the months before your exchange or short-term global experience starts, as this is often the time that your student visa is being processed.

Travel plans should only be confirmed once you have:

- full UQ acceptance (credit approved by your UQ faculty),
- your host university acceptance letter, and
- your student visa (if required).

If you can, it's better to plan to travel after your global experience has finished.

page_title: What do I do if I have a clash between 2 language courses?
breadcrumb: What do I do if I have a clash between 2 language courses?
text:
What do I do if I have a clash between 2 language courses?

Email languages-cultures@uq.edu.au to see if you can be moved to another class. For many courses, there will only be one class time and therefore clashes may be unavoidable. In this case, you may have to drop one of the courses.

Class times rarely change after the timetable is set, however increased enrolments may lead to extra classes being added.

page_title: I have applied through the online application system, how can I make a change to my application?

page_title: What is a Unique Student Identifier?
breadcrumb: What is a Unique Student Identifier?
text:
What is a Unique Student Identifier?

The Unique Student Identifier (USI) is your individual education reference number that creates an online record of all your training and qualifications undertaken in Australia. You can apply for a USI through the Australian Government’s USI Registry website.

To assist in compliance with Australian Government legislative requirements, it is a condition of enrolment at UQ that you provide your USI so it can be verified against the USI Registry. You will need to provide your USI to the University no later than the commencement of your first semester.

For information about how to provide your USI to UQ, see our advice about the Unique Student Identifier.

page_title: Do I need internet access to use Office 365?
breadcrumb: Do I need internet access to use Office 365?
text:
Do I need internet access to use Office 365?

You need internet access to:

- install and activate the latest releases of Office suites and all Office 365 subscription plans
- manage your subscription account to install Office on other devices
- access documents stored on OneDrive unless you install the OneDrive desktop app
- keep your version of Office up-to-date and keep up with automatic updates
- refresh your licence every 30 days.

You don't need to be connected to the internet to use Office applications because they are fully installed on your computer.

page_title: My GP stated I do not need a second Varicella vaccination, as one was sufficient as a childhood dose. Why do I need to provide two dose dates for Varicella?
breadcrumb: My GP stated I do not need a second Varicella vaccination, as one was sufficient as a childhood dose. Why do I need to provide two dose dates for Varicella?
text:
My GP stated I do not need a second Varicella vaccination, as one was sufficient as a childhood dose. Why do I need to provide two dose dates for Varicella?

The Australian Immunisation Handbook strongly recommends 2 doses of varicella vaccine given at least 4 weeks apart for non-immune Healthcare workers, Childhood Educators and Carers. All UQ healthcare worker students and staff must provide evidence of 2 doses of Varicella, or a positive blood test result indicating Varicella immunity, or evidence of documented history of physician diagnosed chickenpox or shingles.

3. Click on the icon for the software when it appears in start menu's search results.

page_title: As an international student, how do I apply for a package offer?
breadcrumb: As an international student, how do I apply for a package offer?
text:
As an international student, how do I apply for a package offer?

If you meet all the academic requirements for entry into an undergraduate or postgraduate program and still need to meet UQ's English language requirements, you may be eligible for a package offer.

If you already have a conditional offer and would like to request a package offer, log in to My Applications and select the 'Request Change' button.

Package offers can be issued when the only outstanding requirement for admission to a UQ program is English language proficiency. You will have to provide evidence of your current English language ability before a package offer can be issued (e.g. IELTS or another recognised test). When you apply, we will assess your skills and place you in the best English course for your level.

page_title: How long does my virtual application or desktop session last?
breadcrumb: How long does my virtual application or desktop session last?
text:
How long does my virtual application or desktop session last?

If you are actively using your virtual application or desktop, you will not be automatically disconnected or logged off. However, the following session limits do apply:

- You will be automatically logged off after 15 minutes if you disconnect.
- You will be automatically disconnected if your session is inactive after 2 hours. After this time, you will be logged off after 15 minutes.

- You will be automatically logged off if you have been disconnected for 2 hours.
- You will be automatically logged off if your session is idle after 2 hours.

- If you are disconnected, you will not lose any work and can reconnect to your session to save it.
- If you are logged off, all data is destroyed. Please ensure you save any work on your network drive or cloud storage.

page_title: Network Share (J Drive) for GIS Courses
breadcrumb: Network Share (J Drive) for GIS Courses > Why a new network share
text:
Network Share (J Drive) for GIS Courses > Why a new network share

To improve performance, a new network share will be deployed in the same location as the UQDW desktops. This brings the data closer to the virtual desktops and increases transfer performance for GIS applications and workflows.

Each user will have up to 20 GB of storage available on the J Drive per semester.

Data on the J Drive will be available only for the duration of each semester.

User data (staff and students) will be removed 28 days after the last day of the exam period of that semester.

Examples:

- Semester 1 2026 – Exams end 20 June; data removed 19 July.
- Semester 2 2026 – Exams end 21 November; data removed 19 December.

Users (staff and students) are responsible for backing up their own data from the J Drive before the removal date. We recommend using OneDrive or another personal storage solution for this purpose.

The J drive will only be available in UQDW, this cannot be mapped to onsite UQ devices.

page_title: Lectern PC Web Sign-In experience
breadcrumb: Lectern PC Web Sign-In experience > Sign in to Lectern PCs > UQ Staff
text:
Lectern PC Web Sign-In experience > Sign in to Lectern PCs > UQ Staff

- Select Sign in to uq.edu.au
 
 
 
- You will receive a UQ Authenticate pop-up. Enter your UQ username and password and complete the multi-factor authentication process.
 
 Note: With the transition from UQ Auth to Okta in progress, it is expected behaviour to be required to type your username and password twice before you can MFA. Once into UQ Auth, and once again into Okta. This will be streamlined once the transition has been finalised.

- On the sign-in screen, select Guest on the bottom left, then select Sign in in the middle of the screen.
 
 
 
- To gain access to the internet, you will need to be supplied with a UQ visitor account.
 You may access the internet by launching the Internet Access desktop shortcut.
 
 
 
- Log in with your visitor account credentials.

The lectern has synced all of my files on my desktop from OneDrive and there is confidential material there. How could this be prevented?

There are 2 options to overcome this.

Option 1: Login to the device as a guest user. This will no longer give you the new sign in experience and you will be required to manually sign into Office, Teams, OneDrive and or the internet to access work resources.

Option 2: Save any confidential work into the documents folder in OneDrive not on the desktop.


What happens to all my synced OneDrive data after I use the lectern?

After you log off the device it will automatically delete your profile cleaning up anything left on that device.


I used to use the lecture account to login?

This has been replaced with the guest account on the login screen.

 

Is there an automatic logout period?

The device will auto lock after 30 minutes of inactivity.


Why do I have to type my username and password in twice to log in with MFA?

With the migration from UQ Auth and Duo to Okta for MFA, there is currently a known issue where users need to sign into UQ Auth and then another time into the Okta window to MFA. This will be streamlined as the Okta authentication is fully cutover from UQ Auth.

 

Why do I need to sign in with MFA again to access Blackboard or Zoom?

Because these systems work with a slightly different authentication flow, they are unable to use the existing MFA you used to log in to the lectern. This is a known concern, and we are working to streamline this process.

page_title: What are the pathways to studying Psychology at UQ?
breadcrumb: What are the pathways to studying Psychology at UQ?
text:
What are the pathways to studying Psychology at UQ?

Three programs at UQ provide a pathway to becoming a psychologist:

- The three-year Bachelor of Arts (Psychology) plus one year Bachelor of Arts (Honours) in Psychology,
- The three-year Bachelor of Science (Psychology) plus one year Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Psychology, and
- The four-year Bachelor of Psychological Science (Honours).

All programs are accredited by the Australian Psychology Accreditation Council.

The degree that you choose to study will depend on the type of courses that you wish to take in addition to psychology, and the amount of flexibility you desire with your choice of courses. Although the content of the Bachelor of Arts, Bachelor of Science and Bachelor of Psychological Science are different in years one to three, the fourth year of honours study is identical for the Bachelor of Psychological Science, the Bachelor of Arts (Honours) Psychology and the Bachelor of Science (Honours) Psychology.

For more information on studying psychology, see the School of Psychology website.

page_title: What do I do if my USI Status is ‘Verification Failed’?
breadcrumb: What do I do if my USI Status is ‘Verification Failed’?
text:
What do I do if my USI Status is ‘Verification Failed’?

Please check that the USI provided is correct. If it is not, please update your USI in your mySI-net account by following the steps below:

- 
	Open mySI-net. 
- 
	Log into your mySI-net account using your UQ student credentials. 
- 
	Click ‘Personal Details’. 
- 
	Click 'Unique Student Identifier (USI)’. 

When you provide your USI to UQ, your personal details in mySI-net and your USI will be checked with the USI Registry. The following personal information must be the same in both systems to be successfully verified:

- 
	First Name 
- 
	Last Name 
- 
	Date of Birth 

To fix this the error, check whether your details are the same on mySI-net and the USI Registry. You may need to update your details so that you can resubmit your USI for it to be verified.

page_title: I have been sent an allegation letter and asked to attend a misconduct hearing. What will happen next?
breadcrumb: I have been sent an allegation letter and asked to attend a misconduct hearing. What will happen next?
text:
I have been sent an allegation letter and asked to attend a misconduct hearing. What will happen next?

The allegation letter should provide full details of the allegation being made against you, as well as the details of where and when the hearing will take place – it will also contain information on where you can obtain support and your right to have a support person (who is not legally qualified) at the hearing. You also have the right to view all substantive evidence on which the allegation has been based before the hearing.

page_title: What is Q Fever and what immunisation is required?
breadcrumb: What is Q Fever and what immunisation is required?
text:
What is Q Fever and what immunisation is required?

Q fever is a zoonotic infection (can pass from animals to humans) caused by exposure to a highly infectious bacteria, *Coxiella burnetii*, typically by inhaling dust and aerosols from infected animals. It is a relatively common yet highly preventable infection, which can cause severe acute illness. Persons working with animals such as cattle, sheep, goats, and wild animals have the greatest risk of transmission, as well as workers from the meat and livestock industry. Staff and students frequently working in environments where these animals are kept may also be at risk of Q fever, as well as workers handling high risk veterinary specimens or working with the Q fever organism (*Coxiella burnetti*).

People seeking Q fever vaccination must have both serum antibody testing and a skin test prior to Q fever vaccination. The pre-vaccination antibody testing and vaccination process involves two appointments with a medical practitioner. The appointments must be exactly seven days apart to allow for the skin test to be read on day seven. Those detected as having had previous exposure to the bacteria, will not require vaccination. If a Q fever vaccination is required, exposure in high-risk environments should be avoided until 15 days after vaccination to allow immunity to develop.  


Please contact medical practices in your area to discuss access to Q Fever screening and vaccinations

page_title: If I'm in my first year, can I apply for exchange next year?
breadcrumb: If I'm in my first year, can I apply for exchange next year?
text:
If I'm in my first year, can I apply for exchange next year?

To be eligible for semester-based exchange, by the end of the semester in which you apply for exchange, you must have completed at least:

- one year of study (#12 UQ units) for **undergraduate students**, or
- one semester of study (#6 UQ units) for **postgraduate students**.


Your application for exchange will not be assessed until grades have been released for that semester and it can be confirmed that you have successfully completed the required number of UQ units.


Keep the application deadlines in mind and consult a faculty academic advisor to plan your program structure from your first year at university to allow for an exchange later in your degree.


Check your eligibility for exchange on the Internships and Global Experiences website.

page_title: Can I go on exchange in a postgraduate degree?
breadcrumb: Can I go on exchange in a postgraduate degree?
text:
Can I go on exchange in a postgraduate degree?

If you're a coursework masters student, you can apply for an exchange, however, there may be restrictions to your program. Ensure that you meet with an academic advisor from your faculty to confirm if your UQ program is eligible for exchange.

You should also be aware that not all host universities accept pos tgraduatestudents on exchange, so make sure you discuss your destination options with the Internships and Global Experiences Team.

For more information on whether you are eligible for an exchange program, see the exchange eligibility criteria.

page_title: Are all programs offered for commencement in Semester 1 and Semester 2?
breadcrumb: Are all programs offered for commencement in Semester 1 and Semester 2?
text:
Are all programs offered for commencement in Semester 1 and Semester 2?

Not all programs are offered for commencement in both semesters.

To find out when you can start a program, search for the program on the Future Students website – program start dates are listed under the 'Program details' tab.

Not all programs are offered for commencement in both semesters.

To find out when you can start a program, search for the program on the Future Students website – program start dates are listed under the 'Program details' tab.

page_title: Can my program be completed through part-time study?
breadcrumb: Can my program be completed through part-time study?
text:
Can my program be completed through part-time study?

At UQ, some programs are offered both full-time and part-time. To see if a program is offered part-time, search for the program on the Future Students website and view the 'Duration' of the program.

If you're studying on a visa and/or scholarship, visa and/or scholarship restrictions may affect your ability to study part-time.

page_title: I have received an offer, but I have not yet accepted it. How do I change my program?
breadcrumb: I have received an offer, but I have not yet accepted it. How do I change my program?
text:
I have received an offer, but I have not yet accepted it. How do I change my program?

**If you are an international student:**

If you have received an offer and have not yet accepted it, you can request to change to a different program by logging into My Applications (student login required) and clicking the request change button. We will need to reassess your application, and if you meet the entry requirements a new offer for the new program will be issued.

**If you are a domestic student applying through QTAC:**

Follow the advice provided to you by QTAC in your offer letter.

**If you are a domestic postgraduate coursework student:**

If you have received an offer and have not yet accepted it, you can request to change to a different program by logging into My Applications (student login required) and clicking the request change button. We will need to reassess your application, and if you meet the entry requirements a new offer for the new program will be issued.

**If you are a Higher Degree by Research (HDR) student:**

As HDR students require a supervisor and school endorsement, you will be required to withdraw your application and submit a new one. We will need to reassess your application, and if you meet the entry requirements a new offer for the new program will be issued.

page_title: Is my UQ program recognised in other countries?
breadcrumb: Is my UQ program recognised in other countries?
text:
Is my UQ program recognised in other countries?

To see a program's accreditations and professional registration bodies, search and select a program on the Future Students website and then select the 'Program details' tab.

The recognition and accreditation of international qualifications can change over time. To learn more about specific requirements, contact the relevant industry body or organisation you are seeking accreditation or registration with.

Do I need a library card to borrow?

**Current UQ students and staff**

Use your ID card to borrow from the library.

**Alumni, community, hospital and other library members**

You will be given a library card when you join the library.

Use your ID card to borrow from the library.
