It may be possible to change hosts and update your application if your new preferred host is still accepting applications. Contact the Internships and Global Experiences Team to discuss your options.

page_title: Will I have a specialisation printed on my testamur and academic transcript?
breadcrumb: Will I have a specialisation printed on my testamur and academic transcript?
text:
Will I have a specialisation printed on my testamur and academic transcript?

If you're a coursework student, the inclusion of your specialisation (often called a major or field of study) on your graduation documents is dependent on the rules for your particular program. Providing the program rules allow for a specialisation to be issued, and you've completed the appropriate courses, the specialisation will be printed on your graduation documents.

Exceptions:

- Minors aren't included on testamurs, but may, in some instances, be included on transcripts.
- Specialisations aren't printed on graduation documents for Higher Degree by Research graduates.

To find your program rules, search for your program on the Programs and Courses website.

page_title: What do I do if I am unavailable to sit the CASPer test during the test dates?
breadcrumb: What do I do if I am unavailable to sit the CASPer test during the test dates?
text:
What do I do if I am unavailable to sit the CASPer test during the test dates?

Unfortunately, individual schedules cannot be accommodated. All test dates are pre-arranged to ensure application processing meets the offer timelines. For test dates, see the CASPer website. Exact dates will be available on the CASPer website once they are finalised.

For more information about how UQ is using the CASPer test, see Applying for the Bachelor of Veterinary Science (Honours).

A unique code number assigned by QTAC to each program for which they process applications for. To find a program's QTAC code, search the Future Students website for your program.

A unique code number assigned by QTAC to each program for which they process applications for. To find a program's QTAC code, search the Future Students website for your program.

page_title: When are graduation dates decided?
breadcrumb: When are graduation dates decided?
text:
When are graduation dates decided?

Dates for graduation periods are usually confirmed one year in advance, however the exact dates for ceremonies are not confirmed until April/May for July ceremonies and October/November for December each year. See my.UQ for advice on ceremony dates and times for December ceremonies this year and for future graduation ceremonies.

page_title: Will I have time to get to my next class if I have classes that are straight after each other?

text:
Will I have time to get to my next class if I have classes that are straight after each other?

Will I have time to get to my next class if I have classes that are straight after each other? Home Information and services Yes, classes usually finish 10 minutes early, so you can get to your next class on time.

page_title: As the outcome of a misconduct hearing, I have been sent a formal warning. Will this go on my permanent record?
breadcrumb: As the outcome of a misconduct hearing, I have been sent a formal warning. Will this go on my permanent record?
text:
As the outcome of a misconduct hearing, I have been sent a formal warning. Will this go on my permanent record?

A formal warning is recorded on the University's Student Conduct database and does not appear on your studies report or official academic transcript. If you are investigated for suspected misconduct in a future incident, the formal warning will be taken into consideration when determining further action.

page_title: How long does it take to complete a Diploma in Languages?
breadcrumb: How long does it take to complete a Diploma in Languages?
text:
How long does it take to complete a Diploma in Languages?

Generally, students take one course per semester for 4 years (alongside the courses from their bachelor's degree). However, the program can be completed sooner if you take courses in Summer Semester or take more than one language course in some semesters.

page_title: As a science student, who do I contact if I'm unsure what courses to enrol in?
breadcrumb: As a science student, who do I contact if I'm unsure what courses to enrol in?
text:
As a science student, who do I contact if I'm unsure what courses to enrol in?

You should first refer to your Program Rules to gain an understanding of the requirements of your program. To find your Program Rules, search the Programs and Courses site for your program and select the year you commenced. Program Rules are found under the 'Program Requirements' section.

Then check the Faculty of Science Study Planners. These planners provide a helpful guide to recommended course selections for each of our programs – these are broken down by year and major.

If you require further assistance, academic advising appointments are also available through the Faculty office, during which you can discuss your enrolment queries and plan out your courses for the remainder of your program. Advisors can also assist with queries regarding study load, basic career advice and assistance if you are having difficulty with course/s or your program.

If you wish to book an appointment, drop into the Faculty of Science office or call the Faculty on (07) 3365 1888. Certain times of the year can book out a number of weeks ahead, so it is recommended that you book as early as possible.

page_title: How do I generate an MFA passcode using the Duo app?
breadcrumb: How do I generate an MFA passcode using the Duo app?
text:
How do I generate an MFA passcode using the Duo app?

MFA passcodes can be generated using the Duo app form Apple iOS and Android mobile devices. This works even when the device is 'offline'; an internet, wifi, or mobile network connection is not required.

This method is useful for authenticating with MFA in areas with limited or intermittent wifi or mobile network connectivity.

- Open the Duo app on your mobile device.
- Tap 'University of Queensland' to view an MFA passcode. Passcodes are automatically generated and last for 30 seconds.
 
 
 
- Use the generated MFA passcode to authenticate:
	- Select 'Other options'.
- Select 'Duo Mobile passcode'.
 

Enter the passcode generated from your mobile device, then click 'Verify'.

page_title: I want to bring dogs on campus for a wellness activity. What are the requirements?
breadcrumb: I want to bring dogs on campus for a wellness activity. What are the requirements?
text:
I want to bring dogs on campus for a wellness activity. What are the requirements?

To bring animals on campus, you'll need to apply for approval as there are legal requirements. To apply, email the Security Events team at seo@pf.uq.edu.au or call (07) 3365 6003.

To bring animals on campus, you'll need to apply for approval as there are legal requirements. To apply, email the Security Events team at seo@pf.uq.edu.au or call (07) 3365 6003.

page_title: How do I get approval for reserved event parking on campus for exhibitors?
breadcrumb: How do I get approval for reserved event parking on campus for exhibitors?
text:
How do I get approval for reserved event parking on campus for exhibitors?

To organise reserved event parking, the event coordinator is required to apply for event parking approval from the UQ Transport Manager. Email parking@pf.uq.edu.au to apply.

To organise reserved event parking, the event coordinator is required to apply for event parking approval from the UQ Transport Manager. Email parking@pf.uq.edu.au to apply.

page_title: Can I access UQ Rentals if I haven't activated my student account?
breadcrumb: Can I access UQ Rentals if I haven't activated my student account?
text:
Can I access UQ Rentals if I haven't activated my student account?

To retain site exclusivity, it is a requirement that all full-access tenant users have a UQ username and password.

To obtain this, you will need to be either a student who has activated their account or a staff member who has received their UQ username and password.

If you are concerned about securing accommodation in time for your arrival in Brisbane, we recommend to stay in Short-term Accommodation until you can inspect and secure your long-term residence.

These options include:

- On-campus accommodation (residential colleges)
- Off-campus approved accommodation
- Use Google to search for local motels in Toowong, St Lucia, Auchenflower
- Budget backpackers and hostels
- Visit Brisbane Accommodation

page_title: Do I need to have a supervisor before submitting my higher degree by research (HDR) application?
breadcrumb: Do I need to have a supervisor before submitting my higher degree by research (HDR) application?
text:
Do I need to have a supervisor before submitting my higher degree by research (HDR) application?

All research higher degree students will complete their research under the guidance of an advisory team. If you haven't done so already, you should proactively investigate supervisory options within your area of interest. UQ Researchers is a useful resource, as are school and institute websites, for finding out more about the supervisory expertise available at UQ.

The majority of enrolling units (schools, institutes and faculties) require you to have reached in-principle agreement with a supervisor prior to submitting an application. If you would like to check the requirements of a particular enrolling unit, please contact the relevant Higher Degrees by Research Liaison Officer.

Sometimes, applicants for particular scholarships will not have agreement from a supervisor prior to submitting an application because the supervisor will select their preferred candidate after the closing date. All scholarship advertisements have a contact person listed, so please check if you are unsure.

If you are applying for a Grant-aligned Priority PhD Scholarship, you should reach agreement with the Chief Investigator prior to applying. The Chief Investigator will be the supervisor for the student undertaking the listed project.

If your application is for a Professional Doctorate by Research, contact us for further details on what information to provide.

page_title: How can I get help completing a Student Fee Refund form?
breadcrumb: How can I get help completing a Student Fee Refund form?
text:
How can I get help completing a Student Fee Refund form?

If you are having trouble submitting a Student Fee Refund form online (through 'My Requests' on my.UQ), check the following:

- all required fields are completed
- there are no error messages displayed on the form
- you are submitting the form using a Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox browser.

If you still need help, contact Student Fees for advice.

page_title: Where can I get advice to help with planning my program and choosing courses?
breadcrumb: Where can I get advice to help with planning my program and choosing courses?
text:
Where can I get advice to help with planning my program and choosing courses?

New undergraduate students should visit Starting at UQ – where first-year planners for your program can be accessed. For information about planning your program, see choosing your courses.

You can also contact your Faculty for information and help with choosing courses.

page_title: What should I do if I can't find full course profiles for my exchange study plan?
breadcrumb: What should I do if I can't find full course profiles for my exchange study plan?
text:
What should I do if I can't find full course profiles for my exchange study plan?

Many host universities have limited course information on their website.


If you can't find the full course profiles, gather as much information as you can about each course from the host university website and save the information on each course in PDF format.


After the Internships and Global Experiences team have deemed you eligible for Part 1 criteria and have allocated you a host university, they will provide you further instruction for gathering the full course profiles.


Please note, some universities will not release full course profiles before students commence at the university. For these universities, students will be required to have a full study load of general electives in order to be eligible for these hosts.

page_title: How long does it take for my enrolment information to be updated in My Timetable?
breadcrumb: How long does it take for my enrolment information to be updated in My Timetable?
text:
How long does it take for my enrolment information to be updated in My Timetable?

How long does it take for my enrolment information to be updated in My Timetable? Home Information and services If you add or drop a course in mySI-net, your enrolment information will be updated in My Timetable within an hour.

page_title: I have a temporary residency (partner)/provisional visa and I am waiting for my permanent residency visa. Am I considered a domestic student?
breadcrumb: I have a temporary residency (partner)/provisional visa and I am waiting for my permanent residency visa. Am I considered a domestic student?
text:
I have a temporary residency (partner)/provisional visa and I am waiting for my permanent residency visa. Am I considered a domestic student?

No, you are considered an international student.


You're an international student if you are:

- intending to study on a student visa, or
- not a citizen of Australia or New Zealand, or
- not an Australian permanent resident, or
- a temporary resident (visa status) of Australia.

page_title: I'm studying a science degree. Who do I contact about my plans to study overseas?
breadcrumb: I'm studying a science degree. Who do I contact about my plans to study overseas?
text:
I'm studying a science degree. Who do I contact about my plans to study overseas?

Our UQ Abroad team run information sessions throughout the year. At these sessions, you'll receive general information about exchange opportunities, the application process, eligibility requirements, costs and scholarship advice.

There is also more information on the Student Exchange website, and the Faculty of Science Student Exchange page.

You can contact UQ Abroad at uqabroad@uq.edu.au to arrange an appointment for further advice. If you have questions about how an exchange semester might fit within your program, contact the Faculty of Science.

Faculty of Science students are not required to have a progress check completed prior to submitting an exchange application, as we'll complete a progress check as part of your study plan approval process. However, it's helpful for you to understand your program rules and the requirements of your major (if applicable), so ensure that you read through your program rules before applying.

page_title: Can I use VPN client software other than Cisco AnyConnect?
breadcrumb: Can I use VPN client software other than Cisco AnyConnect?
text:
Can I use VPN client software other than Cisco AnyConnect?

Cisco AnyConnect is the only supported VPN client for UQ's VPN.

To install Cisco AnyConnect, follow the instructions on the VPN website under 'How to connect'.

If you use Linux it may be possible to use openconnect-sso, however, this is not supported by UQ. Visit the GitHub page for further information.

page_title: I paid my fees, but they still show as owing in mySI-net. What do I do?
breadcrumb: I paid my fees, but they still show as owing in mySI-net. What do I do?
text:
I paid my fees, but they still show as owing in mySI-net. What do I do?

It will take a minimum of 5 business days for payments to be processed and appear in mySI-net. If the amount paid does not appear in mySI-net after a week, please email Student Fees .

It will take a minimum of 5 business days for payments to be processed and appear in mySI-net. If the amount paid does not appear in mySI-net after a week, please email Student Fees .

When is the Academic Calendar for next year available?

page_title: If I interrupt my studies or withdraw will I be eligible for a refund for my OSHC?
breadcrumb: If I interrupt my studies or withdraw will I be eligible for a refund for my OSHC?
text:
If I interrupt my studies or withdraw will I be eligible for a refund for my OSHC?

Please contact your OSHC provider regarding any refund that may be available to you. If UQ arranged your OSHC, your provider is Allianz Care Australia.

For more information about withdrawing and interrupting your studies, visit my.UQ.

Please contact your OSHC provider regarding any refund that may be available to you. If UQ arranged your OSHC, your provider is Allianz Care Australia.

For more information about withdrawing and interrupting your studies, visit my.UQ.

page_title: How can I get help completing an Interruption of Studies form?
breadcrumb: How can I get help completing an Interruption of Studies form?
text:
How can I get help completing an Interruption of Studies form?

You can submit an Interruption of Studies request (through 'My Requests' on my.UQ) if you are currently, or have previously been, active in a UQ program with a continuous enrolment rule; if you are enrolled in a discontinued program or if you are in a 1 year honours program.

If your program does not have a continuous enrolment requirement, you do not need to request to interrupt your studies, and can take a maximum interruption of two years.

For instructions on how to find out if your program has a continuous enrolment rule, see the interrupting studies page.

If you wish to interrupt studies in multiple programs, you will need to submit multiple Interruption of Studies requests.

If you are having trouble submitting the form, check the following:

- all required fields are completed
- there are no error messages displayed on the form
- you are submitting the form using a Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox browser.

If you would like any help, please contact Student Central for advice.

page_title: I'm being investigated for suspected misconduct. What does this mean?
breadcrumb: I'm being investigated for suspected misconduct. What does this mean?
text:
I'm being investigated for suspected misconduct. What does this mean?

Where a concern about possible misconduct has been raised, the University is obliged to conduct a preliminary investigation. Once all available evidence has been collected, a decision will be made to either take no further action, counsel and warn a student, or issue an allegation of misconduct and conduct a hearing. In some cases, you may be asked to attend a meeting to assist in the investigation. All preliminary investigations are dealt with in a timely manner.

page_title: What is O-Week?
breadcrumb: What is O-Week?
text:
What is O-Week?

O-Week, or Orientation Week, is an action-packed week filled with information sessions, entertainment and activities to introduce you to UQ life.

Our Orientation website has all the information on what's happening – plus you can use the personal online planner to map out your week.

page_title: How do I accept my offer?
breadcrumb: How do I accept my offer?
text:
How do I accept my offer?

**Domestic Coursework Applicants**

**If you've applied for an undergraduate program**

- Advice on accepting your offer will be included in your offer letter. Follow our advice on how to respond to your offer.

**If you've applied for a postgraduate program**

- Advice on accepting your offer will be included in your offer letter. Follow our advice on how to respond to your offer.


**International Coursework Applicants**

**If you're an agent:**

The student needs to accept their offer online under their own login. An agent is not permitted to accept on behalf of the student and this is why the agent portal blocks you from accepting. The student should log into their portal using the email address nominated in their application. Once the student has accepted online, you can assist them to make the payment online if you wish.

- If they have not previously accessed the portal, they must first activate their account.
- If they're unsure of their password, they can reset it by clicking on 'Forgotten Password'.


**If you have applied to UQ without an agent: **

- You need to login to the Online Application portal using the email address nominated in your application. If you have forgotten your password, you can reset it by clicking on 'Forgotten Password'.


**Higher Degree by Research Applicants**

- Advice on accepting your offer will be included in your offer letter. Follow our advice on how to respond to your offer.

page_title: What happens if I don't have a tax file number (TFN), but want to apply for a HECS-HELP, FEE-HELP or SA-HELP loan?
breadcrumb: What happens if I don't have a tax file number (TFN), but want to apply for a HECS-HELP, FEE-HELP or SA-HELP loan?
text:
What happens if I don't have a tax file number (TFN), but want to apply for a HECS-HELP, FEE-HELP or SA-HELP loan?

If you have not already done so, apply for a tax file number (TFN) now with the Australian Taxation Office (ATO).

It may take some time for the ATO to issue you with a TFN. Once you have applied, you will need to provide a copy of your TFN Application Summary and lodgement Receipt from Australia Post to Student Fees before the census date. This information will allow us to maintain your enrolment while your TFN application is processed.

If you do not meet the TFN requirement by the census date, you will have to pay your fees upfront or risk having your enrolment cancelled.

Can I open documents created in Microsoft 365 with earlier versions of Office?

page_title: How do I use the shared Duo MFA tokens inside a lab?
breadcrumb: How do I use the shared Duo MFA tokens inside a lab?
text:
How do I use the shared Duo MFA tokens inside a lab?

Some laboratories or special purpose areas at UQ have shared MFA tokens located permanently inside as an MFA option; this is usually only in areas where it is not possible to use mobile phones for MFA. 

 

To use shared MFA tokens in a particular area, you must be registered to use them; this can be managed via the MFA Management portal:

To use shared MFA tokens in a particular area, you must be registered to use them; this can be managed via the MFA Management portal:

- Login to the MFA Management portal.
- Under 'Lab Management', any labs or special purpose areas where you are registered to use shared MFA tokens will be listed.
	- If the area where you require access is listed, then you should already be able to use the available tokens in that are for MFA.
- If the area where you require access is not listed, click 'Associate a new lab' and choose your lab from the drop-down list.
- 
		Setting and renewing the expiry date for access can also be completed within 'Lab Management'.
 

If you require further assistance with MFA tokens in a particular lab, please consult your local lab manager or submit an IT request.

page_title: Is a QTAC course and UQ course the same thing?
breadcrumb: Is a QTAC course and UQ course the same thing?
text:
Is a QTAC course and UQ course the same thing?

No, QTAC refers to your degree as a 'course'. At UQ a 'course' is a subject within your program (degree) – normally completed as units of study over a semester.

For more information about choosing UQ courses see my.UQ - Choose courses or for QTAC course codes see QTAC Course search.

page_title: What's a blue card and how do I apply for one?
breadcrumb: What's a blue card and how do I apply for one?
text:
What's a blue card and how do I apply for one?

A blue card indicates that you have passed the Queensland Government's working with children check. Many UQ programs include working with children, such as teaching, nursing, audiology, and social work. If your program includes working with children, you will be advised of the blue card requirement.

For assistance with your blue card application, contact your faculty or school.

For more information on blue cards, see the Blue Card Services website.

page_title: What is in-person attendance mode?
breadcrumb: What is in-person attendance mode?
text:
What is in-person attendance mode?

An in-person course may incorporate some aspects of online delivery (e.g. online lectures, options for online tutorials, online assessment) but requires students to engage in in-person learning or assessment at a UQ campus or other location at some point.

page_title: Is free counselling available to students?
breadcrumb: Is free counselling available to students?
text:
Is free counselling available to students?

Yes, all currently enrolled UQ students are eligible for 10 free counselling sessions each year. See our my.UQ website for information on counselling.

Yes, all currently enrolled UQ students are eligible for 10 free counselling sessions each year. See our my.UQ website for information on counselling.

page_title: How do I know what courses to take during my Global Experience at the host university?
breadcrumb: How do I know what courses to take during my Global Experience at the host university?
text:
How do I know what courses to take during my Global Experience at the host university?

The most important part of knowing which host university courses to choose, is to know the academic requirements of your UQ program and exactly which UQ courses you still have to gain credit for. If you have electives within your program, we highly recommend saving these for your Global Experience.

- You should first complete a progression check, either online by yourself or with your faculty, to determine which UQ courses you have remaining in your UQ program.
- Go to the host university page, access the host university's course catalogue/list of courses, and search for courses similar to the UQ course/s you still need credit for (as identified by your progression check). It may be helpful to cross-check with the UQ Credit Precedents database for this.
- Select appropriate courses from the host university and write down the titles and course codes on your study plan/application for credit form.
- Download the host Course Profiles of each course you list on your study plan
- For semester-based Exchange, please make sure that you find enough courses to make up double the full-time study load at your host university, so that you can fill in the 'back-up' list of courses on the study plan.

Contact the Internships & Global Experiences Team for more information.

page_title: What is a postgraduate program?
breadcrumb: What is a postgraduate program?
text:
What is a postgraduate program?

Postgraduate programs are usually undertaken after completing an undergraduate degree, or, in some careers, on the basis of relevant work experience. They include graduate certificates, graduate diplomas, masters and doctorates.

See our Future Students website for information on applying to UQ.

page_title: What is a program?
breadcrumb: What is a program?
text:
What is a program?

A program is a sequence of study leading to the award of a qualification such as a degree, diploma or certificate. At UQ, a degree, diploma or certificate is called a program. You can search for all programs offered at UQ on the Future Students website

page_title: What courses can I study in my program?
breadcrumb: What courses can I study in my program?
text:
What courses can I study in my program?

If you are a prospective student, you can find the course list for each of our programs on the Future Students website – search for a program and the course list is available under the 'What you'll study' section in the 'Overview' tab.

If you are a current UQ student, you can find the course list for your program on the Programs and Courses website – search for your program and select the year you commenced. Your course list is listed under the 'What you can study' section.

page_title: Can I do my honours year on exchange?
breadcrumb: Can I do my honours year on exchange?
text:
Can I do my honours year on exchange?

Yes and no.


If you are completing in a one-year honours program, you cannot complete your honours on exchange as it often includes a research/thesis element which must be completed at UQ.


However, if you are completing an embedded honours program (the honours is part of your degree), you can contact your faculty to enquire as to whether you may be eligible to go on exchange.


For more information, see our advice on semester-based exchange.

page_title: I’ve received an allegation of misconduct letter and I’m really worried. Is there anyone I can talk to?
breadcrumb: I’ve received an allegation of misconduct letter and I’m really worried. Is there anyone I can talk to?
text:
I’ve received an allegation of misconduct letter and I’m really worried. Is there anyone I can talk to?

The University of Queensland Union (UQU) can provide advice to help you prepare for your hearing. Contact UQU by phone on (07) 3346 3400 or via their website Student Advocacy and Support (SAS)

Student Services also offer confidential and free online or face-to-face counselling for students who are experiencing anxiety or stress during the misconduct process.

page_title: When can I apply for Elite Athlete Status?
breadcrumb: When can I apply for Elite Athlete Status?
text:
When can I apply for Elite Athlete Status?

The nature and extent of elite athlete commitments can be unpredictable and outside your control. However, you're expected to plan for known commitments well in advance and are required to apply for Elite Athlete Status as soon as possible. The timing of your request may have an impact our ability to assist you.

You can submit a request for Elite Athlete Status any time during the current semester or teaching period. Requests for future semesters and teaching periods open four months before the start date of the semester or teaching period.

For more information about eligibility and considerations for elite athletes, see the Programs and Assessments for Elite Athletes Procedures [3.50.07]. For more information about the UQ Elite Athlete Program, see the UQ Sport website.

page_title: How do I update my personal email address in my.UQ?
breadcrumb: How do I update my personal email address in my.UQ?
text:
How do I update my personal email address in my.UQ?

How do I update my personal email address in my.UQ? Home Information and services You can check your personal email and update it, if necessary, in the My profile section of my.UQ. Log into my.UQ. Go to the 'My profile' section. Under 'Personal contact details', review your personal email.

page_title: Can I get credit for my undergraduate concurrent diploma towards my bachelor degree?
breadcrumb: Can I get credit for my undergraduate concurrent diploma towards my bachelor degree?
text:
Can I get credit for my undergraduate concurrent diploma towards my bachelor degree?

Unfortunately, you cannot transfer credit from a completed diploma to the bachelor degree or from the bachelor degree to the diploma.

If you were to transfer credit from the bachelor degree program to the diploma, that credit would be cancelled from the bachelor degree. If you choose not to complete the diploma, you may be allowed to transfer some credit towards your bachelor degree program, but you'll need to contact your faculty to discuss this.

page_title: Which security companies are authorised to operate on UQ campuses?
breadcrumb: Which security companies are authorised to operate on UQ campuses?
text:
Which security companies are authorised to operate on UQ campuses?

Which security companies are authorised to operate on UQ campuses? Home Information and services To request a list of UQ-approved external security providers, email seo@pf.uq.edu.au.

page_title: What is a bridging course?
breadcrumb: What is a bridging course?
text:
What is a bridging course?

If a program requires a prerequisite in an area that you have not studied before, successfully completing a UQ-approved bridging course (PDF, 130KB) will help you to bridge that gap in your knowledge and meet the prerequisite.

If a program requires a prerequisite in an area that you have not studied before, successfully completing a UQ-approved bridging course (PDF, 130KB) will help you to bridge that gap in your knowledge and meet the prerequisite.

page_title: Is a QTAC code and program code the same thing?
breadcrumb: Is a QTAC code and program code the same thing?
text:
Is a QTAC code and program code the same thing?

No. A **QTAC code** is a unique code number assigned by QTAC to each program for which they process applications for. A **program code** is unique identifying number assigned by UQ for each of our programs.

To find a program's QTAC code or program code, search the Future Students website for your program.

page_title: How do I select my courses for my science program?
breadcrumb: How do I select my courses for my science program?
text:
How do I select my courses for my science program?

The Faculty of Science provides a study planner website and academic advising appointments to assist new and continuing students to select appropriate courses for their program.

For more information, contact the Faculty of Science.

The Faculty of Science provides a study planner website and academic advising appointments to assist new and continuing students to select appropriate courses for their program.

For more information, contact the Faculty of Science.

page_title: What is a specific religious observance?
breadcrumb: What is a specific religious observance?
text:
What is a specific religious observance?

If a specific religious observance clashes with a scheduled exam time, you may apply for alternative examination arrangements.

A specific religious observance is defined as:

- the act of observing or complying with a religious law, custom, command or rule,
- undertaking a regular act of worship, or
- the long-standing custom of celebrating a religious holiday.

page_title: How do I upload a recording to my Echo360 Personal Library?

- Click 'Create' at the top of the screen, then select 'Upload Media'.
 
 
 
- Click 'Select Files to Upload' and locate your downloaded Zoom recorded files.
- Click 'Upload' once you have selected the file(s).

Once completed, the recording files will appear under the 'Library' tab.

Contact your faculty or school for advice on selecting a major.

page_title: I have a disability which may impact my ability to sit the CASPer test, what should I do?
breadcrumb: I have a disability which may impact my ability to sit the CASPer test, what should I do?
text:
I have a disability which may impact my ability to sit the CASPer test, what should I do?

The CASPer test authority is able to provide appropriate accommodations (modifications) for applicants who require this support. They require a formal request and supporting documentation to make a decision on the appropriate accommodation required. This must be completed a number of weeks prior to taking the test. For exact requirements, visit the CASPer website.

For more information about how UQ is using the CASPer test, see Applying for the Bachelor of Veterinary Science (Honours).

page_title: Where can I find my StudentHub log-in information?
breadcrumb: Where can I find my StudentHub log-in information?
text:
Where can I find my StudentHub log-in information?

To log in to StudentHub, use your UQ log-in. If you are not actively enrolled, you will not be able to access StudentHub.

If you receive an error message when logging in, send a screenshot of your browser window showing the error message and URL to studenthub@uq.edu.au for assistance.

page_title: Why is the antivirus software on my UQ computer deleting my files?
breadcrumb: Why is the antivirus software on my UQ computer deleting my files?
text:
Why is the antivirus software on my UQ computer deleting my files?

The antivirus software will place items in quarantine first where possible. You can then review each item, but you'll need to contact IT support to have the files released from quarantine.

The antivirus software will delete any items that are likely to be malicious, based on an algorithm.

page_title: Can I do a progression check on my Bachelor of Arts degree?
breadcrumb: Can I do a progression check on my Bachelor of Arts degree?
text:
Can I do a progression check on my Bachelor of Arts degree?

If you're preparing for graduation, or you just want to ensure that you are progressing correctly through your program, you can complete a progression check.

To complete a progression check, select your program type:

If you have any questions about your outstanding program requirements, once you have completed your progression check, contact your Faculty for advice.

page_title: What clubs can I join at UQ?
breadcrumb: What clubs can I join at UQ?
text:
What clubs can I join at UQ?

UQ's vibrant community life offers plenty of opportunities to meet like-minded people and learn new skills. See the UQ Union website for information on over 200 affiliated clubs and societies offering educational, recreational, sporting, faculty-based, political, religious, cultural and social activities.

page_title: What is short-term study?
breadcrumb: What is short-term study?
text:
What is short-term study?

Short-term study is where you enrol in a course, but the units of study do not lead to the award of a qualification. We also call this non-award study.

There are 3 different types of non-award study at UQ:

- Non-award study program for international students: with this program, you can study up to 4 individual courses per semester, with a total limit of 8 courses. These courses don't have to be attached to a program or faculty. You will need to apply for a student visa to enrol in this program.
- Part-time non-award study: this option is for students who wish to take less than 3 courses per semester. If you are an international student, you are not eligible for a student visa for this program so you will need an alternative valid Australian visa that allows you to study in this way.
- Study Abroad and Incoming Exchange: this option is for students currently studying at a university outside of Australia who wish to come to UQ for one or two semesters.

Find out how to apply for your preferred study option via the Future students – Applying for non-award study page.

page_title: What happens to my HECS-HELP, FEE-HELP or SA-HELP loan if I change programs?
breadcrumb: What happens to my HECS-HELP, FEE-HELP or SA-HELP loan if I change programs?
text:
What happens to my HECS-HELP, FEE-HELP or SA-HELP loan if I change programs?

You will need to immediately submit a new eCAF if you switch UQ programs or add a program (e.g. changing from a single to dual program).

Your enrolment will be cancelled for non-payment of fees if you do not submit the required eCAF by the Census Date.

page_title: I'm struggling with my study. What should I do?
breadcrumb: I'm struggling with my study. What should I do?
text:
I'm struggling with my study. What should I do?

First, have a chat with your course tutors and/or your course coordinators to express your concerns about your study. Your tutors or course coordinators will help you to identify your strengths and weaknesses in your courses and provide advice on how your work can be improved. Our Student Life team offer learning workshops and appointments with Learning Advisers to help you adapt to study. Counselling is also available.

Depending on your circumstances and the time of semester, there may be an option to withdraw from your course. Contact your faculty for advice on withdrawing and your study plan for future semesters.

page_title: What certified documents can I request?
breadcrumb: What certified documents can I request?
text:
What certified documents can I request?

The standard statements available for request are listed on my.UQ. These documents meet the requirements of most third parties.

Variations to these standards statements will only be approved in exceptional circumstances and can take longer to be issued.

For further information about certified documents see my.UQ.

page_title: How do I register my ID card or Go Card for printing?
breadcrumb: How do I register my ID card or Go Card for printing?
text:
How do I register my ID card or Go Card for printing?

To register your ID card or Go Card for printing:

- At the printer, scan your card.
- Select 'Yes' on the touch screen to associate your card.
- Enter your UQ username and password, and select 'Associate'.
- Your card will now be registered. Select 'OK' to confirm.

page_title: What is UQ Rentals?
breadcrumb: What is UQ Rentals?
text:
What is UQ Rentals?

UQ Rentals is an online resource available to UQ students, staff and partners to assist them in finding accommodation during their time at the university.


The platform connects prospective tenants to providers with vacant properties and rooms close to our St Lucia, Herston and Gatton campuses.


The listed properties come from a wide variety of providers, including private landlords, real estate agencies and commercial student accommodation providers.


Though the service assists you in finding a property, we do not participate in the application process nor the ongoing legal arrangement between tenant and provider.


Our commitment to tenants is to provide an intuitive, safe and reliable method of searching for properties in and around the Brisbane area.


Our commitment to our providers is to empower you to confidently provide engaging listings that attract tenants to quality accommodation that keeps them.

page_title: How do I make friends at university, are there social events, programs or networks?
breadcrumb: How do I make friends at university, are there social events, programs or networks?
text:
How do I make friends at university, are there social events, programs or networks?

There are plenty of opportunities available to meet new people and make friends at UQ.

UQ has it's very own free social network! Meet new friends and connect with like-minded students that share your interests through chat groups and facilitated on & off campus meetups. Whether you are into food, drinks, arts, culture, fitness, games or study - find your circle online at Get Set Program.

From anime to yoga, medievalism to touch rugby, and hockey to dancing, you are certain to find like-minded people by joining one of the 200+ clubs and societies.

UQ offers a number of mentoring programs that provide valuable leadership and guidance through all stages of your time at UQ and beyond. From supporting new students in the transition to uni life to fostering positive cultural, social and professional connections, this is your first stop in learning how you can get involved.

Volunteering is an ideal way to build your skills and extend your professional and personal network while contributing to a worthy cause. UQ can assist you to access unique opportunities at UQ, with organisations in Australia and worldwide.

page_title: Where can I sit my off-campus exam?
breadcrumb: Where can I sit my off-campus exam?
text:
Where can I sit my off-campus exam?

Because of the uncertainty and ongoing global impact of COVID-19, some domestic and all international exam centres will not be available until further notice. Visit UQ community COVID-19 advice for more information on changes to policies and procedures.

A limited number of off-campus exam venues and locations are available across Australia for deferred and supplementary exams. If you have applied to sit more than one exam off-campus, you must be available in one location for all exams. View the list of off-campus exam locations. currently available (subject to change without notice).

See the my.UQ website for more information on off-campus exams.

page_title: What does OHS stand for?
breadcrumb: What does OHS stand for?
text:
What does OHS stand for?

What does OHS stand for? Home Information and services OHS stands for occupational health and safety. Our Health, Safety and Wellness (HSW) division manages OHS at UQ.

page_title: What does SOP stand for?
breadcrumb: What does SOP stand for?
text:
What does SOP stand for?

What does SOP stand for? Home Information and services At UQ, SOP stands for safe operating procedure.

page_title: When does my USB drive get scanned by the antivirus software on my UQ computer?
breadcrumb: When does my USB drive get scanned by the antivirus software on my UQ computer?
text:
When does my USB drive get scanned by the antivirus software on my UQ computer?

When does my USB drive get scanned by the antivirus software on my UQ computer?

Files on your USB drive are quickly scanned when you open a file from your USB or external drive. Cortex XDR will continue to monitor for any malicious behaviour whilst the files are open.

page_title: Can I still apply for a global experience if I've failed a course?
breadcrumb: Can I still apply for a global experience if I've failed a course?
text:
Can I still apply for a global experience if I've failed a course?

Can I still apply for a global experience if I've failed a course? Home Information and services You may still be eligible for a global experience if you've failed a course, depending on your overall GPA and academic progression status. For more information. Check the relevant eligibility criteria page below: Semester-based Exchange eligibility criteria Short-term Study eligibility criteria Internships eligibility criteria

page_title: As a third party requiring a student to provide evidence of something, how do I receive or request My eQuals digital documents?
breadcrumb: As a third party requiring a student to provide evidence of something, how do I receive or request My eQuals digital documents?
text:
As a third party requiring a student to provide evidence of something, how do I receive or request My eQuals digital documents?

There are two ways in which you can receive a digital document:

A university student or graduate can provide you with a link, directing you to their official documents. That can be an email from My eQuals or an email direct to you from the student/graduate containing the link. Check that the link starts with https://myequals.net

Students and graduates can also download a cryptographically signed PDF copy of the document and upload it during your application process, or attach to an email to you.

There are specific features to look for to ensure authenticity of the documents.

Digital documents can only be guaranteed when viewed online or using Adobe Acrobat Reader. You should not trust the authenticity of printed format documents unless it is a certified hard copy issued directly by the University.

page_title: How can I cancel an upcoming appointment booking in StudentHub?
breadcrumb: How can I cancel an upcoming appointment booking in StudentHub?
text:
How can I cancel an upcoming appointment booking in StudentHub?

To cancel a booking for an upcoming appointment, go to the My Upcoming Appointment page. Here you will see a list of all of your upcoming appointments, and will be able to cancel any bookings as required.

To cancel a booking for an upcoming appointment, go to the My Upcoming Appointment page. Here you will see a list of all of your upcoming appointments, and will be able to cancel any bookings as required.

page_title: Once approved, how long is the Elite Athlete Status valid for?
breadcrumb: Once approved, how long is the Elite Athlete Status valid for?
text:
Once approved, how long is the Elite Athlete Status valid for?

Approval for Elite Athlete Status is only valid for one semester. Athletes are required to re-apply if Elite Athlete Status is needed in a future semester or teaching period.

For more information about eligibility and considerations for Elite Athletes, see the Programs and Assessments for Elite Athletes Procedures [3.50.07].

For more information about the UQ Elite Athlete Program, see the UQ Sport website.

page_title: I applied for a SA-HELP loan and now wish to pay the Student Services and Amenities Fee (SSAF) up front, what should I do?
breadcrumb: I applied for a SA-HELP loan and now wish to pay the Student Services and Amenities Fee (SSAF) up front, what should I do?
text:
I applied for a SA-HELP loan and now wish to pay the Student Services and Amenities Fee (SSAF) up front, what should I do?

To change from using a SA-HELP loan to paying your SSAF upfront you need to select the 'Manage SA-HELP Request' on your mySI-net 'Financials' homepage and select the up front payment option. This must be done before the relevant SA-HELP payment date. See my.UQ for information on SSAF due dates.

See my.UQ for information on the Student Services and Amenities Fee (SSAF).

page_title: Can I choose which date I graduate on?
breadcrumb: Can I choose which date I graduate on?
text:
Can I choose which date I graduate on?

Can I choose which date I graduate on? Home Information and services No, graduation ceremonies are linked to the faculty and school who administer your program. Unfortunately, you cannot choose which ceremony you attend.

page_title: What is a direct application?
breadcrumb: What is a direct application?
text:
What is a direct application?

A direct application is an application submitted directly to UQ through the online application process, rather than through a tertiary admission centre.

Not all programs at UQ are available through direct application. To find out how you apply for a program, search for the program on the Future Students website and select the 'How to apply' tab.

page_title: How do I get an antivirus exemption on my UQ computer?
breadcrumb: How do I get an antivirus exemption on my UQ computer?
text:
How do I get an antivirus exemption on my UQ computer?

Exemptions will only be considered for special purpose machines or legitimate business reasons.

To request an exemption, submit an IT request with:

- the computer name
- details of software you want excluded
- what the computer is used for
- a supporting business case.

page_title: What is the selection process for Doctor of Medicine (MD)?
breadcrumb: What is the selection process for Doctor of Medicine (MD)?
text:
What is the selection process for Doctor of Medicine (MD)?

What is the selection process for Doctor of Medicine (MD)? Home Information and services See applying for UQ's Doctor of Medicine (MD) for information on the selection process.

page_title: Can I study my degree part-time and still enrol in a concurrent diploma?
breadcrumb: Can I study my degree part-time and still enrol in a concurrent diploma?
text:
Can I study my degree part-time and still enrol in a concurrent diploma?

Yes, you can study a concurrent Diploma in Languages or concurrent Diploma in Music Performance provided that you are enrolled in at least one course in your bachelor program.

Yes, you can study a concurrent Diploma in Languages or concurrent Diploma in Music Performance provided that you are enrolled in at least one course in your bachelor program.

page_title: How do I list my property and pay?
breadcrumb: How do I list my property and pay?
text:
How do I list my property and pay?

To list and pay for your property on UQ Rentals:


Register your account and/ or log in and will land on 'Your Dashboard' - follow the wizard.

1.  Select property type:  Choose the property that best matches the property you wish to advertise. Next - Add main details

 

2.  Add address:  Enter the address of the property. If your address is not located, click on "Manually enter my address"

3.  Add Main Details:  This will take you to the Property and Adverts Tab

- Property tab 
	- Complete Area, Accommodation type etc. The total number of rooms includes all habitable rooms in the property, whether occupied or unoccupied. Next.
- Add Facilities - Complete the facilities the property has
- Add Photos - Provide your photos by either dragging and dropping pictures; or selecting them directly from your local files
- Add Document - Select if your Property has House Rules - If “Yes”, you must upload a copy of your House Rules using the Add button / If “No” you can proceed without uploading a document
- Accreditation - Select YES if your property is accredited under the National Rental Affordability Scheme
 

- Adverts Tab
	- Enter Pricing
- Complete the necessary details including rent, the number of rooms available to rent, selecting the correct advertising period etc.
- Ensure you have the correct advertising period noted.
- Coronavirus Amendment Policy - During the online COVID situation, you have the option to provide a fair cancellation policy to assist students.
- Make payment and advertise.
 


Congratulations, your property is now registered and live!

If you require assistance, please contact the Accommodation team – accommodation@uq.edu.au or 1300 275 870.

page_title: What are residential schools and when are they held?
breadcrumb: What are residential schools and when are they held?
text:
What are residential schools and when are they held?

Residential schools give external students the opportunity to attend tutorials or practical sessions on campus. They provide an opportunity to connect face-to-face with teaching staff and other students, and to access campus facilities.

External students are provided with a study chart in their electronic course profile (ECP) which sets out the semester's schedule. The study chart includes the residential school period, which is usually held mid-semester – this week usually coincides with internal students mid-semester break.

page_title: What do I do if I have Elite Athlete Status and am unable to submit an assessment item on time?
breadcrumb: What do I do if I have Elite Athlete Status and am unable to submit an assessment item on time?
text:
What do I do if I have Elite Athlete Status and am unable to submit an assessment item on time?

If you're an elite athlete representing Australia or an Australian state or territory, you may be eligible to apply for an extension. To apply for an extension, you must hold Elite Athlete Status for the current study period.

For more information about eligibility and considerations for elite athletes, see the Programs and Assessments for Elite Athletes Procedures [3.50.07]. For more information about the UQ Elite Athlete Program, see the UQ Sport website.

page_title: What can I do if the credit for my short-term global experience has been denied?
breadcrumb: What can I do if the credit for my short-term global experience has been denied?
text:
What can I do if the credit for my short-term global experience has been denied?

You'll need to discuss your specific circumstances with the Global Experiences Team and your UQ faculty.

You may be able to change your short-term global experience preference. You can research alternative short-term global experiences. You'll need to submit a new application for credit form if want to apply for credit for an alternative experience.

For more information, email __iget@uq.edu.au__.

page_title: How do I book a UQ playing field?
breadcrumb: How do I book a UQ playing field?
text:
How do I book a UQ playing field?

How do I book a UQ playing field? Home Information and services Contact UQ Sport to book a UQ playing field or oval.

page_title: Are exams scheduled on weekends?
breadcrumb: Are exams scheduled on weekends?
text:
Are exams scheduled on weekends?

Some exams may be scheduled on a weekend. Further information about exam timetables is available on my.UQ.

The Academic Calendar is published one year in advance. As a UQ student, you are expected to consult the list of important exam dates and make yourself available to sit your exams as required.

page_title: How do I add a profile photo to Microsoft 365?
breadcrumb: How do I add a profile photo to Microsoft 365?
text:
How do I add a profile photo to Microsoft 365?

You can add or change your profile photo in Microsoft 365 from Microsoft365.com, or while using Microsoft 365 applications on your computer or in a browser on your phone. Your photo will appear everywhere there's a profile photo circle in Microsoft 365.

- 
	Find a photo you want to use and store it on your computer. The photo needs to be smaller than 4 MB and can be a .png, jpg, or .gif file. 
- 
	Sign in to Microsoft 365 with your UQ account at Microsoft365.com. 
- 
	Select the circle in the upper right hand corner of the page that shows your initials or an icon of a person. 
- 
	In the My accounts pane, select the circle that shows your initials or an icon of a person. Change your photo in the pop-up that appears.

page_title: Can I study a language in the Bachelor of Arts and the Diploma in Languages at the same time?
breadcrumb: Can I study a language in the Bachelor of Arts and the Diploma in Languages at the same time?
text:
Can I study a language in the Bachelor of Arts and the Diploma in Languages at the same time?

Can I study a language in the Bachelor of Arts and the Diploma in Languages at the same time? Home Information and services Yes, if you study different languages in each program.

page_title: Can postgraduate students enrol in the Diploma in Languages?
breadcrumb: Can postgraduate students enrol in the Diploma in Languages?
text:
Can postgraduate students enrol in the Diploma in Languages?

Can postgraduate students enrol in the Diploma in Languages? Home Information and services No, the diploma is only offered concurrently with an undergraduate degree.

page_title: Where can I find my program's course list from the year I commenced?
breadcrumb: Where can I find my program's course list from the year I commenced?
text:
Where can I find my program's course list from the year I commenced?

Search the Programs and Courses website for your program. Under 'what you can study' select 'course list'. At the top of the page under 'show information for' there is a drop-down box. Select the year you commenced here and the relevant course list will show.

If you are unable to find the course list from the year you commenced, contact your Faculty or School.

page_title: What should I be aware of when looking for accommodation?
breadcrumb: What should I be aware of when looking for accommodation?
text:
What should I be aware of when looking for accommodation?

If you are looking for accommodation off campus you should understand your rights and responsibilities as a tenant – this includes lease agreements, rental bonds, property inspections, and obligations regarding repairs and maintenance to the property.

See our my.UQ website for more accommodation information and tenancy advice

page_title: Which courses do I enrol in for the postgraduate coursework programs offered by the School of Economics?
breadcrumb: Which courses do I enrol in for the postgraduate coursework programs offered by the School of Economics?
text:
Which courses do I enrol in for the postgraduate coursework programs offered by the School of Economics?

The School of Economics provides recommended program plans to help you plan your enrolment. Select your program and starting semester to view the recommended plan.

Once you have planned out your courses, to ensure your plan will meet your program requirements, you can email your program plan to the School of Economics at econ.pgrad@uq.edu.au for review.

To see all the courses offered in your program, view your course list on Programs and Courses or mySI-net. You are required to follow the course list for the year you commenced in your program.

To see your course list for the year you commenced:

- on Programs and Courses: Search for your program name, and select your program name from the list. On the program page, change the year in the drop-down box next to 'Show information for', and select 'Change'. Select the green 'Course List' button on the left-hand side to view your course list.
- in mySI-net: Select the 'Enrolments' tile. Select 'Course List' next to your program name. On the program page, change the year in the drop-down box next to 'Show information for', and select 'Change'. Select the green 'Course List' button on the left-hand side to view your course list.

page_title: How can I organise a private BBQ or event on campus?
breadcrumb: How can I organise a private BBQ or event on campus?
text:
How can I organise a private BBQ or event on campus?

How can I organise a private BBQ or event on campus? Home Information and services If you'd like to host an event, see our advice on planning an event at UQ.

page_title: After I submit my exchange application to UQ, how soon can I expect a response?
breadcrumb: After I submit my exchange application to UQ, how soon can I expect a response?
text:
After I submit my exchange application to UQ, how soon can I expect a response?

Once the application deadline has passed, the Internships and Global Experiences Team (IGET) will hold a mandatory Next Steps Session which will provide essential information, including timelines.


In the month following the application deadline, IGET may be email you clarifying aspects of your application.


Most students will receive their Part 1 Eligibility outcome and host allocation within 6 to 9 weeks of the application deadlines, after exam results have been released for that semester.


For more information, see our advice on attending the Next Steps Session and application outcome.

page_title: Do I need to select an honours supervisor when I apply?
breadcrumb: Do I need to select an honours supervisor when I apply?
text:
Do I need to select an honours supervisor when I apply?

Do I need to select an honours supervisor when I apply? Home Information and services Not all honours programs require you to select a supervisor prior to commencement. Check your school website for more information.

page_title: Can I take more than one course in the same language at the same time?
breadcrumb: Can I take more than one course in the same language at the same time?
text:
Can I take more than one course in the same language at the same time?

This will depend on the language you are studying, your proficiency level in that language, and what courses are being offered in a particular semester.

For most languages, you can usually only take one course at a time until you reach 3rd year level; however, you can usually take 2 courses in Japanese, Chinese or Korean at 1st and 2nd year levels.

page_title: What do I do if I find an injured animal on campus?
breadcrumb: What do I do if I find an injured animal on campus?
text:
What do I do if I find an injured animal on campus?

If you find injured wildlife on campus, please contact The Royal Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals (RSPCA) on 1300 ANIMAL (1300 264 625).

If you find injured wildlife on campus, please contact The Royal Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals (RSPCA) on 1300 ANIMAL (1300 264 625).

page_title: I'm having trouble registering to Zoom Webinars. What should I do?
breadcrumb: I'm having trouble registering to Zoom Webinars. What should I do?
text:
I'm having trouble registering to Zoom Webinars. What should I do?

If you're trying to register to a Zoom webinar restricted to UQ accounts and receive the following message, you'll need to register with your primary UQ email address.

'This meeting is for authorized registrants only. Please enter another email address.'

To find your primary UQ email address:

- Launch Zoom and sign in with SSO.
- Select your initials in the top right of the Zoom window.
- Select the 'Show email' icon.
- This is the email address you'll need to register with.

page_title: How can I find rules and requirements for my preferred program?
breadcrumb: How can I find rules and requirements for my preferred program?
text:
How can I find rules and requirements for my preferred program?

If you are a current UQ student, search for your program on the Programs and Courses website and select the year you commenced. You can find your course list under the 'What you can study' section and your program rules under the 'Program requirements' section.

If you are a prospective UQ student, search for your program on the Future Students website. You can find the course list and program rules for your selected program under the 'What you'll study' section in the 'Overview' tab.

page_title: Where can I practice my presentation in the library?
breadcrumb: Where can I practice my presentation in the library?
text:
Where can I practice my presentation in the library?

Central Library features a purpose built room for students to practice presentations. Presentation room 12-N206 is located on Level 2. This room features standard presentation equipment found in all UQ lecture rooms, but in a small-scale setting.

Equipment included in this room:

- Echo 360
- Whiteboard
- Document Camera
- Lectern/Radio Microphone
- PA System
- Instructor PC
- Data Projector with interactive whiteboard
- Touch screen controller.

See also Booking rooms.

page_title: I’m expecting to graduate this semester, can I complete a short-term global experience during the UQ semester break?
breadcrumb: I’m expecting to graduate this semester, can I complete a short-term global experience during the UQ semester break?
text:
I’m expecting to graduate this semester, can I complete a short-term global experience during the UQ semester break?

To meet the eligibility criteria, you must have at least 2 UQ units remaining in your current UQ program. If you're due to complete your UQ program in the semester of application, you're not eligible to participate in a short-term global experience.


For more information, see our advice on short-term experiences.

page_title: Can I complete a course at another university and credit it towards my UQ degree?
breadcrumb: Can I complete a course at another university and credit it towards my UQ degree?
text:
Can I complete a course at another university and credit it towards my UQ degree?

Under special circumstances, you can apply to study on a cross-institutional basis at another university. However, you need to have the course assessed to ensure that it is suitable and of the same standard as a course offered at UQ. Permission must be given by the Head of School. To apply, you will need to ensure that you also meet the application requirements of the other institution.

See my.UQ for more information on cross-institutional enrolment.

page_title: What is tetanus and what immunisation is required?
breadcrumb: What is tetanus and what immunisation is required?
text:
What is tetanus and what immunisation is required?

Tetanus is caused by a bacterium found in soil, which can enter wounds and cause severe illness. Due to the nature of work undertaken within animal and agricultural programs at UQ, students should be up-to-date and protected with tetanus vaccinations prior to commencing their coursework. Workers who are exposed to animals, agricultural, groundswork/soil, waste water, sewage or mortuary activities are also at additional risk of tetanus.

You must ensure you have had a full primary course of tetanus vaccinations and provide evidence of this (e.g. childhood vaccination course) if tetanus evidence has been requested as a requirement. If evidence of a full course is not available, UQ will require a record of one documented adult dose of dTpa or dT vaccine within the last 10 years, and a requirement for 10 yearly boosters. Adults are recommended to receive a tetanus booster dose if they are over 50 years of age and have not received a booster dose of tetanus-containing vaccine in the past 10 years.

page_title: Which host university is the best place for me to go on exchange?
breadcrumb: Which host university is the best place for me to go on exchange?
text:
Which host university is the best place for me to go on exchange?

Choosing a host university is a personal decision, and will depend on:

- the UQ credit you're seeking from your time overseas (electives or discipline-related)
- what you wish to get out of the experience
- your budget and finances.

See our advice about student exchange for information on host universities and what you need to consider before applying.

page_title: What is a companion course?
breadcrumb: What is a companion course?
text:
What is a companion course?

Companion courses are a pair of courses (or a group of courses) that should be studied together. Companion courses can either be studied all together in the same semester, or in separate semesters (i.e. one companion can be studied before another).

See our my.UQ website for advice on choosing your courses.

page_title: Can I get a refund of the Student Services and Amenities Fee (SSAF) if I have to withdraw from study after the census date?
breadcrumb: Can I get a refund of the Student Services and Amenities Fee (SSAF) if I have to withdraw from study after the census date?
text:
Can I get a refund of the Student Services and Amenities Fee (SSAF) if I have to withdraw from study after the census date?

A refund of the Student Services and Amenities Fee (SSAF) is not available for enrolment changes after the census date. Any SA-HELP debt incurred cannot be refunded and will be recorded with the Australian Taxation Office (ATO). See my.UQ for information on the Student Services and Amenities Fee (SSAF)

page_title: What is a program?
breadcrumb: What is a program?
text:
What is a program?

What is a program? Home Information and services A program is a sequence of study involving enrolment, study and graduation normally awarded with a qualification such as a degree, diploma or certificate.

page_title: When can I add courses for Semester 1 next year?
breadcrumb: When can I add courses for Semester 1 next year?
text:
When can I add courses for Semester 1 next year?

When can I add courses for Semester 1 next year? Home Information and services Enrolments for Semester 1 usually open towards the last week of November – check the Academic Calendar for an exact date.

How do I decline my offer?

**If you've applied for an undergraduate program:**

Follow our advice on how to respond to your offer.

**If you've applied for a postgraduate program:**

Follow our advice on how to respond to your offer.

To decline your offer, log in to My Applications (student login required) and click the decline button.

page_title: What does the UQ Digital Workspace mean for computer labs?
breadcrumb: What does the UQ Digital Workspace mean for computer labs?
text:
What does the UQ Digital Workspace mean for computer labs?

Along with the use of the UQ Digital Workspace, UQ is beginning to transition to Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) based computer labs.

As part of this transition, ITS will be reaching out to space owners to discuss potential spaces for decommissioning of computers. The areas targeted for decommissioning will be spaces with out-of-warranty computers.

To minimise the reduction in machines whilst users become familiar with the BYOD concept, computers will be kept running beyond their warranty period with spares kept from other decommissioned rooms.

Computers in labs with specific requirements such as connectivity to lab equipment will continue to be replaced.

page_title: Do I need to re-apply for SA-HELP each year?
breadcrumb: Do I need to re-apply for SA-HELP each year?
text:
Do I need to re-apply for SA-HELP each year?

You only need to submit one Request for SA-HELP Assistance form (SA-HELP eCAF) to UQ for your program. If you change program and you wish to obtain a SA-HELP loan for the new program, you must submit a new SA-HELP eCAF via the mySI-net 'Financials' tile on or before the relevant SA-HELP payment date.

See my.UQ for information on the Student Services and Amenities Fee (SSAF).

page_title: If I'm in my first semester of study, can I complete a short-term global experience at the end of this semester?
breadcrumb: If I'm in my first semester of study, can I complete a short-term global experience at the end of this semester?
text:
If I'm in my first semester of study, can I complete a short-term global experience at the end of this semester?

No, to participate in a short-term global experience, you must have successfully completed at least #6 UQ units in your current UQ program at the time of application to UQ.

For more information, see short-term experiences eligibility.

No, to participate in a short-term global experience, you must have successfully completed at least #6 UQ units in your current UQ program at the time of application to UQ.

For more information, see short-term experiences eligibility.

page_title: Does the intercampus bus go to Herston?
breadcrumb: Does the intercampus bus go to Herston?
text:
Does the intercampus bus go to Herston?

Unfortunately, the intercampus bus only travels between St Lucia and Gatton. However, you can catch public transport from UQ Lakes at the St Lucia campus to the Herston campus. Use the TransLink Journey Planner to plan your journey.

Unfortunately, the intercampus bus only travels between St Lucia and Gatton. However, you can catch public transport from UQ Lakes at the St Lucia campus to the Herston campus. Use the TransLink Journey Planner to plan your journey.

breadcrumb: I'm lost on campus. How do I get around?
text:
I'm lost on campus. How do I get around?

I'm lost on campus. How do I get around? Home Information and services To help you find your way around UQ, you can use the UQnav app on your phone or UQ Maps on your desktop.

page_title: Can I study a concurrent diploma at UQ without being enrolled in a bachelor program?
breadcrumb: Can I study a concurrent diploma at UQ without being enrolled in a bachelor program?
text:
Can I study a concurrent diploma at UQ without being enrolled in a bachelor program?

Can I study a concurrent diploma at UQ without being enrolled in a bachelor program? Home Information and services No, unfortunately you need to be concurrently enrolled in a bachelor program to study a concurrent diploma.

page_title: What supporting documentation will be accepted with an Elite Athlete Status request?
breadcrumb: What supporting documentation will be accepted with an Elite Athlete Status request?
text:
What supporting documentation will be accepted with an Elite Athlete Status request?

Requests must include supporting documentation provided by a state or national sporting authority. Supporting documentation can be accepted from:

- a state or national Coach or Official/Manager
- sporting institutions, or
- professional associations.

Supporting documentation must be in PDF format on an official letterhead and signed. Incorrect or incomplete requests will not be considered.

For more information about eligibility and considerations for elite athletes, see the Programs and Assessments for Elite Athletes Procedures [3.50.07].

For more information about the UQ Elite Athlete Program, see the UQ Sport website.

page_title: Do I have to pay money to use UQ Rentals?
breadcrumb: Do I have to pay money to use UQ Rentals?
text:
Do I have to pay money to use UQ Rentals?

UQ Prospective Tenants

The UQ Rentals service is completely free for UQ prospective tenants to use. There are no hidden fees or other costs on the website.

Landlords/ Real Estates/ Property Managers (Providers)

UQ Rentals has a pay-for-listing model whereby Providers pay to advertise their properties.

Currently, the charge is $20.00 per property, per season.

A property is any residence with its own address, therefore this includes:

- Individual Houses;
- Individual Apartments; and
- Individual Units

A season is a nominated period of time, currently divided into:

- Season 1 (Semester 1) - September 01 to March 31
- Season 2 (Semester 2) - March 01 to September 30

Advertisements made during these periods will remain valid until the season ends.


For example, a listing made on September 30 can be posted, edited, disabled, and reposted an unlimited number of times until March 31.

page_title: What do I do if my class of honours isn't shown on my graduation application form?
breadcrumb: What do I do if my class of honours isn't shown on my graduation application form?
text:
What do I do if my class of honours isn't shown on my graduation application form?

Graduation checks are usually performed before final grades are available, so your Conditional Graduation Application form on mySI-net (student login required) may not include a reference to Honours. Your eligibility for Honours will be checked after final grades are available and displayed on mySI-net.

page_title: When are honours awarded?
breadcrumb: When are honours awarded?
text:
When are honours awarded?

At UQ, you can undertake honours in one of two ways:

**Option 1:** you can apply for a one-year honours program after you graduate from a bachelor's degree in the same discipline (e.g. Bachelor of Business Management (Honours)).

**Option 2:** you can apply for a four-year (or more) program which has on-course honours embedded within the program structure (e.g. Bachelor of Physiotherapy (Honours)).

See our Future Students website for information on applying for honours study.

page_title: Where do I submit my assignment?
breadcrumb: Where do I submit my assignment?
text:
Where do I submit my assignment?

Where do I submit my assignment? Home Information and services You can find information on where to submit your assignment in your course's electronic course profile (ECP) on Blackboard.

If you are feeling distressed, contact Student Services for support.

page_title: Can I transfer my language courses to the Diploma in Languages, and choose another major for my Bachelor of Arts (BA)?
breadcrumb: Can I transfer my language courses to the Diploma in Languages, and choose another major for my Bachelor of Arts (BA)?
text:
Can I transfer my language courses to the Diploma in Languages, and choose another major for my Bachelor of Arts (BA)?

Can I transfer my language courses to the Diploma in Languages, and choose another major for my Bachelor of Arts (BA)?

Yes. If you choose to complete your Diploma in Languages in a language that you have previously studied under the Bachelor of Arts, you must transfer credit – courses can only be counted towards one program.

page_title: What does ‘restrictions’ mean in a course profile?
breadcrumb: What does ‘restrictions’ mean in a course profile?
text:
What does ‘restrictions’ mean in a course profile?

A course with restrictions is only available for enrolment to students who are enrolled in the program/s listed. For example, to enrol in MEDI7121 you must be a Doctor of Medicine (MD) student.

See the my.UQ website for advice on choosing your courses.

How do I check if the antivirus software is running properly on my UQ computer?

A shield icon will display on your task bar. Below are screenshots showing how the icon will appear on your operating system.
