page_title: Who can help me submit a Late Addition of Courses form?
breadcrumb: Who can help me submit a Late Addition of Courses form?
text:
Who can help me submit a Late Addition of Courses form?

Before submitting a Late Addition of Course request, please ensure that you have read and understood the information on my.UQ.

You can only submit a Late Addition of Course request after the final date for addition of courses or alternation of enrolment has passed. Before that date you may simply add the course in mySI-net (assuming you meet all enrolment conditions required).

If you are having trouble submitting the form, check the following:

- all required fields are completed
- there are no error messages displayed on the form
- you are submitting the form using a Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox browser.

If you still need help, contact Student Central for advice.

page_title: An external Zoom account can't join a restricted Zoom meeting after adding 'Authentication Exception'. What should I do?
breadcrumb: An external Zoom account can't join a restricted Zoom meeting after adding 'Authentication Exception'. What should I do?
text:
An external Zoom account can't join a restricted Zoom meeting after adding 'Authentication Exception'. What should I do?

There are a few possible causes for people not being able to join a Zoom meeting when they have been added to the authentication exception list:

- The person connecting to your Zoom meeting needs to be signed into Zoom using the email address added as an exception. If they have clicked the Zoom link without signing into Zoom they will not be allowed to join.
- The email address added needs to match their Zoom email exactly.
- The person connecting needs to be running Zoom client v5.0.0 or newer.

page_title: What happens when I arrive in my host city for my global experience?
breadcrumb: What happens when I arrive in my host city for my global experience?
text:
What happens when I arrive in my host city for my global experience?

Your host university will most likely have provided you with a letter or information pack before you depart from Australia – including information about your orientation dates and helpful hints for when you arrive in the country.

Some universities have airport pick up services or welcoming parties. For more information, see the Pre-departure Guides for exchange and short-term global experiences.

page_title: As a UQ staff member and HDR student, why is it important to submit my publications to UQ eSpace?
breadcrumb: As a UQ staff member and HDR student, why is it important to submit my publications to UQ eSpace?
text:
As a UQ staff member and HDR student, why is it important to submit my publications to UQ eSpace?

There are many benefits in having an up-to-date publications profile, so that UQ eSpace can:

- maintain an accurate profile of your research output and to promote your work via UQ Experts and ORCID
- make your publications more discoverable via Google and other platforms
- assist with promotions and performance appraisals (UQ login required) – UQ eSpace populates the Academic Portal and the online version of your academic portfolio of achievement (Form A)
- allow deposit and reporting on open access publications, as required by most grants and by UQ policy
- track and capture your citation counts via Scopus and Web of Science
- track who is talking about your research via Altmetrics and provides you with social media tools to share your research
- provide data for internal reporting for schools and institutes and for government research assessment exercises.

For more information, contact UQ eSpace.

page_title: What dual degrees can I study with the Bachelor of Science?
breadcrumb: What dual degrees can I study with the Bachelor of Science?
text:
What dual degrees can I study with the Bachelor of Science?

You can find the dual degrees offered on the Bachelor of Science page under the 'Program details' tab.

The Faculty of Science also offers other programs that can be completed as dual degrees. For a list of Science and Mathematics programs, including duals and postgraduate programs, see the Future Students website.

page_title: As I'm studying a dual degree, will I have to complete courses from both degree components while on exchange?
breadcrumb: As I'm studying a dual degree, will I have to complete courses from both degree components while on exchange?
text:
As I'm studying a dual degree, will I have to complete courses from both degree components while on exchange?

You can choose to take courses from either, or both, of your degree components while on exchange if your host university allows enrolment across different faculties.


Be careful during your research, as some host universities may restrict your study to only one school/faculty within their university. See the host university page for any exclusions.


Try and save your elective courses for your exchange overseas. It will be much easier to get faculty approval with these courses, and it provides you with more flexibility when you arrive at your host university.

page_title: I've just been accepted for my global experience. Can I buy my plane tickets now?
breadcrumb: I've just been accepted for my global experience. Can I buy my plane tickets now?
text:
I've just been accepted for my global experience. Can I buy my plane tickets now?

We advise against purchasing any plane tickets or travel until you have:

- received a study plan/credit approval letter from your UQ faculty,
- been officially accepted by your host university/organisation, and
- had your student visa/immigration documents finalised.

Until then, you might like to ask your travel agent about transferable or refundable deposits or check the price of flexible fares, so that changes can be made to your tickets if required.

page_title: How do I set up a Duo token using the MFA portal?
breadcrumb: How do I set up a Duo token using the MFA portal?
text:
How do I set up a Duo token using the MFA portal?

- Sign into the MFA Management portal. If you can't sign in, follow the recovery process.
- Under 'Device Management', select 'Add a new device'.
- Select 'Hardware token', then click 'Continue'.
- Enter the serial number from the back of the token. Make sure you leave out the hyphens.
- Click 'Add Token'.

The hardware token will be assigned to your account and be ready to use.

page_title: Can I study the Diploma in Languages if I only have one semester left to complete my undergraduate program?
breadcrumb: Can I study the Diploma in Languages if I only have one semester left to complete my undergraduate program?
text:
Can I study the Diploma in Languages if I only have one semester left to complete my undergraduate program?

Can I study the Diploma in Languages if I only have one semester left to complete my undergraduate program? Home Information and services You need to have at least 8 units remaining in your program to be eligible to commence the Diploma in Languages.

page_title: Will I need to find temporary accommodation while I look for permanent housing?
breadcrumb: Will I need to find temporary accommodation while I look for permanent housing?
text:
Will I need to find temporary accommodation while I look for permanent housing?

If you are new to Brisbane or arriving from interstate or overseas, staying in short term/ temporary accommodation while you consider your accommodation options can be a good idea.

If you are travelling from an area outside of Brisbane, interstate or overseas and have not yet arranged accommodation, we recommend you book Short Term Accommodation  for around 1-2 weeks so you can physically inspect properties.

page_title: If I'm enrolled in a graduate diploma, can I graduate from a graduate certificate instead?
breadcrumb: If I'm enrolled in a graduate diploma, can I graduate from a graduate certificate instead?
text:
If I'm enrolled in a graduate diploma, can I graduate from a graduate certificate instead?

Yes, it's possible to graduate with the lesser award in a postgraduate suite, but only where the program rules allow. You won't automatically be graduated from the lesser award – you'll need to request this early in the semester by contacting your faculty.

page_title: Do I have to pay the Student Services and Amenities Fee (SSAF) if I do not intend to use any services or amenities?
breadcrumb: Do I have to pay the Student Services and Amenities Fee (SSAF) if I do not intend to use any services or amenities?
text:
Do I have to pay the Student Services and Amenities Fee (SSAF) if I do not intend to use any services or amenities?

Yes, regardless of whether you intend to use any of the services and amenities provided, as a UQ student you will still have access to various services, should you need them. See my.UQ for information on the Student Services and Amenities Fee.

Yes, regardless of whether you intend to use any of the services and amenities provided, as a UQ student you will still have access to various services, should you need them. See my.UQ for information on the Student Services and Amenities Fee.

page_title: Can I study overseas during the Australian summer or winter break?
breadcrumb: Can I study overseas during the Australian summer or winter break?
text:
Can I study overseas during the Australian summer or winter break?

There are many opportunities to study overseas during the summer or winter break through short-term study.


See short-term study for more information.


Additionally, there are opportunities to undertake an internship during the Summer or Winter break.

There are many opportunities to study overseas during the summer or winter break through short-term study.


See short-term study for more information.


Additionally, there are opportunities to undertake an internship during the Summer or Winter break.

page_title: What's a High Use book and where can I get one?
breadcrumb: What's a High Use book and where can I get one?
text:
What's a High Use book and where can I get one?

High Use items are materials that are required for specific courses and are heavily used (e.g. learning resources). They are available on a 24-hour loan.

Your Library Search results will indicate if an item is held in High Use. Select the item in the results and view the **Get It** information. For example, Central > **High Use (Level 1)** > HD62.4 .H55 2025

The following libraries have High Use sections:

- Architecture and Music
- Central Library
- Dorothy Hill Engineering and Sciences
- Dutton Park Health Sciences
- Herston Health Sciences
- JK Murray Library (UQ Gatton).

See also: Borrowing rules and charges.

page_title: I don't want to appeal the penalty imposed as a result of a misconduct hearing and want my grade to be finalised now. What should I do?
breadcrumb: I don't want to appeal the penalty imposed as a result of a misconduct hearing and want my grade to be finalised now. What should I do?
text:
I don't want to appeal the penalty imposed as a result of a misconduct hearing and want my grade to be finalised now. What should I do?

I don't want to appeal the penalty imposed as a result of a misconduct hearing and want my grade to be finalised now. What should I do?

You should advise the Associate Director – Student Conduct & Integrity in writing at studentconduct@uq.edu.au of your request to relinquish your appeal period, including the reasons for your request.

page_title: What do I do if I have received a Progression Alert ?
breadcrumb: What do I do if I have received a Progression Alert ?
text:
What do I do if I have received a Progression Alert ?

Ensure you read the Progression Alert email sent to your student email account carefully. 

In the coming weeks, you will receive an email directing you to complete the Back on Track module on Blackboard. The module is designed to assist you in reflecting and identifying factors to help you improve your academic performance. Please note that the module will be unavailable until you receive the email with instructions on how to complete the module.

See the Progression alert and interventions page for more information.

page_title: Is the St Lucia campus bike friendly?
breadcrumb: Is the St Lucia campus bike friendly?
text:
Is the St Lucia campus bike friendly?

Yes, UQ encourages cycling as an environmentally-sustainable mode of transport.

At our St Lucia campus, you'll find:

- bike racks throughout the campus
- bike boxes for showering and storing your belongings
- bike repair and water refill stations
- designated bike pathways.

For more information, see cycling at UQ

page_title: Who do I call for maintenance issues?
breadcrumb: Who do I call for maintenance issues?
text:
Who do I call for maintenance issues?

Who do I call for maintenance issues? Home Information and services For all maintenance issues, contact PF Assist on (07) 3365 2222.

page_title: How do I cancel my Library training booking?
breadcrumb: How do I cancel my Library training booking?
text:
How do I cancel my Library training booking?

When you book into our Training sessions you will receive an email from StudentHub with a link to the event.

- Click the link in the email to go directly to the event.
- Log in with your UQ username and password.
- Click the **Cancel booking**button.

You can also cancel your booking via StudentHub:

- Go to StudentHub and log in.
- Choose the **Dashboard**tab.
- Select **Event Bookings**.
- Click on the Event title.
- Click the **Cancel Booking**button.

You can also cancel your booking via our Training page:

- Go to our Training page.
- Select the event.
- Click the **Login and Book**button.
- Click the **Cancel Booking**button.

If you see a **Cancellations Closed** button, email training@library.uq.edu.au to ask us to cancel for you.

page_title: Does the campus I studied at appear on my testamur?
breadcrumb: Does the campus I studied at appear on my testamur?
text:
Does the campus I studied at appear on my testamur?

Does the campus I studied at appear on my testamur? Home Information and services No, campus information does not appear on your testamur (degree certificate).

page_title: Can I change the order of my name for graduation?
breadcrumb: Can I change the order of my name for graduation?
text:
Can I change the order of my name for graduation?

You'll be able to change the order of your name for graduation (also referred to as a degree name) in mySI-net after you have been identified as a potential graduand. This normally occurs approximately 6 weeks prior to grade release. Potential graduands will be notified via email at this time.

page_title: How can I get help completing an Elite Athlete Status request?
breadcrumb: How can I get help completing an Elite Athlete Status request?
text:
How can I get help completing an Elite Athlete Status request?

Before submitting a request, make sure that you've read and understood the eligibility criteria and all associated policies and guidelines. For more information about the UQ Elite Athlete Program, see the UQ Sport website.

If you're having trouble submitting the request, check that:

- all required fields are completed
- there are no error messages displayed on the form
- you're submitting the form using a Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox browser

If you still need help, contact ITS Help Desk for assistance.

page_title: I’m completing a concurrent diploma as well as a bachelor degree. Which ceremony do I attend?
breadcrumb: I’m completing a concurrent diploma as well as a bachelor degree. Which ceremony do I attend?
text:
I’m completing a concurrent diploma as well as a bachelor degree. Which ceremony do I attend?

If you're completing a concurrent diploma, you'll need to attend the ceremony for the undergraduate degree. At the ceremony, you'll receive a separate testamur (degree certificate) for the diploma. You should wear the academic dress suitable for your highest degree (i.e. the bachelor gown, hood and trencher).

If you complete your diploma after you have graduated from your bachelor degree, it's not possible for you to attend another ceremony for the conferral of this award. Instead, your graduation documents will be issued to you following the completion of the graduation ceremony period.

page_title: Will I get an offer for all the programs I applied for through QTAC?
breadcrumb: Will I get an offer for all the programs I applied for through QTAC?
text:
Will I get an offer for all the programs I applied for through QTAC?

You're only eligible to receive one offer from QTAC. This will be for your highest eligible preference in order of the major offer round date. If you're offered a place in an early round but would prefer a program offered in a later round, you can rearrange your preferences.

The order in which you list your preferences is very important. Always place programs in order of your personal preference – from most desired to least desired according to each program's start date.

See the QTAC website for information on planning your preferences.

page_title: How can I make a donation to the Library's collections?
breadcrumb: How can I make a donation to the Library's collections?
text:
How can I make a donation to the Library's collections?

The UQ Library appreciates support to help build an outstanding research collection. You can give to the Library online. We also accept donations of rare, unique or valuable materials.

UQ’s Fryer Library collects heritage and unpublished manuscript materials. To learn more, read our donate to Fryer Library page and contact the Fryer Librarian.

For donations to other Library locations or collections, please contact the relevant Faculty Services Librarians Manager. Materials that don't meet our criteria for collection may be donated to the UQ Alumni Book Fair.

page_title: Can I apply for a MBA if I don't have a degree?
breadcrumb: Can I apply for a MBA if I don't have a degree?
text:
Can I apply for a MBA if I don't have a degree?

You may be eligible to apply without a degree, if you have a significant level of relevant work and managerial experience for the Graduate Certificate in Business Administration (GCBA) pathway into UQ's MBA program. More information is available on the Master of Business Administration page – entry requirements are listed under the 'Program details' tab.

page_title: Do I need a Tax File Number to obtain a HELP loan?
breadcrumb: Do I need a Tax File Number to obtain a HELP loan?
text:

page_title: Do I need to get a student visa to go on a global experience?
breadcrumb: Do I need to get a student visa to go on a global experience?
text:
Do I need to get a student visa to go on a global experience?

Depending on the length of your stay, many countries require students to have a student visa or study permit for their global experience. Depending on your destination, it can take up to 3 months for visas to be processed.


You won't be able to apply for your student visa until you've received an official acceptance letter from your host university. However, we strongly advise that you research your visa options and start organising the required documents early.


It's your responsibility to ensure that you have the correct visa or study permit for the country you're entering.

page_title: How are preferences sorted in My Timetable?
breadcrumb: How are preferences sorted in My Timetable?
text:
How are preferences sorted in My Timetable?

Before each semester, you will be given an opportunity to enter your preferred classes in My Timetable. Preferences are then sorted through the system and students are allocated to classes taking these preferences into account while also avoiding any clashes. You will not be allocated to a class you have not preferenced.

For example, if 30 students choose a particular class as their first preference, but there are only 25 places, the system will select 25 students at random and allocate them to this class. The other 5 students will be allocated to lower preference classes.

page_title: Why did UQ become smoke-free?
breadcrumb: Why did UQ become smoke-free?
text:
Why did UQ become smoke-free?

As an internationally recognised leader in health research, education and training, we're committed to matching best-practice education and research with best-practice policy. Tobacco-related harm to health and wellbeing through direct or second-hand exposure is well established.

There is a growing body of evidence to show that smoke-free environments increase the rate of quitting and reduce the number of people taking up smoking. This is part of a broader commitment by all Queensland universities to provide healthier smoke-free environments.

At UQ, we are committed to providing staff, students and visitors with a productive, vibrant, safe and healthy working and learning environment. The decision to become smoke-free further supports and promotes the health and wellbeing of the entire UQ community.

We recognise that our staff, students and visitors have a personal choice to smoke; however, we also recognise the right for staff and students who do not smoke to work and study in an environment free from tobacco smoke.

Additionally, we also have a legal responsibility to provide a safe workplace and to protect staff, students and visitors from as many avoidable health risks as possible. This includes the health risks associated with exposure to second-hand smoke (passive smoking).

page_title: What information should I include and not include in my listing on UQ Rentals?
breadcrumb: What information should I include and not include in my listing on UQ Rentals?
text:
What information should I include and not include in my listing on UQ Rentals?

Your listing should provide clear and accurate information on:

- Type of property
- How many rooms the property has in total
- How many rooms in the property that are occupied or uninhabitable
- The rental price of
	- Each room
- The whole property
- Both
 
- Whether or not the property is furnished
- Any payments required prior to the tenancy commencing, including bond and advanced rent
- Other costs associated with the tenancy not included in the rent (e.g. cleaning, utilities, internet)
- The length of the lease
- The location of the property and details of local amenities, particularly public transport links.

You should also include photos to support your listing, noting:

- An external picture of the building
- Bedrooms and their furnishings
- Common areas and their furnishings (e.g. kitchen, bathrooms, living room)
- Any other areas of interest (e.g. laundry, outside social area, balconies)
- House rules if applicable

Listings should not contain:

- Information irrelevant to UQ students or staff
- Information about other universities
- Offers for homestays
- Offers for accommodation in exchange for work
- Any hyperlinks or other references to external content

page_title: How do I appeal a misconduct decision or penalty?
breadcrumb: How do I appeal a misconduct decision or penalty?
text:
How do I appeal a misconduct decision or penalty?

You can lodge an appeal through the link provided to you in the Notice of Decision letter. You have 28 days from the date of your notice of decision to lodge your appeal. This due date is stated in the Notice of Decision letter. Your appeal should describe the grounds of your appeal (e.g. the decision was materially affected by a failure to act with procedural fairness or otherwise comply with relevant procedures, the decision is materially affected by new evidence which was not necessarily available prior to the date of the decision, the decision imposed a penalty which was manifestly excessive.).

page_title: How do I prove my English language ability to my host university for my global experience?
breadcrumb: How do I prove my English language ability to my host university for my global experience?
text:
How do I prove my English language ability to my host university for my global experience?

Proof of English language proficiency is often required by host universities. When applying for a global experience, you'll be advised by the host university on what documents are required.

If you're from an English speaking background, you can usually show your passport or another recognised document to demonstrate your nationality of an English speaking country.

If you're from a non-English speaking background, you will mostly likely need to provide a recognised English language test score (e.g. IELTS or TOEFL score).

page_title: How do I prepare a study plan to go on exchange?
breadcrumb: How do I prepare a study plan to go on exchange?
text:
How do I prepare a study plan to go on exchange?

Details on how to prepare your application for exchange can be found on the Applying for exchange web page.


You should complete a self-progression check first, to understand what units you have remaining in your UQ program.


You can then use the resources provided on the apply page to step you through the study plan process.


When preparing your plan, you'll need the following details:

- 
	the credit you want to receive at UQ 
- 
	titles of the courses you wish to take at your host university (including the course codes if available) 
- 
	credit values of the courses at the host university (in credits/ECTS/hours) 
- 
	semester/term/quarter the courses are being offered in at the host university 
- 
	course profiles (if available online). 


While the number of individual courses you need to take will vary from university to university, you'll need to study an equivalent full-time study load at UQ (8 units a semester). To find the equivalent full-time study load at your host university, see the host university page.

page_title: What is the deadline to change my mailing address and/or my name before graduation?
breadcrumb: What is the deadline to change my mailing address and/or my name before graduation?
text:
What is the deadline to change my mailing address and/or my name before graduation?

The deadline to change your mailing address in mySI-net or apply for a name change (in relation to your graduation documents) is listed in the 'Graduations' tab in mySI-net (student login required).

Typically, the deadline falls one or two days after grades are released for the given semester, but you should check the exact date in mySI-net to make sure you have made your changes in time. Failure to make the changes prior to the due date will likely mean you will incur costs associated with replacing documents.

See my.UQ for more information on graduation.

page_title: How do I stop unwanted Learn.UQ (Blackboard) notifications?
breadcrumb: How do I stop unwanted Learn.UQ (Blackboard) notifications?
text:
How do I stop unwanted Learn.UQ (Blackboard) notifications?

- Go to Learn.UQ (Blackboard)
- Login with your UQ ID and password
- From the side menu, select your **name**to launch your*Profile*page.
- Hover your mouse over **Stream notifications**,**Email notifications**or**Push notifications**and click on the**Pencil**icon to edit your notification settings. The*Notification Settings*side panel will appear.- **Activity stream:**These are activities which appear in your- *Activity Stream*.
- **Email:**These are activities that you want to receive notifications for via email.
- **Push notifications:**These are activities that you want to appear as pop up notifications on your mobile device (if you have the Blackboard app for students installed).
 
- Select the relevant **radio button**to determine notification frequency (if available for that notification type).
- Check or uncheck the **checkbox**for each activity, depending on which activities you want to be notified about.
- Click on the **Save**button to save your changes.

Visit Learn.UQ Notification Settings for further information.

page_title: Who will hear my appeal against the outcome of a misconduct hearing?
breadcrumb: Who will hear my appeal against the outcome of a misconduct hearing?
text:
Who will hear my appeal against the outcome of a misconduct hearing?

Who will hear your appeal will depend on who heard your original case:

- If your original decision-maker was a Misconduct Committee, your appeal is heard by the University Misconduct Board.
- If your original decision-maker was the Misconduct Board, your appeal is heard by the University Misconduct Appeals Board.

You only have one appeal opportunity – there is no appeal process for appellate decisions.

page_title: How do I manually update my Apple device?
breadcrumb: How do I manually update my Apple device?
text:
How do I manually update my Apple device?

Follow the instructions below to manually update the software for your Apple device.

- Open Settings, then select General > Software Update.
- Select Update Now to install the available update.

#### macOS Tahoe (26), macOS Sequoia (15), macOS Sonoma (14) and macOS Ventura (13)

- Click on the Apple menu on the top left corner of your screen.
- Open System Settings.
- Select General, then Software Update.
- The updates available will be listed. Click Update Now to install all available updates.

#### macOS Monterey (12) and older

- Click on the Apple menu in top left corner of screen.
- Open System Preferences, then select Software Update.

Settings app > General > Software Update

Settings app > System > Software Updates and select Update Software

page_title: Does being a dental hygienist, dental therapist, dental technician or dental assistant increase my chances of gaining entry to the Bachelor of Dental Science (Honours)?
breadcrumb: Does being a dental hygienist, dental therapist, dental technician or dental assistant increase my chances of gaining entry to the Bachelor of Dental Science (Honours)?
text:
Does being a dental hygienist, dental therapist, dental technician or dental assistant increase my chances of gaining entry to the Bachelor of Dental Science (Honours)?

Unfortunately, there is no special entry or sub-quota for those who work in the dental industry. All applicants are required to meet all entry requirements and will be assessed in competition with all other applicants according to the selection procedures.

See the Bachelor of Dental Science (Honours) program page for information on entry requirements.

If you're a domestic applicant, see Applying for the Bachelor of Dental Science (Honours) for information on the selection process.

page_title: I’ve accepted my OS-HELP Loan offer and still haven’t received my payment. Is there anything else I need to do?
breadcrumb: I’ve accepted my OS-HELP Loan offer and still haven’t received my payment. Is there anything else I need to do?
text:
I’ve accepted my OS-HELP Loan offer and still haven’t received my payment. Is there anything else I need to do?

Payment of your OS-HELP Loan can only be made once you have completed your OS-HELP eCaf (available online through mySI-net), and entered your bank account details via the Scholarship Bank Details section under the Financial tab in mySi-Net.

You should also ensure that you have submitted your supporting documentation as listed in How to apply for an OS-HELP loan.

Please note if you are undertaking a Student Exchange or Short Term Global Experience through UQ Abroad, your loan cannot be paid until UQ Abroad have received your host acceptance and approved study plan, and you have submitted your travel details in StudentHub.

page_title: Can UQ Academic Title Holders access library resources?
breadcrumb: Can UQ Academic Title Holders access library resources?
text:
Can UQ Academic Title Holders access library resources?

Yes. If you apply for, and are granted, a UQ Academic Title you'll have staff access to our online and print resources and other benefits.

If you're a UQ Academic Title Holder, make sure you:

- log into the UQ Library using your UQ username and password to access online resources
- check your UQ email account (UQ login required) for requested documents/articles and reminders from UQ Library.

The UQ Academic Title Holders page has more information, including eligibility guidelines.

page_title: How can I find out if an event site is available on the day that I want it?
breadcrumb: How can I find out if an event site is available on the day that I want it?
text:
How can I find out if an event site is available on the day that I want it?

You'll need to complete an Event Application form (RTF, 312KB) and submit it to Security well in advance of the planned event. Security will then advise of availability.

For more information, see approvals for events.

You'll need to complete an Event Application form (RTF, 312KB) and submit it to Security well in advance of the planned event. Security will then advise of availability.

For more information, see approvals for events.

page_title: Why is exporting from Library Search into EndNote not working?
breadcrumb: Why is exporting from Library Search into EndNote not working?
text:
Why is exporting from Library Search into EndNote not working?

Web browsers such as Chrome and FireFox may block pop-ups and prevent you from exporting references from Library Search.

Check the URL bar of your browser for a **pop-up blocked notification**.You may like to adjust your settings to **always allow pop-ups **from Library Search.

For more help, visit:

page_title: Will going on exchange extend my degree?
breadcrumb: Will going on exchange extend my degree?
text:
Will going on exchange extend my degree?

Your UQ program should not be extended if you:

- study courses at the host university that have been pre-approved by your UQ faculty, and
- receive a passing grade for those courses (according to your host university's grading system).

If you study overseas in your final semester, it's likely that your graduation ceremony will be delayed by a semester.

page_title: Can I trust a My eQuals document?
breadcrumb: Can I trust a My eQuals document?
text:
Can I trust a My eQuals document?

Yes, the digital documents on the My eQuals platform are issued by The University of Queensland and are authentic certified copies. Employing advanced security and operational procedures, My eQuals documents are authentic, tamper-evident, and legally valid.

The underlying systems have been independently tested by security experts and documents contain digital signatures that meet the legal standards set by the European Union for authentic electronic documents. This standard exceeds requirements in Australia, New Zealand and the U.S.

There are specific features to look for to ensure authenticity of the documents and these can be viewed at My eQuals: what security features to look for.

page_title: As a Bachelor of Social Science student, when should I go on exchange?
breadcrumb: As a Bachelor of Social Science student, when should I go on exchange?
text:
As a Bachelor of Social Science student, when should I go on exchange?

If you are considering going on UQ Abroad exchange during your program, the second semester of your second year is the best time to go. The equivalent for dual degree students is the second semester of third year. Part of the reason for this is that the third year core courses (SOSC3201 in Semester 1 and SOSC3202 in Semester 2) must be taken consecutively and therefore take up the whole year. We have seen many students get a surprise when planning their final year of the program and suddenly realise that there is not much flexibility in year 3.

For further advice, contact the School of Social Science.

page_title: As an international student, can I work while studying?
breadcrumb: As an international student, can I work while studying?
text:
As an international student, can I work while studying?

As an international student, can I work while studying? Home Information and services See our Future Students website for advice on working in Australia.

page_title: What do I do if my current installation of SigmaPlot has expired?
breadcrumb: What do I do if my current installation of SigmaPlot has expired?
text:
What do I do if my current installation of SigmaPlot has expired?

You'll need to upgrade SigmaPlot to the newest version before trying to renew your licence.

If you're using a UQ computer, you can use the Company Portal to self-install the latest version of SigmaPlot.

If you're using your personal computer, you can download the latest version of SigmaPlot from the UQ Software Licensing website. 

Only full-time, paid UQ staff are allowed to use SigmaPlot on personal computers.

page_title: Can multi-modal students sit exams off-campus?
breadcrumb: Can multi-modal students sit exams off-campus?
text:
Can multi-modal students sit exams off-campus?

If you're enrolled in a mixture of internal and external courses, you must sit all of your end-of-semester exams at the campus of your internal enrolment.

See the my.UQ website for more information on off-campus examinations.

If you're enrolled in a mixture of internal and external courses, you must sit all of your end-of-semester exams at the campus of your internal enrolment.

See the my.UQ website for more information on off-campus examinations.

page_title: How do I save an existing plan to my Program Planner account?
breadcrumb: How do I save an existing plan to my Program Planner account?
text:
How do I save an existing plan to my Program Planner account?

If you created a plan in the Program Planner without logging in, you would have been directed to save the URL for your plan.

To save this plan to your Program Planner account:

- Log in to the Program Planner.
- On the ‘My Plans’ page, select the ‘Copy from existing plan’ button at the bottom of the page.
- Paste the URL of your saved plan into the 'Existing plan UUID' box and select the ‘Copy Template’ button.

A copy of your plan will now appear on your ‘My Plans’ page.

page_title: Can the Faculty of Business, Economics and Law (BEL) help me find a job?
breadcrumb: Can the Faculty of Business, Economics and Law (BEL) help me find a job?
text:
Can the Faculty of Business, Economics and Law (BEL) help me find a job?

The Student Employability Team (SET) is dedicated to assisting students from the BEL Faculty to increase their employability, access opportunities and manage their careers. BEL Student Employability Team's services are supplementary to the services offered by the UQ Student Employability Centre. Our transformational programs can give you the edge by helping you build your industry experience and develop lifelong employability skills.

Our team of employability specialists have extensive industry backgrounds and contacts. We can help you set your career goals and, throughout your degree, help you take the steps needed to reach these goals. For more information visit the BEL Employability website.

page_title: Can I defer my global experience once I've been offered a place?
breadcrumb: Can I defer my global experience once I've been offered a place?
text:
Can I defer my global experience once I've been offered a place?

Unfortunately, we aren't able to hold places for students who wish to defer their global experience.


If you change your mind and you no longer wish to go overseas during the period you applied for, you must withdraw your application and re-apply by the next relevant deadline.


For more information, see applying for exchange, applying for short-term study, or applying for an internship experience.

page_title: In what sequence should I take my language courses?
breadcrumb: In what sequence should I take my language courses?
text:
In what sequence should I take my language courses?

Search for your program on the Future Students website – you'll find information on the program structure under the 'Program details' tab.

The School of Languages and Cultures also publishes suggested pathway documents for each language – select a language and program level to view the available pathways.

page_title: Is postgraduate study required to become a Psychologist?
breadcrumb: Is postgraduate study required to become a Psychologist?
text:
Is postgraduate study required to become a Psychologist?

If you are contemplating a career as a registered psychologist, you are required to undertake a four year psychology degree plus two years of supervised practice. At UQ the options are:

- The three-year Bachelor of Arts (Psychology) plus one year Bachelor of Arts (Honours) in Psychology;
- The three-year Bachelor of Science(Psychology) plus one year Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Psychology; and
- The four-year Bachelor of Psychological Science (Honours).

To obtain full membership of the Australian Psychological Society, an approved coursework masters, professional doctorate or PhD degree in psychology is required.

For more information, see the School of Psychology website.

page_title: What do I do if I had technical issues when submitting my online examination by the submission due date and time?
breadcrumb: What do I do if I had technical issues when submitting my online examination by the submission due date and time?
text:
What do I do if I had technical issues when submitting my online examination by the submission due date and time?

If you find, due to technical issues you are unable to submit your online examination by the submission due date and time, but you were able to successfully submit your completed exam, albeit late, you may apply for exemption of the late penalties.

See my.UQ for more information on requesting exemption from late submission penalties for online exams.

page_title: I would like access to StudentHub but I am not a current student, how can I gain access?
breadcrumb: I would like access to StudentHub but I am not a current student, how can I gain access?
text:
I would like access to StudentHub but I am not a current student, how can I gain access?

Only UQ students who are currently enrolled in coursework are able to access StudentHub. Graduates can access StudentHub by resetting their password via a password reset token that you receive in an automated email upon your status being updated to 'graduate' or you can submit a request to reset your password providing your student number, email and mobile number. If you would like further information, please contact StudentHub.

page_title: What happens to my student IT access if I've taken a break or deferred my studies?
breadcrumb: What happens to my student IT access if I've taken a break or deferred my studies?
text:
What happens to my student IT access if I've taken a break or deferred my studies?

If you've deferred or interrupted your studies, you will temporarily lose UQ internet and access to your student H drive. 

Please download any files you wish to keep from your home directory to ensure you don't lose any of your files.

Your IT access will automatically be reinstated when you re-enrol.

page_title: What should I do if the book I want is not available at UQ Library?
breadcrumb: What should I do if the book I want is not available at UQ Library?
text:
What should I do if the book I want is not available at UQ Library?

You can request the item if we hold a copy, but it is on loan.

For items not held in the Library you can:

- request a document delivery (loan from another library)
- suggest that the Library purchase the book for the collection.

Contact the Faculty Services Librarians for assistance.

page_title: When selecting preferences in My Timetable, what does the percentage (%) next to each class mean?
breadcrumb: When selecting preferences in My Timetable, what does the percentage (%) next to each class mean?
text:
When selecting preferences in My Timetable, what does the percentage (%) next to each class mean?

When selecting preferences in My Timetable, what does the percentage (%) next to each class mean? Home Information and services The percentage shows how popular that class is. A higher percentage means more students are selecting that class as a preference.

page_title: As an international student at UQ, can I return to my country of citizenship for my short-term global experience?
breadcrumb: As an international student at UQ, can I return to my country of citizenship for my short-term global experience?
text:
As an international student at UQ, can I return to my country of citizenship for my short-term global experience?

Students studying at UQ on a temporary residency visa (e.g an international student visa or provisional Business Innovation and Investment visa (subclass 188)) are not permitted to participate in a semester-based exchange or short-term study experience in their country of passport.


However, you are permitted to return to your country of passport to undertake an internship.


For more information, see the relevant pages above, or email iget@uq.edu.au.

page_title: What do I do if my EXCH enrolment is allocated to my main degree on mySI-net and not my Diploma of Languages?
breadcrumb: What do I do if my EXCH enrolment is allocated to my main degree on mySI-net and not my Diploma of Languages?
text:
What do I do if my EXCH enrolment is allocated to my main degree on mySI-net and not my Diploma of Languages?

It's not a problem if the EXCH codes are assigned to your main degree on mySI-net for your exchange, rather than your Diploma of Languages. Your UQ faculty can allocate credit to the Diploma after the exchange has finished, once an official host university academic transcript is provided to them.


Credit will be allocated to your UQ program according to the courses studied at the host university and what they were pre-approved as on your study plan.

page_title: What scholarships are available to international students who want to go on a global experience?
breadcrumb: What scholarships are available to international students who want to go on a global experience?
text:
What scholarships are available to international students who want to go on a global experience?

If you're accepted into a semester-based exchange or a short-term global experience, you may be considered for funding opportunities if you meet the eligibility criteria. For more information, see financial support.


International students at UQ aren't eligible to receive Australian government grants – only Australian citizens or permanent residents are eligible for this funding.


You may be eligible to apply for scholarships provided by your faculty. Contact your faculty for more information.

page_title: What upcoming workshops are available through Student Services?
breadcrumb: What upcoming workshops are available through Student Services?
text:
What upcoming workshops are available through Student Services?

What upcoming workshops are available through Student Services? Home Information and services See our Student Services website for a list of upcoming workshops.

page_title: The antivirus software on my UQ computer is crashing an app or blocking my access to a site. What can I do?
breadcrumb: The antivirus software on my UQ computer is crashing an app or blocking my access to a site. What can I do?
text:
The antivirus software on my UQ computer is crashing an app or blocking my access to a site. What can I do?

The antivirus software on my UQ computer is crashing an app or blocking my access to a site. What can I do? Home Information and services ITS will need to investigate this issue. Submit an IT request for assistance.

page_title: How do I use Duo MFA inside labs?
breadcrumb: How do I use Duo MFA inside labs?
text:
How do I use Duo MFA inside labs?

MFA on networked computers and devices in most lab environments behaves exactly as it does outside the lab; when accessing UQ websites and services that use UQ Authenticate, you can expect to sometimes receive MFA prompts to confirm your identity.

 

There are some additional considerations for MFA authentication inside labs where mobile phones cannot be used.

There are some additional considerations for MFA authentication inside labs where mobile phones cannot be used.

If you are unsure about the MFA requirements in a particular lab, please confirm with the local lab manager.  

 

Note that many labs contain computers which are not connected to the UQ network or only require access to limited resources (e.g. lab instruments). Devices such as these are unlikely to prompt you to MFA.

 

Ask your local lab manager, or submit an IT request if you require further assistance with MFA in your lab.

          Note that many labs contain computers which are not connected to the UQ network or only require access to limited resources (e.g. lab instruments). Devices such as these are unlikely to prompt you to MFA.

Ask your local lab manager, or submit an IT request if you require further assistance with MFA in your lab.

page_title: I have a UQ scholarship managed via Workday. Does this give me staff access to the library?
breadcrumb: I have a UQ scholarship managed via Workday. Does this give me staff access to the library?
text:
I have a UQ scholarship managed via Workday. Does this give me staff access to the library?

No, your Workday record is simply the system used to enable payment of the scholarship. You are not entitled to staff access to the library.

You may have access to the library through your studies. Visit Borrowing rules and charges for the borrowing conditions for different library membership types.

page_title: How can I get more involved with EECS and UQ?
breadcrumb: How can I get more involved with EECS and UQ?
text:
How can I get more involved with EECS and UQ?

As a School of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (EECS) student, there's a range of opportunities for you to get involved with EECS and the broader UQ community:

- If you'd like to represent the student body and provide constructive feedback about EECS and its programs, you can nominate yourself as a member of the Student Consultative Committee.
- If you're an engineering student, you can become an EAIT Student Ambassador.
- If you're an undergraduate or postgraduate student, you may find employment as an EECS tutor. To become a tutor, you'll generally require an enthusiasm for teaching, good English communication skills and an excellent academic record.
- You are also encouraged to get involved in student and professional societies.

page_title: What should I expect when ending a tenancy?
breadcrumb: What should I expect when ending a tenancy?
text:
What should I expect when ending a tenancy?

The Rental Tenancy Authority has a comprehensive list for students of what to expect when Ending a Tenancy including:


- How to end an agreement
- Notice periods
- Tips for moving out
- Bond refunds and so on.

For more information:

- download the Pocket Guide for Tenants and refer to the RTA’s Ending a Tenancy
- Contact the Accommodation Team at accommodation@uq.edu.au or on 1300 275 870.

page_title: Where is UQ International located?
breadcrumb: Where is UQ International located?
text:
Where is UQ International located?

Where is UQ International located? Home Information and services The offices of UQ International are located on level 2, JD Story Building (Building 61) at the St Lucia campus.

page_title: My name is wrong on mySI-net. How do I fix this?
breadcrumb: My name is wrong on mySI-net. How do I fix this?
text:
My name is wrong on mySI-net. How do I fix this?

My name is wrong on mySI-net. How do I fix this? Home Information and services See my.UQ for information on updating your personal details.

page_title: Why do I have two 'Student Account' tiles showing balances on the mySI-net home page?
breadcrumb: Why do I have two 'Student Account' tiles showing balances on the mySI-net home page?
text:
Why do I have two 'Student Account' tiles showing balances on the mySI-net home page?

UQ-Ochsner students pay fees in both USD and AUD, so there are now two accounts to display charges and payments in each currency.

You'll see a summary of your USD and AUD accounts on your mySI-net home page, each in its own 'Student Account' tile. The tiles will show if there are any amounts owing or in credit.

You can download separate invoices for your USD and AUD fees by selecting mySI-net's 'Financials' tab, then 'Invoices' from the menu. The invoices will show statements of payments received, and course charges and payment summaries.

page_title: How do I remove my UQ account in the Duo Mobile app?
breadcrumb: How do I remove my UQ account in the Duo Mobile app?
text:
How do I remove my UQ account in the Duo Mobile app?

How do I remove my UQ account in the Duo Mobile app? Home Information and services To remove your UQ account in the Duo Mobile app, follow the instructions on the Duo Knowledge Base.

page_title: Where at the library can I access NVivo?
breadcrumb: Where at the library can I access NVivo?
text:
Where at the library can I access NVivo?

NVivo is software used to organise and analyse text and data. You can access NVivo on library computers through the Zenworks application.

To open software in Zenworks, log in with your UQ username and password then:

- Open the Zenworks application
- Locate the software by selecting a folder
- Double click the software title to open it.

Further information on access and training is available on the Library website.

page_title: My course reading list is not appearing in the Learn.UQ (Blackboard) course site.
breadcrumb: My course reading list is not appearing in the Learn.UQ (Blackboard) course site.
text:
My course reading list is not appearing in the Learn.UQ (Blackboard) course site.

The course reading list link on your course site launches an LTI (Learning Tools Interoperability) integration tool. The integration only works with published lists.

- Check that your list has been published. Unpublished lists will not appear in Learn.UQ.
- If your list is published but still not displaying, remove the existing link and add the course reading list link again in your course site.

page_title: Can UQ eSpace update my ORCID publications automatically?
breadcrumb: Can UQ eSpace update my ORCID publications automatically?
text:
Can UQ eSpace update my ORCID publications automatically?

From your UQ eSpace Dashboard you can set up a weekly sync of your publications from UQ eSpace to your ORCID.

- In your Dashboard, click the settings icon next to the ORCID logo.
- Turn on the option to enable weekly automatic weekly upload of your works to ORCID.

After the first sync, check your ORCID for any duplicate entries.

page_title: Can I get a job tutoring with EECS?
breadcrumb: Can I get a job tutoring with EECS?
text:
Can I get a job tutoring with EECS?

Undergraduate and postgraduate students can be employed as tutors in the School of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (EECS) courses. For more information, see EECS tutoring.

Undergraduate and postgraduate students can be employed as tutors in the School of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (EECS) courses. For more information, see EECS tutoring.

page_title: What health and medical services are available at UQ?
breadcrumb: What health and medical services are available at UQ?
text:
What health and medical services are available at UQ?

See my.UQ for a list of available health and welfare services.

You can also search UQ Maps for available services on your campus.

See my.UQ for a list of available health and welfare services.

You can also search UQ Maps for available services on your campus.

page_title: What are alumni?
breadcrumb: What are alumni?
text:
What are alumni?

Alumni are UQ graduates and former students. For information on events and being involved with UQ as an alumnus, see Alumni & Community.

Alumni are UQ graduates and former students. For information on events and being involved with UQ as an alumnus, see Alumni & Community.

page_title: Why has the due date on my loan changed?
breadcrumb: Why has the due date on my loan changed?
text:
Why has the due date on my loan changed?

Your loan due date has changed because another client has placed a request to borrow the item. This means your loan has been recalled to the library.

Return the item by the new due date to avoid replacement charges.

Visit your Library Account (login required) to check the due dates for your loans.

page_title: Where can I learn the language of the country I am going on exchange to?
breadcrumb: Where can I learn the language of the country I am going on exchange to?
text:
Where can I learn the language of the country I am going on exchange to?

The Institute of Modern Languages (IML) at St Lucia offers language courses in more than 30 languages. IML courses may help you to prepare for your exchange and are open to everyone. You don't need a degree or previous knowledge of the language prior to enrolling.

page_title: If I plan to travel overseas, where can I learn the basics of a foreign language?
breadcrumb: If I plan to travel overseas, where can I learn the basics of a foreign language?
text:
If I plan to travel overseas, where can I learn the basics of a foreign language?

The Institute of Modern Languages (IML) offers personal language tuitions as well as 8 week short courses for beginners in a range of languages at UQ St Lucia. For more information, see IML.

The Institute of Modern Languages (IML) offers personal language tuitions as well as 8 week short courses for beginners in a range of languages at UQ St Lucia. For more information, see IML.

page_title: What if I can't enrol in my pre-approved courses at my exchange host university?
breadcrumb: What if I can't enrol in my pre-approved courses at my exchange host university?
text:
What if I can't enrol in my pre-approved courses at my exchange host university?

It's quite common for students to have difficulties getting into the host university courses they've had approved for their exchange due to:

- timetable clashes
- limited places
- a change in the language of instruction.

If you experience any difficulties with enrolment, follow our advice on how to handle problems with your courses.

page_title: How do I create an account as a new provider on UQ Rentals?
breadcrumb: How do I create an account as a new provider on UQ Rentals?
text:
How do I create an account as a new provider on UQ Rentals?

To register for UQ Rentals as a Provider:

- Navigate to the UQ Rentals home page at rental.uq.edu.au

- Click on the "List your property with us - " link at the top-right corner of screen

- Click on the "First time providers" button

- Review the regarding listing rates information

- Click on the "Click here to go to the Control Site to register" button

- Review and accept the Terms and Conditions and Privacy policies

- Enter in a valid email and password combination

- Enter in your personal details

- Answer all other prompts

Congratulations! Your account is now registered. Now you are ready to list your property and pay. 

If you do require assistance, please contact the Accommodation Team – accommodation@uq.edu.au or 1300 275 870.

page_title: How long are Zoom Cloud recordings retained for?
breadcrumb: How long are Zoom Cloud recordings retained for? > Staff
text:
How long are Zoom Cloud recordings retained for? > Staff

Zoom Cloud recordings made by UQ Staff are automatically moved to Zoom Cloud Storage Trash after a period of one year (365 days).

Zoom Cloud recordings made by UQ Staff are automatically moved to Zoom Cloud Storage Trash after a period of 30 days.

page_title: What does IBSC stand for?
breadcrumb: What does IBSC stand for?
text:
What does IBSC stand for?

What does IBSC stand for? Home Information and services At UQ, IBSC stands for Institutional Biosafety Sub-Committee.

page_title: Can I do a progression check on my Bachelor of International Studies degree?
breadcrumb: Can I do a progression check on my Bachelor of International Studies degree?
text:
Can I do a progression check on my Bachelor of International Studies degree?

If you're preparing for graduation, or you just want to ensure that you are progressing correctly through your program, you can complete a progression check.

To ensure that students in the Bachelor of International Studies program are familiar with their program rules and requirements, we ask that all students complete a self-progression check. Then, if you still have questions or concerns about your outstanding program requirements, the Faculty can review your progression check and provide advice.

page_title: When trying to pay my fees through mySI-net a 'Popup blocked' message is displayed. What can I do to stop this?
breadcrumb: When trying to pay my fees through mySI-net a 'Popup blocked' message is displayed. What can I do to stop this?
text:
When trying to pay my fees through mySI-net a 'Popup blocked' message is displayed. What can I do to stop this?

This issue appears to affect iPhone users using the Safari browser with pop-ups blocked.

To enable pop-ups on your device:

- Select 'Settings'.
- Click 'Safari'.
- Under the 'General' section, tap the toggle next to 'Block Pop-ups'. The toggle bar will turn white to show pop-ups have been enabled.

You should now be able to pay your fees.

If you're still having problems, try paying your fees using a PC or another web-enabled device.

page_title: I have a disability or health condition that may impact my ability to sit an exam in standard exam conditions, what can I do?
breadcrumb: I have a disability or health condition that may impact my ability to sit an exam in standard exam conditions, what can I do?
text:
I have a disability or health condition that may impact my ability to sit an exam in standard exam conditions, what can I do?

You are eligible for alternative examination arrangements if you have a disability, impairment, injury or condition that needs to be taken into consideration during an exam.

These disabilities or conditions may be permanent or temporary, and might include mobility, sensory, learning, physical, mental health, medical or other conditions.

See my.UQ for information on alternative exam arrangements and how to apply.

page_title: What does RSPP stand for?
breadcrumb: What does RSPP stand for?
text:
What does RSPP stand for?

What does RSPP stand for? Home Information and services At UQ, RSPP stands for Radiation Safety and Protection Plan.

page_title: Do I need to notify UQ if I have obtained my permanent residency visa?
breadcrumb: Do I need to notify UQ if I have obtained my permanent residency visa?
text:
Do I need to notify UQ if I have obtained my permanent residency visa?

If you are a current student, you must change your enrolment from international to domestic. See our my.UQ website for advice on changes to residency, visa or citizenship status.

If you are a current student, you must change your enrolment from international to domestic. See our my.UQ website for advice on changes to residency, visa or citizenship status.

page_title: As an international student, do I need to tell UQ if my address changes?
breadcrumb: As an international student, do I need to tell UQ if my address changes?
text:
As an international student, do I need to tell UQ if my address changes?

Yes. You should update your details in mySI-net within 7 days if you move from one residence to another – this is often a visa condition. Use the 'Personal Details' tile in mySI-net to update your address details.

Yes. You should update your details in mySI-net within 7 days if you move from one residence to another – this is often a visa condition. Use the 'Personal Details' tile in mySI-net to update your address details.

page_title: What should I do if I receive an unexpected Duo login request?
breadcrumb: What should I do if I receive an unexpected Duo login request?
text:
What should I do if I receive an unexpected Duo login request?

If you do not expect or recognise the login request:

- Select 'Deny' or 'I'm not logging in' on the push notification
- Select 'Yes' to mark it as a suspicious login.

You should immediately change your UQ account password and submit an IT request to report the incident.

Push phishing can occur when an actor steals someone's UQ username and password, uses them to log in and hopes that the user will simply approve the push out of habit, allowing the actor through.

Be mindful when approving push notifications. Consider the following when you receive a Duo push:

- Is there anything unusual about the resource or application you are accessing?
- Did you just attempt to access an application?
- Where is the push request coming from? Check the location details provided with the push in the Duo Mobile app.
- Did an application session just end, or are you being prompted to re-login to an account?

page_title: How do I navigate the Library website with a keyboard?
breadcrumb: How do I navigate the Library website with a keyboard?
text:
How do I navigate the Library website with a keyboard?

Our site aims to comply with WCAG 2.1 to Level AA (Web Accessibility Guidelines) to ensure access for clients with disabilities.

To navigate through the Library website with a keyboard, use the **Tab** key to navigate between major page elements and sections.

To navigate our site menu via the keyboard:

- **Tab**into the first site menu item ('UQ Home'). Use the Tab key to navigate down the menu and- **Shift + Tab**to move up
- Use the **Enter**key to open menu items.

You may need to adjust your operating system settings in some cases, e.g. if using Firefox for OS X you may need to set Full Keyboard Access to 'All controls' within the keyboard shortcut settings.

In general we recommend that if you're using Apple OS, you use Safari for the best accessibility experience.

page_title: Do I have to speak a foreign language to go on a global experience in a non-English speaking country?

text:
Do I have to speak a foreign language to go on a global experience in a non-English speaking country?

Not necessarily. Many universities in non-English speaking countries offer courses taught in both English and the native language of that country.

If you want to study on exchange or a short-term global experience, at a university which doesn't teach in English, you'll need to demonstrate that you're proficient in the language of instruction before your application can be approved.

page_title: Will I gain practical experience teaching while studying Education?
breadcrumb: Will I gain practical experience teaching while studying Education?
text:
Will I gain practical experience teaching while studying Education?

Yes, you will. UQ's teacher-preparation programs are built around extensive practical experiences in operating schools. You'll work with real students and be supervised by experienced teachers.

The practicum blocks are vital to your development as a teaching professional, and are designed to link directly with your on-campus experiences. You'll be offered a variety of practicum experiences, ranging from one day per week to a six-week full-time block. This immersion in a school setting will help you transition successfully from being a pre-service teacher to a qualified educator.

Unfortunately if the event is showing as fully booked, this means another student on the waitlist has booked the spot before you.

page_title: Troubleshooting common problems with the touch panel audiovisual system
breadcrumb: Troubleshooting common problems with the touch panel audiovisual system > The touch panel is showing 'No signal detected'
h2: The touch panel is showing 'No signal detected'
text:
Troubleshooting common problems with the touch panel audiovisual system > The touch panel is showing 'No signal detected'

Make sure you have selected the correct input source.

If you're using the room's desktop PC, make sure it is turned on and awake.

If you're using your own device (e.g. a laptop), make sure your display settings are correct. Follow the instructions for checking your display settings in the 'Connecting your own device' section of the system user guide.

Check that volume is turned up and not muted on:

- your laptop or the room's desktop PC
- the AV system
- the software you're using to play sound.

Make sure you've selected the correct audio playback option on the desktop PC or your device. Follow the instructions for selecting audio playback options in the 'Audio and volume controls' section of the system user guide.

Make sure:

- the microphone is set to the correct channel and frequency for the room. Check the radio microphone channels and frequencies used at UQ
- the switch at the top of the bodypack is set to 'MIC'
- the microphone is turned up
- the microphone is not muted on the touch panel.

page_title: I am dealing with an emergency. What do I do?
breadcrumb: I am dealing with an emergency. What do I do?
text:
I am dealing with an emergency. What do I do?

Triple zero (000) is Australia’s main emergency service number. You should call 000 if you need urgent help from policy, fire or ambulance services, You should only call 000 when:

- Someone is seriously injured or in need of urgent medical help
- Your life or property is being threatened.
- You have just witnessed a serious accident or crime,

If a crime is happening now, an offender is in the area, a life is threatened by crime, sickness or injury, or there is a fire please call the Australian emergency number Triple Zero – 000. This is a 24 hour, toll-free number.

For non-urgent police matters, contact:  Policelink – 13 1444

If you or someone else needs mental health support immediately, please call:

Lifeline - 13 11 14

**The UQ Counselling and Crisis Line - **1300 851 998

**Rachel Panousis**

page_title: How do I use Office 365 sensitivity labels if I'm a student and a staff member?
breadcrumb: How do I use Office 365 sensitivity labels if I'm a student and a staff member?
text:
How do I use Office 365 sensitivity labels if I'm a student and a staff member?

Office 365 sensitivity labels are not enabled for students.

When you're composing or accessing documents and sending emails related to your work as a staff member, it's recommended you switch to your Office 365 staff account.

To switch between Office 365 accounts, follow the instructions on the Microsoft website.

This is important to maintain the key safety objectives of Office 365 sensitivity labels, securing data with the appropriate labelling and encryption, for both the benefit of your work and your colleagues and students.

Find out more about how to use Office 365 sensitivity labels at UQ. If you're using a personal computer, make sure you install and sign into the AIP Client.

breadcrumb: Can I do a progression check on my Bachelor of Journalism degree?
text:
Can I do a progression check on my Bachelor of Journalism degree?

If you are preparing for graduation, or you just want to ensure that you are progressing correctly through your program, you can complete a progression check.

To ensure that students in the Bachelor of Journalism program are familiar with their program rules and requirements, we ask that all students complete a self-progression check. Then, if you still have questions or concerns about your outstanding program requirements, the Faculty can review your progression check and provide advice.

For students who started pre-2017, you may wish to review the information and program planners on the program restructure.

page_title: Am I travelling to a safe country? Can I travel to a Level 3 or 4 destination as rated by smartraveller.gov.au?
breadcrumb: Am I travelling to a safe country? Can I travel to a Level 3 or 4 destination as rated by smartraveller.gov.au?
text:
Am I travelling to a safe country? Can I travel to a Level 3 or 4 destination as rated by smartraveller.gov.au?

Check the security situation for your destination/s (both for approved University travel and any private travel in conjunction with your approved University travel) on https://smartraveller.gov.au.

UQ approved travellers have access to International SOS. This service provides travellers with security and medical advice and provides travel-related emergency and routine assistance while travelling. For up-to-date alerts and assistance while in country, it is recommended you download the International SOS mobile app or visit the International SOS UQ travel safety portal.

As travel to level 3 or level 4 countries are very high risk, approved private days to these countries should be kept to a minimum. You must comply with the specific restricted area approval requirements that apply from the UQ Travel Policy and Procedures. You must also separately notify Insurance Services if you are travelling to Afghanistan, Chad, Chechnya, Cote d'Ivoire (Ivory Coast), Cuba, Democratic Republic of Congo, Iran, Iraq, Israel, Libya, Myanmar, Somalia, South Sudan, Sudan, Syria or Yemen so that the University's insurer can be notified.

page_title: How do I improve my SECaT/ SETutor survey response rates and evaluation results?
breadcrumb: How do I improve my SECaT/ SETutor survey response rates and evaluation results?
text:
How do I improve my SECaT/ SETutor survey response rates and evaluation results?

- Listen to how other UQ teaching staff improve their SECaT response rate
- Listen to how other UQ teaching staff encourage constructive student feedback

It's good practice to encourage your students to participate by doing the following before and during the evaluation period:

- Give notice that online evaluation will begin soon (e.g. inform students to check their student email accounts on the week that evaluations open, and the ‘Have Your Say’ module on the welcome page of Blackboard).
- Tell students why evaluations are conducted and what they're used for. You can use the promotion slide with QR code (PPTX, 322.4 KB).
- Set aside class time for students to use their own device to complete the evaluation. Academics that use this approach have been found to achieve higher response rates than those who don't set aside class time.

page_title: How do I publish a recording from my Echo360 Personal Library to a Blackboard course?
breadcrumb: How do I publish a recording from my Echo360 Personal Library to a Blackboard course?
text:
How do I publish a recording from my Echo360 Personal Library to a Blackboard course?

- Open the course Blackboard site.
- From the Course Menu, select 'Learning Resources'.
- Select 'Lecture_Recordings' to launch Echo360.
- Click the 'New Class' button.
 Note: 'Class' refers to an individual lecture recording (e.g. 8 September 2017, 4pm - 6pm).
 
 
 
- Enter a title, start date, start time and duration for the class and click 'OK'.
 
 
 
- The class will then appear in your Class List. Click on the blue + button, then select 'Add video' from the drop down list.
- Click 'Import from your library'.
- Locate the video, then click 'Done'.
- The media icon will appear green, indicating that the file has been successfully attached to your class.
 
 
 
- The recording can now be viewed from the Class List by students.

page_title: What are the EECS 'special topic' courses?
breadcrumb: What are the EECS 'special topic' courses?
text:
What are the EECS 'special topic' courses?

The Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (EECS) special topic courses are typically research projects where you – and possibly a small group of other students – are directly supervised by an academic.

These courses are not routinely offered, and they rely on you identifying an academic that you want to work with and who is willing to supervise you in this task. Subject to approval of the Head of School, enrolment is generally only approved for students with a high GPA.

For more information, see ITEE's special topic courses.

page_title: Can I work while on exchange?
breadcrumb: Can I work while on exchange?
text:
Can I work while on exchange?

Work rights for international students vary from country to country and also vary depending on your visa/residency status.

In some countries, you may be able to work for a limited number of hours, but on-campus only. In other countries, you can work part-time off-campus, and in some countries you cannot work at all.

For more information on the work rights of your student visa, contact the consulate or embassy of your host country.

page_title: Can I go on exchange to another Australian university?
breadcrumb: Can I go on exchange to another Australian university?
text:
Can I go on exchange to another Australian university?

Exchange through the Internships and Global Experience team can only be taken at one of our partner institutes overseas. If you want to study at another Australian university during your UQ degree, see our advice on 

          __cross-institutional study__.

page_title: Why do I need to report a suspicious email?
breadcrumb: Why do I need to report a suspicious email?
text:
Why do I need to report a suspicious email?

By reporting suspicious emails, you are helping protect yourself and UQ from cyber threats. Since the email has evaded the automated systems UQ uses for protection, you are the last line of defence.

If you have received the email, it is highly likely that others have too, but they may not have identified it as suspicious. Your report could contribute to stopping a significant attack against the University and its people. The UQ Cyber Security Operations Centre would prefer multiple reports of the same email, rather than none.

page_title: Will my personal device be removed from UQ's device management platform?
breadcrumb: Will my personal device be removed from UQ's device management platform?
text:
Will my personal device be removed from UQ's device management platform?

UQ plans to remove existing personal devices from UQ's device management platform to eliminate the risk of unintentionally distributing changes not intended for personally owned devices. If your personal device has been enrolled in UQ's device management platform, you will recieve an email notification to advise the planned date of removal.

When signing into Office 365 on your personal device, a checkbox is enabled by default which granted UQ permission to manage your device. This was unintentional and is not desired by UQ. UQ has now disabled the ability for users to enrol their personal devices in its device management platform.

The option is enabled by default on all Windows 10 and Windows 11 devices:

This will have no impact to your personal data or the configuration of your device.

The expected behaviour of removing your computer from UQ's device management platform is as follows:

- You may receive a notification stating that your workplace account and associated data has been removed from your device:
 
 
- When you open an Office 365 product, such as Outlook, you may be prompted to sign in to your UQ account again. During the sign-in process, please ensure that you deselect the "Allow my organization to manage my device" checkbox.
 
 
 Please note, if you do not deselect this checkbox, your device will not be managed by UQ. You will instead receive an error message stating that the MDM server does not support this platform or version. This error can be safely ignored.

page_title: Why are some of my grades missing on my AHEGS?
breadcrumb: Why are some of my grades missing on my AHEGS?
text:
Why are some of my grades missing on my AHEGS?

The Australian Higher Education Graduation Statement (AHEGS) displays courses and results for a specific degree. Results for courses that were studied in a previous UQ program, or at a different university will not appear on the AHEGS – except in cases where specific credit is transferred from another UQ program. The reader will be directed to the appropriate academic transcript for any results not included on the AHEGS.
