page_title: What is the difference between the project and the thesis subjects in the Master of Communication programs?
breadcrumb: What is the difference between the project and the thesis subjects in the Master of Communication programs?
text:
What is the difference between the project and the thesis subjects in the Master of Communication programs?

Students must complete either a thesis (COMU7002) or a project (COMU7009) to earn a Master of Communication.

The thesis involves 6 units of study and as such is a substantial amount of work. It requires candidates to research and write an original thesis on a topic in communication relevant to their field of study.

The project involves 2 units of study. It requires candidates to design and conduct a small communication research project, critically evaluate results, and produce a professionally written report.

page_title: What can the Library Chatbot do? How can it help me?
breadcrumb: What can the Library Chatbot do? How can it help me?
text:
What can the Library Chatbot do? How can it help me?

Use the Library Chatbot (available on the Library website) to get a quick answer to your library or student IT question including when the AskUs team are unavailable.

The Chatbot uses natural language processing to determine the intent of your question.

It provides a summarised answer and links to further information provided on a range of UQ websites including:

- the Library website and Library guides
- Open Textbooks at UQ
- UQ Frequently Asked Questions
- some additional UQ websites that cover topics frequently asked at the Library.

If the Bot can't determine an appropriate answer, it will suggest that you contact Library staff for support.

We encourage you to experiment when asking the Chatbot questions to see what works for you.

When the answers are not quite right:

- short questions may provide better results than long complicated ones
- you can adjust your question using descriptive phrases and keywords.

The Bot may not have access to the correct website to answer your question. When this happens, you can contact AskUs for further support.

Visit Need help? Ask the Library Chatbot for more about the Library Chatbot.

page_title: What should I do if a computer in a computer lab isn't working?
breadcrumb: What should I do if a computer in a computer lab isn't working?
text:
What should I do if a computer in a computer lab isn't working?

If a lab computer isn't working, first try logging in to a different computer to check the problem is with the computer and not your account.

Once you've worked out what the problem is, submit an IT request for assistance.

page_title: Why do I have a Zoom basic account? Why are my meetings limited to 40 minutes?
breadcrumb: Why do I have a Zoom basic account? Why are my meetings limited to 40 minutes?
text:
Why do I have a Zoom basic account? Why are my meetings limited to 40 minutes?

To manage UQs Zoom licences, a regular clean-up of licences is undertaken to allow new staff and students to have a licensed Zoom account. Accounts that haven’t been signed into for an extended period are changed to a Zoom basic account.

Staff and students are automatically given the correct licence type when they sign into the Zoom client with single sign-on (SSO) or sign into the UQ Zoom web interface.

In some cases, you may need to force your Zoom account to sign out of all devices:

- Sign into the UQ Zoom web interface.
- Scroll down to the sign-in section.
- Select ‘Sign Me Out From All Devices’ and confirm.
- Sign back into the Zoom client with single sign-on (SSO) or sign into the UQ Zoom web interface.

page_title: Zoom cloud recordings older than 365 days will be automatically removed. What do I need to do?
breadcrumb: Zoom cloud recordings older than 365 days will be automatically removed. What do I need to do?
text:
Zoom cloud recordings older than 365 days will be automatically removed. What do I need to do?

From Monday 10 July 2023, all Zoom recordings older than 365 days will be automatically removed from the Cloud, as UQ has reached its storage limit within the Zoom platform.

Most teaching staff will have had their Zoom recordings automatically published to Echo360 since Semester 1, 2022. For advice on using Echo360 recordings as part of a learning journey, please refer to the Learning Journeys guide. You can also follow the guide to set up publishing of your Zoom recordings with Echo360.

If you have Zoom recordings for teaching purposes that are not available to students via Echo360, (for example, the recordings are directly linked from within your Learn.UQ [Blackboard] course to Zoom) and you want to continue sharing them, make sure to download your recording when the meeting is complete.

You can then upload the recording to either Echo360 or Kaltura Media or publish the downloaded recordings directly to your Learn.UQ (Blackboard) course. For advice on which option is best for your needs, please book a consultation with an eLearning Adviser.

If you have Zoom recordings that are not for teaching purposes (for example staff meetings) that you wish to keep or share, you will need to download and save your recording. If you then need to share that recording with others, please do so using OneDrive.

page_title: I am interested in completing a thesis as part of my School of Economics postgraduate coursework masters degree. What do I need to do?
breadcrumb: I am interested in completing a thesis as part of my School of Economics postgraduate coursework masters degree. What do I need to do?
text:
I am interested in completing a thesis as part of my School of Economics postgraduate coursework masters degree. What do I need to do?

If you're thinking about completing a thesis, it's essential that you plan your program in advance. You must account for the scheduling of compulsory Part A courses in your plan of study.

**Please note:**

- You cannot study ECON7950 and complete a thesis at the same time.
- You must have completed ECON7950 and one of ECON7310, ECON7350 or ECON7360 before commencing a thesis.

Your thesis will need to be supervised by an Advisor, who is usually an academic staff member of the School of Economics. You must make arrangements with an Advisor who agrees to work with you.

You must also determine the research area you're interested in. Ideally, your topic will align with your Advisor's academic interests – learn more about individual staff and their research interests.

To complete a thesis, you will conduct independent study and be required to produce a 20,000-word document of a high academic standard through in-depth research.

Thesis courses seek to encourage critical thinking and informed judgement. Your thesis topic must be an original one which can contribute to economic knowledge. A thesis normally uses empirical methods such as data analysis or econometrics.

Students will need to confirm their topics and Advisor details with the Course Coordinator for their thesis course (ECON7930, ECON7931 or ECON7932). Further details are available in the course's electronic course profile (ECP).

There are three different courses which allow you to complete a thesis. You only need to enrol in one of these courses:

- ECON7930 is the course you enrol in to complete a thesis in a single semester,
- ECON7931 starts in Semester 1 and continues into Semester 2, and
- ECON7932 starts in Semester 2 and continues into Semester 1 of the following year.

Students enrolling in ECON7931 or ECON7932 must use that same course code for both semesters of their thesis subject.

You may be able to enrol in ECON7930 and complete a thesis course over Summer Semester if an academic staff member is available and agrees to be your Advisor. As not all academic staff are available over Summer Semester, we strongly recommended that you **make arrangements with an Advisor well before Summer Semester commences**.

Once an academic staff member has confirmed that they will be your supervisor over the Summer Semester, email the School of Economics at econ.pgrad@uq.edu.au. They will place you on a permission list, and you'll receive an email letting you know that you are able to enrol in ECON7930.

page_title: How do I change my Zoom waiting room settings?
breadcrumb: How do I change my Zoom waiting room settings?
text:
How do I change my Zoom waiting room settings?

By default, non-UQ participants are initially placed in the waiting room. The meeting host is required to admit people into the meeting before they can participate.

In some cases, meeting hosts may require all participants (including UQ accounts) to be placed in the waiting room, particularly in some situations such as online clinical scenarios, much like a physical waiting room until the previous consultation is complete. This setting will apply to all your Zoom meetings.

- Open the UQ Zoom web interface and sign in with your UQ username and password.
- Select 'Settings', then under 'Waiting Room Options', select 'Edit Options'.
 
 
 
 
- Select 'Everyone', then select 'Continue'.

You can also allow anyone joining from a UQ account to admit participants from the waiting room into the Zoom meeting when a host isn't present. This setting will apply to all your Zoom meetings.

- Open the UQ Zoom web interface and sign in with your UQ username and password.
- Select 'Settings', then under 'Waiting Room Options', select 'Edit Options'.
- Select 'Host, co-hosts, and anyone who bypassed the waiting room', then select 'Continue'.

breadcrumb: What should I do if I have a medical condition and cannot be vaccinated?
text:
What should I do if I have a medical condition and cannot be vaccinated?

Most people with medical conditions can be safely vaccinated. You should discuss this with your medical practitioner. If it is deemed that there is an unacceptable risk for any of the mandatory vaccinations, please contact immunisation@uq.edu.au for further advice. Where risk of infection from a vaccine preventable disease (VPD) cannot be acceptably managed, students and workers will be denied placements or be unable to undertake tasks that place them or others at risk of VPD transmission. Further advice regarding suitability for enrolment or work tasks can be obtained by contacting your shcool, faculty or supervisor.

The suite of postgraduate clinical pharmacy programs (Graduate Certificate in Clinical Pharmacy, Graduate Diploma in Clinical Pharmacy and Master of Clinical Pharmacy) are delivered fully online – allowing pharmacists from all over Australia, practicing in a variety of clinical settings, to enrol and participate.

3-4 online tutorials for each course per semester will be held in a virtual classroom with input from leading experts. Tutorial attendance is an important component of the program. You are expected to attend at least 2 of the 3 tutorials for each course per semester.

page_title: Troubleshooting common problems with button panel audiovisual system
breadcrumb: Troubleshooting common problems with button panel audiovisual system > Nothing is showing on the screen in the room
h2: Nothing is showing on the screen in the room
text:
Troubleshooting common problems with button panel audiovisual system > Nothing is showing on the screen in the room

Make sure you have selected the correct input source.

If you're using the room's desktop PC, make sure it is turned on and awake.

If you're using your own device (e.g. a laptop), make sure your display settings are correct. Follow the instructions in the 'Connecting your own device' section of the system user guide.

Check that volume is turned up and not muted on:

- your laptop or the room's desktop PC
- the AV system
- the software you're using to play sound.

Make sure you've selected the correct audio playback option on the desktop PC or your device. Follow the instructions for audio and volume controls in the system user guide.

Make sure:

- the microphone is set to the correct channel and frequency for the room. Check the radio microphone channels and frequencies used at UQ
- the switch at the top of the bodypack is set to 'MIC'
- the microphone is turned up and not muted.

page_title: Will I get to interact with my classmates in the postgraduate Clinical Pharmacy programs?
breadcrumb: Will I get to interact with my classmates in the postgraduate Clinical Pharmacy programs?
text:
Will I get to interact with my classmates in the postgraduate Clinical Pharmacy programs?

Yes, our learning community model in the suite of postgraduate Clinical Pharmacy programs (Graduate Certificate in Clinical Pharmacy, Graduate Diploma in Clinical Pharmacy and Master of Clinical Pharmacy) allows you to share experiences with and learn from other students to develop professional networks. There are some group learning tasks online to prepare for tutorials, but most learning activities (including group blogs and assignments) will be completed and assessed individually.

Optional weekend intensives in Brisbane are offered for PHRM7010 in March and for PHRM7040 in August. These intensives are delivered in a workshop format and small group work, facilitated by experienced practitioners, to maximise interactivity and the development of clinical skills .These intensives provide an opportunity for you to meet other students that you will be working with and your course coordinators. Attendance at the intensive will provide credit for some online content and assessment for the respective course.

page_title: When using ArcGIS I get a 'tool not licensed' error message. What should I do?
breadcrumb: When using ArcGIS I get a 'tool not licensed' error message. What should I do?
text:
When using ArcGIS I get a 'tool not licensed' error message. What should I do?

You'll need to enable extensions (Spacial Analyst, 3D Analyst etc.) and display the extension toolbars in ArcGIS before you can use them.

To enable extensions in ArcGIS:

- Click 'Customize' and then select 'Extensions...'.
- Tick the box next to each extension you want to use.
- Click 'Close'.

To display extension toolbars:

- Click ‘Customize’ and then select ‘Toolbars’.
- Select the extension you want to use.

Make sure you turn off any extension toolbars when you're finished. This keeps the data sets smaller in file size.

Detailed instructions on this process with screenshots are available on the ArcGIS website.

text:
As an American student, can I defer repayments on my US student loans while I study at UQ?

Full-time students at UQ can defer their US Federal student loan repayments provided they remain enrolled full time. For more information, contact the UQ Financial Aid office at financialaid@uq.edu.au.

page_title: Are the placement tests for language courses written tests or interviews?
breadcrumb: Are the placement tests for language courses written tests or interviews?
text:
Are the placement tests for language courses written tests or interviews?

The School of Languages and Cultures have an online course placement advisor where students complete a questionnaire describing their previous education and experience with the language. Part of the questionnaire may involve writing a short paragraph in the language. After completing the questionnaire, students are then emailed with instructions as to which course(s) to enrol in. Occasionally, after completing the questionnaire, students may be contacted to attend a short (15 minute) placement interview.

Visit the online language course advisor to complete a course placement questionnaire.

page_title: Why did we wait until 1 July 2018 to become smoke-free?
breadcrumb: Why did we wait until 1 July 2018 to become smoke-free?
text:
Why did we wait until 1 July 2018 to become smoke-free?

The decision to transition to smoke-free was approved by the University Senior Management Group mid-July 2017. The policy commencement date of 1 July 2018 was to allow an appropriate period of time for the promotion, communication and socialisation of the transition to smoke-free campuses.

This period also provided an opportunity for staff and students to avail themselves of the support options offered to assist them with quitting smoking.

page_title: Is second-hand smoke really harmful?
breadcrumb: Is second-hand smoke really harmful?
text:
Is second-hand smoke really harmful?

Medical evidence is irrefutable that second-hand smoke (passive smoke) causes serious diseases and harm to others, including non-smokers. There are links to relevant research on a range of sites including the International Agency for Research of Cancer (IARC) and the World Health Organisation (WHO).

page_title: Have other universities gone smoke-free?
breadcrumb: Have other universities gone smoke-free?
text:
Have other universities gone smoke-free?

Have other universities gone smoke-free? Home Information and services Yes. The smoke-free initiative undertaken by UQ has already been implemented by many other Australian universities and many more are in the process of transitioning to smoke-free.

page_title: Does completing the suite of postgraduate clinical pharmacy programs provide accreditation to perform medication reviews?
breadcrumb: Does completing the suite of postgraduate clinical pharmacy programs provide accreditation to perform medication reviews?
text:
Does completing the suite of postgraduate clinical pharmacy programs provide accreditation to perform medication reviews?

No, completing the postgraduate clinical pharmacy programs (Graduate Certificate in Clinical Pharmacy, Graduate Diploma in Clinical Pharmacy and Master of Clinical Pharmacy) will provide you with the foundational knowledge to prepare you to undertake the accreditation process through the Society of Hospital Pharmacists of Australia (SHPA) or the Australian Association of Consultant Pharmacy (AACP).

If you receive an error message when logging in, send a screenshot of your browser window showing the error message and URL to studenthub@uq.edu.au for assistance.

A recommended companion is a course that students are advised to take before or at the same time as another course.

See the my.UQ website for advice on choosing your courses.

A recommended companion is a course that students are advised to take before or at the same time as another course.

page_title: How can I get help completing an Alternative examination arrangement request?
breadcrumb: How can I get help completing an Alternative examination arrangement request?
text:
How can I get help completing an Alternative examination arrangement request?

Before submitting an alternative examination arrangement request, please ensure that you have read and understood the information on my.UQ.

If you are having trouble submitting the request, check the following:

- All required fields are completed.
- There are no error messages displayed on the request.
- You are submitting the request using a Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox browser.

If you still need help, contact Student Central for advice.

page_title: Who can see my immunisation and screening history when I upload my medical evidence to my.UQ?
breadcrumb: Who can see my immunisation and screening history when I upload my medical evidence to my.UQ?
text:
Who can see my immunisation and screening history when I upload my medical evidence to my.UQ?

The medical documentation you upload to my.UQ will be visible to the central Immunisation Records Team (IRT) comprised of a small team of immunisation nurses and administration staff, and their medical advisor (Occupational Physician). It is not visible to the faculty or organisational unit staff.

Further information regarding privacy is detailed in Section 6 (Recording and Reporting) of the University's Vaccinations and Immunisation Procedure.

page_title: The comment left by the Immunisation Records Team (IRT) states I have received three doses of Hepatitis B on an accelerated schedule. Why must I provide evidence of a fourth dose?
breadcrumb: The comment left by the Immunisation Records Team (IRT) states I have received three doses of Hepatitis B on an accelerated schedule. Why must I provide evidence of a fourth dose?
text:
The comment left by the Immunisation Records Team (IRT) states I have received three doses of Hepatitis B on an accelerated schedule. Why must I provide evidence of a fourth dose?

Occasionally, GP’s will administer patients with an accelerated hepatitis B vaccination schedule where vaccinations are administered over a shortened timeframe. This schedule is only recommended for persons with an imminent risk of exposure when there is limited time for completion of the usual vaccination course. To complete an accelerated course a fourth dose must be administered 12 months after the initial dose date to ensure long lasting immunity.

page_title: What is acceptable supporting evidence when submitting my request for exemption from late submission penalties for online exams?
breadcrumb: What is acceptable supporting evidence when submitting my request for exemption from late submission penalties for online exams?
text:
What is acceptable supporting evidence when submitting my request for exemption from late submission penalties for online exams?

You will be required to provide time-stamped photos or screenshots in English that show when you started submitting your exam, and progress of the online submission up until you successfully submitted your exam. Your name and/or student number needs to be visible in the photos or screenshots. You will also need to provide a transcript of your online chat seeking assistance from AskUs Exam Support, ProctorU, your course coordinator or school.

page_title: Can I keep my student account internet and H drive access if I'm not enrolled?
breadcrumb: Can I keep my student account internet and H drive access if I'm not enrolled?
text:
Can I keep my student account internet and H drive access if I'm not enrolled?

If you're not enrolled as a UQ student, you'll no longer be able to access the internet or your home directory (also called H drive).

If you have a valid reason to maintain full student account access whilst not enrolled, please contact your School manager or supervisor. They'll need to submit an IT request outlining your case.

page_title: There is more than one option when searching for a course code for a late addition of course request, which one do I choose?
breadcrumb: There is more than one option when searching for a course code for a late addition of course request, which one do I choose?
text:
There is more than one option when searching for a course code for a late addition of course request, which one do I choose?

If a course is available for late addition across multiple teaching periods, campuses, or delivery modes, then you will see multiple search results to choose from.

Please ensure that you select the correct course by checking the mode, campus and session.

For example, if you would like to enrol in a course in Semester 2, 2022 and will be enrolling in the External Delivery of the course, you should select the course that has the mode of ‘External Delivery’ and the session of ‘Semester 2, 2022’. For courses that are offered across multiple campuses, please check that you select the course with the correct campus.

It is possible to apply for the same course code in different sessions, delivery modes or campuses within one request.

If you still need help, contact Student Central for advice.

page_title: I log into my personal Windows computer with my UQ email address. How do I copy my personal data to another account?
breadcrumb: I log into my personal Windows computer with my UQ email address. How do I copy my personal data to another account?
text:
I log into my personal Windows computer with my UQ email address. How do I copy my personal data to another account?

Please close all files and programs before following the instructions below.

- Click the 'Start' menu.
- Search for and open 'This PC'.
- Navigate to C:\Users, then open your user profile.
- Select the folders you want to copy.
 The screenshot below shows an example of a user folder. The folders highlighted in blue are the suggested folders to copy.
- Right-click and select 'Copy'.
 
 
 
- Go back to the 'Users' folder and open the user folder you want to copy the folders to.
- Right-click and select 'Paste'.

page_title: Why is there a new process to report suspicious emails?
breadcrumb: Why is there a new process to report suspicious emails?
text:
Why is there a new process to report suspicious emails?

UQ staff and students continue to be faced with a variety of email-based scams and threats. The complexity of these scams and their threat to the UQ community only continues to grow, requiring proactive action from the University to mitigate these threats.

Suspicious emails can be reported using the Phish Alert (staff only) and Microsoft (students) buttons.

page_title: I was a Guest in Microsoft Teams, but no longer have access after the student mailbox migration. What should I do?
breadcrumb: I was a Guest in Microsoft Teams, but no longer have access after the student mailbox migration. What should I do?
text:
I was a Guest in Microsoft Teams, but no longer have access after the student mailbox migration. What should I do?

Following the student mailbox migration, you may no longer have access to previously joined teams in Microsoft Teams.

An automated process has been delivered to reinstate your access to the team(s) you were a member of in the UQ M365 environment.

If there are teams you still need access to that are external to UQ, please reach out to the Team Owner. If uncertain of the owner, contact the administrator of the external organisation for assistance. They can help identify the appropriate Team Owner and facilitate re-inviting you as a guest user.
